title: Fundamentals

hero.label: // Book 01 of 19 — Fundamentals
hero.title:
Application, Document,
Elements & Parameters
hero.description: The foundational layer of every Revit add-in. Master these and everything else becomes straightforward.

sections.title: Application & Document
sections.content:
// 01

Application & Document

Every command starts with ExternalCommandData. From it you navigate down to the document and model elements.

Object Hierarchy

hierarchy
UIApplication          // Revit application + UI layer
  └── UIDocument       // Active open document (with selection)
        └── Document   // The Revit project/family file (.rvt/.rfa)
              └── Element  // Any object in the model

Getting the Document — Standard Pattern

C#
public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData commandData,
    ref string message, ElementSet elements)
{
    // Application layer
    UIApplication uiapp = commandData.Application;
    Application   app   = uiapp.Application;

    // Document layer
    UIDocument uidoc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument;
    Document   doc   = uidoc.Document;

    // Document info
    string title    = doc.Title;
    bool   isFam    = doc.IsFamilyDocument;
    string filePath = doc.PathName;

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

Key Document Members

MemberTypeDescription
doc.TitlestringFile name without extension
doc.PathNamestringFull file path. Empty if unsaved
doc.IsFamilyDocumentboolTrue if this is a .rfa family file
doc.ActiveViewViewCurrently active view
doc.ApplicationApplicationThe Revit Application object
doc.CreateCreation.DocumentFactory for creating elements
doc.FamilyCreateCreation.FamilyItemFactoryFactory for family geometry (family docs only)
doc.GetElement(id)ElementLook up element by ElementId
doc.Delete(id)ICollection<ElementId>Delete element, returns all deleted ids
doc.Save()voidSave the document
doc.IsModifiedboolWhether document has unsaved changes
// Opening Other Documents Use uiapp.Application.OpenDocumentFile(filePath) to open another .rvt. Use doc.LoadFamily(path, out family) to load a .rfa into the current project.

sections.title: Element Hierarchy
sections.content:
// 02

Element Hierarchy

Everything in Revit is an Element. Understanding the type hierarchy is critical for filtering and casting correctly.

hierarchy
Element
  ├── Instance elements    // physical objects placed in model
  │     ├── Wall, Floor, Ceiling, Roof
  │     ├── FamilyInstance  // doors, windows, furniture, columns
  │     ├── Room, Space, Area
  │     └── MEP: Pipe, Duct, CableTray, Conduit
  ├── Type elements        // define the 'blueprint'
  │     ├── WallType, FloorType, RoofType
  │     ├── FamilySymbol    // a specific size/variant of a family
  │     └── Family          // the family container
  └── Non-geometric        // configuration / meta
        ├── Level, Grid, ReferencePlane
        ├── View, ViewSheet, ViewPlan
        └── Material, LineStyle

Accessing Element Properties

C#
Element elem = doc.GetElement(new ElementId(12345));

// Universal properties on every element
ElementId id       = elem.Id;
string    name     = elem.Name;
Category  cat      = elem.Category;
ElementId typeId   = elem.GetTypeId();
Level     level    = doc.GetElement(elem.LevelId) as Level;

// Get the element type
ElementType type = doc.GetElement(elem.GetTypeId()) as ElementType;

// Check the category
if (elem.Category.Id.IntegerValue == (int)BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls)
{
    Wall wall = elem as Wall;
}

// Bounding box (null if element has no geometry)
BoundingBoxXYZ bb = elem.get_BoundingBox(doc.ActiveView);
// Instance vs Type Instance elements are placed in the model. Type elements define their template. A FamilyInstance (door) references a FamilySymbol (door type) which belongs to a Family (door family). Always check both instance AND type parameters.

sections.title: Parameters
sections.content:
// 03

Parameters

Parameters are the data attached to elements. There are four types and five ways to access them.

🔧
Built-in
Hardcoded by Autodesk. Most reliable. Use BuiltInParameter enum.
BuiltInParameter
📋
Project
Created by user in project. Access by name or GUID.
LookupParameter()
🔗
Shared
Defined in external .txt file. Access by GUID (most stable).
get_Parameter(Guid)
🌐
Global
Document-level parameters not attached to a category.
GlobalParametersManager

Reading Parameters

C#
Element elem = ...;

// Method 1: BuiltIn (BEST — language independent, fast)
Parameter p1 = elem.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);

// Method 2: By Name (fragile — breaks with language change)
Parameter p2 = elem.LookupParameter("Mark");

// Method 3: By GUID (shared params — very stable)
Parameter p3 = elem.get_Parameter(new Guid("your-guid-here"));

// Method 4: Get all parameters
ParameterSet allParams = elem.Parameters;

// Reading values — check StorageType first!
if (p1 != null)
{
    switch (p1.StorageType)
    {
        case StorageType.String:
            string val = p1.AsString();          break;
        case StorageType.Double:
            double dbl = p1.AsDouble();          break;
        case StorageType.Integer:
            int    num = p1.AsInteger();         break;
        case StorageType.ElementId:
            ElementId eid = p1.AsElementId();   break;
    }

    // AsValueString() always returns a human-readable string
    string display = p1.AsValueString();
}

Setting Parameters

C#
// Always inside a Transaction!
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Set Parameter"))
{
    tx.Start();

    Parameter p = wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);

    if (p != null && !p.IsReadOnly)
    {
        p.Set("W-001");         // string
        // p.Set(3.5);           // double (internal units = feet)
        // p.Set(1);             // integer (or enum int value)
        // p.Set(new ElementId(x)); // ElementId
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

Parameter StorageType Reference

StorageTypeRead MethodSet MethodNotes
StringAsString()Set(string)Text values. AsValueString() also works.
DoubleAsDouble()Set(double)Always in internal units (feet). Convert with UnitUtils.
IntegerAsInteger()Set(int)Also used for Yes/No (1/0) and enums.
ElementIdAsElementId()Set(ElementId)References to other elements (material, level, etc).
// Unit Conversion All double parameters are stored in internal units (feet). To set 3000mm: p.Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters)). To read in mm: UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits(p.AsDouble(), UnitTypeId.Millimeters).

sections.title: Filtering Elements
sections.content:
// 04

Filtering Elements

FilteredElementCollector is the main query engine. Always filter with native (quick) filters first, LINQ second.

Quick Filters (Fast — run in Revit's native engine)

C#
// By Class (fastest — use when possible)
var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .Cast<Wall>()
    .ToList();

// By Category
var doors = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Doors)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()   // instances only
    .ToList();

// Element Types only (families, wall types etc.)
var wallTypes = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(WallType))
    .Cast<WallType>()
    .ToList();

// In a specific view (only elements visible in that view)
var inView = new FilteredElementCollector(doc, doc.ActiveView.Id)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls)
    .ToList();

// At a specific level
var levelFilter = new ElementLevelFilter(level.Id);
var onLevel = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(levelFilter)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls)
    .ToList();

// Multiple categories at once
var cats = new List<BuiltInCategory>
    { BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls, BuiltInCategory.OST_Floors };
var multiCat = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(new ElementMulticategoryFilter(cats))
    .ToList();

Parameter Filters (Slow — but powerful)

C#
// Filter walls where Mark contains "EXT"
var provider  = new ParameterValueProvider(
    new ElementId(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK));
var evaluator = new FilterStringContains();
var rule      = new FilterStringRule(provider, evaluator, "EXT");

var paramFilter = new ElementParameterFilter(rule);

var extWalls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WherePasses(paramFilter)
    .Cast<Wall>()
    .ToList();

Retrieval Methods

MethodReturnsUse When
.ToElements()IList<Element>You need the elements themselves (most common)
.ToElementIds()ICollection<ElementId>You only need IDs (faster, less memory)
.FirstElement()ElementYou only need one result
.FirstOrDefault()ElementLINQ — first or null
.Cast<T>()IEnumerable<T>After OfClass() — gives typed results
.Count()intJust count — don't load elements
// Performance Rule Chain quick filters first: .OfClass() → .OfCategory() → .WhereElementIsNotElementType() then apply LINQ .Where() last. Never call .ToList() mid-chain before all native filters are applied.

sections.title: Selection
sections.content:
// 05

Selection

Get elements the user has selected, or prompt them to pick elements interactively.

C#
Selection sel = uidoc.Selection;

// 1. Get currently selected elements
ICollection<ElementId> selectedIds = sel.GetElementIds();
foreach (var id in selectedIds)
{
    Element e = doc.GetElement(id);
}

// 2. Pick a single element
Reference pickedRef = sel.PickObject(
    ObjectType.Element, "Select an element");
Element picked = doc.GetElement(pickedRef);

// 3. Pick a specific type (e.g. Wall only)
Reference wallRef = sel.PickObject(
    ObjectType.Element,
    new WallSelectionFilter(),   // custom ISelectionFilter
    "Select a wall");

// 4. Pick multiple elements
IList<Reference> multiRefs = sel.PickObjects(
    ObjectType.Element, "Select walls");

// 5. Pick a point in the model
XYZ pt = sel.PickPoint("Click a point");

// 6. Set selection programmatically
var idsToSelect = walls.Select(w => w.Id).ToList();
sel.SetElementIds(idsToSelect);

Custom Selection Filter

C#
// Implement ISelectionFilter to restrict what the user can pick
public class WallSelectionFilter : ISelectionFilter
{
    public bool AllowElement(Element elem)
        => elem is Wall;    // only walls can be picked

    public bool AllowReference(Reference reference, XYZ position)
        => true;
}

sections.title: Transactions
sections.content:
// 06

Transactions

Any modification to the Revit model MUST happen inside a Transaction. This is non-negotiable.

Transaction Types

📝
Transaction
Single operation. Start → do work → Commit or RollBack.
most common
📦
TransactionGroup
Wraps multiple Transactions into one undo step for the user.
batch operations
🔄
SubTransaction
Nested within an open Transaction. Can be rolled back independently.
partial rollback

Transaction Patterns

C#
// Pattern 1: Basic (using = auto-dispose)
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "My Operation"))
{
    tx.Start();
    // ... modify elements ...
    tx.Commit();
}

// Pattern 2: With error handling
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Safe Operation"))
{
    tx.Start();
    try
    {
        // ... work ...
        tx.Commit();
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        tx.RollBack();
        TaskDialog.Show("Error", ex.Message);
    }
}

// Pattern 3: TransactionGroup (one undo step)
using (var tg = new TransactionGroup(doc, "Batch Update"))
{
    tg.Start();

    using (var tx1 = new Transaction(doc, "Step 1"))
    { tx1.Start(); /* ... */ tx1.Commit(); }

    using (var tx2 = new Transaction(doc, "Step 2"))
    { tx2.Start(); /* ... */ tx2.Commit(); }

    tg.Assimilate();  // merges all into one undo step
}

// Pattern 4: SubTransaction (partial rollback)
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Main"))
{
    tx.Start();
    using (var sub = new SubTransaction(doc))
    {
        sub.Start();
        try { /* risky work */ sub.Commit(); }
        catch { sub.RollBack(); /* main tx continues */ }
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

Transaction Status

StatusMeaning
TransactionStatus.StartedTransaction is open and ready
TransactionStatus.CommittedChanges saved to model
TransactionStatus.RolledBackChanges discarded
TransactionStatus.ErrorSomething went wrong — check exceptions
// Golden Rules 1) Never start a Transaction inside an event handler that fires during a transaction. 2) Never use a Transaction from a background thread — use IExternalEventHandler instead. 3) Always check TransactionStatus after Commit() in critical flows.

sections.title: Units
sections.content:
// 07

Units

Revit stores ALL distances in decimal feet internally. You must convert when reading/writing length parameters.

C#
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;

// Convert FROM internal (feet) TO display unit
double internalValue = wall.get_Parameter(
    BuiltInParameter.WALL_USER_HEIGHT_PARAM).AsDouble();

double mm      = UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits(
    internalValue, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double meters  = UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits(
    internalValue, UnitTypeId.Meters);

// Convert TO internal (feet) FROM display unit
double feet = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

// Simple manual conversion (legacy approach)
double mmVal    = internalValue * 304.8;
double feetVal  = mmVal / 304.8;

Common UnitTypeId Values

UnitTypeIdUse For
UnitTypeId.MillimetersLength in mm (most common in metric)
UnitTypeId.MetersLength in metres
UnitTypeId.FeetLength in feet (imperial)
UnitTypeId.SquareMetersArea
UnitTypeId.CubicMetersVolume
UnitTypeId.DegreesAngles

desc: Application hierarchy, Elements, Parameters, Filtering, Selection and Transactions. The foundation of every add-in.

title: Namespace Reference Vol. 2

hero.label: // Book 10 of 19 — API Reference · DB (F–Z) · UI · Events · Failures
hero.title:
Namespace Reference
Volume 2
hero.description: Complete class reference for Autodesk.Revit.DB (F–Z), the full UI namespace including IExternalCommand, IExternalEventHandler, ExternalEvent, TaskDialog, Selection, and RibbonPanel, plus all document and application event argument classes.

sections.title: Face
sections.content:
ClassFace
Inherits: GeometryObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a face (surface) on a Revit solid. Faces can be planar (PlanarFace), cylindrical (CylindricalFace), conical, ruled, or Nurbs. Each Face provides access to its normal, area, UV parameters, edge loops, and a Reference for dimensioning. Always obtain faces through a Solid — Face cannot be instantiated directly.
// NoteFace.Reference is only valid when Options.ComputeReferences = true was set during get_Geometry(). Without this, Reference will be null and cannot be used for dimensioning.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public Reference Reference { get; }
Gets a Reference to this face that can be used for dimensioning with doc.Create.NewDimension(). Only valid when Options.ComputeReferences = true was set.
🟠public double Area { get; }
Gets the surface area of this face in internal units (square feet). Convert to m² by multiplying by 0.092903.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public XYZ ComputeNormal(UV uv)
Computes the outward normal vector of this face at the given UV parameter position. For a PlanarFace the normal is constant across the entire face. For curved faces the normal varies.
ParameterTypeDescription
uvUVThe UV parameter at which to compute the normal. Use (0.5, 0.5) for the face centre.
Returns: XYZ — Unit normal vector pointing outward from the solid.
// Example — Face normal and area extraction
Options opts = new Options { ComputeReferences = true };
GeometryElement geom = wall.get_Geometry(opts);

foreach (GeometryObject obj in geom)
{
    if (obj is Solid solid && solid.Volume > 0)
    {
        foreach (Face face in solid.Faces)
        {
            XYZ    normal  = face.ComputeNormal(new UV(.5, .5));
            double areaSqM = face.Area * 0.092903;
            Reference fRef = face.Reference;  // for dimensions

            // Classify face direction
            if (Math.Abs(normal.Z) > 0.9)
                System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Horizontal face: {areaSqM:F2}m²");
            else
                System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Vertical face: {areaSqM:F2}m²");
        }
    }
}
🟢public Mesh Triangulate()
Returns a triangulated mesh approximation of this face. Used for custom geometry export, rendering, area calculations, and 3D printing workflows.
Returns: Mesh — Triangulated mesh with Vertices and Triangles collections.
// Example — Triangulate a face for OBJ export
Face face = ...;
Mesh mesh = face.Triangulate();

IList<XYZ>   verts     = mesh.Vertices;
int          numTris  = mesh.NumTriangles;

for (int i = 0; i < numTris; i++)
{
    MeshTriangle tri = mesh.get_Triangle(i);
    XYZ v0 = tri.get_Vertex(0);
    XYZ v1 = tri.get_Vertex(1);
    XYZ v2 = tri.get_Vertex(2);
    // Write "f i0 i1 i2" to OBJ file
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"f {tri.get_Index(0)} {tri.get_Index(1)} {tri.get_Index(2)}");
}
🟢public IList<CurveLoop> GetEdgesAsCurveLoops()
Returns the boundary of this face as a list of CurveLoops — one outer loop and any inner loops (holes). Used for extracting face profiles for re-use as floor/ceiling boundaries or for computing perimeter.
Returns: IList<CurveLoop> — Index 0 = outer boundary; subsequent loops = holes.
🟢public IntersectionResultArray Intersect(Curve curve)
Finds intersections between this face and a curve. Returns each point where the curve crosses the face surface. Used for ray casting, pipe-to-floor intersection detection etc.

sections.title: FamilyInstance
sections.content:
ClassFamilyInstance
Inherits: Instance → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a placed instance of a loaded family in the model — doors, windows, columns, furniture, equipment, light fixtures, mechanical equipment, plumbing fixtures etc. Every FamilyInstance has a FamilySymbol (type) and a Family. FamilyInstance is the most common class in large Revit models.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public FamilySymbol Symbol { get; set; }
Gets or sets the FamilySymbol (type) of this instance. Setting changes the type and requires an open Transaction. The new symbol must be from the same Family.
🟠public Element Host { get; }
Gets the host element if this is a hosted family instance (door in wall, window in wall, light in ceiling). Returns null for unhosted (level-based) instances.
🟠public bool HandFlipped { get; }
Gets whether this instance has been hand-flipped (mirrored across the hand orientation plane). Relevant for asymmetric families like doors.
🟠public bool FacingFlipped { get; }
Gets whether this instance has been facing-flipped (mirrored across the facing orientation plane).
🟠public StructuralType StructuralType { get; }
Gets the structural role of this instance (NonStructural, Beam, Column, Brace, Footing). Used to distinguish structural from non-structural family instances.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public IList<Reference> GetReferences(FamilyInstanceReferenceType referenceType)
Returns references to named reference planes within the family geometry. Used to get dimensionable references to specific planes like Left/Right/Front/Back/CenterLeftRight/CenterFrontBack.
ParameterTypeDescription
referenceTypeFamilyInstanceReferenceTypeLeft, Right, Front, Back, Top, Bottom, CenterLeftRight, CenterFrontBack, CenterElevation, StrongReference, WeakReference
// Example — FamilyInstance references for dimensioning
FamilyInstance door = ...;

// Get references to the left and right planes of the door
IList<Reference> leftRefs  = door.GetReferences(FamilyInstanceReferenceType.Left);
IList<Reference> rightRefs = door.GetReferences(FamilyInstanceReferenceType.Right);

if (leftRefs.Count > 0 && rightRefs.Count > 0)
{
    ReferenceArray refs = new ReferenceArray();
    refs.Append(leftRefs[0]);
    refs.Append(rightRefs[0]);

    LocationPoint lp = door.Location as LocationPoint;
    Line dimLine = Line.CreateBound(
        lp.Point + new XYZ(0, 2, 0),
        lp.Point + new XYZ(3, 2, 0));

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Dimension Door"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        doc.Create.NewDimension(doc.ActiveView, dimLine, refs);
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// Navigate the family hierarchy
FamilySymbol sym    = door.Symbol;
Family       family = sym.Family;
string info = $"Family: {family.Name} | Type: {sym.Name}";

// Change the type
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Change Type"))
{
    tx.Start();
    if (!newSym.IsActive) newSym.Activate();
    door.Symbol = newSym;
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: FamilySymbol
sections.content:
ClassFamilySymbol
Inherits: ElementType → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a specific type within a loaded family. For example, a "900×2100 Single Flush" door type is a FamilySymbol within the "Single-Flush" Family. You must call Activate() before placing a FamilySymbol that has never been used in the model.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public Family Family { get; }
Gets the Family that this symbol belongs to.
🟠public bool IsActive { get; }
Gets whether this symbol is active (has been loaded into the current session). Must be true before placing. Call Activate() if false.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public void Activate()
Activates this FamilySymbol so it can be placed in the model. Must be called inside a Transaction before the first NewFamilyInstance() call if IsActive is false. Harmless to call even if already active.
// Example — Activate and place a family symbol
FamilySymbol sym = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Furniture)
    .Cast<FamilySymbol>()
    .FirstOrDefault(s => s.Name.Contains("Desk"));

if (sym == null) return Result.Failed;

Level level = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Place Desk"))
{
    tx.Start();
    if (!sym.IsActive) sym.Activate();   // Always check!

    FamilyInstance desk = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
        new XYZ(5, 5, 0), sym, level,
        StructuralType.NonStructural);
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Floor
sections.content:
ClassFloor
Inherits: HostObject → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a floor slab element. Floors are created using Floor.Create() with a list of CurveLoops defining the boundary (outer loop + optional inner holes). Access the compound structure layers via FloorType.
🟢 Static Factory Methods
🟣public static Floor Create(Document doc, IList<CurveLoop> profile, ElementId floorTypeId, ElementId levelId)
Creates a floor from one or more CurveLoops. The first CurveLoop is the outer boundary; additional loops define holes/openings in the floor. All loops must be planar and co-planar.
ParameterTypeDescription
docDocumentThe document to create the floor in.
profileIList<CurveLoop>Boundary loops. [0] = outer profile, [1..n] = inner holes.
floorTypeIdElementIdThe ElementId of the FloorType to use.
levelIdElementIdThe ElementId of the Level this floor sits on.
Returns: Floor — The newly created Floor element.
// Example — Floor with a rectangular hole
FloorType ft = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FloorType)).Cast<FloorType>().First();
Level lv = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

// Outer boundary 10×8 metres
CurveLoop outer = new CurveLoop();
outer.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0),  new XYZ(32.8,0,0)));
outer.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(32.8,0,0),new XYZ(32.8,26.2,0)));
outer.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(32.8,26.2,0),new XYZ(0,26.2,0)));
outer.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,26.2,0),new XYZ(0,0,0)));

// Inner hole 2×2 metres (stairwell opening)
CurveLoop hole = new CurveLoop();
hole.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(10,10,0),new XYZ(16.6,10,0)));
hole.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(16.6,10,0),new XYZ(16.6,16.6,0)));
hole.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(16.6,16.6,0),new XYZ(10,16.6,0)));
hole.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(10,16.6,0),new XYZ(10,10,0)));

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Floor"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Floor floor = Floor.Create(doc,
        new List<CurveLoop> { outer, hole },
        ft.Id, lv.Id);
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Grid
sections.content:
ClassGrid
Inherits: DatumPlane → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a grid datum element. Grids are used as reference lines for structural and architectural coordination. They appear in plan views and can be dimensioned using new Reference(grid) — NOT grid.Curve.Reference.
// Critical — Grid ReferencesUse new Reference(grid) to get a reference to a grid for dimensioning. Do NOT use grid.Curve.Reference — that returns null for grids.
🟢 Static Factory + Methods
🟣public static Grid Create(Document doc, Line line)
Creates a straight grid from a line. The grid is infinite in plan view but clipped to the line endpoints as the default extent.
// Example — Grid system creation and dimensioning
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Grids"))
{
    tx.Start();
    double spacing = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(6000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

    // Create 4 vertical grids A-D
    string[] names = { "A", "B", "C", "D" };
    List<Grid> grids = new List<Grid>();
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
    {
        double x = i * spacing;
        Line line = Line.CreateBound(
            new XYZ(x, -5, 0), new XYZ(x, 25, 0));
        Grid g = Grid.Create(doc, line);
        g.Name = names[i];
        grids.Add(g);
    }

    // Dimension between grids using new Reference(grid)
    ReferenceArray refs = new ReferenceArray();
    foreach (var g in grids)
        refs.Append(new Reference(g));   // ← CORRECT

    Line dimLine = Line.CreateBound(
        new XYZ(0, -8, 0), new XYZ(3 * spacing, -8, 0));
    doc.Create.NewDimension(doc.ActiveView, dimLine, refs);

    tx.Commit();
}
🟢public Curve Curve { get; }
Gets the curve defining the grid line's position and extent. For straight grids this is a Line; for arc grids this is an Arc.

sections.title: JoinGeometryUtils
sections.content:
ClassJoinGeometryUtils
Inherits: System.Object (static utility)
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Static utility class for joining and unjoining element geometry. When elements are joined, Revit cuts the overlapping volume to show a proper geometric union (e.g. wall-to-slab, column-to-beam). Joining and unjoining requires a Transaction.
🟢 Static Methods
🟢public static void JoinGeometry(Document doc, Element firstElement, Element secondElement)
Joins the geometry of two intersecting elements. After joining, one element's solid is cut by the other based on join order. Both elements must intersect geometrically.
// Example — Join wall to floor and check status
Wall  wall  = ...;
Floor floor = ...;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Join"))
{
    tx.Start();

    if (!JoinGeometryUtils.AreElementsJoined(doc, wall, floor))
        JoinGeometryUtils.JoinGeometry(doc, wall, floor);

    // Swap join order so wall wins (cuts floor)
    if (JoinGeometryUtils.GetJoinedElements(doc, wall).Contains(floor.Id))
        JoinGeometryUtils.SwitchJoinOrder(doc, wall, floor);

    tx.Commit();
}
🟢public static bool AreElementsJoined(Document doc, Element firstElement, Element secondElement)
Returns true if the two elements are currently joined. Use before calling JoinGeometry() to avoid errors on already-joined pairs.
Returns: bool — true if joined.
🟢public static void SwitchJoinOrder(Document doc, Element firstElement, Element secondElement)
Reverses which element cuts the other. In a wall-to-column join, if the column is being cut by the wall, switching makes the wall be cut by the column instead.
🟢public static void UnjoinGeometry(Document doc, Element firstElement, Element secondElement)
Removes the join between two elements, restoring their independent geometry.

sections.title: Level
sections.content:
ClassLevel
Inherits: DatumPlane → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a horizontal datum plane (floor level) in the Revit model. All model elements are attached to a level. Levels drive floor plan views and define the reference elevation for all associated elements.
🟢 Static Factory + Properties
🟣public static Level Create(Document doc, double elevation)
Creates a new level at the specified elevation in internal units (feet). The level name defaults to "Level N" but should be set immediately after creation.
// Example — Create levels from floor heights
double[] floorHeightsMM = { 0, 3500, 7000, 10500, 14000 };
string[] levelNames     = { "Ground", "Level 1", "Level 2", "Level 3", "Roof" };

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Levels"))
{
    tx.Start();
    for (int i = 0; i < floorHeightsMM.Length; i++)
    {
        double elevFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(
            floorHeightsMM[i], UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
        Level lvl = Level.Create(doc, elevFt);
        lvl.Name = levelNames[i];
    }
    tx.Commit();
}
🟠public double Elevation { get; set; }
Gets or sets the level elevation in internal units (feet). Setting this moves all attached elements. Always convert from/to mm using UnitUtils.

sections.title: Line
sections.content:
ClassLine
Inherits: Curve → APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a straight line in 3D space. The most common curve type — used for walls, CurveLoops, dimensions, grids, model lines, and detail lines. Created via static factory methods.
🟢 Static Factory Methods
🟣public static Line CreateBound(XYZ endpoint0, XYZ endpoint1)
Creates a bounded (finite) line between two points. This is the most commonly used Line factory method. Both endpoints are required; the line has a definite start and end.
// Example — Line usage patterns
// Bounded line (has start/end points)
Line bounded = Line.CreateBound(
    new XYZ(0, 0, 0),
    new XYZ(10, 0, 0));

// Unbound line (infinite — direction only)
Line infinite = Line.CreateUnbound(new XYZ(0,0,0), XYZ.BasisX);

// Line properties
XYZ    start  = bounded.GetEndPoint(0);
XYZ    end    = bounded.GetEndPoint(1);
double length = bounded.Length;
XYZ    dir    = bounded.Direction;   // unit vector start→end
XYZ    mid    = bounded.Evaluate(0.5, true);

// Project a point onto the line
XYZ pt = new XYZ(5, 3, 0);
IntersectionResult proj = bounded.Project(pt);
XYZ projected = proj.XYZPoint;
double dist = proj.Distance;
🟣public static Line CreateUnbound(XYZ origin, XYZ direction)
Creates an unbounded (infinite) line through origin in the given direction. Used for infinite geometry operations like plane intersections. IsBound = false.
🟠public XYZ Direction { get; }
Gets the unit direction vector from start to end point. Always normalised to length 1.0.

sections.title: Material
sections.content:
ClassMaterial
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a Revit material definition including surface graphics, cut graphics, render appearance, physical properties, and thermal properties. Materials are referenced by CompoundStructure layers and by element geometry parameters.
🟢 Static Factory + Properties + Methods
🟣public static ElementId Create(Document doc, string name)
Creates a new material with the given name. Returns the ElementId of the new material. The material starts with default white colour and no patterns.
// Example — Create and configure a material
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Material"))
{
    tx.Start();
    ElementId matId = Material.Create(doc, "Concrete - Exposed");
    Material  mat   = doc.GetElement(matId) as Material;

    mat.Color            = new Color(180, 175, 170);
    mat.Transparency     = 0;    // 0–100
    mat.Shininess        = 20;   // 0–128
    mat.SmoothnessFactor = 50;
    mat.MaterialClass    = "Concrete";

    // Set cut fill colour
    mat.CutForegroundPatternColor = new Color(100, 100, 100);
    tx.Commit();
}

// Find material by name
Material concrete = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Material))
    .Cast<Material>()
    .FirstOrDefault(m => m.Name.Contains("Concrete"));

sections.title: Options
sections.content:
ClassOptions
Inherits: System.Object
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Controls how geometry is retrieved via Element.get_Geometry(). Three key settings: View (which view's visibility rules apply), ComputeReferences (needed for dimensioning), and IncludeNonVisibleObjects.
🟠 Properties
🟠public View View { get; set; }
The view whose visibility settings are applied during geometry retrieval. Pass null for model geometry without view filtering. Setting a view may return different geometry (e.g. symbolic representation in plan views).
🟠public bool ComputeReferences { get; set; }
When true, Face.Reference and Edge.Reference are populated, enabling dimensioning. Setting this to true is slower but required for any workflow involving NewDimension().
🟠public bool IncludeNonVisibleObjects { get; set; }
When true, geometry from invisible lines, reference planes and symbolic lines is included. Usually set to false for solid geometry extraction.
// Example — Options configurations
// For solid extraction (volume, area calculations)
var solidOpts = new Options
{
    ComputeReferences        = false,
    IncludeNonVisibleObjects = false
};

// For dimensioning (must have references)
var dimOpts = new Options
{
    ComputeReferences        = true,
    View                     = doc.ActiveView,
    IncludeNonVisibleObjects = false
};

// For symbolic geometry in plan view
var symOpts = new Options
{
    View                     = doc.ActiveView,
    IncludeNonVisibleObjects = true
};

sections.title: OverrideGraphicSettings
sections.content:
ClassOverrideGraphicSettings
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Defines graphic overrides applied to elements or categories in a view. Used with View.SetElementOverrides() (per element) or ParameterFilterElement + View.SetFilterOverrides() (per rule). Controls colour, line weight, pattern, transparency, and halftone for projection and cut representations.
🟢 Key Methods
// Example — Full override workflow
var ogs = new OverrideGraphicSettings();

// Projection (visible faces) settings
ogs.SetProjectionLineColor(new Color(255, 0, 0));
ogs.SetProjectionLineWeight(6);        // 1–16
ogs.SetSurfaceTransparency(30);        // 0–100
ogs.SetSurfaceForegroundPatternColor(new Color(255, 200, 200));
ogs.SetIsHalftone(false);

// Cut (section cut) settings
ogs.SetCutLineColor(new Color(200, 0, 0));
ogs.SetCutLineWeight(8);
ogs.SetCutFillColor(new Color(255, 200, 200));

// Apply to specific elements
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Override"))
{
    tx.Start();
    foreach (var wall in walls)
        doc.ActiveView.SetElementOverrides(wall.Id, ogs);
    tx.Commit();
}

// Clear overrides — pass a new empty OverrideGraphicSettings
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Clear"))
{
    tx.Start();
    doc.ActiveView.SetElementOverrides(wall.Id,
        new OverrideGraphicSettings());  // empty = reset to default
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Parameter
sections.content:
ClassParameter
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a single parameter on an element. Provides typed access to the parameter's value (AsString, AsDouble, AsInteger, AsElementId) and metadata (StorageType, IsReadOnly, Definition). All writes require an open Transaction.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public StorageType StorageType { get; }
Gets how the value is stored internally: String, Double, Integer, or ElementId. Always check StorageType before calling the read methods.
🟠public bool IsReadOnly { get; }
True if the parameter cannot be written. Computed parameters (like Host Area Computed) are always read-only. Always check before calling Set().
🟠public Definition Definition { get; }
Gets the definition of this parameter, including its name, parameter type, and group. Cast to InternalDefinition for built-in parameters or ExternalDefinition for shared parameters.
🟠public bool IsShared { get; }
True if this is a shared parameter (defined in a shared parameter file with a GUID). Shared parameters are accessible by GUID via elem.get_Parameter(Guid).
🟢 Read Methods
🟢public string AsString()
Returns the value as a string. Only valid when StorageType == StorageType.String. Returns null for other types.
🟢public double AsDouble()
Returns the value as a double in internal units (feet for lengths, sq.ft for areas etc.). Only valid when StorageType == StorageType.Double. Always convert with UnitUtils.
🟢public int AsInteger()
Returns the value as an integer. Valid for StorageType.Integer. Used for enums, Yes/No (1/0), and integer counts.
🟢public ElementId AsElementId()
Returns the value as an ElementId. Valid for StorageType.ElementId. Used for material parameters, level parameters, phase parameters etc.
🟢public string AsValueString()
Returns the value as a user-facing formatted string regardless of StorageType. For length doubles this returns "4500mm" rather than a raw number. Useful for display purposes only.
🟢 Write Methods
🟢public bool Set(string value) // also: Set(double), Set(int), Set(ElementId)
Sets the parameter value. Must match the parameter's StorageType — call the correct overload. Returns true if set successfully. Must be inside an open Transaction. Check IsReadOnly before calling.
// Example — Complete parameter read/write pattern
Element elem = ...;
Parameter p  = elem.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);

if (p == null) return;

// Read based on StorageType
switch (p.StorageType)
{
    case StorageType.String:
        string s = p.AsString();                 break;
    case StorageType.Double:
        double mm = p.AsDouble() * 304.8;        break;
    case StorageType.Integer:
        int    n  = p.AsInteger();               break;
    case StorageType.ElementId:
        ElementId eid = p.AsElementId();         break;
}

// Write (inside a transaction)
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Set Param"))
{
    tx.Start();
    if (!p.IsReadOnly)
    {
        bool ok = p.Set("W-001");   // string overload
        // p.Set(3.281);              // double overload (feet)
        // p.Set(1);                  // int overload
        // p.Set(new ElementId(x));   // ElementId overload
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Plane
sections.content:
ClassPlane
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents an infinite 2D plane in 3D space, defined by an origin and a normal vector. Used to create SketchPlanes, to project points, and to define arc orientations. Plane is immutable — create a new one rather than modifying an existing one.
🟢 Static Factory Methods
🟣public static Plane CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ normal, XYZ origin)
Creates a plane from a normal vector and an origin point. The most common factory method. The normal defines which direction is "up" for the plane.
// Example — Plane creation patterns
// XY plane at Z=0 (horizontal)
Plane xy = Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisZ, XYZ.Zero);

// Vertical plane along X-axis
Plane xz = Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisY, XYZ.Zero);

// Plane from 3 points
Plane p3 = Plane.CreateByThreePoints(
    new XYZ(0,0,0),
    new XYZ(1,0,0),
    new XYZ(0,1,0));

// Create a SketchPlane from a Plane (needed for family geometry)
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "SketchPlane"))
{
    tx.Start();
    SketchPlane sp = SketchPlane.Create(doc, xy);
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: ReferenceIntersector
sections.content:
ClassReferenceIntersector
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Performs ray casting against element geometry in a 3D view. Given a ray (origin + direction), ReferenceIntersector finds all elements that the ray hits. Used for proximity detection, height finding, automatic pipe-to-floor opening creation, and sight-line analysis.
🟣 Constructors
🟣public ReferenceIntersector(View3D view)
Creates a ReferenceIntersector that tests against all geometry visible in the given 3D view. The view must be a View3D — the ray casting works in the 3D view's coordinate space.
// Example — Ray cast downward to find floor elevation
// Get or create a 3D view for ray casting
View3D view3D = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(View3D))
    .Cast<View3D>()
    .FirstOrDefault(v => !v.IsTemplate);

if (view3D == null) return Result.Failed;

// Create ray caster — filter for floors only
var floorFilter = new ElementClassFilter(typeof(Floor));
var ri = new ReferenceIntersector(floorFilter,
    FindReferenceTarget.Face, view3D)
{
    FindReferencesInRevitLinks = false
};

// Cast a ray downward from a point at ceiling height
XYZ origin    = new XYZ(10, 10, 20);   // high up
XYZ direction = -XYZ.BasisZ;            // straight down

ReferenceWithContext nearest = ri.FindNearest(origin, direction);
if (nearest != null)
{
    double floorZ = origin.Z - nearest.Proximity;
    TaskDialog.Show("Floor Z",
        $"Floor elevation: {floorZ * 304.8:F0}mm");
}

// Find ALL hits along the ray
IList<ReferenceWithContext> allHits = ri.Find(origin, direction);
foreach (var hit in allHits.OrderBy(h => h.Proximity))
{
    Element elem = doc.GetElement(hit.GetReference());
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Hit: {elem.Category.Name} at {hit.Proximity * 304.8:F0}mm");
}

sections.title: Solid
sections.content:
ClassSolid
Inherits: GeometryObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a 3D solid body in Revit geometry. Solids have Faces, Edges, Volume, and SurfaceArea. They are retrieved from Element.get_Geometry() and used for volume calculations, boolean operations, and clash detection. Solids cannot be created directly — they come from element geometry.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public double Volume { get; }
Gets the volume of the solid in internal units (cubic feet). Multiply by 0.0283168 to get m³. A volume of 0 or near-zero indicates an empty/degenerate solid.
🟠public double SurfaceArea { get; }
Gets the total surface area of all faces in internal units (sq.ft). Multiply by 0.092903 for m².
🟠public FaceArray Faces { get; }
Gets all the Face objects that bound this solid. Enumerate with foreach to access each face's area, normal, and geometry.
🟠public EdgeArray Edges { get; }
Gets all Edge objects of this solid. Each edge is bounded by two faces and provides a curve.
// Example — Extract solid and perform Boolean intersection
// Helper: extract first valid solid from element
Solid GetSolid(Element e)
{
    var opts = new Options { ComputeReferences = false };
    foreach (GeometryObject obj in e.get_Geometry(opts))
    {
        if (obj is Solid s && s.Volume > 1e-9) return s;
        if (obj is GeometryInstance gi)
            foreach (GeometryObject o in gi.GetInstanceGeometry())
                if (o is Solid s2 && s2.Volume > 1e-9) return s2;
    }
    return null;
}

// Boolean intersection between two solids
Solid sa = GetSolid(elem1);
Solid sb = GetSolid(elem2);

if (sa != null && sb != null)
{
    Solid intersection = BooleanOperationsUtils
        .ExecuteBooleanOperation(sa, sb,
            BooleanOperationsType.Intersect);

    if (intersection?.Volume > 1e-9)
        TaskDialog.Show("Clash",
            $"Clash volume: {intersection.Volume * 0.0283168:F5}m³");
}

sections.title: StorageType
sections.content:
EnumStorageType
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Identifies how a parameter's value is stored internally. Always check StorageType before calling AsString/AsDouble/AsInteger/AsElementId to avoid InvalidOperationException.
🟡 Enum Values
MemberValueRead MethodWrite MethodNotes
None0——Unset or invalid parameter
Integer1AsInteger()Set(int)Also used for Yes/No (1/0) and enum int values
Double2AsDouble()Set(double)Always in internal units (feet). Must convert!
String3AsString()Set(string)Text values
ElementId4AsElementId()Set(ElementId)References to other elements (material, level, etc.)

sections.title: Transaction
sections.content:
ClassTransaction
Inherits: APIObject · IDisposable
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Wraps a set of model changes into a single undo/redo step. Every write to the Revit model (creating elements, modifying parameters, deleting) MUST happen inside an open Transaction. Transactions must be Started, then Committed or RolledBack. Use the using() pattern for automatic disposal.
// Critical Rules1) Never start a Transaction inside an event handler that fires during a transaction. 2) Never call Revit API from a background thread — use IExternalEventHandler. 3) Always check tx.GetStatus() in critical workflows. 4) Use TransactionGroup to batch multiple Transactions into one undo step.
🟣 Constructors
🟣public Transaction(Document doc, string name)
Creates a Transaction for the given document. The name appears in the Undo history. The transaction is NOT yet started — call Start() before making changes.
ParameterTypeDescription
docDocumentThe document to create the transaction in.
namestringName shown in the Revit Undo/Redo history. Should be descriptive.
// Example — All Transaction patterns
// Pattern 1: Basic (using = auto dispose)
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Wall"))
{
    tx.Start();
    // ... modify model ...
    tx.Commit();
}

// Pattern 2: With error handling
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Safe Create"))
{
    tx.Start();
    try
    {
        // ... risky work ...
        tx.Commit();
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        tx.RollBack();
        TaskDialog.Show("Error", ex.Message);
    }
}

// Pattern 3: TransactionGroup — one undo step for multiple transactions
using (var tg = new TransactionGroup(doc, "Batch Update"))
{
    tg.Start();
    using (var tx1 = new Transaction(doc, "Step 1"))
    { tx1.Start(); /* work */ tx1.Commit(); }
    using (var tx2 = new Transaction(doc, "Step 2"))
    { tx2.Start(); /* work */ tx2.Commit(); }
    tg.Assimilate();   // merge into one undo step
}

// Pattern 4: SubTransaction (partial rollback inside a transaction)
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Main"))
{
    tx.Start();
    using (var sub = new SubTransaction(doc))
    {
        sub.Start();
        try { /* risky work */ sub.Commit(); }
        catch { sub.RollBack(); }   // main tx continues
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

// Pattern 5: Suppress warnings
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "No Warnings"))
{
    FailureHandlingOptions opts = tx.GetFailureHandlingOptions();
    opts.SetFailuresPreprocessor(new SuppressWarningsPreprocessor());
    tx.SetFailureHandlingOptions(opts);
    tx.Start();
    /* work that might produce warnings */
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Transform
sections.content:
ClassTransform
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents an affine transformation: a combination of translation, rotation, and scaling. The transform is defined by an origin (translation) and three basis vectors (BasisX, BasisY, BasisZ) that define the local coordinate frame's orientation. Used extensively for section view creation, element placement, and coordinate system conversions.
🟠 Key Properties + Static Factory
🟠public static Transform Identity { get; }
Gets the identity transform (no translation, no rotation, scale=1). The BasisX=X, BasisY=Y, BasisZ=Z, Origin=(0,0,0).
🟠public XYZ Origin { get; set; }
Gets or sets the translation vector (origin of the local coordinate system in world space).
🟠public XYZ BasisX { get; set; }
Gets or sets the X-axis of the local coordinate frame (horizontal direction for sections).
🟠public XYZ BasisY { get; set; }
Gets or sets the Y-axis of the local coordinate frame (vertical direction for sections).
🟠public XYZ BasisZ { get; set; }
Gets or sets the Z-axis (camera/view direction for sections). Must form a right-handed coordinate system with BasisX and BasisY.
🟢public static Transform CreateRotation(XYZ axis, double angle)
Creates a rotation transform around the given axis by the given angle in radians. The rotation is about the world origin — combine with a translation if you need to rotate around a specific point.
// Example — Transform for section view and element rotation
// Build a Transform for a section view cut perpendicular to Y-axis
Transform t = Transform.Identity;
t.Origin = new XYZ(5, 0, 3);       // where the section cuts
t.BasisX = XYZ.BasisX;              // horizontal = X
t.BasisY = XYZ.BasisZ;              // vertical = Z (up)
t.BasisZ = XYZ.BasisY;              // into screen = Y

// Rotate 45° around Z-axis
Transform rot45 = Transform.CreateRotation(XYZ.BasisZ, Math.PI / 4);

// Translate by (5, 0, 0)
Transform move = Transform.CreateTranslation(new XYZ(5, 0, 0));

// Combine: first rotate, then translate
Transform combined = move.Multiply(rot45);

// Transform a point
XYZ worldPt = combined.OfPoint(new XYZ(1, 0, 0));

// Transform a vector (ignores translation)
XYZ worldVec = combined.OfVector(XYZ.BasisX);

sections.title: UnitUtils
sections.content:
ClassUnitUtils
Inherits: System.Object (static utility)
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Static utility class for converting between Revit's internal units (always decimal feet) and any display unit. Every parameter of type Double is stored in internal units. Always use UnitUtils to convert — never hardcode conversion factors for units other than feet↔mm (×304.8).
🟢 Static Methods
🟢public static double ConvertToInternalUnits(double value, ForgeTypeId unitTypeId)
Converts a value FROM the specified unit TO internal units (feet for lengths). Use this before passing any measurement to the API (wall height, pipe diameter, etc.).
// Example — Complete unit conversion reference
// TO internal units (for writing to API)
double heightFt  = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double widthFt   = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(4.5,  UnitTypeId.Meters);
double areaFt2   = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(25.5, UnitTypeId.SquareMeters);
double volFt3    = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(100,  UnitTypeId.CubicMeters);
double angleFt   = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(30,   UnitTypeId.Degrees);

// FROM internal units (for reading from API / displaying to user)
double wallH = wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.WALL_USER_HEIGHT_PARAM)
    .AsDouble();
double wallH_mm = UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits(wallH, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double wallH_m  = UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits(wallH, UnitTypeId.Meters);

// Common UnitTypeId values:
// Length:  Millimeters, Meters, Feet, Inches
// Area:    SquareMillimeters, SquareMeters, SquareFeet
// Volume:  CubicMeters, CubicFeet, Liters
// Angle:   Degrees, Radians
// Force:   Kilonewtons, PoundsForce
// Temp:    Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin
🟢public static double ConvertFromInternalUnits(double value, ForgeTypeId unitTypeId)
Converts a value FROM internal units TO the specified display unit. Use this when reading a parameter value to display to the user.

sections.title: Wall
sections.content:
ClassWall
Inherits: HostObject → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a wall element. Walls are created using Wall.Create() overloads. A wall has a WallType (defines compound layers), a location curve (its centerline or face line), height, and can host doors/windows. The most commonly created host element in the Revit API.
🟢 Static Factory Methods
🟣public static Wall Create(Document doc, Curve curve, ElementId wallTypeId, ElementId levelId, double height, double offset, bool flip, bool structural)
Creates a wall from a curve. The most common overload. Height and offset are in internal units (feet). Set structural=true for walls that contribute to the structural model.
ParameterTypeDescription
docDocumentTarget document
curveCurveLocation curve (Line or Arc). Wall follows this curve.
wallTypeIdElementIdWallType to use (determines layer thickness and materials)
levelIdElementIdBase level — wall's base is at this level
heightdoubleUnconnected height in feet. 0 = top connected to next level
offsetdoubleBase offset from level in feet. Usually 0.
flipboolFlips exterior/interior face orientation
structuralbooltrue = structural wall (contributes to analytical model)
Returns: Wall — The created wall element.
// Example — Create a wall and read its properties
// Get first WallType and Level
WallType wt = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(WallType)).Cast<WallType>().First();
Level lv = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

double heightFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
Line   curve    = Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(10,0,0));

Wall wall;
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Wall"))
{
    tx.Start();
    wall = Wall.Create(doc, curve, wt.Id, lv.Id,
        heightFt, 0.0, false, false);
    tx.Commit();
}

// Read wall properties
WallKind      kind     = wall.WallType.Kind;          // Basic/Stacked/Curtain
bool           flipped  = wall.Flipped;
double         widthFt  = wall.Width;                  // thickness in feet
double         widthMm  = widthFt * 304.8;
LocationCurve  lc       = wall.Location as LocationCurve;
double         lengthFt = lc.Curve.Length;
XYZ            start    = lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(0);
XYZ            end      = lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(1);

// Flip the wall (swap exterior/interior)
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Flip Wall"))
{ tx.Start(); wall.flip(); tx.Commit(); }

sections.title: XYZ
sections.content:
ClassXYZ
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents both a 3D point and a 3D vector. XYZ is immutable — all operations return new XYZ instances. Used for everything: element placement, geometry, normals, axes, force vectors, and coordinate transformations.
🟣 Constructor + Static Members
🟣public XYZ(double x, double y, double z)
Creates a new XYZ with the given X, Y, Z coordinates in internal units (feet for positions). Also serves as a vector constructor.
ParameterTypeDescription
xdoubleX coordinate (feet for positions, unitless for vectors)
ydoubleY coordinate
zdoubleZ coordinate
// Example — Complete XYZ operations reference
// Static constants
XYZ origin = XYZ.Zero;      // (0,0,0)
XYZ bx     = XYZ.BasisX;    // (1,0,0)
XYZ by     = XYZ.BasisY;    // (0,1,0)
XYZ bz     = XYZ.BasisZ;    // (0,0,1)

// Arithmetic (operator overloads)
XYZ a = new XYZ(3, 0, 0);
XYZ b = new XYZ(0, 4, 0);
XYZ sum  = a + b;         // (3,4,0)
XYZ diff = a - b;         // (3,-4,0)
XYZ neg  = -a;            // (-3,0,0)
XYZ scl  = a * 2.5;      // (7.5,0,0)

// Vector operations
double dot   = a.DotProduct(b);     // scalar
XYZ    cross = a.CrossProduct(b);   // perpendicular vector (0,0,12)
XYZ    norm  = a.Normalize();       // unit vector (1,0,0)
double len   = a.GetLength();       // 3.0
double dist  = a.DistanceTo(b);    // 5.0

// Angle between vectors (radians)
double angle = a.AngleTo(b);       // Math.PI/2 = 90°
double angleDeg = angle * (180 / Math.PI);

// Signed angle (in a plane)
double signedAngle = a.AngleOnPlaneTo(b, XYZ.BasisZ);

// Component access
double x = a.X;   // 3.0
double y = a.Y;   // 0.0
double z = a.Z;   // 0.0

// Equality (within tolerance)
bool eq = a.IsAlmostEqualTo(b);         // false
bool eq2 = a.IsAlmostEqualTo(b, 0.001); // with custom tolerance

sections.title: IExternalCommand
sections.content:
InterfaceIExternalCommand
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI · Assembly: RevitAPIUI.dll
The entry point interface for every Revit add-in command. Any class decorated with [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)] or .Automatic and implementing this interface becomes a Revit command that can be triggered from the ribbon, keyboard shortcuts, or PostCommand.
🟢 Required Method
🟢public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData commandData, ref string message, ElementSet elements)
The method called by Revit when the command runs. Return Result.Succeeded, Result.Failed (sets message), or Result.Cancelled. The message and elements parameters are used to report errors to the user.
ParameterTypeDescription
commandDataExternalCommandDataProvides access to UIApplication, UIDocument, and the active view.
messageref stringSet this to an error description if returning Result.Failed.
elementsElementSetPopulate with elements related to the failure for Revit to highlight them.
Returns: Result — Succeeded, Failed, or Cancelled.
// Example — Complete IExternalCommand implementation
[Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
[Regeneration(RegenerationOption.Manual)]
public class WallCountCommand : IExternalCommand
{
    public Result Execute(
        ExternalCommandData commandData,
        ref string message,
        ElementSet elements)
    {
        try
        {
            // Access document
            UIApplication uiapp = commandData.Application;
            UIDocument    uidoc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument;
            Document      doc   = uidoc.Document;

            // Null check
            if (doc == null)
            {
                message = "No active document.";
                return Result.Failed;
            }

            // Do work
            int count = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
                .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
                .GetElementCount();

            TaskDialog.Show("Wall Count", $"Found {count} walls");
            return Result.Succeeded;
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            message = ex.Message;
            return Result.Failed;
        }
    }
}

sections.title: IExternalApplication
sections.content:
InterfaceIExternalApplication
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI · Assembly: RevitAPIUI.dll
The lifecycle interface for Revit add-ins. OnStartup fires when Revit loads the add-in — use it to subscribe to events, create ribbon panels, and register ExternalEvents and DMU updaters. OnShutdown fires when Revit closes — always unsubscribe events and dispose ExternalEvents here.
🟢 Required Methods
🟢public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication application)
Called once when Revit loads the add-in. Set up all persistent infrastructure here: ribbon UI, event subscriptions, ExternalEvent registration, DMU updater registration.
// Example — Complete IExternalApplication implementation
public class MyApplication : IExternalApplication
{
    public static ExternalEvent   MyEvent    { get; private set; }
    public static MyEventHandler  MyHandler  { get; private set; }

    public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        // 1. Create ribbon
        RibbonPanel panel = app.CreateRibbonPanel("Studio Tools");
        var btnData = new PushButtonData(
            "WallCount", "Count\nWalls",
            System.Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location,
            "MyPlugin.WallCountCommand");
        panel.AddItem(btnData);

        // 2. Register ExternalEvent
        MyHandler = new MyEventHandler();
        MyEvent   = ExternalEvent.Create(MyHandler);

        // 3. Subscribe to document events
        app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened  += OnDocOpened;
        app.ControlledApplication.DocumentChanged += OnDocChanged;
        app.ViewActivated                          += OnViewActivated;

        return Result.Succeeded;
    }

    public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        // Always unsubscribe — prevents memory leaks
        app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened  -= OnDocOpened;
        app.ControlledApplication.DocumentChanged -= OnDocChanged;
        app.ViewActivated                          -= OnViewActivated;
        MyEvent.Dispose();
        return Result.Succeeded;
    }

    private void OnDocOpened(object s, DocumentOpenedEventArgs e) { }
    private void OnDocChanged(object s, DocumentChangedEventArgs e) { }
    private void OnViewActivated(object s, ViewActivatedEventArgs e) { }
}
🟢public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication application)
Called when Revit is closing or the add-in is unloaded. ALWAYS unsubscribe all events and dispose all ExternalEvent instances here to prevent memory leaks and crashes.

sections.title: IExternalEventHandler
sections.content:
InterfaceIExternalEventHandler
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI · Assembly: RevitAPIUI.dll
The bridge between background threads/WPF windows and the Revit API. Because the Revit API cannot be called from background threads, implement this interface to queue work that will be executed in the main Revit thread at the next idle cycle when ExternalEvent.Raise() is called.
// The Bridge PatternBackground thread → sets data on handler properties → calls ExternalEvent.Raise() → Revit calls Execute(uiapp) on the main thread → reads data → calls API. This is thread-safe because all API calls happen on the main thread.
🟢 Required Methods
🟢public void Execute(UIApplication app)
Called by Revit on the main thread when ExternalEvent.Raise() has been called. This runs in a valid Revit API context — you can start transactions, modify elements, etc. Use public properties on the handler to pass data from the background thread.
// Example — Complete IExternalEventHandler + ExternalEvent pattern
// Step 1: Implement the handler
public class SetMarkHandler : IExternalEventHandler
{
    // Thread-safe data transfer via properties
    public ElementId TargetId    { get; set; }
    public string    MarkValue   { get; set; }
    public bool      Success     { get; private set; }

    public void Execute(UIApplication app)
    {
        try
        {
            Document doc = app.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
            if (doc == null) { Success = false; return; }

            using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Set Mark"))
            {
                tx.Start();
                Element   elem = doc.GetElement(TargetId);
                Parameter mark = elem?.get_Parameter(
                    BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);
                if (mark != null && !mark.IsReadOnly)
                    mark.Set(MarkValue);
                tx.Commit();
                Success = true;
            }
        }
        catch { Success = false; }
    }

    public string GetName() => "Set Mark Handler";
}

// Step 2: Register in OnStartup
var handler = new SetMarkHandler();
var extEvent = ExternalEvent.Create(handler);

// Step 3: Raise from WPF button click
private void BtnApply_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
{
    handler.TargetId  = selectedElementId;
    handler.MarkValue = txtMark.Text;

    ExternalEventRequest req = extEvent.Raise();

    switch (req)
    {
        case ExternalEventRequest.Accepted:
            lblStatus.Content = "Queued ✔"; break;
        case ExternalEventRequest.Pending:
            lblStatus.Content = "Previous request still pending"; break;
        default:
            lblStatus.Content = $"Request {req}"; break;
    }
}
🟢public string GetName()
Returns a display name for this handler. Used in Revit's UI and debug logs. Should be a short, descriptive string like "Set Mark Handler".

sections.title: ExternalEvent
sections.content:
ClassExternalEvent
Inherits: IDisposable
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI · Assembly: RevitAPIUI.dll
The event object that bridges background threads to the Revit API. Created from an IExternalEventHandler, it accepts Raise() calls from any thread and causes Revit to execute the handler's Execute() method on the main thread at the next idle cycle.
🟢 Static Factory + Key Methods
🟣public static ExternalEvent Create(IExternalEventHandler handler)
Creates an ExternalEvent from the given handler. Must be called during OnStartup() or Execute() — not from background threads. Store the result as a static field for access from WPF windows.
Returns: ExternalEvent — The registered event object. Keep this reference alive for the add-in's lifetime.
🟢public ExternalEventRequest Raise()
Requests that Revit execute the handler at the next available idle cycle. Can be called from any thread. Returns the request status — check for Accepted before assuming success.
Returns: ExternalEventRequest — Accepted (queued), Pending (previous still running), or Denied (Revit busy).
🟠public bool IsPending { get; }
True if a previous Raise() is still queued and has not been executed yet. Check this before calling Raise() again to avoid queuing duplicate requests.

sections.title: TaskDialog
sections.content:
ClassTaskDialog
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI · Assembly: RevitAPIUI.dll
Revit's built-in modal dialog. Provides a Revit-styled dialog with optional command links, radio buttons, and expanded content. Use for simple notifications, confirmations, and option selection. For complex UI use WPF windows instead.
🟢 Static Show + Instance Usage
🟣public static TaskDialogResult Show(string title, string content)
Shows a simple notification dialog with title and content text. Blocks until the user closes it. Returns TaskDialogResult.Ok.
// Example — TaskDialog patterns
// Simple notification
TaskDialog.Show("Wall Count", $"Found {count} walls");

// Yes/No confirmation
TaskDialog dlg = new TaskDialog("Confirm Delete");
dlg.MainInstruction = "Delete all unused views?";
dlg.MainContent = $"This will permanently delete {count} views.";
dlg.CommonButtons = TaskDialogCommonButtons.Yes | TaskDialogCommonButtons.No;
dlg.DefaultButton = TaskDialogResult.No;

TaskDialogResult result = dlg.Show();
if (result == TaskDialogResult.Yes)
{
    // proceed with deletion
}

// Dialog with command links
TaskDialog td = new TaskDialog("Choose Action");
td.MainInstruction = "Select operation:";
td.AddCommandLink(TaskDialogCommandLinkId.CommandLink1, "Create floor plans");
td.AddCommandLink(TaskDialogCommandLinkId.CommandLink2, "Create sections");
td.AddCommandLink(TaskDialogCommandLinkId.CommandLink3, "Create both");

TaskDialogResult chosen = td.Show();
switch (chosen)
{
    case TaskDialogResult.CommandLink1: /* plans */  break;
    case TaskDialogResult.CommandLink2: /* sections */break;
    case TaskDialogResult.CommandLink3: /* both */    break;
}

sections.title: Selection
sections.content:
ClassSelection
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI.Selection · Assembly: RevitAPIUI.dll
Manages the user selection in the active document. Provides methods to get currently selected elements, prompt the user to pick elements, pick points in the model, and programmatically set the selection. Access via UIDocument.Selection.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public ICollection<ElementId> GetElementIds()
Gets the IDs of all currently selected elements. Returns an empty collection (not null) if nothing is selected.
🟢public Reference PickObject(ObjectType objectType, string statusPrompt)
Prompts the user to pick a single element or sub-element (face, edge, point). Blocks execution until the user picks or presses Escape (which throws OperationCanceledException).
// Example — All Selection patterns
Selection sel = uidoc.Selection;

// Get current selection
ICollection<ElementId> ids = sel.GetElementIds();

// Pick one element
try
{
    Reference ref1 = sel.PickObject(
        ObjectType.Element, "Select any element");
    Element picked = doc.GetElement(ref1);
}
catch (OperationCanceledException) { return Result.Cancelled; }

// Pick with filter (walls only)
try
{
    Reference wallRef = sel.PickObject(
        ObjectType.Element,
        new WallOnlyFilter(),
        "Select a wall");
}
catch (OperationCanceledException) { }

// Pick multiple elements
IList<Reference> refs = sel.PickObjects(
    ObjectType.Element, "Select multiple elements");

// Pick a point
XYZ pt = sel.PickPoint("Click a point in the model");

// Set selection programmatically
var toSelect = walls.Select(w => w.Id).ToList();
sel.SetElementIds(toSelect);

// Custom selection filter
public class WallOnlyFilter : ISelectionFilter
{
    public bool AllowElement(Element elem) => elem is Wall;
    public bool AllowReference(Reference r, XYZ p) => true;
}

sections.title: DocumentChangedEventArgs
sections.content:
ClassDocumentChangedEventArgs
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Events · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Event arguments for Application.DocumentChanged. Fires after every transaction commit, undo, or redo. The context is READ-ONLY — you CANNOT modify the document inside this handler. Use DMU (IUpdater) for write reactions.
// Read-Only ContextDocumentChanged fires in a read-only context. Any attempt to start a Transaction will throw InvalidOperationException. Use IUpdater (DMU) for write reactions, or queue work via ExternalEvent.Raise().
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public ICollection<ElementId> GetAddedElementIds()
Returns IDs of elements added in the transaction that triggered this event.
🟢public ICollection<ElementId> GetDeletedElementIds()
Returns IDs of elements deleted in the triggering transaction.
🟢public ICollection<ElementId> GetModifiedElementIds()
Returns IDs of elements modified (parameter change, geometry change, etc.) in the triggering transaction.
🟢public Document GetDocument()
Returns the document that changed. Use to access elements via doc.GetElement(id).
🟢public ICollection<string> GetTransactionNames()
Returns the names of the transactions that caused this change. Useful for detecting undo/redo operations (the transaction name will be the original operation's name).
// Example — DocumentChanged handler
private void OnDocumentChanged(object sender, DocumentChangedEventArgs e)
{
    Document doc = e.GetDocument();

    ICollection<ElementId> added    = e.GetAddedElementIds();
    ICollection<ElementId> deleted  = e.GetDeletedElementIds();
    ICollection<ElementId> modified = e.GetModifiedElementIds();

    // Log new walls only
    foreach (var id in added)
    {
        Element elem = doc.GetElement(id);
        if (elem is Wall wall)
        {
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"New wall: {wall.Id} length={wall.Width*304.8:F0}mm");
        }
    }

    // Detect undo/redo
    var txNames = e.GetTransactionNames();
    // If names contains previous transaction names → undo/redo happened

    // CANNOT write to model here — READ-ONLY context!
    // Use ExternalEvent.Raise() or IUpdater instead
}

sections.title: DocumentOpenedEventArgs
sections.content:
ClassDocumentOpenedEventArgs
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Events
Event arguments for Application.DocumentOpened. Fires after a document is fully opened and ready. Safe to read all document data here.
🟠 Key Properties + Example
🟠public Document Document { get; }
Gets the Document that was opened.
🟠public bool IsCancelled { get; }
True if the open operation was cancelled before completion (rare).
// Example
private void OnDocumentOpened(object sender, DocumentOpenedEventArgs e)
{
    Document doc = e.Document;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Opened: {doc.Title} at {doc.PathName}");

    // Can read the document — cannot write without a transaction
    int wallCount = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).GetElementCount();
}

sections.title: DocumentSavingEventArgs
sections.content:
ClassDocumentSavingEventArgs
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Events
Event arguments for Application.DocumentSaving. Fires BEFORE the document is saved, giving you the opportunity to run validation or cancel the save.
🟢 Key Method
🟢public void Cancel()
Cancels the save operation. Call this when validation fails and you want to prevent the save from completing.
// Example — Pre-save validator
private void OnDocumentSaving(object sender, DocumentSavingEventArgs e)
{
    Document doc = e.Document;

    // Check for duplicate marks before saving
    var marks = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
        .Cast<Wall>()
        .Select(w => w.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)?.AsString())
        .Where(m => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(m))
        .ToList();

    bool hasDuplicates = marks.Count != marks.Distinct().Count();
    if (hasDuplicates)
    {
        TaskDialog.Show("Save Blocked", "Duplicate wall marks found! Fix before saving.");
        e.Cancel();   // prevent save
    }
}

sections.title: ViewActivatedEventArgs
sections.content:
ClassViewActivatedEventArgs
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI.Events
Arguments for UIApplication.ViewActivated. Fires whenever the user switches to a different view. Use to update dockable panels, ribbon state, or filter ComboBoxes based on the new active view type.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public View CurrentActiveView { get; }
The view the user just switched TO.
🟠public View PreviousActiveView { get; }
The view the user switched FROM. May be null on the first activation.
// Example — Update ribbon based on view type
private void OnViewActivated(object sender, ViewActivatedEventArgs e)
{
    View cur  = e.CurrentActiveView;
    View prev = e.PreviousActiveView;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Switched from {prev?.Name} to {cur.Name} ({cur.ViewType})");

    // Enable floor-plan specific tools
    bool isFloorPlan = cur.ViewType == ViewType.FloorPlan;
    // _myFloorPlanButton.Enabled = isFloorPlan;
}

sections.title: IdlingEventArgs
sections.content:
ClassIdlingEventArgs
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI.Events
Arguments for UIApplication.Idling. Fires during Revit idle time — a valid API context. Prefer ExternalEvent over Idling for modeless dialog integration since Idling fires constantly and hurts performance.
🟢 Key Method
🟢public void SetRaiseWithoutDelay(bool value)
Controls how frequently Idling fires. Pass false to allow Revit to delay the next Idling call (kinder to CPU — recommended for polling tasks). Pass true for maximum frequency (use only when immediately needed).
// Example — Idling with CPU management
private int _idleTick = 0;

private void OnIdling(object sender, IdlingEventArgs e)
{
    // Reduce CPU — only act every 50th idle cycle
    e.SetRaiseWithoutDelay(false);

    if (++_idleTick % 50 != 0) return;

    UIApplication uiapp = sender as UIApplication;
    if (uiapp?.ActiveUIDocument == null) return;

    // Valid API context — can create a transaction here
    // But prefer ExternalEvent for modeless dialog integration!
}

sections.title: IFailuresPreprocessor
sections.content:
InterfaceIFailuresPreprocessor
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Allows a command to intercept and handle transaction failures (warnings and errors) before Revit shows its default failure dialog. Implement to silently suppress warnings or handle specific error types programmatically.
🟢 Required Method
🟢public FailureProcessingResult PreprocessFailures(FailuresAccessor failuresAccessor)
Called when failures occur during transaction commit. Inspect failures, delete warnings, or return ProceedWithRollBack to abort. Return Continue to proceed with commit after handling warnings.
// Example — Suppress all warnings silently
public class SuppressAllWarnings : IFailuresPreprocessor
{
    public FailureProcessingResult PreprocessFailures(
        FailuresAccessor accessor)
    {
        foreach (var failure in accessor.GetFailureMessages())
        {
            if (failure.GetSeverity() == FailureSeverity.Warning)
                accessor.DeleteWarning(failure);
            else
                return FailureProcessingResult.ProceedWithRollBack;
        }
        return FailureProcessingResult.Continue;
    }
}

// Usage in a transaction
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Safe Op"))
{
    FailureHandlingOptions fho = tx.GetFailureHandlingOptions();
    fho.SetFailuresPreprocessor(new SuppressAllWarnings());
    tx.SetFailureHandlingOptions(fho);
    tx.Start();
    /* ... */
    tx.Commit();
}

desc: Complete class reference for Autodesk.Revit.DB (F–Z), full UI namespace, document and application events, and failure handling.

sections.title: CableTray
sections.content:
ClassCableTray
Inherits: MEPCurve → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Electrical · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a cable tray element used to support and route electrical cables. Cable trays have width and height dimensions, a system type, and connectors at both ends for joining to fittings or other trays. Created using CableTray.Create().
🟢 Static Factory + Key Methods
🟣public static CableTray Create(Document doc, ElementId cableTrayTypeId, ElementId levelId, XYZ startPoint, XYZ endPoint)
Creates a straight cable tray segment between two 3D points at the specified level. The cable tray type determines the tray's available sizes and material. Both points must be at the same elevation for a horizontal tray.
ParameterTypeDescription
docDocumentThe target document.
cableTrayTypeIdElementIdElementId of the CableTrayType to use.
levelIdElementIdElementId of the reference level.
startPointXYZStart point of the tray in model coordinates (feet).
endPointXYZEnd point of the tray in model coordinates (feet).
Returns: CableTray — The newly created cable tray element.
// Example — Create cable tray run at ceiling height
CableTrayType ctType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(CableTrayType))
    .Cast<CableTrayType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(t => t.Name.Contains("300"));  // 300mm wide tray

Level level = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

// Create at 2700mm above level (ceiling zone)
double zFt = level.Elevation +
    UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(2700, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

XYZ start = new XYZ(0,  0, zFt);
XYZ end   = new XYZ(20, 0, zFt);   // 6m run

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Cable Tray"))
{
    tx.Start();
    CableTray ct = CableTray.Create(doc, ctType.Id, level.Id, start, end);

    // Set width and height
    ct.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CABLETRAY_WIDTH_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(300, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));
    ct.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CABLETRAY_HEIGHT_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(100, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    tx.Commit();
}

// Read cable tray properties
CableTray existingCT = ...;
double widthMm  = existingCT.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CABLETRAY_WIDTH_PARAM)
    .AsDouble() * 304.8;
double lengthMm = (existingCT.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.Length * 304.8;

// Navigate connectors
ConnectorManager cm = existingCT.GetMEPModel().ConnectorManager;
foreach (Connector c in cm.Connectors)
{
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Connector at {c.Origin} connected={c.IsConnected}");
}

sections.title: Conduit
sections.content:
ClassConduit
Inherits: MEPCurve → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Electrical
Represents a conduit (protective tube) for electrical wiring. Conduits are cylindrical and sized by nominal diameter. Like pipes, they use ConnectorManager for joining to fittings.
🟢 Static Factory
🟣public static Conduit Create(Document doc, ElementId conduitTypeId, ElementId levelId, XYZ startPoint, XYZ endPoint)
Creates a conduit segment between two points. Uses the same pattern as CableTray.Create().
// Example — Create conduit with diameter
ConduitType ct = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ConduitType)).Cast<ConduitType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Conduit"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Conduit conduit = Conduit.Create(doc, ct.Id, level.Id,
        new XYZ(0, 0, 8), new XYZ(10, 0, 8));

    // Set nominal diameter to 25mm
    conduit.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CONDUIT_DIAMETER_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(25, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: ElectricalSystem
sections.content:
ClassElectricalSystem
Inherits: MEPSystem → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Electrical
Represents an electrical circuit system connecting a panel to one or more electrical devices. Provides access to connected load, panel, circuit number and all elements in the circuit.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public ElectricalSystemType SystemType { get; }
