Gets the type of electrical system: PowerCircuit, Data, Telephone, Security, FireAlarm, Nurse, Controls, Communication, or Other.
🟠public FamilyInstance BaseEquipment { get; }
Gets the panel or distribution board (FamilyInstance) this circuit originates from.
🟠public string CircuitNumber { get; }
Gets the circuit number string as shown on the panel schedule (e.g. "1", "3", "5-7").
// Example — Electrical system audit
var systems = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ElectricalSystem))
    .Cast<ElectricalSystem>()
    .Where(s => s.SystemType == ElectricalSystemType.PowerCircuit)
    .ToList();

foreach (var sys in systems)
{
    string         circuit   = sys.CircuitNumber;
    FamilyInstance panel     = sys.BaseEquipment;
    string         panelName = panel?.Name ?? "No Panel";

    // Get connected load (VA)
    double loadVA = sys.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.RBS_ELEC_CIRCUIT_TOTAL_DEMAND_CURRENT_PARAM)
        ?.AsDouble() ?? 0;

    // Get all elements on this circuit
    ConnectorSet elems = sys.ConnectorManager.Connectors;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Panel:{panelName} Circuit:{circuit} Load:{loadVA:F1}VA");
}

sections.title: Duct
sections.content:
ClassDuct
Inherits: MEPCurve → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Mechanical
Represents a duct segment for HVAC air distribution. Ducts can be rectangular (width × height), round (diameter), or oval. They connect to fittings and terminal devices via connectors. Created with Duct.Create() using a MechanicalSystemType.
🟢 Static Factory + Usage
🟣public static Duct Create(Document doc, ElementId systemTypeId, ElementId ductTypeId, ElementId levelId, XYZ startPoint, XYZ endPoint)
Creates a straight duct segment. The system type (supply/return/exhaust) determines airflow direction. The duct type determines shape (rectangular/round/oval) and material.
ParameterTypeDescription
systemTypeIdElementIdMechanicalSystemType ID (supply air, return air, exhaust, etc.)
ductTypeIdElementIdDuctType ID (rectangular, round, oval)
levelIdElementIdReference level
startPointXYZStart point (feet)
endPointXYZEnd point (feet)
Returns: Duct — The created duct element.
// Example — Create rectangular supply air duct
// Get supply air system type
MechanicalSystemType supplyType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(MechanicalSystemType))
    .Cast<MechanicalSystemType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(t => t.SystemClassification ==
        MEPSystemClassification.SupplyAir);

// Get rectangular duct type
DuctType rectDuct = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(DuctType)).Cast<DuctType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(d => d.Shape == ConnectorProfileType.Rectangular);

double zFt = level.Elevation +
    UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(2800, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Duct"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Duct duct = Duct.Create(doc,
        supplyType?.Id ?? ElementId.InvalidElementId,
        rectDuct.Id, level.Id,
        new XYZ(0, 0, zFt),
        new XYZ(16.4, 0, zFt));  // ~5m

    // Set dimensions (rectangular duct)
    duct.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CURVE_WIDTH_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(400, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));
    duct.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CURVE_HEIGHT_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    tx.Commit();
}

// Read duct dimensions
Duct d2 = ...;
double wMm = d2.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CURVE_WIDTH_PARAM)
    .AsDouble() * 304.8;
double hMm = d2.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CURVE_HEIGHT_PARAM)
    .AsDouble() * 304.8;
double lMm = (d2.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.Length * 304.8;

sections.title: Space
sections.content:
ClassSpace
Inherits: SpatialElement → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Mechanical
Represents an MEP Space — the mechanical equivalent of an architectural Room. Spaces carry HVAC load data, supply/return air volumes, occupancy data, and link to their corresponding architectural rooms. Used for energy analysis and HVAC system design.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public Room Room { get; }
Gets the architectural Room this MEP Space is associated with (if any). The association is based on geometric overlap at the space's reference point.
🟠public double Area { get; }
Gets the floor area of the space in sq.ft internal units. Multiply by 0.092903 for m².
🟠public double Volume { get; }
Gets the volume in cubic feet. Multiply by 0.0283168 for m³.
// Example — MEP Space HVAC data extraction
var spaces = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_MEPSpaces)
    .Cast<Space>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var space in spaces)
{
    string name     = space.Name;
    string number   = space.Number;
    double areaSqM  = space.Area * 0.092903;
    double volCubM  = space.Volume * 0.0283168;

    // Linked architectural room
    Room   room     = space.Room;
    string roomName = room?.Name ?? "No linked room";

    // HVAC parameters
    double supplyAir = space.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.ROOM_DESIGN_SUPPLY_AIRFLOW_PARAM)
        ?.AsDouble() ?? 0;
    double coolingLoad = space.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.ROOM_ACTUAL_COOLING_LOAD_PARAM)
        ?.AsDouble() ?? 0;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"{name}: {areaSqM:F1}m² | Supply: {supplyAir:F0}L/s | Cooling: {coolingLoad:F0}W");
}

// Create a new Space
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Space"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Space newSpace = doc.Create.NewSpace(level, new UV(5, 5));
    newSpace.Name   = "Office 101";
    newSpace.Number = "SP-101";
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Pipe
sections.content:
ClassPipe
Inherits: MEPCurve → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Plumbing
Represents a pipe element in a plumbing or fire protection system. Pipes are round, defined by their nominal diameter, and connect to fittings and fixtures through connectors. The most critical MEP element for clash detection with structural members.
🟢 Static Factory + Properties
🟣public static Pipe Create(Document doc, ElementId systemTypeId, ElementId pipeTypeId, ElementId levelId, XYZ startPoint, XYZ endPoint)
Creates a pipe segment between two points. The PipingSystemType determines the fluid type (domestic cold water, hot water, fire sprinkler, etc.) and system classification.
// Example — Create pipe network segment
// Get domestic cold water system type
PipingSystemType coldWater = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PipingSystemType))
    .Cast<PipingSystemType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(s => s.SystemClassification ==
        MEPSystemClassification.DomesticColdWater);

PipeType pipeType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PipeType)).Cast<PipeType>().First();

// Pipe height: 2500mm above level (in ceiling void)
double zFt = level.Elevation +
    UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(2500, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Pipe"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Pipe pipe = Pipe.Create(doc,
        coldWater?.Id ?? ElementId.InvalidElementId,
        pipeType.Id,
        level.Id,
        new XYZ(0, 0, zFt),
        new XYZ(16.4, 0, zFt));

    // Set diameter to 50mm
    pipe.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_PIPE_DIAMETER_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(50, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    tx.Commit();
}

// Read pipe properties
var pipes = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Pipe)).Cast<Pipe>().ToList();

double totalRunMm = 0;
foreach (var p in pipes)
{
    double diamMm = p.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_PIPE_DIAMETER_PARAM)
        .AsDouble() * 304.8;
    double lenMm  = (p.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.Length * 304.8;
    totalRunMm += lenMm;

    // Check slope
    XYZ start = (p.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.GetEndPoint(0);
    XYZ end   = (p.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.GetEndPoint(1);
    double slope = Math.Abs(end.Z - start.Z) / Math.Abs(end.X - start.X + end.Y - start.Y + 0.001);
    if (slope < 0.01)
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Low slope pipe: {p.Id} slope={slope:P1}");
}

sections.title: PipingSystem
sections.content:
ClassPipingSystem
Inherits: MEPSystem → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Plumbing
Represents a plumbing system grouping connected pipes, fittings, and fixtures. Provides access to all elements in the system, total flow rate, and system classification. Used for pipe network traversal and system-level analysis.
🟢 Key Methods + Properties
🟠public MEPSystemClassification SystemClassification { get; }
Gets the system classification: SupplyHydronic, ReturnHydronic, DomesticColdWater, DomesticHotWater, Sanitary, FireProtectWet, etc.
🟢public double GetFlow()
Returns the system's total flow rate in internal units (cubic feet per second). Convert to L/s by multiplying by 28.317.
// Example — Traverse a piping system
var pipingSystems = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PipingSystem))
    .Cast<PipingSystem>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var sys in pipingSystems)
{
    MEPSystemClassification cls   = sys.SystemClassification;
    double                  flow  = sys.GetFlow() * 28.317;  // L/s
    string                  name  = sys.Name;

    // Get all pipes in the system
    ConnectorManager cm = sys.ConnectorManager;
    var pipeIds = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Pipe))
        .Cast<Pipe>()
        .Where(p => p.GetMEPModel()?.ConnectorManager?.Connectors != null)
        .Select(p => p.Id).ToList();

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"System: {name} | Class: {cls} | Flow: {flow:F2}L/s");
}

sections.title: Connector
sections.content:
ClassConnector
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a connection point on an MEP element (pipe end, duct end, fitting port, equipment port). Every MEP element has a ConnectorManager with one or more Connectors. Connectors define the size, shape, flow direction, and connection status. Two connectors on adjacent elements are connected by calling ConnectTo().
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public XYZ Origin { get; }
Gets the 3D position of this connector in model coordinates (feet). Used to find matching connectors on adjacent elements.
🟠public bool IsConnected { get; }
True if this connector is already joined to another connector. Check before calling ConnectTo() to avoid redundant connections.
🟠public ConnectorType ConnectorType { get; }
Gets the connector type: End (pipe/duct ends), Curve (along a curve), Physical (equipment port), or Logical.
🟠public double Radius { get; }
For round connectors (pipes, conduit), gets the connector radius in feet. Diameter = Radius × 2.
🟠public ConnectorSet AllRefs { get; }
Gets all connectors that this connector is connected to. Typically one for pipe ends (the matching connector on the next element).
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public void ConnectTo(Connector connector)
Connects this connector to another connector. Both connectors must be unconnected, compatible (same size/shape), and in close proximity. Must be called inside a Transaction.
// Example — Connect two pipe endpoints
// Helper: find connector nearest to a 3D point
Connector GetConnectorAt(Element elem, XYZ point, double tol = 0.01)
{
    ConnectorManager cm = (elem as MEPCurve)?.GetMEPModel()?.ConnectorManager
                        ?? (elem as FamilyInstance)?.GetMEPModel()?.ConnectorManager;
    if (cm == null) return null;
    foreach (Connector c in cm.Connectors)
        if (c.Origin.DistanceTo(point) < tol) return c;
    return null;
}

// Connect pipe1 end to pipe2 start
Pipe pipe1 = ...;
Pipe pipe2 = ...;

XYZ pipe1End   = (pipe1.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.GetEndPoint(1);
XYZ pipe2Start = (pipe2.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.GetEndPoint(0);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Connect Pipes"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Connector c1 = GetConnectorAt(pipe1, pipe1End);
    Connector c2 = GetConnectorAt(pipe2, pipe2Start);

    if (c1 != null && c2 != null && !c1.IsConnected && !c2.IsConnected)
        c1.ConnectTo(c2);

    tx.Commit();
}

// Traverse a connected network from a seed element
Pipe seedPipe = ...;
var visited  = new HashSet<ElementId>();
var queue    = new Queue<Element>();
queue.Enqueue(seedPipe);

while (queue.Count > 0)
{
    Element cur = queue.Dequeue();
    if (!visited.Add(cur.Id)) continue;

    ConnectorManager cm = (cur as MEPCurve)?.GetMEPModel()?.ConnectorManager;
    if (cm == null) continue;

    foreach (Connector c in cm.Connectors)
        foreach (Connector neighbor in c.AllRefs)
        {
            Element next = doc.GetElement(neighbor.Owner.Id);
            if (next != null && !visited.Contains(next.Id))
                queue.Enqueue(next);
        }
}

sections.title: ConnectorManager
sections.content:
ClassConnectorManager
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Manages all connectors on a single MEP element. Obtained via MEPModel.ConnectorManager. Provides access to all connectors and the unused (open) connectors at element endpoints.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public ConnectorSet Connectors { get; }
Gets all connectors on this element. Enumerate with foreach. Typically 2 for pipe segments, more for fittings and equipment.
🟠public ConnectorSet UnusedConnectors { get; }
Gets only the connectors that are NOT yet connected to another element. Used to find open endpoints for network traversal or routing.

sections.title: Room
sections.content:
ClassRoom
Inherits: SpatialElement → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Architecture
Represents an architectural room — a bounded space with name, number, area, volume, and finish data. Rooms are placed in floor plan views and bounded by walls, separation lines, or room bounding elements. The Room is the most important element for space management, area calculations, and BIM data assignment.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public string Name { get; set; }
Gets or sets the room name. Displayed in the room tag. Setting requires an open Transaction.
🟠public string Number { get; set; }
Gets or sets the room number. Unique identifier for the room within the project.
🟠public double Area { get; }
Gets the floor area of the room in sq.ft internal units. Multiply by 0.092903 for m². Returns 0 for unplaced rooms.
🟠public double Volume { get; }
Gets the room volume in cubic feet. Only computed when Room Volume calculation is enabled in project settings.
🟠public Level Level { get; }
Gets the Level this room is placed on.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public bool IsPointInRoom(XYZ point)
Returns true if the given 3D point is inside this room's boundary. Used for spatial queries: finding which room a pipe passes through, assigning rooms to equipment, etc. The point must be within the room's elevation range.
ParameterTypeDescription
pointXYZThe point to test (in model coordinates, feet).
Returns: bool — true if the point is inside this room.
🟢public IList<IList<BoundarySegment>> GetBoundarySegments(SpatialElementBoundaryOptions options)
Returns the boundary of the room as a list of loops, each containing BoundarySegment objects. Index 0 is the outer boundary; subsequent loops are holes. Each BoundarySegment identifies the bounding wall element.
// Example — Complete Room operations
// Collect all placed rooms (Area > 0)
var rooms = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
    .Cast<Room>()
    .Where(r => r.Area > 1)    // placed rooms only
    .OrderBy(r => r.Number)
    .ToList();

foreach (var room in rooms)
{
    string name    = room.Name;
    string number  = room.Number;
    double areaSqM = room.Area * 0.092903;
    string dept    = room.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ROOM_DEPARTMENT)
                        ?.AsString() ?? "";

    // Get room boundary to create a floor
    var bOpts = new SpatialElementBoundaryOptions();
    IList<IList<BoundarySegment>> segs = room.GetBoundarySegments(bOpts);

    if (segs.Count > 0)
    {
        CurveLoop boundary = new CurveLoop();
        foreach (var seg in segs[0])  // outer loop only
            boundary.Append(seg.GetCurve());

        // Identify bounding walls
        foreach (var seg in segs[0])
        {
            ElementId wallId = seg.ElementId;
            if (wallId != ElementId.InvalidElementId)
            {
                Wall w = doc.GetElement(wallId) as Wall;
                System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                    $"  Bounded by: {w?.WallType?.Name}");
            }
        }
    }

    // Check if a point is in the room
    XYZ testPt = new XYZ(5, 5, room.Level.Elevation + 1);
    bool inside = room.IsPointInRoom(testPt);
}

// Create a room programmatically
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Room"))
{
    tx.Start();
    UV   location = new UV(5, 5);  // XY position on level
    Room newRoom  = doc.Create.NewRoom(level, location);
    newRoom.Name   = "Conference Room";
    newRoom.Number = "101";
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: TopographySurface
sections.content:
ClassTopographySurface
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Architecture
Represents the topography/site surface. Contains a mesh of 3D points defining the terrain. Points can be read, added, moved, or deleted. Sub-regions are child TopographySurface elements with different materials applied to portions of the surface.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public IList<XYZ> GetPoints()
Returns all the XYZ points that define the topography mesh. Points are in model coordinates (feet). Useful for elevation analysis, contour generation, and cut/fill calculations.
Returns: IList<XYZ> — All mesh points defining the terrain surface.
// Example — Topography analysis and export
TopographySurface topo = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(TopographySurface))
    .Cast<TopographySurface>()
    .FirstOrDefault(t => !t.IsSiteSubRegion);

if (topo == null) return Result.Failed;

IList<XYZ> pts = topo.GetPoints();

// Statistics
double minElev = pts.Min(p => p.Z) * 304.8;
double maxElev = pts.Max(p => p.Z) * 304.8;
double avgElev = pts.Average(p => p.Z) * 304.8;

TaskDialog.Show("Topo",
    $"Points: {pts.Count}\nMin: {minElev:F0}mm\nMax: {maxElev:F0}mm\nAvg: {avgElev:F0}mm");

// Export as XYZ CSV
var csv = new System.Text.StringBuilder("X,Y,Z\n");
foreach (var p in pts)
    csv.AppendLine($"{p.X * 304.8:F3},{p.Y * 304.8:F3},{p.Z * 304.8:F3}");

// Modify topo points
using (TopographyEditScope scope = new TopographyEditScope(doc, "Edit Topo"))
{
    scope.Start(topo.Id);
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Move Points"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        // Raise points within a region by 500mm
        var ptsToRaise = pts.Where(p => p.X > 0 && p.Y > 0).ToList();
        double raise = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(500, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
        var newPts = ptsToRaise.Select(p => new XYZ(p.X, p.Y, p.Z + raise)).ToList();
        topo.ChangePointsToElevation(ptsToRaise, newPts);
        tx.Commit();
    }
    scope.Commit(new TopographyEditFailuresPreprocessor());
}

sections.title: Stairs
sections.content:
ClassStairs
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Architecture
Represents a stair assembly. Stairs contain runs (StairsRun) and landings (StairsLanding). Stairs are created and modified using StairsEditScope. Key parameters: riser height, tread depth, number of risers.
🟠 Key Properties + Usage
🟠public int DesiredRisersNumber { get; set; }
Gets or sets the desired number of risers. Revit calculates the actual riser height based on this and the floor-to-floor height.
// Example — Read stair properties and check compliance
var stairs = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Stairs))
    .Cast<Stairs>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var stair in stairs)
{
    int    risers   = stair.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STAIRS_RISER_NUMBER)?.AsInteger() ?? 0;
    double riserH   = stair.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STAIRS_ACTUAL_RISER_HEIGHT)?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;
    double treadD   = stair.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STAIRS_ACTUAL_TREAD_DEPTH)?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;

    // UK Building Regs: max riser 220mm, min going 220mm
    bool riserOK  = riserH <= 220;
    bool treadOK  = treadD >= 220;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Stair {stair.Id}: Risers={risers} H={riserH:F0}mm T={treadD:F0}mm " +
        $"{(riserOK && treadOK ? "PASS" : "FAIL")}");
}

sections.title: Schema
sections.content:
ClassSchema
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.ExtensibleStorage
Defines the structure (field names and types) for extensible storage data. A Schema is like a table definition — it describes what data can be stored, but not the data itself. Schemas are identified by GUID and are shared across all documents. Created via SchemaBuilder.
🟢 Static Lookup
🟢public static Schema Lookup(Guid schemaGuid)
Looks up a Schema by its GUID. Returns null if no schema with that GUID has been registered in this session. Call this before creating a new schema to avoid duplicates.
Returns: Schema — The existing schema, or null if not found.
🟢public Field GetField(string fieldName)
Gets a named Field from this schema. Use the returned Field when reading/writing Entity data.

sections.title: SchemaBuilder
sections.content:
ClassSchemaBuilder
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.ExtensibleStorage
Fluent builder for creating a new Schema definition. Define fields (typed data columns), access levels, and GUID, then call Finish() to register the schema. Only create a schema if Schema.Lookup() returns null.
🟢 Complete ExtensibleStorage Pattern
// Example — Complete ExtensibleStorage write & read cycle
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.ExtensibleStorage;

// ── STEP 1: Create or lookup the Schema ──
Guid   schemaGuid = new Guid("A1B2C3D4-E5F6-7890-ABCD-EF1234567890");
Schema schema     = Schema.Lookup(schemaGuid);

if (schema == null)
{
    var builder = new SchemaBuilder(schemaGuid);
    builder.SetSchemaName("WallBIMData");
    builder.SetReadAccessLevel(AccessLevel.Public);
    builder.SetWriteAccessLevel(AccessLevel.Public);

    // Add fields (typed columns)
    builder.AddSimpleField("FireRating",  typeof(string));
    builder.AddSimpleField("UValue",      typeof(double))
           .SetUnitType(UnitTypeId.WattsPerSquareMeterKelvin);
    builder.AddSimpleField("Approved",    typeof(bool));
    builder.AddSimpleField("ReviewDate",  typeof(string));

    schema = builder.Finish();
}

// ── STEP 2: WRITE data to an element ──
Wall wall = ...;
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Write BIM Data"))
{
    tx.Start();

    Entity entity = new Entity(schema);
    entity.Set("FireRating",  "2HR");
    entity.Set("UValue",      0.35);
    entity.Set("Approved",    true);
    entity.Set("ReviewDate",  DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd"));

    wall.SetEntity(entity);
    tx.Commit();
}

// ── STEP 3: READ data from an element ──
Entity stored = wall.GetEntity(schema);
if (stored.IsValid())
{
    string fireRating  = stored.Get<string>("FireRating");
    double uValue      = stored.Get<double>("UValue");
    bool   approved    = stored.Get<bool>("Approved");
    string reviewDate  = stored.Get<string>("ReviewDate");

    TaskDialog.Show("BIM Data",
        $"Fire:{fireRating} U:{uValue:F3} Approved:{approved} Date:{reviewDate}");
}

// ── STEP 4: FIND all elements with this schema ──
var extFilter = new ExtensibleStorageFilter(schemaGuid);
var wallsWithData = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WherePasses(extFilter)
    .Cast<Wall>()
    .ToList();

// ── STEP 5: DELETE entity from element ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Delete Entity"))
{
    tx.Start();
    wall.DeleteEntity(schema);
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: IUpdater
sections.content:
InterfaceIUpdater
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
The core interface for Dynamic Model Update. Implement this to react to element changes with model writes. Unlike DocumentChanged (read-only), DMU updaters fire INSIDE the triggering transaction, so you can modify related elements in response to changes without starting a new transaction.
// Warning — No New TransactionsInside IUpdater.Execute(), you are already inside an open transaction. Do NOT start a new Transaction — just modify elements directly. Starting a nested transaction will throw InvalidOperationException.
🟢 Required Methods + Complete Pattern
// Example — Complete IUpdater implementation
public class WallMarkAutoUpdater : IUpdater
{
    private static AddInId   _appId;
    private static UpdaterId _id;

    public WallMarkAutoUpdater(AddInId appId)
    {
        _appId = appId;
        _id    = new UpdaterId(appId,
            new Guid("AABBCCDD-1234-5678-ABCD-EF0123456789"));
    }

    // Fires INSIDE the active transaction — no new Transactions here!
    public void Execute(UpdaterData data)
    {
        Document doc = data.GetDocument();

        // Handle modified walls
        foreach (ElementId id in data.GetModifiedElementIds())
        {
            Wall wall = doc.GetElement(id) as Wall;
            if (wall == null) continue;

            // Auto-update Mark with length
            double lenMm = (wall.Location as LocationCurve)
                .Curve.Length * 304.8;
            Parameter mark = wall.get_Parameter(
                BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);
            if (mark != null && !mark.IsReadOnly)
                mark.Set($"W-{lenMm:F0}mm");  // write directly, no tx needed
        }

        // Handle newly added walls
        foreach (ElementId id in data.GetAddedElementIds())
        {
            Wall wall = doc.GetElement(id) as Wall;
            if (wall == null) continue;
            // Tag new walls automatically
            wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
                ?.Set($"NEW-{wall.Id.IntegerValue}");
        }
    }

    public string      GetUpdaterName()             => "Wall Mark Auto Updater";
    public string      GetAdditionalInformation()   => "Auto-tags walls with length on change";
    public ChangePriority GetChangePriority()       => ChangePriority.MEPFixtures;
    public UpdaterId  GetUpdaterId()               => _id;
}

// ── Register in OnStartup ──
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    var updater = new WallMarkAutoUpdater(app.ActiveAddInId);
    UpdaterRegistry.RegisterUpdater(updater);

    // Trigger: fire on geometry change of any Wall
    UpdaterRegistry.AddTrigger(
        updater.GetUpdaterId(),
        new ElementClassFilter(typeof(Wall)),
        Element.GetChangeTypeGeometry());

    // Also trigger on any Wall addition
    UpdaterRegistry.AddTrigger(
        updater.GetUpdaterId(),
        new ElementClassFilter(typeof(Wall)),
        Element.GetChangeTypeElementAddition());

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

// ── Unregister in OnShutdown ──
public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    UpdaterRegistry.UnregisterUpdater(_updater.GetUpdaterId());
    return Result.Succeeded;
}

sections.title: UpdaterRegistry
sections.content:
ClassUpdaterRegistry
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Static class for registering, unregistering, and querying IUpdater instances. All DMU updaters must be registered before they can receive triggers. Updaters are registered per session — they must be re-registered on every Revit startup.
🟢 Key Static Methods
🟢public static void RegisterUpdater(IUpdater updater)
Registers an IUpdater so it can receive trigger events. Call in OnStartup(). Does NOT add any triggers — call AddTrigger() separately.
🟢public static void UnregisterUpdater(UpdaterId updaterId)
Removes the updater and all its triggers. Call in OnShutdown(). Failing to unregister causes memory leaks and may cause crashes on Revit close.
🟢public static void AddTrigger(UpdaterId updaterId, ElementFilter filter, ChangeType changeType)
Registers a trigger: when elements matching the filter change in the specified way, the updater's Execute() is called. Can add multiple triggers per updater.
🟢public static void AddTrigger(UpdaterId updaterId, ElementFilter filter, ElementId changeParamId)
Adds a parameter-specific trigger that fires only when a specific parameter on matching elements changes. More efficient than geometry change triggers for parameter-only reactions.
// Example — Change types reference
// All available change types:
Element.GetChangeTypeGeometry()           // geometry changed (move, resize)
Element.GetChangeTypeAny()                // any change at all
Element.GetChangeTypeElementAddition()    // element created
Element.GetChangeTypeElementDeletion()    // element deleted
Element.GetChangeTypeParameter(           // specific param changed
    new ElementId(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK))

// Scope: can limit trigger to specific document
UpdaterRegistry.AddTrigger(
    updaterId,
    doc,                                    // document scope
    new ElementClassFilter(typeof(Wall)),
    Element.GetChangeTypeGeometry());

sections.title: View3D
sections.content:
ClassView3D
Inherits: View → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a 3D view — orthographic or perspective. 3D views support section boxes, camera positioning, ray casting (ReferenceIntersector requires View3D), and walkthrough paths. Section boxes define the visible region of the model in the view.
🟢 Static Factory + Key Methods
🟣public static View3D CreateIsometric(Document doc, ElementId viewFamilyTypeId)
Creates an orthographic (isometric) 3D view. This is the standard 3D view type that shows the whole model.
// Example — Create 3D view and set section box
// Get 3D view family type
ElementId vftId = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType))
    .Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .First(x => x.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.ThreeD).Id;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "3D View"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create isometric 3D view
    View3D v3d = View3D.CreateIsometric(doc, vftId);
    v3d.Name = "ISO - Full Building";

    // Set section box to building extents
    BoundingBoxXYZ bb = new BoundingBoxXYZ();
    bb.Min = new XYZ(-5, -5, -1);
    bb.Max = new XYZ(50, 50, 30);
    v3d.SetSectionBox(bb);
    v3d.IsSectionBoxActive = true;

    // Create perspective view
    View3D persp = View3D.CreatePerspective(doc, vftId);
    persp.Name = "Camera - Entrance";

    // Set camera orientation
    ViewOrientation3D orient = new ViewOrientation3D(
        new XYZ(-10, -10, 5.5),    // eye position
        new XYZ(0, 0, 1),             // up direction
        new XYZ(15, 15, 2));          // forward direction
    persp.SetOrientation(orient);

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: ViewSchedule
sections.content:
ClassViewSchedule
Inherits: View → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a schedule view (element schedule, key schedule, material takeoff, or sheet list). Schedules are created programmatically with fields, filters, sorting, and grouping. Data is read via TableData.
🟢 Static Factory + Data Reading
🟣public static ViewSchedule CreateSchedule(Document doc, ElementId categoryId)
Creates a new schedule for the given category. After creation, use ScheduleDefinition to add fields, filters, and sorting.
// Example — Create wall schedule + read data
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Schedule"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create wall schedule
    ViewSchedule sched = ViewSchedule.CreateSchedule(doc,
        new ElementId(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls));
    sched.Name = "Wall Schedule";

    ScheduleDefinition def = sched.Definition;

    // Add fields from schedulable fields
    IList<SchedulableField> schedulable = def.GetSchedulableFields();

    // Find and add: Mark, Length, Area, Type
    var wantedBIPs = new []
    {
        BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK,
        BuiltInParameter.CURVE_ELEM_LENGTH,
        BuiltInParameter.HOST_AREA_COMPUTED,
        BuiltInParameter.ELEM_TYPE_PARAM
    };
    foreach (var bip in wantedBIPs)
    {
        var sf = schedulable.FirstOrDefault(
            f => f.ParameterId == new ElementId(bip));
        if (sf != null)
            def.AddField(sf);
    }

    // Sort by Mark
    var sortField = def.GetField(0);
    var sortOrder = new ScheduleSortGroupField(sortField.FieldId);
    sortOrder.SortOrder = ScheduleSortOrder.Ascending;
    def.SetSortGroupFields(new List<ScheduleSortGroupField> { sortOrder });

    tx.Commit();
}

// Read schedule data row by row
ViewSchedule existingSched = ...;
TableData    table = existingSched.GetTableData();
TableSectionData body = table.GetSectionData(SectionType.Body);

int rows = body.NumberOfRows;
int cols = body.NumberOfColumns;

for (int r = 0; r < rows; r++)
{
    var rowData = new List<string>();
    for (int c = 0; c < cols; c++)
        rowData.Add(body.GetCellText(r, c));
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(string.Join(" | ", rowData));
}

sections.title: ViewFamilyType
sections.content:
ClassViewFamilyType
Inherits: ElementType → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Defines the type of a view — its family (FloorPlan, CeilingPlan, Section, Elevation, etc.). The ViewFamilyType ID is required when creating views with ViewPlan.Create(), ViewSection.CreateSection(), etc. This is one of the most common sources of bugs — always use ViewFamilyType.Id, NOT ViewPlan.Id.
// Critical Bug PreventionViewPlan.Create(doc, viewFamilyTypeId, levelId) requires a ViewFamilyType ID in the second parameter — NOT a ViewPlan ID. These are completely different classes. Always use the lookup pattern below.
🟢 Lookup Patterns
🟠public ViewFamily ViewFamily { get; }
Gets the ViewFamily classification: FloorPlan, CeilingPlan, Section, Elevation, ThreeD, DraftingView, Schedule, DrawingSheet, etc.
// Example — ViewFamilyType lookup for all view types
// ── Get IDs for all common view types ──
var allVFTs = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType))
    .Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .ToList();

// Floor plan type ID
ElementId fpId = allVFTs
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.FloorPlan).Id;

// Ceiling plan type ID
ElementId cpId = allVFTs
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.CeilingPlan).Id;

// Section type ID
ElementId secId = allVFTs
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.Section).Id;

// Elevation type ID
ElementId elevId = allVFTs
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.Elevation).Id;

// 3D view type ID
ElementId v3dId = allVFTs
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.ThreeD).Id;

// Drafting view type ID
ElementId draftId = allVFTs
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.DraftingView).Id;

// Use to create views
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Views"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // CORRECT — fpId is ViewFamilyType.Id
    ViewPlan fp = ViewPlan.Create(doc, fpId, level.Id);
    fp.Name = "FP - Ground Floor";

    // WRONG (common bug) — do NOT pass fp.Id or level.Id as type
    // ViewPlan.Create(doc, fp.Id, level.Id); ← CRASH

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Viewport
sections.content:
ClassViewport
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a view placed on a sheet. A Viewport is the container that holds a view on a ViewSheet. Created with Viewport.Create() and positioned on the sheet by setting its center point. Each viewport has a type (label visible, no label, etc.) and can be queried for its bounding box on the sheet.
🟢 Static Factory + Key Methods
🟣public static Viewport Create(Document doc, ElementId viewSheetId, ElementId viewId, XYZ point)
Places a view on a sheet at the given center point (in sheet coordinates, feet from sheet origin). The view must not already be placed on another sheet (most views), or must be a schedule (which can appear on multiple sheets).
// Example — Create sheet and place views
// Get title block
FamilySymbol titleBlock = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_TitleBlocks)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
    .Cast<FamilySymbol>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Sheet"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create sheet
    ViewSheet sheet = ViewSheet.Create(doc, titleBlock.Id);
    sheet.SheetNumber = "A-101";
    sheet.Name        = "Floor Plans";

    // Place a floor plan view at sheet centre
    ViewPlan floorPlan = ...;
    XYZ centre = new XYZ(1.1, 0.7, 0);  // approx centre of A1 sheet

    if (Viewport.CanAddViewToSheet(doc, sheet.Id, floorPlan.Id))
    {
        Viewport vp = Viewport.Create(doc, sheet.Id, floorPlan.Id, centre);

        // Read viewport box on sheet (paper coordinates)
        BoundingBoxXYZ box = vp.GetBoxOutline();
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"VP width={( box.Max.X - box.Min.X)*304.8:F0}mm");
    }

    // List all viewports on a sheet
    foreach (ElementId vpId in sheet.GetAllViewports())
    {
        Viewport v  = doc.GetElement(vpId) as Viewport;
        View     vi = doc.GetElement(v.ViewId) as View;
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"  View: {vi.Name}");
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Rebar
sections.content:
ClassRebar
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure
Represents a reinforcement bar in a concrete element. Rebar elements have a bar type (diameter), shape, cover distance, and can be straight, hooked, or shaped. Created using Rebar.CreateFromCurves() inside a host structural element.
🟢 Static Factory + Properties
🟣public static Rebar CreateFromCurves(Document doc, RebarStyle style, RebarBarType barType, RebarHookType startHook, RebarHookType endHook, Element host, XYZ normal, IList<Curve> curves, RebarHookOrientation startHookOrient, RebarHookOrientation endHookOrient)
Creates a rebar element from a list of curves within a host element. The normal defines the plane of the rebar shape.
// Example — Create bottom reinforcement bar in a beam
// Get bar type (e.g. 16mm diameter)
RebarBarType barType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(RebarBarType))
    .Cast<RebarBarType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(b => b.BarNominalDiameter *304.8 > 15 &&
                        b.BarNominalDiameter * 304.8 < 17);

FamilyInstance beam = ...;  // structural beam host

// Get beam geometry to find its centreline
LocationCurve lc   = beam.Location as LocationCurve;
XYZ           s    = lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(0);
XYZ           e    = lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(1);

// Offset downward 50mm for cover
double coverFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(50, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
XYZ    offset  = new XYZ(0, 0, -coverFt);
Line   barPath = Line.CreateBound(s + offset, e + offset);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Rebar"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Rebar rebar = Rebar.CreateFromCurves(
        doc,
        RebarStyle.Standard,
        barType,
        null, null,         // no hooks
        beam,              // host
        XYZ.BasisY,         // normal to bar plane
        new List<Curve> { barPath },
        RebarHookOrientation.Left,
        RebarHookOrientation.Left);
    tx.Commit();
}

// Read rebar properties
var allRebar = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar)
    .Cast<Rebar>().ToList();

foreach (var rb in allRebar)
{
    RebarBarType bt     = doc.GetElement(rb.GetTypeId()) as RebarBarType;
    double       dia    = bt?.BarNominalDiameter * 304.8 ?? 0;
    double       len    = rb.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_LENGTH)?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Ø{dia:F0}mm L={len:F0}mm");
}

desc: Complete class reference for all MEP namespaces, Architecture, Structure, ExtensibleStorage, DMU, and advanced View classes.

title: Family, Print& Utility Reference

hero.label: // Book 12 of 19 — Family API · Print · Export · Worksets · Analysis · Utilities
hero.title:
Family, Print
& Utility Reference
hero.description: Complete reference for the Family API (FamilyManager, FamilyParameter, DirectShape, Extrusion), Print & Export (PDF, DWG, IFC, Image), Worksharing, Analysis display, and all remaining utility classes including Filters, Dimension, Tags, TextNote, and ProjectInfo.

sections.title: Family
sections.content:
ClassFamily
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a loaded family in the project. A Family is the container for one or more FamilySymbol types. It provides access to all symbols, indicates whether it's an in-place family, and its category. Use EditFamily() to open the family for editing, or LoadFamily() to load it back after changes.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public bool IsInPlace { get; }
True if this is an in-place family (created within the project rather than loaded from a .rfa file). In-place families cannot be directly converted to loadable families through the API.
🟠public bool IsEditable { get; }
True if the family document can be opened for editing. System families (walls, floors etc.) are not editable.
🟠public Category FamilyCategory { get; set; }
Gets or sets the category of this family (Doors, Windows, Furniture, etc.).
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public ISet<ElementId> GetFamilySymbolIds()
Returns the ElementIds of all FamilySymbol types within this family. Iterate and cast each to FamilySymbol to access type parameters.
Returns: ISet<ElementId> — Set of FamilySymbol IDs.
// Example — Family inventory and editing workflow
// ── Inventory all families ──
var families = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Family))
    .Cast<Family>()
    .OrderBy(f => f.Name)
    .ToList();

foreach (var fam in families)
{
    int    typeCount = fam.GetFamilySymbolIds().Count;
    bool   inPlace  = fam.IsInPlace;
    string cat      = fam.FamilyCategory?.Name ?? "Unknown";

    // Count placed instances of this family
    int instances = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(FamilyInstance))
        .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
        .Count(fi => fi.Symbol?.Family?.Id == fam.Id);

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"{fam.Name} [{cat}] Types:{typeCount} Instances:{instances} InPlace:{inPlace}");
}

// ── Load a .rfa file ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Load Family"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Family loaded;
    bool ok = doc.LoadFamily(
        @"C:\Revit\Families\SingleFlushDoor.rfa",
        new FamilyLoadOverwriteOptions(),
        out loaded);
    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Open family for editing ──
Family doorFamily = families.First(f => f.Name.Contains("Door"));
if (doorFamily.IsEditable)
{
    Document famDoc = doc.EditFamily(doorFamily);
    // ... edit famDoc ...
    famDoc.LoadFamily(doc, new FamilyLoadOverwriteOptions());
    famDoc.Close(false);
}

sections.title: FamilyManager
sections.content:
ClassFamilyManager
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · only in family documents
Manages family types and parameters within an open family document. Access via famDoc.FamilyManager. Lets you create/delete/rename types, add/remove/modify parameters, set parameter values, and define formulas. Only available when the active document is a .rfa family document.
// Family Document OnlyFamilyManager is only valid inside a family document (doc.IsFamilyDocument == true). Calling it on a project document throws an exception. Always check before use.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public FamilyType CurrentType { get; set; }
Gets or sets the currently active type. Parameter values read/written via FamilyManager apply to this type. Set this before calling Get/Set to target a specific type.
🟠public FamilyTypeSet Types { get; }
Gets all FamilyType objects in this family. Enumerate to iterate over all types.
🟠public FamilyParameterSet Parameters { get; }
Gets all FamilyParameter definitions in this family.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public FamilyType NewType(string name)
Creates a new family type with the given name. The new type becomes CurrentType. Parameter values are copied from the previous current type. Must be inside a Transaction.
🟢public FamilyParameter AddParameter(string parameterName, ForgeTypeId groupTypeId, ForgeTypeId specTypeId, bool isInstance)
Adds a new family parameter. groupTypeId controls which group it appears under in properties (GroupTypeId.Dimensions, GroupTypeId.Data, etc.). specTypeId is the data type (SpecTypeId.Length, SpecTypeId.Boolean, SpecTypeId.String, etc.).
🟢public void Set(FamilyParameter familyParameter, double value)
Sets a parameter value for the CurrentType. Also has overloads for Set(param, int), Set(param, string), Set(param, ElementId). Value must be in internal units for double parameters.
🟢public void SetFormula(FamilyParameter familyParameter, string formula)
Sets a parameter formula (e.g. "Width * 2"). The formula string uses other parameter names and arithmetic operators. Pass null or empty string to remove the formula.
// Example — Complete FamilyManager workflow
// Open a family document
Document famDoc = doc.EditFamily(doorFamily);
FamilyManager mgr = famDoc.FamilyManager;

using (var tx = new Transaction(famDoc, "Edit Family"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Add a new Length parameter
    FamilyParameter frameWidth = mgr.AddParameter(
        "FrameWidth",
        GroupTypeId.Dimensions,
        SpecTypeId.Length,
        false);  // false = type parameter

    // Add a Yes/No instance parameter
    FamilyParameter hasGlass = mgr.AddParameter(
        "HasGlassPanel",
        GroupTypeId.General,
        SpecTypeId.Boolean.YesNo,
        true);   // true = instance parameter

    // Set formula: FrameWidth = Width / 10
    FamilyParameter width = mgr.Parameters.Cast<FamilyParameter>()
        .FirstOrDefault(p => p.Definition.Name == "Width");
    if (width != null)
        mgr.SetFormula(frameWidth, "Width / 10");

    // Create new types from a data table
    var typeSizes = new (string name, double widthMm, double heightMm)[]
    {
        ("900x2100", 900, 2100),
        ("1000x2100", 1000, 2100),
        ("1200x2400", 1200, 2400),
    };

    foreach (var (name, wMm, hMm) in typeSizes)
    {
        mgr.NewType(name);   // creates and makes current

        var wParam = mgr.Parameters.Cast<FamilyParameter>()
            .First(p => p.Definition.Name == "Width");
        var hParam = mgr.Parameters.Cast<FamilyParameter>()
            .First(p => p.Definition.Name == "Height");

        mgr.Set(wParam, UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(wMm, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));
        mgr.Set(hParam, UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(hMm, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));
    }

    // List all types with their parameter values
    foreach (FamilyType ft in mgr.Types)
    {
        mgr.CurrentType = ft;
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Type: {ft.Name}");
        foreach (FamilyParameter fp in mgr.Parameters)
        {
            Parameter val = mgr.GetValue(fp);
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"  {fp.Definition.Name} = {val?.AsValueString()}");
        }
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

// Load modified family back into project
famDoc.LoadFamily(doc, new FamilyLoadOverwriteOptions());
famDoc.Close(false);

sections.title: IFamilyLoadOptions
sections.content:
InterfaceIFamilyLoadOptions
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Controls behaviour when loading a family that already exists in the project. Implement this interface to silently overwrite existing families without showing a confirmation dialog.
🟢 Implementation Pattern
// Example — Silent overwrite implementation
// Implement IFamilyLoadOptions to always overwrite
public class FamilyLoadOverwriteOptions : IFamilyLoadOptions
{
    // Called when a family with the same name already exists
    public bool OnFamilyFound(bool familyInUse,
        out bool overwriteParamValues)
    {
        overwriteParamValues = true;  // overwrite parameter values too
        return true;                  // true = proceed with load
    }

    // Called when a shared family is found
    public bool OnSharedFamilyFound(Family sharedFamily,
        bool familyInUse,
        out FamilySource source,
        out bool overwriteParamValues)
    {
        source = FamilySource.Family;  // use the file version
        overwriteParamValues = true;
        return true;
    }
}

// Usage
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Reload Family"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Family result;
    doc.LoadFamily(path, new FamilyLoadOverwriteOptions(), out result);
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: DirectShape
sections.content:
ClassDirectShape
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
A lightweight element that displays custom geometry without requiring a full family document. DirectShapes are created from Solid, Mesh, or TessellatedShapeBuilder geometry. They're ideal for visualisation, IFC import, analysis overlays, and temporary geometry. DirectShapes appear in views but cannot host other elements.
🟢 Static Factory + Usage
🟣public static DirectShape CreateElement(Document doc, ElementId categoryId)
Creates an empty DirectShape element assigned to the given category. After creation, call SetShape() to assign geometry. The categoryId determines how the shape appears in schedules and visibility filters.
// Example — DirectShape from solid + from tessellated builder
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "DirectShape"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // ── Method 1: From an existing Solid ──
    Solid sourceSolid = GetSolid(someElement);  // your helper
    if (sourceSolid != null)
    {
        DirectShape ds = DirectShape.CreateElement(doc,
            new ElementId(BuiltInCategory.OST_GenericModel));
        ds.SetShape(new GeometryObject[] { sourceSolid });
        ds.Name = "Clash Volume";
    }

    // ── Method 2: From TessellatedShapeBuilder (arbitrary mesh) ──
    var builder = new TessellatedShapeBuilder();
    builder.OpenConnectedFaceSet(true);  // true = closed solid

    // Add 4 triangular faces of a tetrahedron
    XYZ[] verts = {
        new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(5,0,0),
        new XYZ(0,5,0), new XYZ(0,0,5)
    };
    builder.AddFace(new TessellatedFace(
        new List<XYZ> { verts[0], verts[1], verts[2] },
        ElementId.InvalidElementId));
    builder.AddFace(new TessellatedFace(
        new List<XYZ> { verts[0], verts[1], verts[3] },
        ElementId.InvalidElementId));
    builder.AddFace(new TessellatedFace(
        new List<XYZ> { verts[0], verts[2], verts[3] },
        ElementId.InvalidElementId));
    builder.AddFace(new TessellatedFace(
        new List<XYZ> { verts[1], verts[2], verts[3] },
        ElementId.InvalidElementId));

    builder.CloseConnectedFaceSet();
    builder.Build();
    TessellatedShapeBuilderResult result = builder.GetBuildResult();

    DirectShape ds2 = DirectShape.CreateElement(doc,
        new ElementId(BuiltInCategory.OST_GenericModel));
    ds2.SetShape(result.GetGeometricalObjects());
    ds2.Name = "Tetrahedron";

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: SketchPlane
sections.content:
ClassSketchPlane
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Family documents only
A reference plane used as the base for sketching family geometry. Required for creating extrusions, blends, revolves and sweeps in family documents. Created from a Plane using SketchPlane.Create().
🟢 Factory + Extrusion Pattern
🟣public static SketchPlane Create(Document doc, Plane geometryPlane)
Creates a SketchPlane from a geometric Plane. The SketchPlane defines the drawing plane for family geometry (extrusions, sweeps, revolves).
// Example — Create extrusion in family document
// Must be in a family document
if (!famDoc.IsFamilyDocument) return;

using (var tx = new Transaction(famDoc, "Create Extrusion"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // 1. Create sketch plane (XY plane at Z=0)
    Plane       plane      = Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisZ, XYZ.Zero);
    SketchPlane sketchPln  = SketchPlane.Create(famDoc, plane);

    // 2. Create profile as CurveArrArray
    double wFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(600, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    double hFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(400, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

    var loop = new CurveArray();
    loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(wFt,0,0)));
    loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(wFt,0,0), new XYZ(wFt,hFt,0)));
    loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(wFt,hFt,0), new XYZ(0,hFt,0)));
    loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,hFt,0), new XYZ(0,0,0)));

    var profile = new CurveArrArray();
    profile.Append(loop);

    // 3. Create the extrusion
    double depth = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(2100, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    Extrusion extr = famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewExtrusion(
        true,       // isSolid
        profile,
        sketchPln,
        depth);

    // 4. Assign material
    ElementId matId = new FilteredElementCollector(famDoc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Material)).Cast<Material>()
        .First(m => m.Name.Contains("Wood")).Id;
    extr.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.MATERIAL_ID_PARAM).Set(matId);

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: PrintManager
sections.content:
ClassPrintManager
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Manages print operations for a document. Allows setting the printer, paper size, print range (all sheets, selection, or current view), and submitting print jobs. Access via doc.PrintManager.
🟢 Key Usage Pattern
// Example — Print all sheets to a printer
PrintManager pm = doc.PrintManager;

// Set printer name
pm.SelectNewPrintDriver("Microsoft Print to PDF");

// Configure print setup
pm.PrintRange = PrintRange.Select;
pm.CombinedFile = false;   // one file per sheet

// Build view set of all sheets
ViewSet viewSet = pm.ViewSheetSetting.CurrentViewSheetSet.Views;
var sheets = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewSheet))
    .Cast<ViewSheet>()
    .OrderBy(s => s.SheetNumber)
    .ToList();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Print"))
{
    tx.Start();
    var viewSheetSet = pm.ViewSheetSetting.CurrentViewSheetSet;
    viewSheetSet.Views = new ViewSet();
    foreach (var s in sheets) viewSheetSet.Views.Insert(s);
    pm.ViewSheetSetting.SaveAs("All Sheets");
    tx.Commit();
}

// Submit print job (shows print dialog unless InSession=true)
pm.SubmitPrint();
System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Sent {sheets.Count} sheets to printer");

sections.title: PDFExportOptions
sections.content:
ClassPDFExportOptions
Inherits: ExportOptions
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Options for exporting views or sheets to PDF format. Introduced in Revit 2022 as a native PDF export (replacing the virtual printer workaround). Supports individual files per sheet or combined multi-page PDF.
🟢 PDF Export Pattern
// Example — Export all sheets as individual PDFs
// Build list of sheet view IDs
var sheetIds = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewSheet))
    .ToElementIds()
    .ToList();

var opts = new PDFExportOptions
{
    FileName                    = "Project Drawings",
    Combine                     = false,   // false = one PDF per sheet
    ExportQuality               = PDFExportQualityType.DPI300,
    ColorDepth                  = ColorDepthType.Color,
    RasterQuality               = RasterQualityType.High,
    HideScopeBoxes              = true,
    HideReferencePlanes         = true,
    HideUnreferencedViewTags    = true
};

// Set naming rule: sheet number + sheet name
opts.SetNamingRule("{Sheet Number}-{Sheet Name}");

// Export
bool success = doc.Export(
    @"C:\Output\PDFs",   // folder
    sheetIds,
    opts);

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    success ? $"Exported {sheetIds.Count} PDFs" : "Export failed");

// Combined single PDF
opts.Combine  = true;
opts.FileName = "All_Sheets_Combined";
doc.Export(@"C:\Output", sheetIds, opts);

sections.title: DWGExportOptions
sections.content:
ClassDWGExportOptions
Inherits: ExportOptions
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Options for exporting to AutoCAD DWG format. Allows controlling DWG version, layer settings, linetype scaling, coordinate system, and whether to merge views into one file.
🟢 DWG Export Pattern
// Example — Export floor plans as DWG files
var floorPlanIds = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewPlan))
    .Cast<ViewPlan>()
    .Where(v => !v.IsTemplate)
    .Select(v => v.Id)
    .ToList();

var dwgOpts = new DWGExportOptions
{
    FileVersion         = ACADVersion.R2018,     // DWG 2018 format
    ExportingAreas      = false,
    MergedViews         = false,   // one DWG per view
    SharedCoords        = true,    // use shared coordinates
    HideUnreferenced    = true
};

bool ok = doc.Export(@"C:\DWG_Output", floorPlanIds, dwgOpts);
System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(ok ? "DWG export OK" : "DWG export failed");

sections.title: ImageExportOptions
sections.content:
ClassImageExportOptions
Inherits: ExportOptions
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Options for exporting views as raster image files (PNG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP). Allows setting resolution (DPI), pixel size, zoom, and view range. Each view exports to a separate image file.
🟢 Image Export Pattern
// Example — Export 3D view as 300 DPI PNG
View3D v3d = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(View3D)).Cast<View3D>()
    .First(v => !v.IsTemplate);

var imgOpts = new ImageExportOptions
{
    ExportRange          = ExportRange.SetOfViews,
    FilePath             = @"C:\Images\3D_View.png",
    HLRandWFViewsFileType = ImageFileType.PNG,
    ImageResolution      = ImageResolution.DPI_300,
    PixelSize            = 1920,   // width in pixels
    ShadowViewsFileType  = ImageFileType.PNG,
    ZoomType             = ZoomFitType.FitToPage
};
imgOpts.SetViewsAndSheets(new List<ElementId> { v3d.Id });

doc.ExportImage(imgOpts);
System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine("Image exported");

sections.title: IFCExportOptions
sections.content:
ClassIFCExportOptions
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Options for exporting the model to IFC format. Supports IFC2x3, IFC4, and various coordination view configurations. Allows setting property sets, geometric representation, and coordinate systems.
🟢 IFC Export Pattern
// Example — Export to IFC2x3 Coordination View
var ifcOpts = new IFCExportOptions();
ifcOpts.FileVersion           = IFCVersion.IFC2x3CV2;   // IFC2x3 Coordination View
ifcOpts.ExportBaseQuantities  = true;                    // include quantities
ifcOpts.SpaceBoundaryLevel    = 2;                        // space boundary LOD
ifcOpts.AddOption("ExportInternalRevitPropertySets", "true");
ifcOpts.AddOption("ExportIFCCommonPropertySets",     "true");
ifcOpts.AddOption("Export2DElements",                "false");
ifcOpts.AddOption("ExportPartsAsBuildingElementParts", "false");

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "IFC Export"))
{
    tx.Start();
    doc.Export(@"C:\IFC_Output", "Building_Model.ifc", ifcOpts);
    tx.Commit();
}

// IFC version options:
// IFCVersion.IFC2x3         — basic IFC2x3
// IFCVersion.IFC2x3CV2      — IFC2x3 Coordination View 2.0 (most common)
// IFCVersion.IFC2x3BFM      — BIM Collaboration Format
// IFCVersion.IFC4           — IFC4 (latest)
// IFCVersion.IFC4DTV        — IFC4 Design Transfer View
// IFCVersion.IFC4RV         — IFC4 Reference View

sections.title: WorksharingUtils
sections.content:
ClassWorksharingUtils
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Static utility class for workshared document operations — checking out elements, querying ownership status, and getting checkout information. Only meaningful for workshared (central file) models.
🟢 Key Static Methods
🟢public static CheckoutStatus GetCheckoutStatus(Document doc, ElementId elementId)
Returns the checkout/ownership status of the specified element. Check this before attempting to modify elements in a workshared model.
Returns: CheckoutStatus — OwnedByCurrentUser, OwnedByOtherUser, NotOwned, or Unknown.
🟢public static ICollection<ElementId> CheckoutElements(Document doc, ICollection<ElementId> elementIds)
Attempts to check out (borrow) the specified elements. Returns the IDs of elements that were successfully checked out. Elements owned by other users are silently skipped.
Returns: ICollection<ElementId> — IDs of successfully checked-out elements.
🟢public static WorksharingTooltipInfo GetWorksharingTooltipInfo(Document doc, ElementId elementId)
Returns detailed ownership information including owner name, last changed by, and last changed on date. Use for building ownership audit reports.
// Example — Worksharing audit
if (!doc.IsWorkshared) return;

var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).ToElementIds();

var ownedByOthers = new List<ElementId>();
var ownedByMe     = new List<ElementId>();

foreach (var id in walls)
{
    CheckoutStatus status =
        WorksharingUtils.GetCheckoutStatus(doc, id);

    switch (status)
    {
        case CheckoutStatus.OwnedByCurrentUser:
            ownedByMe.Add(id); break;
        case CheckoutStatus.OwnedByOtherUser:
            ownedByOthers.Add(id);
            // Get owner name
            var info = WorksharingUtils
                .GetWorksharingTooltipInfo(doc, id);
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"Owned by: {info.Owner} | Last changed: {info.LastChangedBy}");
            break;
    }
}

// Try to check out un-owned elements
var unowned = walls.Where(id =>
    WorksharingUtils.GetCheckoutStatus(doc, id)
        == CheckoutStatus.NotOwned).ToList();

var checkedOut = WorksharingUtils.CheckoutElements(doc, unowned);
System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Checked out {checkedOut.Count} of {unowned.Count} requested");

sections.title: Workset
sections.content:
ClassWorkset
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a single workset in a workshared model. Provides the workset name, ID, kind, and whether it's currently open. Worksets are managed via WorksetTable obtained from doc.GetWorksetTable().
🟠 Properties + WorksetTable Usage
🟠public WorksetId Id { get; }
Gets the workset's unique identifier. Use this ID to assign elements to a workset via ELEM_PARTITION_PARAM.
🟠public string Name { get; }
Gets the display name of the workset.
🟠public WorksetKind Kind { get; }
Gets the workset kind: UserCreated, FamilyWorkset, ViewWorkset, or StandardWorkset.
// Example — Workset operations
if (!doc.IsWorkshared) return;

WorksetTable wt = doc.GetWorksetTable();

// List all user-created worksets
IList<Workset> worksets =
    WorksetTable.GetWorksets(doc, WorksetKind.UserCreated);

foreach (var ws in worksets)
{
    // Count elements on this workset
    int count = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .WherePasses(new ElementWorksetFilter(ws.Id))
        .GetElementCount();

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Workset: {ws.Name} | Elements: {count} | Kind: {ws.Kind}");
}

// Move a wall to a specific workset
Workset targetWs = worksets.First(w => w.Name == "Architecture");

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Move to Workset"))
{
    tx.Start();
    wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ELEM_PARTITION_PARAM)
        .Set(targetWs.Id.IntegerValue);
    tx.Commit();
}

// Create a new workset
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "New Workset"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Workset.Create(doc, "MEP Systems");
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: ProjectInfo
sections.content:
ClassProjectInfo
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Stores project-level information: name, number, address, client name, author, issue date and project status. Access via doc.ProjectInformation. Parameters are the same as in Manage → Project Information.
🟢 Usage Pattern
// Example — Read and write project information
ProjectInfo pi = doc.ProjectInformation;

// Read project info
string projName   = pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_NAME)?.AsString();
string projNumber = pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_NUMBER)?.AsString();
string projStatus = pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_STATUS)?.AsString();
string clientName = pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.CLIENT_NAME)?.AsString();
string author     = pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_AUTHOR)?.AsString();
string issueDate  = pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_ISSUE_DATE)?.AsString();
string address    = pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_ADDRESS)?.AsString();
string buildingName = pi.BuildingName;    // direct property
string orgName    = pi.OrganizationName;  // direct property

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Project: {projName} | Number: {projNumber} | Client: {clientName}");

// Write project info
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Update Project Info"))
{
    tx.Start();
    pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_NAME)  .Set("Office Building A");
    pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_NUMBER).Set("2024-001");
    pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.CLIENT_NAME)   .Set("Acme Corporation");
    pi.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PROJECT_STATUS).Set("Construction Documents");
    tx.Commit();
}

// Also accessible as doc.Application.Username for current user
string user = doc.Application.Username;

sections.title: Dimension
sections.content:
ClassDimension
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a dimension annotation in a view. Dimensions are created using doc.Create.NewDimension() with a ReferenceArray and a dimension line. They can be single-segment or multi-segment. Dimension values are read-only (derived from geometry) but text can be overridden.
🟢 Key Properties + Creation
🟠public double? Value { get; }
Gets the dimension value in internal units (feet) for single-segment dimensions. Returns null for multi-segment dimensions (use Segments instead).
🟠public DimensionSegmentArray Segments { get; }
Gets the individual segments of a multi-segment dimension string. Each segment has its own Value and optional text override.
// Example — Create dimension between wall faces
// ── REFERENCE TYPES SUMMARY ──
// Wall face:      face.Reference (from get_Geometry with ComputeReferences=true)
// Grid:           new Reference(grid)
// Curve endpoint: curve.GetEndPointReference(0 or 1)
// FamilyInstance: fi.GetReferences(FamilyInstanceReferenceType.Left)

Options opts = new Options { ComputeReferences = true };
GeometryElement geom = wall.get_Geometry(opts);
Reference face1 = null, face2 = null;

foreach (GeometryObject obj in geom)
{
    if (obj is Solid s)
        foreach (Face f in s.Faces)
        {
            XYZ n = f.ComputeNormal(new UV(.5,.5));
            if      (n.X >  0.9) face1 = f.Reference;  // exterior face
            else if (n.X < -0.9) face2 = f.Reference;  // interior face
        }
}

if (face1 == null || face2 == null) return;

ReferenceArray refs = new ReferenceArray();
refs.Append(face1);
refs.Append(face2);

XYZ p1     = (wall.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.GetEndPoint(0);
Line dimLn = Line.CreateBound(
    p1 + new XYZ(0, 3, 0),
    p1 + new XYZ(10, 3, 0));

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Dimension"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Dimension dim = doc.Create.NewDimension(doc.ActiveView, dimLn, refs);
    tx.Commit();

    // Read value
    if (dim.Value.HasValue)
    {
        double mm = dim.Value.Value * 304.8;
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Wall thickness: {mm:F0}mm");
    }
}

// Multi-segment: read each segment
Dimension multiDim = ...;
foreach (DimensionSegment seg in multiDim.Segments)
{
    double segMm = (seg.Value ?? 0) * 304.8;
    string ovr  = seg.ValueOverride ?? "(none)";
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Seg: {segMm:F0}mm override={ovr}");
}

sections.title: IndependentTag
sections.content:
ClassIndependentTag
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a tag annotation attached to an element in a view. Tags display parameter values from the tagged element. Created with IndependentTag.Create() and associated with a specific element via Reference.
🟢 Static Factory + Usage
🟣public static IndependentTag Create(Document doc, ElementId viewId, Reference reference, bool addLeader, TagMode tagMode, TagOrientation orientation, XYZ point)
Creates a tag in the specified view for the element identified by reference. The point is where the tag head is placed. addLeader controls whether a leader line is shown.
// Example — Batch tag all untagged walls
View activeView = doc.ActiveView;

// Get all walls visible in this view
var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc, activeView.Id)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>().ToList();

// Find already-tagged element IDs
var taggedIds = new FilteredElementCollector(doc, activeView.Id)
    .OfClass(typeof(IndependentTag))
    .Cast<IndependentTag>()
    .Where(t => t.GetTaggedLocalElement() != null)
    .Select(t => t.GetTaggedLocalElement().Id)
    .ToHashSet();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Tag Walls"))
{
    tx.Start();
    foreach (var wall in walls)
    {
        if (taggedIds.Contains(wall.Id)) continue;  // skip tagged

        // Place tag at wall midpoint + offset
        LocationCurve lc  = wall.Location as LocationCurve;
        XYZ           mid = lc.Curve.Evaluate(0.5, true);
        XYZ           pos = mid + new XYZ(0, 1, 0);  // 300mm offset

        IndependentTag.Create(
            doc,
            activeView.Id,
            new Reference(wall),
            false,                              // no leader
            TagMode.TM_ADDBY_CATEGORY,
            TagOrientation.Horizontal,
            pos);
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: TextNote
sections.content:
ClassTextNote
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a text annotation in a view. TextNotes are view-specific and have a TextNoteType controlling font, size, and style. Used for drawing notes, callout labels, and automated annotation.
🟢 Static Factory + Edit
🟣public static TextNote Create(Document doc, ElementId viewId, XYZ origin, string text, ElementId typeId)
Creates a text note at the specified position in the specified view. The typeId must be a TextNoteType element ID.
// Example — Text note creation and styling
// Get or find a TextNoteType
TextNoteType tnt = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(TextNoteType))
    .Cast<TextNoteType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(t => t.Name.Contains("3mm"))
    ?? new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(TextNoteType)).Cast<TextNoteType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Text Note"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Simple text note
    TextNote note = TextNote.Create(
        doc,
        doc.ActiveView.Id,
        new XYZ(5, 5, 0),
        "NOTE: All dimensions in mm unless stated",
        tnt.Id);

    // Text note with formatting options
    var opts = new TextNoteOptions(tnt.Id)
    {
        HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalTextAlignment.Left,
        Rotation            = 0              // angle in radians
    };
    TextNote note2 = TextNote.Create(
        doc, doc.ActiveView.Id,
        new XYZ(5, 3, 0),
        "TYPICAL DETAIL - SEE SHEET A-401",
        opts);

    tx.Commit();
}

// Edit existing text note
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Update Note"))
{
    tx.Start();
    note.Text = "REVISED NOTE: Metric units apply throughout";
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: FilledRegion
sections.content:
ClassFilledRegion
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a filled region in a view — a 2D hatched or coloured closed boundary used for legends, masking, or drawing annotation. Created from CurveLoops with a FilledRegionType that defines the fill pattern and colour.
🟢 Static Factory
🟣public static FilledRegion Create(Document doc, ElementId filledRegionTypeId, ElementId viewId, IList<CurveLoop> boundaries)
Creates a filled region in the specified view from CurveLoops. Index 0 is the outer boundary; subsequent loops are holes inside the region.
// Example — Create filled region legend item
FilledRegionType frt = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FilledRegionType))
    .Cast<FilledRegionType>().First();

// Build a small rectangular boundary (legend swatch)
double w = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(20, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double h = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(10, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
XYZ origin = new XYZ(0.5, 0.5, 0);

var boundary = new CurveLoop();
boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(origin, origin + new XYZ(w,0,0)));
boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(origin + new XYZ(w,0,0), origin + new XYZ(w,h,0)));
boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(origin + new XYZ(w,h,0), origin + new XYZ(0,h,0)));
boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(origin + new XYZ(0,h,0), origin));

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Filled Region"))
{
    tx.Start();
    FilledRegion fr = FilledRegion.Create(
        doc, frt.Id,
        doc.ActiveView.Id,
        new List<CurveLoop> { boundary });
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: RevitLinkInstance
sections.content:
ClassRevitLinkInstance
Inherits: Instance → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a Revit link instance placed in the host document. Provides access to the linked document, transformation from link coordinates to host coordinates, and load/unload operations.
🟢 Key Properties + Usage
// Example — Navigate linked models
var links = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(RevitLinkInstance))
    .Cast<RevitLinkInstance>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var link in links)
{
    Document  linkDoc   = link.GetLinkDocument();
    Transform linkTrans = link.GetTotalTransform();
    string    linkName  = link.Name;

    if (linkDoc == null)
    {
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"{linkName}: NOT LOADED");
        continue;
    }

    // Access elements in the linked model
    int wallCount = new FilteredElementCollector(linkDoc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).GetElementCount();

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Link: {linkName} | Walls: {wallCount}");

    // Transform a point from link space to host space
    XYZ linkPt   = new XYZ(0, 0, 0);
    XYZ worldPt  = linkTrans.OfPoint(linkPt);
}

// Reload all unloaded links
var linkTypes = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(RevitLinkType))
    .Cast<RevitLinkType>()
    .Where(lt => !lt.IsLoaded(doc, lt.Id))
    .ToList();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Reload Links"))
{
    tx.Start();
    foreach (var lt in linkTypes)
        lt.Load();
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: PartUtils
sections.content:
ClassPartUtils
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Static utility class for creating and dividing Part elements from host elements (walls, floors, roofs). Parts allow the model to be divided into construction pieces (precast panels, formwork sections). Each Part represents a portion of its source host element.
🟢 Key Static Methods
🟢public static void CreateParts(Document doc, IList<ElementId> elementIds)
Creates Part elements from the specified host elements. Each host element layer becomes a separate Part. The original elements remain but Parts are now visible when Show Parts is enabled in the view.
🟢public static void DivideParts(Document doc, IList<ElementId> partIds, IList<ElementId> intersectingReferenceIds, IList<Curve> curves, SketchPlane sketchPlane)
Divides existing Parts using cutting curves on a sketch plane. Used to split floor slabs into precast panels or wall parts into construction blocks.
// Example — Create and divide floor parts
Floor floor = ...;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Parts"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Step 1: Create parts from floor
    if (PartUtils.AreElementsValidForCreateParts(doc,
        new List<ElementId> { floor.Id }))
    {
        PartUtils.CreateParts(doc,
            new List<ElementId> { floor.Id });
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

// Get the created parts
var parts = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Part))
    .Cast<Part>()
    .Where(p => p.GetSourceElementIds().Any(
        sid => sid.HostElementId == floor.Id))
    .Select(p => p.Id)
    .ToList();

// Step 2: Divide parts with cutting lines at panel spacing
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Divide Parts"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Plane       divPlane  = Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisZ, XYZ.Zero);
    SketchPlane skPln     = SketchPlane.Create(doc, divPlane);

    // Vertical cut lines every 1200mm
    double spacing = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(1200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    var cutLines = new List<Curve>();
    for (int i = 1; i < 5; i++)
        cutLines.Add(Line.CreateBound(
            new XYZ(i * spacing, -20, 0),
            new XYZ(i * spacing,  20, 0)));

    PartUtils.DivideParts(doc, parts,
        new List<ElementId>(), cutLines, skPln);

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: ElementLevelFilter
sections.content:
ClassElementLevelFilter
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Quick filter that restricts results to elements at a specific level. Pass the Level's ElementId to the constructor.
🟢 Usage
// Example — All filters in one reference
// ── ElementLevelFilter ──
var lvlFilter = new ElementLevelFilter(level.Id);
var onLevel = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls)
    .WherePasses(lvlFilter).ToList();

// ── BoundingBoxIntersectsFilter ──
var outline = new Outline(
    new XYZ(0, 0, 0),
    new XYZ(10, 10, 10));
var bboxFilter = new BoundingBoxIntersectsFilter(outline);
var inBox = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(bboxFilter).ToList();

// ── ElementParameterFilter ──
var provider   = new ParameterValueProvider(
    new ElementId(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK));
var evaluator  = new FilterStringEquals();
var rule       = new FilterStringRule(provider, evaluator, "W-001");
var paramFilt  = new ElementParameterFilter(rule);
var marked     = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WherePasses(paramFilt).ToList();

// ── LogicalAndFilter — combine two filters ──
var andFilt = new LogicalAndFilter(lvlFilter, bboxFilter);
var both    = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(andFilt).ToList();

// ── LogicalOrFilter ──
var wallFilter  = new ElementClassFilter(typeof(Wall));
var floorFilter = new ElementClassFilter(typeof(Floor));
var orFilt      = new LogicalOrFilter(wallFilter, floorFilter);
var either      = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(orFilt).ToList();

// ── ExclusionFilter — exclude specific IDs ──
var toExclude  = walls.Take(5).Select(w => w.Id).ToList();
var exclFilter = new ExclusionFilter(toExclude);
var remaining  = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WherePasses(exclFilter).ToList();

// ── ElementWorksetFilter (workshared models) ──
var wsFilter = new ElementWorksetFilter(worksetId);
var onWS     = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(wsFilter).ToList();

// ── ExtensibleStorageFilter ──
var esFilter = new ExtensibleStorageFilter(schemaGuid);
var withData = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(esFilter).ToList();

// ── String rule evaluators (FilterStringRule) ──
// FilterStringEquals       — exact match
// FilterStringContains     — substring match
// FilterStringBeginsWith   — prefix match
// FilterStringEndsWith     — suffix match
// FilterStringGreater      — alphabetic greater
// FilterStringLess         — alphabetic less

// ── Numeric rule evaluators (FilterDoubleRule / FilterIntegerRule) ──
// FilterNumericEquals      — exact double equality (with tolerance)
// FilterNumericGreater     — greater than
// FilterNumericGreaterOrEqual
// FilterNumericLess        — less than
// FilterNumericLessOrEqual

// Example: walls taller than 3000mm
var hProvider = new ParameterValueProvider(
    new ElementId(BuiltInParameter.WALL_USER_HEIGHT_PARAM));
double minH  = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
var hRule     = new FilterDoubleRule(hProvider,
    new FilterNumericGreater(), minH, 1e-6);
var tallWalls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WherePasses(new ElementParameterFilter(hRule))
    .Cast<Wall>().ToList();

sections.title: SunAndShadowSettings
sections.content:
ClassSunAndShadowSettings
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Controls sun position and shadow settings for a view. Access via view.GetSunAndShadowSettings(). Set sun path date, time, ground plane, and whether shadows are displayed.
🟢 Key Usage
// Example — Set sun position for summer solstice
