View3D view3D = ...;
SunAndShadowSettings sun = view3D.GetSunAndShadowSettings();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Sun Settings"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Set to single-frame sun study mode
    sun.SunAndShadowType = SunStudyType.StillImage;

    // Set date and time (summer solstice at noon)
    sun.ActiveFrameTime = new DateTime(2024, 6, 21, 12, 0, 0);

    // Enable shadows in this view
    view3D.AreAnalyticalModelCategoriesHidden = false;

    // Sun altitude and azimuth (read-only — computed from date/location)
    double azimuth  = sun.GetFrameAzimuth(sun.ActiveFrameTime);
    double altitude = sun.GetFrameAltitude(sun.ActiveFrameTime);
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Sun: Az={azimuth * 180/Math.PI:F1}° Alt={altitude * 180/Math.PI:F1}°");

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: BooleanOperationsUtils
sections.content:
ClassBooleanOperationsUtils
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Static utility class for performing Boolean operations (union, intersection, difference) on Solid objects. Used for clash detection, volume calculation of overlapping elements, and custom geometry creation.
🟢 Key Static Methods
🟢public static Solid ExecuteBooleanOperation(Solid solidA, Solid solidB, BooleanOperationsType operationType)
Performs a Boolean operation between two solids. Returns the result solid, or null if the operation results in empty geometry. Throws if solids are incompatible.
ParameterTypeDescription
solidASolidFirst operand solid.
solidBSolidSecond operand solid.
operationTypeBooleanOperationsTypeUnion, Intersect, or Difference.
Returns: Solid — Result solid, or null if empty result.
// Example — All three Boolean operations
Solid sA = GetSolid(wallElem);
Solid sB = GetSolid(columnElem);

if (sA == null || sB == null) return;

try
{
    // Intersection — overlap volume (clash detection)
    Solid intersection = BooleanOperationsUtils
        .ExecuteBooleanOperation(sA, sB,
            BooleanOperationsType.Intersect);

    if (intersection?.Volume > 1e-9)
    {
        double clashVol = intersection.Volume * 0.0283168;  // m³
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"CLASH: {clashVol:F6}m³");
    }

    // Union — merged volume
    Solid union = BooleanOperationsUtils
        .ExecuteBooleanOperation(sA, sB,
            BooleanOperationsType.Union);

    // Difference — A minus B (cut)
    Solid difference = BooleanOperationsUtils
        .ExecuteBooleanOperation(sA, sB,
            BooleanOperationsType.Difference);
}
catch (Autodesk.Revit.Exceptions.InvalidOperationException)
{
    // Solids may be incompatible — handle gracefully
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine("Boolean operation failed - incompatible geometry");
}

// BooleanOperationsType values:
// BooleanOperationsType.Union       — A ∪ B
// BooleanOperationsType.Intersect   — A ∩ B
// BooleanOperationsType.Difference  — A − B

sections.title: CompoundStructureLayer
sections.content:
ClassCompoundStructureLayer
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a single layer within a CompoundStructure (wall, floor, or roof type). Each layer has a material, thickness, function, and deck profile. Accessed via CompoundStructure.GetLayers().
🟢 Complete Layer Analysis Pattern
// Example — Full compound structure analysis
// Get compound structure from a wall type
var wallTypes = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(WallType)).Cast<WallType>().ToList();

foreach (var wt in wallTypes)
{
    CompoundStructure cs = wt.GetCompoundStructure();
    if (cs == null) continue;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"WallType: {wt.Name}");
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"  Total width: {cs.GetWidth() * 304.8:F0}mm");

    for (int i = 0; i < cs.LayerCount; i++)
    {
        double  thickness = cs.GetLayerWidth(i) * 304.8;
        ElementId matId  = cs.GetMaterialId(i);
        Material mat     = doc.GetElement(matId) as Material;
        MaterialFunctionAssignment fn = cs.GetLayerFunction(i);

        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"  Layer {i}: {fn} | {mat?.Name ?? "No material"} | {thickness:F0}mm");
    }

    // Identify structural layer index
    int structLayer = cs.StructuralLayerIndex;

    // Modify layer thickness
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Modify Layer"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        cs.SetLayerWidth(0,
            UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(20, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));
        wt.SetCompoundStructure(cs);
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// MaterialFunctionAssignment values:
// Structure, Finish1, Finish2, Membrane, Substrate
// Insulation, InsulationBelowGrade, etc.

sections.title: Application
sections.content:
ClassApplication
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.ApplicationServices · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents the Revit application session. Provides access to application-level settings, open documents, product version, username, and the ability to open/create documents. Accessed from IExternalCommand via commandData.Application.Application, or from IExternalApplication via ControlledApplication.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public string VersionName { get; }
Gets the full version name string, e.g. "Autodesk Revit 2024". Use for logging and version-specific feature branching.
🟠public string VersionNumber { get; }
Gets the version number string, e.g. "2024". Parse to int for numeric comparisons.
🟠public string VersionBuild { get; }
Gets the full build number string (e.g. "24.0.1.0"). Useful for logging exact build information in bug reports.
🟠public string Username { get; }
Gets the current Revit username (from Autodesk account or network login). Used for worksharing ownership tracking and audit logs.
🟠public DocumentSet Documents { get; }
Gets all currently open documents (project and family files). Iterate to find a specific document or to perform batch operations across all open files.
🟠public ProductType Product { get; }
Gets the Revit product type: Revit, RevitLT, MEP, Structure, or Architecture. Used for feature availability checks.
🟠public LanguageType Language { get; }
Gets the current Revit UI language (English_USA, English_GB, German, French, Arabic, Chinese_Simplified, etc.). Use for parameter name lookups and localised text.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public Document OpenDocumentFile(string filename)
Opens a Revit file (.rvt, .rfa, .rte) by file path and returns the Document. The file is opened as a regular project — not workshared. Use for batch processing multiple files.
🟢public Document OpenDocumentFile(ModelPath modelPath, OpenOptions openOptions)
Opens a document from a ModelPath (supports Revit Server and cloud models). OpenOptions allows setting audit, detach from central, and workset loading options.
🟢public Document NewFamilyDocument(string templateFileName)
Creates a new empty family document from the specified template (.rft file). The template determines the family category, reference planes, and coordinate system. Used when programmatically building new families from scratch.
// Example — Application-level operations
Application app = commandData.Application.Application;

// Version checks
string ver      = app.VersionNumber;     // "2024"
string build    = app.VersionBuild;     // "24.0.1.0"
string user     = app.Username;
bool   isRevit  = app.Product == ProductType.Revit;

// List all open documents
foreach (Document d in app.Documents)
{
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Open: {d.Title} | Family: {d.IsFamilyDocument} | Modified: {d.IsModified}");
}

// Open another file (for batch processing)
Document batchDoc = app.OpenDocumentFile(@"C:\Projects\Building_B.rvt");
// ... process batchDoc ...
batchDoc.Close(false);   // close without saving

// Open with options (detach from central, audit)
var openOpts = new OpenOptions();
openOpts.DetachFromCentralOption = DetachFromCentralOption.DetachAndPreserveWorksets;
openOpts.Audit = true;
ModelPath mp = ModelPathUtils.ConvertUserVisiblePathToModelPath(@"C:\test.rvt");
Document detached = app.OpenDocumentFile(mp, openOpts);

// Create a new family document
string template  = @"C:\ProgramData\Autodesk\RVT 2024\Family Templates\English\Generic Model.rft";
Document newFam  = app.NewFamilyDocument(template);
// ... add parameters and geometry to newFam ...

sections.title: ControlledApplication
sections.content:
ClassControlledApplication
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.ApplicationServices
A restricted view of the Application accessible in IExternalApplication.OnStartup(). Unlike Application, ControlledApplication does not allow document-level operations during startup. Used primarily for subscribing to application-level events and reading version/language info.
🟠 Key Properties + Event Subscription
🟠public string VersionName { get; }
Same as Application.VersionName — full version string.
// Example — ControlledApplication in OnStartup
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    ControlledApplication cApp = app.ControlledApplication;

    // Subscribe to all document lifecycle events
    cApp.DocumentOpened     += OnDocOpened;
    cApp.DocumentCreated    += OnDocCreated;
    cApp.DocumentSaving     += OnDocSaving;
    cApp.DocumentSaved      += OnDocSaved;
    cApp.DocumentClosing    += OnDocClosing;
    cApp.DocumentClosed     += OnDocClosed;
    cApp.DocumentChanged    += OnDocChanged;
    cApp.DocumentPrinting   += OnDocPrinting;
    cApp.DocumentSynchronizingWithCentral += OnSyncing;
    cApp.DocumentSynchronizedWithCentral  += OnSynced;

    // UI events on UIControlledApplication
    app.ViewActivated    += OnViewActivated;
    app.Idling           += OnIdling;
    app.ApplicationInitialized += OnAppInit;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Plugin loaded for Revit {cApp.VersionName} | User: {cApp.Username}");

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    // !! Always unsubscribe ALL events here !!
    app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened  -= OnDocOpened;
    app.ControlledApplication.DocumentChanged -= OnDocChanged;
    app.ControlledApplication.DocumentSaving  -= OnDocSaving;
    app.ControlledApplication.DocumentClosing -= OnDocClosing;
    app.ViewActivated                          -= OnViewActivated;
    app.Idling                                 -= OnIdling;
    return Result.Succeeded;
}

sections.title: Creation.Document
sections.content:
ClassCreation.Document
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Creation · Accessed via doc.Create
Factory object for creating model elements in a project document. Access as doc.Create. All methods require an open Transaction. This is the primary factory for annotations, openings, model curves, spaces, and text notes that don't have static Create() methods on their own class.
🟢 Complete Factory Method Reference
// Example — All doc.Create factory methods
// ── FAMILY INSTANCES ──
// Level-based (furniture, equipment)
FamilyInstance fi1 = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
    point, sym, level, StructuralType.NonStructural);

// Wall-hosted (door, window)
FamilyInstance fi2 = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
    point, sym, wall, StructuralType.NonStructural);

// Face-based
FamilyInstance fi3 = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
    faceRef, point, XYZ.BasisX, sym, StructuralType.NonStructural);

// Structural column (must use StructuralType.Column)
FamilyInstance col = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
    point, sym, level, StructuralType.Column);

// Structural beam (must use StructuralType.Beam)
FamilyInstance beam = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
    line, sym, level, StructuralType.Beam);

// ── DIMENSIONS ──
Dimension dim = doc.Create.NewDimension(view, dimLine, refArray);
SpotDimension spot = doc.Create.NewSpotElevation(
    view, ref1, origin, bend, end, false);
SpotDimension spotCoord = doc.Create.NewSpotCoordinate(
    view, ref1, origin, bend, end, false);
SpotDimension slope = doc.Create.NewSpotSlope(
    view, ref1, origin, end, false);

// ── ANNOTATIONS ──
DetailLine dl = doc.Create.NewDetailCurve(view, curve) as DetailLine;
ModelCurve mc = doc.Create.NewModelCurve(curve, sketchPlane);

// ── OPENINGS ──
Opening wallOp  = doc.Create.NewOpening(wall, pt1, pt2);
Opening floorOp = doc.Create.NewOpening(floor, boundary, true);
Opening shaftOp = doc.Create.NewOpening(btmLevel, topLevel, boundary);

// ── ROOMS & SPACES ──
Room  room  = doc.Create.NewRoom(level, new UV(5,5));
Space space = doc.Create.NewSpace(level, new UV(5,5));

// ── TAGS ──
SpatialElementTag roomTag = doc.Create.NewRoomTag(
    new LinkElementId(room.Id), new UV(5,5), null);

// ── REVISION CLOUDS ──
RevisionCloud cloud = doc.Create.NewRevisionCloud(
    view, revision, loops);

// ── FOOTPRINT ROOF ──
ModelCurveArray mapping;
FootPrintRoof roof = doc.Create.NewFootPrintRoof(
    footprintCurves, level, roofType, out mapping);

// ── SWEPT BLEND / BLEND / REVOLVE ──
// (in family documents only — use doc.FamilyCreate)

sections.title: Creation.FamilyItemFactory
sections.content:
ClassCreation.FamilyItemFactory
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Creation · Accessed via famDoc.FamilyCreate · Family docs only
Factory for creating geometry elements inside a family document. Access via famDoc.FamilyCreate. All geometry creation methods require a SketchPlane and CurveArrArray profile. Only available in family (.rfa) documents.
🟢 Complete FamilyCreate Method Reference
// Example — All famDoc.FamilyCreate methods
// All require: Transaction + SketchPlane + CurveArrArray profile

// ── EXTRUSION ──
Extrusion extr = famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewExtrusion(
    true,         // isSolid (false = void)
    profile,      // CurveArrArray
    sketchPlane,
    depth);        // feet

// ── BLEND (loft between two profiles) ──
Blend blend = famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewBlend(
    true,         // isSolid
    topProfile,   // CurveArray at top
    bottomProfile,// CurveArray at bottom
    sketchPlane,
    topElevation); // feet

// ── REVOLVE (rotation solid) ──
RevolveOptions revOpts = new RevolveOptions();
Revolve revolve = famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewRevolve(
    true,
    profile,
    sketchPlane,
    axis,          // Line — axis of revolution
    startAngle,    // radians
    endAngle,      // radians
    revOpts);

// ── SWEEP (profile along path) ──
Sweep sweep = famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewSweep(
    true,
    sweepPath,     // ReferenceArray along path curves
    sketchPlane,
    profileStart,  // profile at start
    0,             // profile index
    ProfileLocationLine.Start);

// ── VOID FORMS ──
// Same methods but isSolid = false
Extrusion voidExtr = famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewExtrusion(
    false, profile, sketchPlane, depth);

// ── CUT GEOMETRY (apply void to solid) ──
famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewCutGeometry(solidExtrusion, voidExtr);

// ── DIMENSIONS IN FAMILY ──
famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewDimension(view, dimLine, refArray);

// ── REFERENCE PLANES ──
famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewReferencePlane(
    bubbleEnd, freeEnd, cutVector, view);

sections.title: All Exception Types
sections.content:
ReferenceAll Exception Types
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.Exceptions · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Revit defines its own exception hierarchy separate from System.Exception. All Revit exceptions inherit from RevitExceptions. Catch specific types for fine-grained error handling; catch the base RevitException for generic Revit errors.
🟡 Exception Types Reference
ExceptionThrown WhenHow to Handle
InvalidOperationExceptionAPI call made at wrong time (e.g. starting Transaction inside event, calling API from background thread)Check context — use ExternalEvent for background thread calls
ArgumentExceptionArgument value is invalid (null curve, degenerate geometry, etc.)Validate inputs before API call
ArgumentNullExceptionRequired argument is nullNull-check all inputs
ApplicationExceptionGeneral Revit application-level errorLog and report to user
OperationCanceledExceptionUser pressed Escape during PickObject, PickPoint, or PickObjectsAlways catch this after Selection.PickObject() calls
ExternalApplicationExceptionError in an external application's startup/shutdownCheck OnStartup/OnShutdown for exceptions
ForbiddenForDynamicUpdateExceptionIllegal operation inside IUpdater.Execute() (e.g. starting new Transaction)Modify elements directly inside Execute() — no new Transaction
RegenerationFailedExceptionModel regeneration failed after an operationRollBack the transaction; simplify the operation
InternalExceptionInternal Revit error — usually geometry or corrupted elementSkip element or rollback; log element ID
FileNotFoundExceptionFamily .rfa or template file not foundValidate file path before calling LoadFamily/OpenDocumentFile
FileAccessExceptionFile is locked, read-only, or permission deniedCheck file permissions; warn user
DocumentValidationExceptionDocument is in an invalid state for the operationCheck doc.IsReadOnly, doc.IsModified
// Example — Comprehensive exception handling pattern
public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd, ref string msg, ElementSet els)
{
    try
    {
        Document doc = cd.Application.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
        if (doc == null) { msg = "No active document"; return Result.Failed; }

        // Selection — catch Escape (OperationCanceledException)
        Reference picked;
        try
        {
            picked = cd.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Selection
                .PickObject(ObjectType.Element, "Select element");
        }
        catch (Autodesk.Revit.Exceptions.OperationCanceledException)
        {
            return Result.Cancelled;   // user pressed Escape
        }

        using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Operation"))
        {
            tx.Start();
            try
            {
                // ... work ...
                tx.Commit();
            }
            catch (Autodesk.Revit.Exceptions.InvalidOperationException ex)
            {
                tx.RollBack();
                msg = $"Invalid operation: {ex.Message}";
                return Result.Failed;
            }
            catch (Autodesk.Revit.Exceptions.RegenerationFailedException)
            {
                tx.RollBack();
                msg = "Geometry regeneration failed — operation rolled back.";
                return Result.Failed;
            }
        }
        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        // Catch-all for unexpected errors
        msg = $"Unexpected error: {ex.Message}";
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(ex.StackTrace);
        return Result.Failed;
    }
}

sections.title: ObjectType
sections.content:
EnumObjectType
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI.Selection
Specifies what type of object the user can pick in a PickObject() call. Must match what you expect to retrieve — mismatched ObjectType causes empty or null references.
🟡 Enum Values
MemberWhat user can pickReference gives
ElementAny visible element in the modelReference to the element itself
FaceA face of an elementReference to the specific face (for dimensioning)
EdgeAn edge of an elementReference to the specific edge
SubelementA subelement (like a curtain wall panel)Reference to the subelement
NothingPick a point only (like PickPoint)No reference — use for coordinate selection
LinkedElementAn element inside a Revit linkReference with LinkedElementId set
PointOnElementA specific point on an element surfaceReference to the face + the picked UV

sections.title: ISelectionFilter
sections.content:
InterfaceISelectionFilter
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI.Selection
Controls what the user can select during a PickObject() or PickObjects() call. AllowElement() returns true for elements that should be selectable; AllowReference() filters sub-element picks (faces, edges).
🟢 Complete Selection Filter Patterns
// Example — All ISelectionFilter patterns
// ── Filter 1: Category-based ──
public class WallFilter : ISelectionFilter
{
    public bool AllowElement(Element e) => e is Wall;
    public bool AllowReference(Reference r, XYZ p) => true;
}

// ── Filter 2: Multiple categories ──
public class StructuralFilter : ISelectionFilter
{
    private static readonly HashSet<BuiltInCategory> _cats =
        new HashSet<BuiltInCategory>
        {
            BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralColumns,
            BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralFraming,
            BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralFoundation
        };
    public bool AllowElement(Element e)
        => e.Category != null &&
           _cats.Contains((BuiltInCategory)e.Category.Id.IntegerValue);
    public bool AllowReference(Reference r, XYZ p) => true;
}

// ── Filter 3: Face-only (for dimensioning) ──
public class WallFaceFilter : ISelectionFilter
{
    public bool AllowElement(Element e) => e is Wall;
    public bool AllowReference(Reference r, XYZ p)
    {
        // Only allow face references (not edge or point references)
        return r.ElementReferenceType == ElementReferenceType.REFERENCE_TYPE_SURFACE;
    }
}

// ── Filter 4: Custom predicate ──
public class PredicateFilter : ISelectionFilter
{
    private readonly Func<Element, bool> _predicate;
    public PredicateFilter(Func<Element, bool> pred) => _predicate = pred;
    public bool AllowElement(Element e) => _predicate(e);
    public bool AllowReference(Reference r, XYZ p) => true;
}

// ── Usage ──
var sel = uidoc.Selection;

// Pick one wall only
try
{
    Reference r = sel.PickObject(ObjectType.Element,
        new WallFilter(), "Pick a wall");
    Wall wall = doc.GetElement(r) as Wall;
}
catch (Autodesk.Revit.Exceptions.OperationCanceledException) { }

// Pick multiple using custom predicate
var refs = sel.PickObjects(ObjectType.Element,
    new PredicateFilter(e => e.is FamilyInstance), "Pick family instances");

// Pick a face
Reference faceRef = sel.PickObject(ObjectType.Face,
    new WallFaceFilter(), "Click a wall face");

// Pick linked element
Reference linkRef = sel.PickObject(ObjectType.LinkedElement,
    "Pick element in linked model");
RevitLinkInstance link = doc.GetElement(linkRef.ElementId) as RevitLinkInstance;
Document          lDoc = link?.GetLinkDocument();
ElementId          lId  = linkRef.LinkedElementId;
Element            lElem = lDoc?.GetElement(lId);

sections.title: MyPlugin.addin
sections.content:
FILEMyPlugin.addin
Location: %AppData%\Autodesk\Revit\Addins\2024\ or same folder as DLL
The .addin manifest file tells Revit which DLL to load, what commands and applications it contains, and how to display them. There are two entry types: IExternalApplication (for IExternalApplication add-ins with OnStartup/OnShutdown) and IExternalCommand (for standalone commands).
🟢 Complete Manifest Template
// Complete .addin manifest — all elements
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RevitAddIns>

  <!-- ═══ APPLICATION ENTRY (IExternalApplication) ═══ -->
  <AddIn Type="Application">

    <!-- REQUIRED: Unique GUID — generate once, never change -->
    <AddInId>12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789ABC</AddInId>

    <!-- REQUIRED: Display name in Revit -->
    <Name>Studio BIM Tools</Name>

    <!-- REQUIRED: Full class name (namespace.classname) -->
    <FullClassName>StudioTools.MyApplication</FullClassName>

    <!-- REQUIRED: DLL file path (absolute or relative to .addin) -->
    <Assembly>C:\Program Files\StudioTools\StudioTools.dll</Assembly>

    <!-- OPTIONAL: Vendor / company info -->
    <VendorId>STUDIO</VendorId>
    <VendorDescription>Studio Architecture Ltd, www.studio.com</VendorDescription>

  </AddIn>


  <!-- ═══ COMMAND ENTRY (IExternalCommand) ═══ -->
  <!-- Use ONLY when you don't have an IExternalApplication -->
  <AddIn Type="Command">

    <!-- Different GUID from Application entry -->
    <AddInId>AAAABBBB-CCCC-DDDD-EEEE-FFFFAAAABBBB</AddInId>

    <Name>Count Walls</Name>
    <FullClassName>StudioTools.WallCountCommand</FullClassName>
    <Assembly>StudioTools.dll</Assembly>

    <!-- OPTIONAL: Description shown in Revit -->
    <Description>Counts all walls in the active document</Description>

    <!-- OPTIONAL: Button image path (32x32 PNG) -->
    <LargeImage>C:\...\images\icon_32.png</LargeImage>

    <!-- OPTIONAL: Small image (16x16 PNG) for stack panels -->
    <SmallImage>C:\...\images\icon_16.png</SmallImage>

    <VendorId>STUDIO</VendorId>

  </AddIn>

</RevitAddIns>

sections.title: Assembly & Project Setup
sections.content:
SETUPAssembly & Project Setup
Essential Visual Studio project configuration for a Revit add-in.
🟢 .csproj settings + Minimal Command Template
// .csproj — essential settings
<!-- Target .NET Framework (NOT .NET Core/.NET 5+) -->
<TargetFramework>net48</TargetFramework>

<!-- Output path to Revit Addins folder -->
<OutputPath>
  $(AppData)\Autodesk\Revit\Addins\2024\
</OutputPath>

<!-- References — mark as CopyLocal=false so DLLs aren't copied -->
<Reference Include="RevitAPI">
  <HintPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2024\RevitAPI.dll</HintPath>
  <Private>False</Private>
</Reference>
<Reference Include="RevitAPIUI">
  <HintPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2024\RevitAPIUI.dll</HintPath>
  <Private>False</Private>
</Reference>
// Minimal complete command — production template
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using System;

namespace StudioTools
{
    [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
    [Regeneration(RegenerationOption.Manual)]
    public class MyCommand : IExternalCommand
    {
        public Result Execute(
            ExternalCommandData commandData,
            ref string message,
            ElementSet elements)
        {
            try
            {
                UIApplication uiapp = commandData.Application;
                UIDocument    uidoc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument;
                Document      doc   = uidoc?.Document;

                if (doc == null)
                {
                    message = "No active document open.";
                    return Result.Failed;
                }

                // ── YOUR CODE HERE ──
                using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "My Operation"))
                {
                    tx.Start();
                    // ... modify model ...
                    tx.Commit();
                }

                return Result.Succeeded;
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                message = ex.Message;
                return Result.Failed;
            }
        }
    }
}

Advanced UI
Reference

sections.title: UIControlledApplication
sections.content:
ClassUIControlledApplication
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI · Available only in IExternalApplication.OnStartup()
The entry point for all ribbon construction. Provided as the parameter to IExternalApplication.OnStartup(). Used to create ribbon tabs, panels, register DockablePanes, and subscribe to UI-level events. After OnStartup() completes, the ribbon structure is frozen — you cannot add new tabs or panels at runtime.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public void CreateRibbonTab(string tabName)
Creates a new custom ribbon tab. The tab appears in the Revit ribbon between the standard Revit tabs and the Add-Ins tab. Tab names must be unique — if the tab already exists (e.g. from a previous Revit session), Revit throws. Always wrap in try/catch.
🟢public RibbonPanel CreateRibbonPanel(string tabName, string panelName)
Creates a panel on a specific tab. Use after CreateRibbonTab() for your custom tabs. Panel names must be unique within a tab.
🟢public RibbonPanel CreateRibbonPanel(string panelName)
Creates a panel on the built-in "Add-Ins" tab. Quick option when you don't need a custom tab.
🟢public void RegisterDockablePane(DockablePaneId id, string title, IDockablePaneProvider provider)
Registers a dockable pane. Must be called in OnStartup() before any pane operations. The provider creates the WPF content. See DockablePane section for the full lifecycle.
// Example — Complete tab + panel construction in OnStartup
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    // ── Step 1: Create custom tab ──
    const string TAB   = "Studio Tools";
    const string DLL   = System.Reflection.Assembly
        .GetExecutingAssembly().Location;

    try { app.CreateRibbonTab(TAB); }
    catch { /* tab already exists — ignore */ }

    // ── Step 2: Create panels on the tab ──
    RibbonPanel panelModel = app.CreateRibbonPanel(TAB, "Model Tools");
    RibbonPanel panelView  = app.CreateRibbonPanel(TAB, "View Tools");
    RibbonPanel panelData  = app.CreateRibbonPanel(TAB, "Data Tools");

    // ── Step 3: Add buttons to panels ──
    BuildModelPanel(panelModel, DLL);
    BuildViewPanel(panelView, DLL);
    BuildDataPanel(panelData, DLL);

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

sections.title: PushButton
sections.content:
ClassPushButton
Inherits: RibbonButton → RibbonItem
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
A standard clickable button that triggers one IExternalCommand. The most common ribbon control. Created via panel.AddItem(PushButtonData). After creation you can update its text, image, tooltip, and availability class at any time.
🟠 Key Properties (runtime update)
🟠public string ItemText { get; set; }
Gets or sets the button label. Can be updated at runtime — e.g. changing "Show Panel" to "Hide Panel".
🟠public ImageSource LargeImage { get; set; }
32×32 button image (shown when panel is wide). Set with BitmapImage or BitmapFrame from WPF.
🟠public ImageSource Image { get; set; }
16×16 small button image (shown in stacked items or narrow panels).
🟠public string ToolTip { get; set; }
Short tooltip text shown on hover. Keep under 50 characters.
🟠public string LongDescription { get; set; }
Expanded tooltip shown after 1 second hover. Use to explain what the button does in detail.
🟠public bool Enabled { get; set; }
Enables or disables the button at runtime. Disabled buttons are greyed out. Prefer IExternalCommandAvailability for context-sensitive enabling.
🟠public bool Visible { get; set; }
Shows or hides the button. Hidden buttons don't occupy ribbon space.

sections.title: PushButtonData
sections.content:
ClassPushButtonData
Inherits: ButtonData → RibbonItemData
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
Data class used to configure a PushButton before adding it to a panel. Contains the command class name, assembly path, and all display properties. Create PushButtonData, configure it, then pass to panel.AddItem().
🟣 Constructor
🟣public PushButtonData(string name, string text, string assemblyName, string className)
Creates button data. name = internal unique ID (no spaces), text = display label, assemblyName = full DLL path, className = fully qualified class name of the IExternalCommand.
ParameterTypeDescription
namestringInternal unique name. No spaces. Used as the button's programmatic ID.
textstringDisplay label on the button. Use \n to force a line break.
assemblyNamestringFull absolute path to the .dll containing the command class.
classNamestringFully qualified class name: "Namespace.ClassName"
// Example — Complete PushButton with all properties
string dll = System.Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location;

// Helper: load image from embedded resource
System.Windows.Media.ImageSource LoadImg(string resourcePath)
{
    var uri = new Uri($"pack://application:,,,/{resourcePath}");
    return new System.Windows.Media.Imaging.BitmapImage(uri);
}

// ── Create PushButtonData ──
var btnData = new PushButtonData(
    "btn_WallCount",                    // internal name (no spaces)
    "Count\nWalls",                     // label (\n = line break)
    dll,                               // DLL path
    "StudioTools.WallCountCommand");    // fully qualified class

// Display properties
btnData.LargeImage   = LoadImg("StudioTools;component/Resources/wall_count_32.png");
btnData.Image        = LoadImg("StudioTools;component/Resources/wall_count_16.png");
btnData.ToolTip      = "Count all walls in the active document";
btnData.LongDescription = "Counts walls by type and outputs a summary report. "
                         + "Works on the active document or all open documents.";

// Availability — only enabled when a document is open
btnData.AvailabilityClassName = "StudioTools.RequiresDocument";

// Help file link (optional)
btnData.SetContextualHelp(new ContextualHelp(
    ContextualHelpType.Url,
    "https://docs.mystudio.com/wall-count"));

// ── Add to panel ──
PushButton btn = panelModel.AddItem(btnData) as PushButton;

// ── Runtime updates ──
btn.Enabled = false;                  // disable
btn.ItemText = "Wall\nReport";        // change label
btn.LargeImage = LoadImg("...");      // change icon
btn.ToolTip    = "Updated tooltip";   // change tooltip

// ── Panel separator (visual divider) ──
panelModel.AddSeparator();

// ── Slide-out panel (hidden controls below ──) ──
panelModel.AddSlideOut();              // items added after this go to the slide-out
panelModel.AddItem(anotherBtnData);    // this button only shows when expanded

sections.title: SplitButton
sections.content:
ClassSplitButton
Inherits: RibbonItem
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
A button that shows the last-used command on the main face, with a drop-down arrow revealing other commands. The clicked command becomes the new "current" command shown on the face. Ideal for related commands where one is used most often (e.g. "Create Wall" variants).
🟢 Creation + Usage
// Example — SplitButton with 3 commands
// Create SplitButtonData
var splitData = new SplitButtonData("splitWallCreate", "Create Wall");

// Add to panel — returns SplitButton
SplitButton splitBtn = panel.AddItem(splitData) as SplitButton;

// Add commands to the split button's drop-down
PushButton pb1 = splitBtn.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData(
    "createBasicWall", "Basic Wall", dll, "StudioTools.CreateBasicWall"));
pb1.LargeImage = LoadImg("...");

PushButton pb2 = splitBtn.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData(
    "createCurtainWall", "Curtain Wall", dll, "StudioTools.CreateCurtainWall"));
pb2.LargeImage = LoadImg("...");

PushButton pb3 = splitBtn.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData(
    "createStackedWall", "Stacked Wall", dll, "StudioTools.CreateStackedWall"));
pb3.LargeImage = LoadImg("...");

// IsSynchronizedWithCurrentItem controls whether the face shows last-used command
splitBtn.IsSynchronizedWithCurrentItem = true;  // default and recommended

// Set default (current) button programmatically
splitBtn.CurrentButton = pb1;

sections.title: PulldownButton
sections.content:
ClassPulldownButton
Inherits: RibbonItem
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
A button with a permanent drop-down arrow that always shows a menu of commands. Unlike SplitButton, the face never changes — it always shows the same icon and text. Ideal for grouping a category of related but equally important commands (e.g. an "Export" button with PDF, DWG, IFC options).
🟢 Creation + Usage
// Example — PulldownButton export menu
var pdData = new PulldownButtonData("pdExport", "Export");
pdData.LargeImage = LoadImg("export_32.png");
pdData.ToolTip    = "Export model to various formats";

PulldownButton pd = panel.AddItem(pdData) as PulldownButton;

// Add commands to the drop-down
PushButton pbPdf = pd.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData(
    "exportPDF", "Export PDF", dll, "StudioTools.ExportPDFCommand"));
pbPdf.Image = LoadImg("pdf_16.png");

PushButton pbDwg = pd.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData(
    "exportDWG", "Export DWG", dll, "StudioTools.ExportDWGCommand"));
pbDwg.Image = LoadImg("dwg_16.png");

pd.AddSeparator();   // visual divider in the dropdown

PushButton pbIfc = pd.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData(
    "exportIFC", "Export IFC", dll, "StudioTools.ExportIFCCommand"));
pbIfc.Image = LoadImg("ifc_16.png");

sections.title: ComboBox
sections.content:
ClassComboBox
Inherits: RibbonItem
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
A dropdown list control that shows text items. The user can select an item from the list or type a value. Items can be grouped using ComboBoxMember.GroupName. The CurrentChanged event fires when selection changes — useful for filter dropdowns, phase selectors, or view template pickers.
🟢 Creation + Events + Runtime Update
// Example — ComboBox phase selector updated on document open
// ── Create ComboBox in OnStartup ──
var cbData = new ComboBoxData("cbPhase");
cbData.ToolTip = "Select active phase";

static ComboBox _phaseComboBox;
_phaseComboBox = panel.AddItem(cbData) as ComboBox;

// Add static items at startup
_phaseComboBox.AddItem(new ComboBoxMemberData("existing", "Existing"));
_phaseComboBox.AddItem(new ComboBoxMemberData("newconstruction", "New Construction"));
_phaseComboBox.AddItem(new ComboBoxMemberData("demolition", "Demolition"));

// Subscribe to selection change event
_phaseComboBox.CurrentChanged += OnPhaseComboChanged;

// ── Update items dynamically when document opens ──
private void OnDocumentOpened(object s, DocumentOpenedEventArgs e)
{
    Document doc = e.Document;

    // Get all phases from document
    var phases = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Phase)).Cast<Phase>()
        .OrderBy(p => p.Id.IntegerValue).ToList();

    // Clear and repopulate ComboBox
    _phaseComboBox.ClearList();
    foreach (var phase in phases)
    {
        var mbr = new ComboBoxMemberData(
            phase.Id.IntegerValue.ToString(),
            phase.Name);
        _phaseComboBox.AddItem(mbr);
    }
    // Set to last phase
    _phaseComboBox.Current = _phaseComboBox.GetItems().LastOrDefault();
}

// ── Handle selection change ──
private void OnPhaseComboChanged(object s, Autodesk.Revit.UI.Events.ComboBoxCurrentChangedEventArgs e)
{
    ComboBoxMember selected = e.NewValue;
    if (selected == null) return;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Phase changed to: {selected.Name}");
    // Store selection for use by commands
    MyApp.SelectedPhaseId = new ElementId(int.Parse(selected.Name));
}

sections.title: TextBox
sections.content:
ClassTextBox
Inherits: RibbonItem
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
An editable text input field embedded in the ribbon. Used for quick filter inputs, search boxes, or numeric entry that commands can read. The EnterPressed and CurrentChanged events fire when the user presses Enter or changes the text.
🟢 Creation + Events
// Example — TextBox search filter in ribbon
var tbData = new TextBoxData("tbSearch");
tbData.Name        = "Search Rooms";
tbData.ToolTip     = "Filter rooms by name or number";
tbData.PromptText  = "Search rooms…";  // placeholder text
tbData.ShowImageAsButton = false;

static TextBox _searchBox;
_searchBox = panel.AddItem(tbData) as TextBox;

// Set width
_searchBox.Width = 140;   // pixels

// Enter pressed event
_searchBox.EnterPressed += (sender, args) =>
{
    string query = (_searchBox.Value as string) ?? "";
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Search: {query}");
    // Trigger a search command with the query
    MyApp.PendingSearchQuery = query;
    // Raise external event to execute the search
    MyApp.SearchEvent.Raise();
};

// Read value from a command
string currentSearch = _searchBox.Value as string;

sections.title: RadioButtonGroup
sections.content:
ClassRadioButtonGroup
Inherits: RibbonItem
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
A group of mutually exclusive toggle buttons. Exactly one button in the group is active at any time. Used for mode selection (e.g. "Metric / Imperial", "Gross / Net areas", "2D / 3D view"). The CurrentChanged event fires when selection changes.
🟢 Creation + Usage
// Example — Unit mode toggle
var rbgData = new RadioButtonGroupData("rbgUnits");
RadioButtonGroup rbg = panel.AddItem(rbgData) as RadioButtonGroup;

// Add toggle buttons
ToggleButton metricBtn = rbg.AddItem(new ToggleButtonData(
    "rbMetric", "Metric", dll, "StudioTools.SetMetricCommand"));
metricBtn.LargeImage = LoadImg("metric_32.png");
metricBtn.ToolTip    = "Display values in metric (mm)";

ToggleButton imperialBtn = rbg.AddItem(new ToggleButtonData(
    "rbImperial", "Imperial", dll, "StudioTools.SetImperialCommand"));
imperialBtn.LargeImage = LoadImg("imperial_32.png");

// Set default selection
rbg.Current = metricBtn;

// Handle change
rbg.CurrentChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    bool isMetric = rbg.Current.Name == "rbMetric";
    MyApp.UseMetric = isMetric;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Unit mode: {(isMetric ? "Metric" : "Imperial")}");
};

sections.title: AddStackedItems — Stacked Controls
sections.content:
MethodAddStackedItems — Stacked Controls
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI · Called on RibbonPanel
Places 2 or 3 compact controls stacked vertically in one column width. Each item shows only a 16×16 icon and text. Used to pack more controls into limited ribbon space. Supports PushButtonData, ComboBoxData, and TextBoxData as stacked items.
🟢 Stacked Items Pattern
// Example — 3-item stack + 2-item stack
// ── 3 stacked PushButtons ──
var s1 = new PushButtonData("sBtn1", "Renumber Rooms",  dll, "StudioTools.RenumberRooms");
var s2 = new PushButtonData("sBtn2", "Tag All Rooms",    dll, "StudioTools.TagAllRooms");
var s3 = new PushButtonData("sBtn3", "Export Room List", dll, "StudioTools.ExportRooms");

s1.Image = LoadImg("room_renumber_16.png");
s2.Image = LoadImg("room_tag_16.png");
s3.Image = LoadImg("room_export_16.png");

IList<RibbonItem> stacked3 = panel.AddStackedItems(s1, s2, s3);
PushButton renumberBtn = stacked3[0] as PushButton;

// ── 2 stacked: ComboBox + PushButton ──
var cbData  = new ComboBoxData("cbViewType");
var btnGo   = new PushButtonData("btnGoView", "Go to View", dll, "StudioTools.GoToView");
btnGo.Image = LoadImg("go_16.png");

IList<RibbonItem> stacked2 = panel.AddStackedItems(cbData, btnGo);
ComboBox viewCombo = stacked2[0] as ComboBox;

sections.title: IExternalCommandAvailability
sections.content:
InterfaceIExternalCommandAvailability
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
Controls whether a ribbon button is enabled or disabled based on the current Revit context. Implement this interface to make buttons context-sensitive — e.g. disabled when no document is open, or only active when a 3D view is active, or only when walls are selected. Set via PushButtonData.AvailabilityClassName.
// Performance NoteIsCommandAvailable() is called very frequently — on every selection change, view activation, and idle cycle. Keep it fast: no FilteredElementCollector queries inside. Only check UIApplication.ActiveUIDocument and UIDocument.ActiveGraphicalView.
🟢 Complete Availability Patterns
// Example — 5 common availability implementations
// ── 1. Requires any document open ──
public class RequiresDocument : IExternalCommandAvailability
{
    public bool IsCommandAvailable(UIApplication app,
        CategorySet selectedCategories)
    {
        return app.ActiveUIDocument != null;
    }
}

// ── 2. Requires a project document (not family) ──
public class RequiresProject : IExternalCommandAvailability
{
    public bool IsCommandAvailable(UIApplication app,
        CategorySet selectedCategories)
    {
        var doc = app.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
        return doc != null && !doc.IsFamilyDocument;
    }
}

// ── 3. Only in a 3D view ──
public class Requires3DView : IExternalCommandAvailability
{
    public bool IsCommandAvailable(UIApplication app,
        CategorySet selectedCategories)
    {
        return app.ActiveUIDocument?.ActiveGraphicalView
            is View3D;
    }
}

// ── 4. Requires at least one wall selected ──
public class RequiresWallSelection : IExternalCommandAvailability
{
    public bool IsCommandAvailable(UIApplication app,
        CategorySet selectedCategories)
    {
        // selectedCategories — categories of currently selected elements
        // Fast — already computed by Revit, no collector needed
        foreach (Category cat in selectedCategories)
            if (cat.Id.IntegerValue == (int)BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls)
                return true;
        return false;
    }
}

// ── 5. Requires workshared document ──
public class RequiresWorkshared : IExternalCommandAvailability
{
    public bool IsCommandAvailable(UIApplication app,
        CategorySet selectedCategories)
    {
        var doc = app.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
        return doc != null && doc.IsWorkshared;
    }
}

// ── Assign to button ──
btnData.AvailabilityClassName = typeof(RequiresWallSelection).FullName;
// Or as string: "StudioTools.RequiresWallSelection"

sections.title: PostableCommand
sections.content:
EnumPostableCommand
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
Identifies built-in Revit commands that can be triggered programmatically via UIApplication.PostCommand(). Covers every Revit ribbon button — from Undo/Redo to Open/Save to specific tools. This is how you trigger Revit's native tools from your add-in code without having to re-implement them.
🟡 Key PostableCommand Values
MemberDescription
UndoTriggers the Undo command
RedoTriggers the Redo command
SaveSaves the active document
SaveAsOpens Save As dialog
CloseCloses the active document
SynchronizeWithCentralSync to central file (workshared)
WallActivates the Wall placement tool
DoorActivates Door placement tool
WindowActivates Window placement tool
ColumnActivates Column placement tool
GridActivates Grid drawing tool
LevelActivates Level drawing tool
AlignDimensionActivates the aligned dimension tool
AnnotateTagActivates tag placement tool
TextNoteActivates text note tool
ThreeDViewOpens a new default 3D view
ViewPropertiesOpens View Properties dialog
PrintOpens Print dialog
ProjectInformationOpens Project Information dialog
ManageLinksOpens Manage Links dialog
PurgeOpens Purge Unused dialog
WorksetsDialogOpens Worksets dialog
DesignOptionsBarOpens Design Options bar
SelectionBoxCreates selection box in active 3D view
ResetTemporaryHideIsolateResets all temporary hide/isolate overrides
HideInViewHides selected elements in current view
IsolateElementInViewIsolates selected elements in view

sections.title: RevitCommandId
sections.content:
ClassRevitCommandId
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
Represents a reference to a built-in Revit command. Obtained from RevitCommandId.LookupPostableCommandId() or RevitCommandId.LookupCommandId(). Used with UIApplication.PostCommand() to trigger commands and with CanExecuteNextCommand to check availability.
🟢 Complete PostCommand Patterns
// Example — PostCommand usage patterns
UIApplication uiapp = commandData.Application;

// ── Method 1: From PostableCommand enum (most common) ──
RevitCommandId saveId = RevitCommandId
    .LookupPostableCommandId(PostableCommand.Save);

if (uiapp.CanPostCommand(saveId))
    uiapp.PostCommand(saveId);

// ── Method 2: From command name string ──
// Get command names by running the journal or using RevitCommandId.LookupCommandId
RevitCommandId purgeId = RevitCommandId
    .LookupCommandId("ID_PURGE_UNUSED");
if (purgeId != null && uiapp.CanPostCommand(purgeId))
    uiapp.PostCommand(purgeId);

// ── Practical examples ──

// Trigger Sync to Central after batch operation
var syncId = RevitCommandId.LookupPostableCommandId(
    PostableCommand.SynchronizeWithCentral);
if (uiapp.CanPostCommand(syncId))
    uiapp.PostCommand(syncId);

// Reset temporary hide/isolate after batch visual changes
var resetId = RevitCommandId.LookupPostableCommandId(
    PostableCommand.ResetTemporaryHideIsolate);
uiapp.PostCommand(resetId);

// Open a new 3D view
var v3dId = RevitCommandId.LookupPostableCommandId(
    PostableCommand.ThreeDView);
uiapp.PostCommand(v3dId);

// IMPORTANT: PostCommand is asynchronous!
// The command fires AFTER your current Execute() returns.
// You cannot wait for its result in the same Execute() call.
// If you need to react to it, subscribe to the relevant event.

sections.title: Contextual Ribbon Tabs
sections.content:
PatternContextual Ribbon Tabs
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
Contextual tabs appear and disappear automatically based on user selection — exactly like Revit's own "Modify | Walls" tab. Implemented by subscribing to UIApplication.ViewActivated and Selection.SelectionChanged and showing/hiding panel visibility via RibbonPanel.Visible.
🟢 Contextual Tab Pattern
// Example — Show/hide panel based on selected element type
// In OnStartup — create panel but start hidden
RibbonPanel wallContextPanel = app.CreateRibbonPanel("Studio Tools", "Wall Tools");
wallContextPanel.Visible = false;   // hidden by default

// Store reference statically
MyApp.WallContextPanel = wallContextPanel;

// Subscribe to selection change via Idling (selection API)
app.Idling += OnIdlingCheckSelection;

// ── Handle selection changes ──
private static ICollection<ElementId> _lastSelection = new List<ElementId>();

private void OnIdlingCheckSelection(object sender, IdlingEventArgs e)
{
    e.SetRaiseWithoutDelay(false);   // be CPU-kind

    var uiapp = sender as UIApplication;
    var uidoc = uiapp?.ActiveUIDocument;
    if (uidoc == null) { MyApp.WallContextPanel.Visible = false; return; }

    var selIds = uidoc.Selection.GetElementIds();

    // Only update if selection changed
    if (selIds.SequenceEqual(_lastSelection)) return;
    _lastSelection = selIds;

    // Check if any selected element is a Wall
    bool hasWall = selIds
        .Select(id => uidoc.Document.GetElement(id))
        .Any(el => el is Wall);

    // Show/hide the contextual panel
    MyApp.WallContextPanel.Visible = hasWall;
}

sections.title: IDockablePaneProvider
sections.content:
InterfaceIDockablePaneProvider
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.UI
Implemented by the class that provides the WPF content for a DockablePane. Revit calls SetupDockablePane() when the pane is first created. This method configures the pane — setting the content (your WPF UserControl) and initial state.
🟢 Complete DockablePane Implementation
// Example — Complete DockablePane: provider + registration + show/hide + update
// ════════════════════════════════════
// STEP 1: Create WPF UserControl (RoomInspectorPane.xaml.cs)
// ════════════════════════════════════
public partial class RoomInspectorPane : System.Windows.Controls.UserControl,
    IDockablePaneProvider
{
    public void SetupDockablePane(DockablePaneProviderData data)
    {
        // Set this UserControl as the pane content
        data.FrameworkElement = this;

        // Configure initial docking state
        data.InitialState = new DockablePaneState
        {
            DockPosition = DockPosition.Right,        // dock to right side
            MinimumWidth = 280,
            MinimumHeight = 200
        };
    }

    // Method to update WPF content from Revit API thread
    public void UpdateRoomData(Room room)
    {
        // This runs on the main thread via ExternalEvent — safe to update UI
        if (room == null) { lblRoomName.Content = "No room selected"; return; }
        lblRoomName.Content  = $"{room.Number} — {room.Name}";
        lblRoomArea.Content  = $"{room.Area * 0.092903:F1} m²";
        lblRoomLevel.Content = room.Level?.Name ?? "—";
    }
}

// ════════════════════════════════════
// STEP 2: Register in OnStartup
// ════════════════════════════════════
public class MyApplication : IExternalApplication
{
    public static DockablePaneId       PaneId;
    public static RoomInspectorPane   PaneContent;

    public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        // Create a unique GUID for this pane — must be stable across sessions
        PaneId      = new DockablePaneId(
            new Guid("AABBCCDD-1234-5678-ABCD-EF0123456789"));
        PaneContent = new RoomInspectorPane();

        // Register pane
        app.RegisterDockablePane(PaneId, "Room Inspector", PaneContent);

        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
}

// ════════════════════════════════════
// STEP 3: Show / Hide from a command
// ════════════════════════════════════
[Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
public class ToggleRoomInspectorCommand : IExternalCommand
{
    public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd, ref string msg, ElementSet els)
    {
        DockablePane pane = cd.Application.GetDockablePane(MyApplication.PaneId);

        if (pane.IsShown())
            pane.Hide();
        else
            pane.Show();

        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
}

// ════════════════════════════════════
// STEP 4: Update content via ExternalEvent
// ════════════════════════════════════
public class UpdateRoomPaneHandler : IExternalEventHandler
{
    public ElementId RoomId { get; set; }

    public void Execute(UIApplication app)
    {
        Document doc  = app.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
        Room     room = doc?.GetElement(RoomId) as Room;

        // Update WPF content (we're on main thread — safe)
        MyApplication.PaneContent.UpdateRoomData(room);
    }
    public string GetName() => "Update Room Pane";
}

// DockPosition options:
// DockPosition.Left   — left panel area (like Properties)
// DockPosition.Right  — right panel area
// DockPosition.Top    — top area
// DockPosition.Bottom — bottom area  
// DockPosition.Floating — floating window (default if not set)
// DockPosition.Tabbed — tab alongside existing panes

sections.title: IDirectContext3DServer
sections.content:
InterfaceIDirectContext3DServer
Inherits: IExternalServer
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.DirectContext3D
The core interface for custom 3D rendering in Revit views. Implement this interface, register it with ExternalServiceRegistry, and Revit will call RenderScene() on every view redraw. Your implementation creates vertex/index buffers and submits draw calls to the DrawContext.
🟢 Complete IDirectContext3DServer Implementation
// Example — Clash highlight server: draw red transparent boxes on clashes
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.DirectContext3D;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.ExternalService;

public class ClashHighlightServer : IDirectContext3DServer
{
    private Guid _serviceId = ExternalServices.BuiltInExternalServices.DirectContext3DService;
    private Guid _serverId  = new Guid("CCDD1122-3344-5566-7788-AABBCCDD1122");

    // External list of bounding boxes to highlight as clash volumes
    public List<BoundingBoxXYZ> ClashBoxes { get; set; } = new();

    // ── IExternalServer required methods ──
    public Guid   GetServiceId()      => _serviceId;
    public Guid   GetServerId()       => _serverId;
    public string GetVendorId()        => "STUDIO";
    public string GetName()            => "Clash Highlight Server";
    public string GetDescription()     => "Draws clash volumes as red boxes";
    public string GetApplicationId()   => "StudioTools";

    // ── IDirectContext3DServer core methods ──

    // Should this server draw in the given view?
    public bool CanExecute(View dBView)
    {
        // Only draw in 3D views
        return dBView is View3D && ClashBoxes.Count > 0;
    }

    // Return outline that encompasses all your custom geometry
    // Used for view clipping and level-of-detail decisions
    public Outline GetBoundingBox(View dBView)
    {
        if (ClashBoxes.Count == 0)
            return new Outline(XYZ.Zero, XYZ.Zero);

        XYZ min = new XYZ(
            ClashBoxes.Min(b => b.Min.X),
            ClashBoxes.Min(b => b.Min.Y),
            ClashBoxes.Min(b => b.Min.Z));
        XYZ max = new XYZ(
            ClashBoxes.Max(b => b.Max.X),
            ClashBoxes.Max(b => b.Max.Y),
            ClashBoxes.Max(b => b.Max.Z));
        return new Outline(min, max);
    }

    // Does your geometry need to be re-rendered every frame?
    public bool UseInTransparentPass(View dBView) => true; // transparent = semi-opaque

    // ── The main draw method — called every frame ──
    public void RenderScene(View dBView, DisplayStyle displayStyle)
    {
        try
        {
            foreach (var bb in ClashBoxes)
                DrawBox(bb, new ColorWithTransparency(255, 0, 0, 128));  // red, 50% transparent
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"DC3D render error: {ex.Message}");
        }
    }

    // Helper: draw a wireframe + transparent solid box
    private static void DrawBox(BoundingBoxXYZ bb, ColorWithTransparency color)
    {
        XYZ mn = bb.Min, mx = bb.Max;

        // 8 vertices of the box
        XYZ[] v =
        {
            new XYZ(mn.X,mn.Y,mn.Z), new XYZ(mx.X,mn.Y,mn.Z),
            new XYZ(mx.X,mx.Y,mn.Z), new XYZ(mn.X,mx.Y,mn.Z),
            new XYZ(mn.X,mn.Y,mx.Z), new XYZ(mx.X,mn.Y,mx.Z),
            new XYZ(mx.X,mx.Y,mx.Z), new XYZ(mn.X,mx.Y,mx.Z)
        };

        // Build vertex buffer (position + colour per vertex)
        VertexFormatBits vfBits = VertexFormatBits.PositionColored;
        int vertexCount = v.Length;  // 8
        VertexBufferStorage vbs = new VertexBufferStorage(vfBits, vertexCount);

        VertexBuffer vb = vbs.VertexBuffer;
        vb.Map(vertexCount * VertexPositionColored.GetSizeInFloats());
        {
            VertexStreamPositionColored vs = vb.GetVertexStreamPositionColored();
            foreach (var pt in v)
                vs.AddVertex(new VertexPositionColored(pt, color));
        }
        vb.Unmap();

        // 12 triangles for 6 faces (2 triangles per face)
        int[] faces = {
            0,1,2, 0,2,3,  // bottom
            4,5,6, 4,6,7,  // top
            0,1,5, 0,5,4,  // front
            2,3,7, 2,7,6,  // back
            0,3,7, 0,7,4,  // left
            1,2,6, 1,6,5   // right
        };
        IndexBuffer ib = new IndexBuffer(faces.Length);
        ib.Map(faces.Length);
        {
            IndexStream ixs = ib.GetIndexStreamTriangle();
            for (int i = 0; i < faces.Length; i += 3)
                ixs.AddTriangle(new IndexTriangle(faces[i], faces[i+1], faces[i+2]));
        }
        ib.Unmap();

        // Submit draw call to Revit's render pipeline
        var effect = new EffectInstance(vfBits);
        DrawContext.FlushBuffer(vb, vertexCount, ib,
            faces.Length / 3,
            new VertexFormat(vfBits),
            effect,
            PrimitiveType.TriangleList);
    }
}

// ════════════════════════════════════
// REGISTRATION in OnStartup
// ════════════════════════════════════
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    var server = new ClashHighlightServer();
    MyApp.ClashServer = server;

    MultiServerService svc = ExternalServiceRegistry.GetService(
        ExternalServices.BuiltInExternalServices.DirectContext3DService)
        as MultiServerService;

    svc?.AddServer(server);

    // Activate the server for all 3D views
    IList<Guid> activeServerIds = svc?.GetActiveServerIds() ?? new List<Guid>();
    activeServerIds.Add(server.GetServerId());
    svc?.SetActiveServers(activeServerIds);

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

// ════════════════════════════════════
// USAGE: update clash boxes and invalidate view
// ════════════════════════════════════
public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd, ref string msg, ElementSet els)
{
    // Set clash boxes to highlight
    MyApp.ClashServer.ClashBoxes = DetectClashes(cd.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Document);

    // Force view to redraw (triggers RenderScene)
    cd.Application.ActiveUIDocument.RefreshActiveView();

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

sections.title: DirectContext3D Key Classes
sections.content:
ReferenceDirectContext3D Key Classes
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.DirectContext3D
🟡 Class Summary
ClassPurpose
VertexBufferGPU buffer holding vertex data (positions, colors, normals, UVs)
IndexBufferGPU index buffer defining triangle/line connectivity
VertexFormatDescribes which attributes each vertex has
VertexFormatBitsFlags: Position, PositionColored, PositionNormal, PositionNormalColored, PositionNormalTextured
EffectInstanceShader effect — controls lighting model and blending mode
DrawContextStatic class with FlushBuffer() — submits draw call to Revit's pipeline
ColorWithTransparencyRGBA color for per-vertex coloring (R,G,B,A each 0–255)
VertexStreamPositionColoredTyped stream for writing Position+Color vertices to a VertexBuffer
IndexStreamTriangleTyped stream for writing triangle indices to an IndexBuffer
IndexStreamLineTyped stream for writing line segment indices
PrimitiveTypeTriangleList, LineList, PointList
RenderingPassBufferStorageHigher-level helper combining VertexBuffer + IndexBuffer into a reusable cache

sections.title: All Attribute Types
sections.content:
ReferenceAll Attribute Types
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.Attributes
C# attribute classes used to decorate IExternalCommand and IExternalApplication implementations. These attributes are mandatory — without them Revit will not load your command correctly.
🟡 Attribute Reference Table
AttributeValuesUsageNotes
[Transaction(...)]TransactionMode.Manual
TransactionMode.Automatic
TransactionMode.ReadOnly		Required on every IExternalCommand classAlways use Manual — gives you full transaction control
[Regeneration(...)]RegenerationOption.Manual
RegenerationOption.Automatic		Optional on IExternalCommandUse Manual — Automatic is deprecated and causes performance issues
[Journaling(...)]JournalingMode.NoCommandData
JournalingMode.UsingCommandData		Optional on IExternalCommandControls whether command data is saved in Revit journal files for replay
// Example — Correct attribute decoration
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;

// ── IExternalCommand — required attributes ──
[Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]       // REQUIRED — always Manual
[Regeneration(RegenerationOption.Manual)]  // optional but recommended
public class MyCommand : IExternalCommand
{
    public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd,
        ref string msg, ElementSet els) { ... }
}

// ── Read-only command (no writes) ──
[Transaction(TransactionMode.ReadOnly)]
public class MyQueryCommand : IExternalCommand
{
    // Can read elements and parameters but cannot create/modify/delete
    public Result Execute(...) { ... }
}

// ── IExternalApplication — NO attributes needed ──
public class MyApplication : IExternalApplication
{
    // No Transaction attribute required on IExternalApplication
    public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app) { ... }
    public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app) { ... }
}

// TransactionMode comparison:
// Manual     — You control Transaction.Start/Commit/RollBack (use this always)
// Automatic  — Revit wraps your Execute() in one auto-transaction (deprecated)
// ReadOnly   — No transactions allowed. Perfect for analysis/reporting commands.

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
🟢 Full Production Template
// MyApplication.cs — complete production-grade add-in skeleton
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI.Selection;
using System;
using System.Reflection;

namespace StudioTools
{
    // ══════════════════════════════════════════
    // APPLICATION
    // ══════════════════════════════════════════
    public class StudioApplication : IExternalApplication
    {
        public static ExternalEvent       UpdatePaneEvent  { get; private set; }
        public static UpdatePaneHandler   PaneHandler      { get; private set; }
        public static MyDockablePane      PaneContent      { get; private set; }
        public static DockablePaneId      PaneId           { get; private set; }
        public static RibbonPanel         ContextPanel     { get; private set; }

        private const string TAB = "Studio Tools";

        public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
        {
            string dll = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location;

            // ── 1. Create Tab ──
            try { app.CreateRibbonTab(TAB); } catch { }

            // ── 2. Create Panels ──
            RibbonPanel panelMain = app.CreateRibbonPanel(TAB, "Tools");
            RibbonPanel panelExport = app.CreateRibbonPanel(TAB, "Export");
            ContextPanel = app.CreateRibbonPanel(TAB, "Wall Context");
            ContextPanel.Visible = false;

            // ── 3. Large button ──
            var mainBtn = new PushButtonData(
                "btnMain", "Open\nInspector", dll,
                "StudioTools.TogglePaneCommand");
            mainBtn.LargeImage = LoadImg("inspector_32.png");
            mainBtn.ToolTip    = "Open the Studio Inspector panel";
            panelMain.AddItem(mainBtn);

            panelMain.AddSeparator();

            // ── 4. Stacked small buttons ──
            var s1 = new PushButtonData("sWalls", "Audit Walls",  dll, "StudioTools.AuditWalls");
            var s2 = new PushButtonData("sRooms", "Audit Rooms",  dll, "StudioTools.AuditRooms");
            var s3 = new PushButtonData("sViews", "Audit Views",  dll, "StudioTools.AuditViews");
            s1.AvailabilityClassName = typeof(RequiresProject).FullName;
            s2.AvailabilityClassName = typeof(RequiresProject).FullName;
            s3.AvailabilityClassName = typeof(RequiresProject).FullName;
            panelMain.AddStackedItems(s1, s2, s3);

            // ── 5. PulldownButton export menu ──
            var pdData = new PulldownButtonData("pdExport", "Export");
            PulldownButton pd = panelExport.AddItem(pdData) as PulldownButton;
            pd.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData("ePDF", "PDF", dll, "StudioTools.ExportPDF"));
            pd.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData("eDWG", "DWG", dll, "StudioTools.ExportDWG"));
            pd.AddPushButton(new PushButtonData("eIFC", "IFC", dll, "StudioTools.ExportIFC"));

            // ── 6. Register DockablePane ──
            PaneId      = new DockablePaneId(new Guid("AABB1122-3344-5566-7788-CCDD9900AABB"));
            PaneContent = new MyDockablePane();
            app.RegisterDockablePane(PaneId, "Studio Inspector", PaneContent);

            // ── 7. ExternalEvent ──
            PaneHandler     = new UpdatePaneHandler();
            UpdatePaneEvent = ExternalEvent.Create(PaneHandler);

            // ── 8. Subscribe to events ──
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened  += OnDocOpened;
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentChanged += OnDocChanged;
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentClosing += OnDocClosing;
            app.Idling                                 += OnIdling;

            return Result.Succeeded;
        }

        public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
        {
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened  -= OnDocOpened;
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentChanged -= OnDocChanged;
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentClosing -= OnDocClosing;
            app.Idling                                 -= OnIdling;
            UpdatePaneEvent?.Dispose();
            return Result.Succeeded;
        }

        private void OnDocOpened(object s, Autodesk.Revit.DB.Events.DocumentOpenedEventArgs e) { }
        private void OnDocChanged(object s, Autodesk.Revit.DB.Events.DocumentChangedEventArgs e) { }
        private void OnDocClosing(object s, Autodesk.Revit.DB.Events.DocumentClosingEventArgs e) { }
        private void OnIdling(object s, Autodesk.Revit.UI.Events.IdlingEventArgs e)
        {
            e.SetRaiseWithoutDelay(false);
            // check selection, update context panel visibility etc.
        }

        private static System.Windows.Media.ImageSource LoadImg(string name)
        {
            try {
                var uri = new Uri($"pack://application:,,,/StudioTools;component/Resources/{name}");
                return new System.Windows.Media.Imaging.BitmapImage(uri);
            } catch { return null; }
        }
    }

    // ══════════════════════════════════════════
    // AVAILABILITY CLASS
    // ══════════════════════════════════════════
    public class RequiresProject : IExternalCommandAvailability
    {
        public bool IsCommandAvailable(UIApplication app, CategorySet cats)
            => app.ActiveUIDocument?.Document?.IsFamilyDocument == false;
    }

    // ══════════════════════════════════════════
    // COMMAND: Toggle DockablePane
    // ══════════════════════════════════════════
    [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
    public class TogglePaneCommand : IExternalCommand
    {
        public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd, ref string msg, ElementSet els)
        {
            DockablePane pane = cd.Application.GetDockablePane(StudioApplication.PaneId);
            if (pane.IsShown()) pane.Hide(); else pane.Show();
            return Result.Succeeded;
        }
    }
}
