sections.title: IExternalServer
sections.content:
InterfaceIExternalServer
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.ExternalService
The base interface for all external servers. Any class that plugs into Revit's service framework must implement IExternalServer (or a sub-interface like IDirectContext3DServer, IRoutingPreferenceServer). Provides the server's identity: GUID, vendor, name, and which service it belongs to.
🟢 Required Methods
// Example — Routing Preference Server (MEP pipe routing)
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.ExternalService;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Plumbing;

// ── Custom Routing Preference Server ──
// Controls how Revit routes pipes: which fittings to prefer,
// how to handle junctions, what sizes to use.

public class StudioRoutingPreferenceServer :
    IRoutingPreferenceServer  // sub-interface of IExternalServer
{
    private Guid _id = new Guid("11223344-AABB-CCDD-EEFF-001122334455");

    // IExternalServer required
    public Guid   GetServiceId()   =>
        ExternalServices.BuiltInExternalServices.RoutingPreferenceService;
    public Guid   GetServerId()    => _id;
    public string GetVendorId()    => "STUDIO";
    public string GetName()        => "Studio Routing Preferences";
    public string GetDescription() => "Custom pipe routing preference rules";
    public string GetApplicationId() => "StudioTools";

    // IRoutingPreferenceServer — return preferred fitting for a junction type
    public IList<RoutingCriteria> GetPreferences(
        RoutingConditions conditions)
    {
        var result = new List<RoutingCriteria>();

        // Prefer elbows over mitered connections
        result.Add(new RoutingCriteria
        {
            CriteriaName    = "ElbowPreference",
            PreferenceType  = RoutingPreferenceRuleGroupType.Elbows,
            PriorityLevel   = 1
        });

        return result;
    }
}

// ── Registration in OnStartup ──
SingleServerService svc = ExternalServiceRegistry
    .GetService(ExternalServices.BuiltInExternalServices.RoutingPreferenceService)
    as SingleServerService;

var server = new StudioRoutingPreferenceServer();
svc?.AddServer(server);
svc?.SetActiveServer(server.GetServerId());

sections.title: ExternalServiceRegistry
sections.content:
ClassExternalServiceRegistry
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.ExternalService
Static registry for all Revit external services. Use GetService() to retrieve a service by its GUID, then add/remove/activate servers in that service.
🟡 BuiltInExternalServices — Key Service GUIDs
Service PropertyTypePurpose
DirectContext3DServiceMultiServerServiceCustom 3D rendering (IDirectContext3DServer) — Book 14
RoutingPreferenceServiceSingleServerServiceMEP pipe/duct routing fitting preferences
PipeSizingServiceSingleServerServiceCustom pipe sizing rules (velocity/pressure methods)
DuctSizingServiceSingleServerServiceCustom duct sizing rules
WireServiceSingleServerServiceElectrical wire calculation server
LoadClassificationServiceSingleServerServiceStructural load classification rules
AnalyticalSupportServiceSingleServerServiceAnalytical model support conditions
CableTrayRoutingPreferenceServiceSingleServerServiceCable tray fitting preferences
ConduitRoutingPreferenceServiceSingleServerServiceConduit fitting preferences
// Example — ExternalServiceRegistry patterns
// Get MultiServerService (e.g. DirectContext3D — many servers active)
MultiServerService multiSvc = ExternalServiceRegistry.GetService(
    ExternalServices.BuiltInExternalServices.DirectContext3DService)
    as MultiServerService;

multiSvc?.AddServer(myServer);
IList<Guid> active = multiSvc?.GetActiveServerIds() ?? new List<Guid>();
active.Add(myServer.GetServerId());
multiSvc?.SetActiveServers(active);

// Get SingleServerService (e.g. pipe sizing — only one server active)
SingleServerService singleSvc = ExternalServiceRegistry.GetService(
    ExternalServices.BuiltInExternalServices.PipeSizingService)
    as SingleServerService;

singleSvc?.AddServer(mySizingServer);
singleSvc?.SetActiveServer(mySizingServer.GetServerId());

// Remove a server on shutdown
multiSvc?.RemoveServer(myServer.GetServerId());

// List all registered servers in a service
foreach (var id in multiSvc?.GetServerIds() ?? new List<Guid>())
{
    IExternalServer srv = multiSvc.GetServer(id);
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Server: {srv.GetName()} [{srv.GetVendorId()}]");
}

sections.title: CurtainGrid
sections.content:
ClassCurtainGrid
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Accessed via Wall.CurtainGrid
Represents the grid system of a curtain wall — the system of U (horizontal) and V (vertical) grid lines that divide the wall into panels. Only available on Wall elements whose WallType.Kind == WallKind.Curtain. Provides access to all grid lines, panels (cells), and mullion layouts.
// Curtain Wall CheckAlways check wall.WallType.Kind == WallKind.Curtain before accessing wall.CurtainGrid. For non-curtain walls, CurtainGrid returns null. Modifying the grid (adding/removing lines) requires a Transaction.
🟠 Key Properties
🟠public ICollection<ElementId> GetUGridLineIds()
Returns the IDs of all U-direction (horizontal) grid lines. Cast each to CurtainGridLine.
🟠public ICollection<ElementId> GetVGridLineIds()
Returns the IDs of all V-direction (vertical) grid lines.
🟠public ICollection<ElementId> GetPanelIds()
Returns the IDs of all curtain panels (the infill elements — glazing, solid panels etc.) in this grid.
🟠public ICollection<ElementId> GetMullionIds()
Returns the IDs of all Mullion elements (the structural/decorative framing members between panels).
// Example — Complete CurtainGrid analysis
// Get all curtain walls
var curtainWalls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>()
    .Where(w => w.WallType.Kind == WallKind.Curtain)
    .ToList();

foreach (var cw in curtainWalls)
{
    CurtainGrid grid = cw.CurtainGrid;
    if (grid == null) continue;

    // Count grid lines and panels
    int uLines   = grid.GetUGridLineIds().Count;
    int vLines   = grid.GetVGridLineIds().Count;
    int panels   = grid.GetPanelIds().Count;
    int mullions = grid.GetMullionIds().Count;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Curtain Wall {cw.Id}: U={uLines} V={vLines} Panels={panels} Mullions={mullions}");

    // Analyse each panel
    foreach (ElementId panelId in grid.GetPanelIds())
    {
        Panel      panel    = doc.GetElement(panelId) as Panel;
        FamilySymbol sym  = panel?.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ELEM_TYPE_PARAM)
                                 ?.AsValueString() is string typeName
                                 ? null : null;

        // Panel bounding box
        BoundingBoxXYZ bb = panel?.get_BoundingBox(null);
        if (bb != null)
        {
            double widthMm  = (bb.Max.X - bb.Min.X) * 304.8;
            double heightMm = (bb.Max.Z - bb.Min.Z) * 304.8;
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"  Panel: W={widthMm:F0}mm H={heightMm:F0}mm");
        }
    }

    // Add a U grid line at a specific elevation
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Add Grid Line"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        ElementId newLineId = grid.AddGridLine(false, 0.5, false);
        // AddGridLine(isUGrid, position, lockToNearest)
        // position 0.0 = bottom, 1.0 = top, 0.5 = midpoint
        tx.Commit();
    }

    // Remove an empty grid line
    ICollection<ElementId> uLineIds = grid.GetUGridLineIds();
    if (uLineIds.Count > 2)
    {
        using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Remove Grid Line"))
        {
            tx.Start();
            ElementId lineToRemove = uLineIds.Last();
            grid.RemoveGridLine(lineToRemove);
            tx.Commit();
        }
    }
}

// Change panel type for all empty panels
FamilySymbol solidPanelType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_CurtainWallPanels)
    .Cast<FamilySymbol>()
    .FirstOrDefault(s => s.Name.Contains("Solid"));

if (solidPanelType != null)
{
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Change Panels"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        foreach (var cw in curtainWalls)
        {
            foreach (ElementId pid in cw.CurtainGrid?.GetPanelIds()
                ?? new List<ElementId>())
            {
                Panel p = doc.GetElement(pid) as Panel;
                if (p != null)
                    p.ChangeTypeId(solidPanelType.Id);
            }
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils
sections.content:
ClassAdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Static utility for placing, querying, and updating adaptive family instances. Adaptive families have "adaptive points" that you position in 3D space — the family geometry deforms to fit. Used for parametric panels, structural connections, and complex facades.
🟢 Key Static Methods
🟢public static bool IsAdaptiveComponentInstance(FamilyInstance instance)
Returns true if the given FamilyInstance is an adaptive component. Always call this before trying to access adaptive point methods — it prevents exceptions on regular families.
🟢public static IList<ElementId> GetInstancePlacementPointElementRefIds(FamilyInstance instance)
Returns the IDs of the ReferencePoint elements that define this instance's adaptive placement. The order matches the adaptive point indices in the family (point 1, 2, 3…).
🟢public static FamilyInstance PlaceAdaptiveComponentInstance(Document doc, FamilySymbol symbol)
Creates a new adaptive component instance without any placement points set. After calling this, use SetInstancePlacementPointElementRefIds() or move the ReferencePoint elements to position the adaptive instance.
// Example — Place and position a 4-point adaptive panel
// Find the adaptive family symbol
FamilySymbol adaptiveSym = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
    .Cast<FamilySymbol>()
    .FirstOrDefault(s => AdaptiveComponentFamilyUtils
        .IsAdaptiveComponentFamily(s.Family));

if (adaptiveSym == null) return Result.Failed;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Place Adaptive"))
{
    tx.Start();

    if (!adaptiveSym.IsActive) adaptiveSym.Activate();

    // Place the instance (unpositioned)
    FamilyInstance instance =
        AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils
            .PlaceAdaptiveComponentInstance(doc, adaptiveSym);

    // Get the adaptive placement point elements
    IList<ElementId> ptIds =
        AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils
            .GetInstancePlacementPointElementRefIds(instance);

    // Position each adaptive point in 3D space
    XYZ[] positions = {
        new XYZ( 0,  0, 0),   // Point 1 — bottom-left
        new XYZ(10,  0, 0),   // Point 2 — bottom-right
        new XYZ(10, 10, 5),   // Point 3 — top-right (elevated)
        new XYZ( 0, 10, 3),   // Point 4 — top-left (elevated)
    };

    for (int i = 0; i < ptIds.Count && i < positions.Length; i++)
    {
        ReferencePoint pt = doc.GetElement(ptIds[i]) as ReferencePoint;
        if (pt == null) continue;
        // Move the reference point to the target position
        (pt.Location as LocationPoint).Point = positions[i];
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Read adaptive point positions ──
var adaptives = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilyInstance)).Cast<FamilyInstance>()
    .Where(AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils.IsAdaptiveComponentInstance)
    .ToList();

foreach (var inst in adaptives)
{
    IList<ElementId> ptIds =
        AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils
            .GetInstancePlacementPointElementRefIds(inst);

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Adaptive instance {inst.Id}: {ptIds.Count} points");

    foreach (var ptId in ptIds)
    {
        ReferencePoint rp = doc.GetElement(ptId) as ReferencePoint;
        if (rp == null) continue;
        XYZ pos = (rp.Location as LocationPoint)?.Point;
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"  Point: ({pos?.X * 304.8:F0}, {pos?.Y * 304.8:F0}, {pos?.Z * 304.8:F0})mm");
    }
}

sections.title: ColorFillScheme
sections.content:
ClassColorFillScheme
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a colour fill scheme — a mapping from room/area parameter values to colours displayed in floor plan views. Each scheme has a category (Rooms, Areas, Spaces), a title parameter, and a list of ColorFillSchemeEntry objects defining the value-to-colour mapping.
🟢 Creation + Usage
// Example — Create department colour fill scheme for rooms
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Color Fill Scheme"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create scheme for rooms by department
    ColorFillScheme scheme = ColorFillScheme.Create(
        doc,
        ColorFillType.Rooms,                             // category
        new ElementId(BuiltInParameter.ROOM_DEPARTMENT)); // title parameter

    scheme.Title = "Departments";

    // Add colour entries for each department value
    var deptColors = new Dictionary<string, (byte R, byte G, byte B)>
    {
        { "Office",      (100, 180, 240) },  // blue
        { "Meeting",     ( 80, 200, 130) },  // green
        { "Reception",   (240, 200,  80) },  // yellow
        { "Circulation", (200, 140,  80) },  // orange
        { "WC",          (180,  80, 180) },  // purple
        { "Storage",     (160, 160, 160) },  // grey
    };

    int idx = 0;
    foreach (var kvp in deptColors)
    {
        var entry = new ColorFillSchemeEntry(StorageType.String)
        {
            Color       = new Color(kvp.Value.R, kvp.Value.G, kvp.Value.B),
            Caption     = kvp.Key,
            IsVisible   = true,
            StorageValue = kvp.Key
        };
        scheme.SetEntry(idx++, entry);
    }

    // Apply scheme to active floor plan view
    ViewPlan floorPlan = doc.ActiveView as ViewPlan;
    if (floorPlan != null)
        floorPlan.SetColorFillSchemeId(
            BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms, scheme.Id);

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Read existing scheme entries ──
var existingScheme = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ColorFillScheme))
    .Cast<ColorFillScheme>()
    .FirstOrDefault(s => s.Title == "Departments");

if (existingScheme != null)
{
    int count = existingScheme.GetEntryCount();
    for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
    {
        ColorFillSchemeEntry e = existingScheme.GetEntry(i);
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"  {e.Caption}: RGB({e.Color.Red},{e.Color.Green},{e.Color.Blue})");
    }
}

sections.title: DesignOption
sections.content:
ClassDesignOption
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a design option — an alternative design variant stored within the same Revit model. Design options are grouped into DesignOptionSets. Elements can be assigned to a specific design option so they only appear when that option is active. Used for presenting multiple design alternatives to clients.
🟢 Create + Manage Design Options
// Example — Create design option set + options + move elements
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Design Options"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // ── Create DesignOptionSet ──
    DesignOptionSet optionSet = DesignOptionSet.Create(doc);
    optionSet.Name = "Facade Design";

    // ── Create options within the set ──
    // Note: One option is automatically created as primary
    // Use DesignOption.Create() to add alternates
    DesignOption optA = DesignOption.Create(doc, optionSet.Id);
    optA.Name = "Option A - Glass";

    DesignOption optB = DesignOption.Create(doc, optionSet.Id);
    optB.Name = "Option B - Solid";

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Move elements to a design option ──
DesignOption targetOption = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(DesignOption)).Cast<DesignOption>()
    .FirstOrDefault(o => o.Name == "Option A - Glass");

if (targetOption != null)
{
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Assign to Option"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        // Assign selected walls to Option A
        foreach (var wall in selectedWalls)
        {
            Parameter dp = wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.DESIGN_OPTION_ID);
            dp?.Set(targetOption.Id);
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── Read which option an element belongs to ──
foreach (var wall in walls)
{
    ElementId doId = wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.DESIGN_OPTION_ID)
                          ?.AsElementId();
    if (doId != null && doId != ElementId.InvalidElementId)
    {
        DesignOption opt = doc.GetElement(doId) as DesignOption;
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Wall {wall.Id} in: {opt?.Name ?? "Main Model"}");
    }
}

// ── List all design option sets and options ──
var optSets = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(DesignOptionSet)).Cast<DesignOptionSet>();
foreach (var os in optSets)
{
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Set: {os.Name}");
    foreach (ElementId doId in os.GetDesignOptionIds())
    {
        DesignOption opt = doc.GetElement(doId) as DesignOption;
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"  Option: {opt?.Name} Primary={opt?.IsPrimary}");
    }
}

sections.title: AssemblyInstance
sections.content:
ClassAssemblyInstance
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents an assembly — a named group of elements treated as a fabrication unit. Assemblies can have dedicated views, sheets, and assembly codes. Used for precast concrete, steel prefabrication, and MEP coordination packages.
🟢 Creation + AssemblyViewUtils
// Example — Create assembly from structural beam + generate views + sheet
// Collect elements for the assembly (a beam and its connections)
var beamIds = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralFraming)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .ToElementIds()
    .Take(3).ToList();  // first 3 beams for demo

AssemblyInstance assembly;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Assembly"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Validate elements can form an assembly
    if (!AssemblyInstance.AreElementsValidForAssembly(doc, beamIds, ElementId.InvalidElementId))
    {
        tx.RollBack();
        TaskDialog.Show("Error", "Elements cannot form an assembly");
        return Result.Failed;
    }

    // Create the assembly
    assembly = AssemblyInstance.Create(doc, beamIds, beamIds[0]);
    // Second param = naming category element ID

    assembly.AssemblyTypeName = "Beam Assembly Type A";

    // Set assembly code (for schedules/BOQ)
    assembly.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ASSEMBLY_CODE)
        ?.Set("BA-001");

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Generate assembly views using AssemblyViewUtils ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Assembly Views"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Get 3D view type for assembly views
    ElementId v3dTypeId = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType)).Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
        .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.ThreeD).Id;

    // Create a 3D view of the assembly
    View3D assemblyView3D =
        AssemblyViewUtils.Create3DOrthographic(doc, assembly.Id, v3dTypeId);
    assemblyView3D.Name = $"3D - {assembly.AssemblyTypeName}";

    // Create detail views (front, back, left, right, top, bottom)
    ElementId sectionTypeId = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType)).Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
        .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.Section).Id;

    ViewSection frontView =
        AssemblyViewUtils.CreateDetailSection(
            doc, assembly.Id, sectionTypeId,
            AssemblyDetailViewOrientation.ElevationFront,
            50);   // view scale 1:50

    ViewSection sideView =
        AssemblyViewUtils.CreateDetailSection(
            doc, assembly.Id, sectionTypeId,
            AssemblyDetailViewOrientation.ElevationLeft, 50);

    // Create an assembly sheet with all views placed
    FamilySymbol titleBlock = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_TitleBlocks)
        .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
        .Cast<FamilySymbol>().First();

    ViewSheet sheet =
        AssemblyViewUtils.CreateSheet(doc, assembly.Id, titleBlock.Id);
    sheet.SheetNumber = "FA-001";
    sheet.Name        = $"Fabrication - {assembly.AssemblyTypeName}";

    // Automatically place the views on the sheet
    AssemblyViewUtils.PopulateSheet(sheet, assembly.Id);

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: PointCloudInstance
sections.content:
ClassPointCloudInstance
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.PointClouds
Represents a linked point cloud file (RCP/RCS/PCG) placed in the Revit model. Provides access to point data through filters, the transform from point cloud space to model space, and cloud metadata. The actual point data is retrieved via GetPoints() with an optional PointCloudFilter.
🟢 Key Methods + Usage
🟢public PointCollection GetPoints(PointCloudFilter filter, double averageDistance, int numPoints)
Returns a collection of CloudPoint objects matching the filter. averageDistance controls the average spacing between returned points (larger = fewer points, faster). numPoints limits the maximum number returned.
ParameterTypeDescription
filterPointCloudFilterSpatial or colour filter. Pass null for no filter.
averageDistancedoubleDesired average distance between returned points in feet. Use 0 for max density.
numPointsintMaximum number of points to return. Use int.MaxValue for all.
Returns: PointCollection — Collection of CloudPoint (X, Y, Z, Color) values.
// Example — Query point cloud within a bounding region
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.PointClouds;

// Get all loaded point cloud instances
var pcs = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PointCloudInstance))
    .Cast<PointCloudInstance>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var pc in pcs)
{
    // Get the point cloud's transform (cloud space → model space)
    Transform xf = pc.GetTransform();

    // Create a bounding box filter for a specific room area
    BoundingBoxXYZ searchBB = new BoundingBoxXYZ();
    searchBB.Min = new XYZ(0, 0, 0);
    searchBB.Max = new XYZ(20, 20, 5);

    // Create axis-aligned bounding box filter in model coordinates
    PointCloudFilter filter = PointCloudFilterFactory
        .CreateMultiPlaneFilter(
            new List<Plane>
            {
                Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisX, searchBB.Min),
                Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(-XYZ.BasisX, searchBB.Max),
                Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisY, searchBB.Min),
                Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(-XYZ.BasisY, searchBB.Max),
                Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisZ, searchBB.Min),
                Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(-XYZ.BasisZ, searchBB.Max)
            });

    // Query points at ~50mm spacing, max 10,000 points
    double spacingFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(50, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    PointCollection points = pc.GetPoints(filter, spacingFt, 10000);

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Point cloud '{pc.Name}': {points.Count} points in search area");

    // Iterate points
    double minZ = double.MaxValue;
    double maxZ = double.MinValue;

    foreach (CloudPoint pt in points)
    {
        // CloudPoint has X, Y, Z (in feet) and Color (ARGB)
        if (pt.Z < minZ) minZ = pt.Z;
        if (pt.Z > maxZ) maxZ = pt.Z;
    }

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Z range: {minZ * 304.8:F0}mm to {maxZ * 304.8:F0}mm");

    // Transform a point from cloud space to model space
    if (points.Count > 0)
    {
        CloudPoint firstPt     = points[0];
        XYZ        cloudPt     = new XYZ(firstPt.X, firstPt.Y, firstPt.Z);
        XYZ        modelPt     = xf.OfPoint(cloudPt);
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"First point in model coords: {modelPt}");
    }
}

// ── Check if a PointCloudType is loaded ──
var pct = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PointCloudType))
    .Cast<PointCloudType>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var t in pct)
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Type: {t.Name} | File: {t.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.POINTCLOUD_FILENAME)?.AsString()}");

sections.title: SteelElementProperties
sections.content:
ClassSteelElementProperties
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Steel
Provides access to Advance Steel connection properties attached to Revit structural elements. When elements are connected using Advance Steel, SteelElementProperties holds the connection geometry, bolt patterns, weld data, and manufacturing metadata.
🟢 Key Usage
// Example — Read Steel element properties
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Steel;

// Get structural framing elements with steel data
var beams = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralFraming)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var beam in beams)
{
    // Get Steel element properties if available
    SteelElementProperties steelProps =
        SteelElementProperties.GetSteelElementProperties(beam);

    if (steelProps == null)
    {
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Beam {beam.Id}: No Advance Steel data");
        continue;
    }

    // Access steel-specific data
    string grade  = steelProps.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STEEL_ELEM_MATERIAL_STEEL_GRADE)
        ?.AsString() ?? "Unknown";

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Beam {beam.Id}: Grade={grade}");
}

// ── Check if document has Steel integration ──
bool hasSteelData = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(SteelElementProperties))
    .GetElementCount() > 0;

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Document has Advance Steel data: {hasSteelData}");

sections.title: FabricationPart
sections.content:
ClassFabricationPart
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Fabrication
Represents a MEP fabrication part — a manufacturer-specific duct, pipe, or electrical fitting created from fabrication service databases. Fabrication parts have exact manufactured dimensions, part numbers, CID codes, and hanger data. Used for fabrication-level detailing and shop drawing workflows.
🟢 Key Properties + Methods
// Example — Fabrication part data extraction
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Fabrication;

// Get all fabrication parts
var fabParts = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FabricationPart))
    .Cast<FabricationPart>()
    .ToList();

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Fabrication parts in model: {fabParts.Count}");

foreach (var fp in fabParts)
{
    // Part identity
    string partName  = fp.Name;
    int    cid        = fp.CID;    // Content Item Description code
    string partAlias  = fp.Alias;  // short description code
    string itemNumber = fp.ItemNumber;
    string orderInfo  = fp.OrderInfo;

    // Service and product list info
    int    serviceId     = fp.ServiceId;
    string productList   = fp.ProductList;

    // Dimensions
    double lengthMm = fp.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.CURVE_ELEM_LENGTH)
        ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;

    // Hanger count
    int hangers = fp.GetPartHangerData()?.Count ?? 0;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Part: {partName} CID={cid} L={lengthMm:F0}mm Hangers={hangers}");

    // Check if part is a straight segment
    bool isStraight = fp.IsAStraight();
    bool isElbow    = fp.IsAElbow();
    bool isTee      = fp.IsATee();
    bool isTransition = fp.IsATransition();

    // Convert fabrication part to equivalent MEP element
    // (for workflows that need standard Revit MEP element after fab)
    if (isStraight)
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine("  → Straight segment");
}

// ── Check available fabrication services ──
FabricationConfiguration config = FabricationConfiguration.GetFabricationConfiguration(doc);
if (config != null)
{
    int serviceCount = config.GetAllLoadedServices().Count;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Fabrication services loaded: {serviceCount}");
}

sections.title: SynchronizeWithCentralOptions
sections.content:
ClassSynchronizeWithCentralOptions
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Controls behaviour of the Synchronize with Central operation. Allows configuring which elements to relinquish, whether to compact the file, save local before/after sync, and comment for the sync operation.
🟢 Complete Worksharing Automation
// Example — Automated sync + save + model path operations
if (!doc.IsWorkshared)
{
    TaskDialog.Show("Error", "Document is not workshared");
    return Result.Failed;
}

// ── Synchronize With Central ──
var syncOpts = new SynchronizeWithCentralOptions();

// Configure relinquish options (what to give back after sync)
var relinquishOpts = new RelinquishOptions(true);
// true = relinquish all by default
relinquishOpts.CheckedOutElements        = true;   // give back borrowed elements
relinquishOpts.UserWorksets              = true;   // give back owned worksets
relinquishOpts.StandardWorksets          = true;   // give back views, families etc.
relinquishOpts.FamilyWorksets            = true;

syncOpts.SetRelinquishOptions(relinquishOpts);

// Other sync settings
syncOpts.SaveLocalBefore           = true;   // save local copy first
syncOpts.SaveLocalAfter            = true;   // save local after sync
syncOpts.Comment                   = $"Auto-sync by StudioTools at {DateTime.Now:HH:mm}";

// Perform sync (no dialog shown)
doc.SynchronizeWithCentral(
    new TransactWithCentralOptions(), syncOpts);

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine("Sync complete");

// ── Relinquish without sync ──
var justRelinquish = new RelinquishOptions(false); // false = none by default
justRelinquish.CheckedOutElements = true;   // only give back borrowed elements
WorksharingUtils.Relinquish(doc,
    justRelinquish, new TransactWithCentralOptions());

// ── Save As with Worksharing options ──
var saveAsOpts = new SaveAsOptions();
saveAsOpts.MaximumBackups = 5;
saveAsOpts.OverwriteExistingFile = true;

// Convert to Central (create a new central file)
var wsOpts = new WorksharingSaveAsOptions();
wsOpts.SaveAsCentral = true;
saveAsOpts.SetWorksharingOptions(wsOpts);

ModelPath newPath = ModelPathUtils
    .ConvertUserVisiblePathToModelPath(@"C:\Projects\Central_v2.rvt");
doc.SaveAs(newPath, saveAsOpts);

// ── ModelPath operations ──

// Get current file path as ModelPath
ModelPath currentPath = doc.GetWorksharingCentralModelPath();

// Convert to user-visible string
string pathStr = ModelPathUtils
    .ConvertModelPathToUserVisiblePath(currentPath);
System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Central: {pathStr}");

// ── TransmittedModelElements — strip links/worksets for transmittal ──
ModelPath transmitPath = ModelPathUtils
    .ConvertUserVisiblePathToModelPath(@"C:\Transmittal\Project.rvt");

TransmittedModelElements tme =
    TransmittedModelElements.GetTransmittedModelElements(doc, transmitPath);

if (tme != null)
{
    int missingLinks = tme.GetMissingFiles().Count;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Missing linked files: {missingLinks}");
}

// ── Check which elements are borrowed by whom ──
var allIds = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType().ToElementIds().Take(50).ToList();

var borrowed = allIds.Where(id =>
    WorksharingUtils.GetCheckoutStatus(doc, id)
        == CheckoutStatus.OwnedByOtherUser).ToList();

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Elements borrowed by others (sample): {borrowed.Count}");

foreach (var id in borrowed.Take(5))
{
    WorksharingTooltipInfo info =
        WorksharingUtils.GetWorksharingTooltipInfo(doc, id);
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"  Element {id}: owned by {info.Owner}");
}

sections.title: LightSource
sections.content:
ClassLightSource
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Lighting
Represents a light source element within a lighting fixture family. Each LightSource defines the photometric data for one lamp within a fixture — its position, initial intensity (lumens), distribution type, colour temperature, and rendering appearance.
🟢 Key Properties + LightGroupManager
// Example — Light audit: extract fixture and lamp data
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Lighting;

// ── Light inventory ──
var fixtures = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_LightingFixtures)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
    .ToList();

double totalLumens  = 0;
int    totalLamps   = 0;

foreach (var fixture in fixtures)
{
    // Get light data from the fixture's family type
    FamilySymbol sym = fixture.Symbol;

    // Lamp parameters (on the type)
    string lampType   = sym.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.LIGHTING_FIXTURE_LAMP)
                           ?.AsString() ?? "Unknown";
    int    lampCount  = sym.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.LIGHTING_FIXTURE_NUMBER_OF_LAMPS)
                           ?.AsInteger() ?? 1;
    double wattage    = sym.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.LIGHTING_FIXTURE_WATTAGE)
                           ?.AsDouble() ?? 0;
    double lumens     = sym.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.LIGHTING_FIXTURE_LUMINOUS_FLUX)
                           ?.AsDouble() ?? 0;

    // Fixture instance location
    XYZ location = (fixture.Location as LocationPoint)?.Point;

    // Room the fixture is in
    var rooms = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
        .Cast<Room>()
        .Where(r => r.Area > 0 && location != null
            && r.IsPointInRoom(new XYZ(location.X, location.Y,
                r.Level.Elevation + 0.5)))
        .FirstOrDefault();

    totalLumens += lumens * lampCount;
    totalLamps  += lampCount;

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Fixture: {fixture.Symbol.Name} | Lamp: {lampType} ×{lampCount} | {wattage:F0}W | {lumens:F0}lm | Room: {rooms?.Name ?? "Outside"}");
}

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Total: {totalLamps} lamps | {totalLumens:F0} total lumens | {totalLumens / Math.Max(fixtures.Count, 1):F0} avg lm/fixture");

// ── LightGroupManager — manage light groups for rendering ──
LightGroupManager lgm = LightGroupManager.GetLightGroupManager(doc);
if (lgm != null)
{
    int groupCount = lgm.GetGroupCount();
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Light groups: {groupCount}");

    for (int i = 0; i < groupCount; i++)
    {
        string groupName    = lgm.GetGroupName(i);
        double dimFactor    = lgm.GetGroupDimmingFactor(i); // 0.0–1.0
        bool   isOn         = lgm.GetGroupOnOff(i);

        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Group: {groupName} | Dimmed: {dimFactor:P0} | On: {isOn}");
    }

    // Modify light group settings in transaction
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Light Groups"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        // Dim all groups to 80%
        for (int i = 0; i < groupCount; i++)
            lgm.SetGroupDimmingFactor(i, 0.8);
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── LightFamily — access IES/photometric file path ──
var lightFamilies = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Family)).Cast<Family>()
    .Where(f => f.FamilyCategory?.Id.IntegerValue
               == (int)BuiltInCategory.OST_LightingFixtures)
    .ToList();

foreach (var fam in lightFamilies)
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Light family: {fam.Name}");

sections.title: Batch Processing Multiple Documents
sections.content:
Pattern 1Batch Processing Multiple Documents
🟢 Open → Process → Save → Close Pattern
// Example — Batch process all .rvt files in a folder
Application app = commandData.Application.Application;
string folder    = @"C:\Projects\Batch";
string[] files   = System.IO.Directory.GetFiles(folder, "*.rvt");

var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
sb.AppendLine($"Batch report — {DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm}");

foreach (var filePath in files)
{
    Document batchDoc = null;
    try
    {
        // Open with audit, detach from central if workshared
        var openOpts = new OpenOptions();
        openOpts.DetachFromCentralOption =
            DetachFromCentralOption.DetachAndPreserveWorksets;
        openOpts.Audit = false;   // true for audit pass, slower

        ModelPath mp = ModelPathUtils
            .ConvertUserVisiblePathToModelPath(filePath);
        batchDoc = app.OpenDocumentFile(mp, openOpts);

        // Process the document
        int wallCount = new FilteredElementCollector(batchDoc)
            .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).GetElementCount();
        int roomCount = new FilteredElementCollector(batchDoc)
            .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
            .Cast<Room>().Count(r => r.Area > 0);

        sb.AppendLine(
            $"{System.IO.Path.GetFileName(filePath)}: W={wallCount} R={roomCount}");

        // Make changes (if needed) — inside transaction
        using (var tx = new Transaction(batchDoc, "Batch Update"))
        {
            tx.Start();
            // ... modifications ...
            tx.Commit();
        }

        // Save (overwrite)
        var saveOpts = new SaveAsOptions { OverwriteExistingFile = true };
        batchDoc.SaveAs(filePath, saveOpts);
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        sb.AppendLine($"  ERROR: {ex.Message}");
    }
    finally
    {
        batchDoc?.Close(false);   // always close
    }
}

TaskDialog.Show("Batch Complete", sb.ToString());

sections.title: Complete Clash Detection Workflow
sections.content:
Pattern 2Complete Clash Detection Workflow
🟢 Structural vs MEP Boolean Intersection Clash Detection
// Example — Find pipe-to-beam clashes using BooleanOperationsUtils
// Helper: get first valid solid from element
Solid GetSolid(Element e)
{
    var opts = new Options { ComputeReferences = false };
    foreach (var obj in e.get_Geometry(opts))
    {
        if (obj is Solid s && s.Volume > 1e-9) return s;
        if (obj is GeometryInstance gi)
            foreach (var o in gi.GetInstanceGeometry())
                if (o is Solid s2 && s2.Volume > 1e-9) return s2;
    }
    return null;
}

// Collect structural beams and pipes
var beams = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralFraming)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType().ToList();
var pipes = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Autodesk.Revit.DB.Plumbing.Pipe))
    .ToList();

var clashes = new List<(ElementId beamId, ElementId pipeId, double volumeM3)>();

foreach (var beam in beams)
{
    Solid beamSolid = GetSolid(beam);
    if (beamSolid == null) continue;

    // Pre-filter: only check pipes whose bounding box overlaps
    BoundingBoxXYZ beamBB = beam.get_BoundingBox(null);
    if (beamBB == null) continue;

    var outline    = new Outline(beamBB.Min, beamBB.Max);
    var bboxFilter = new BoundingBoxIntersectsFilter(outline);
    var nearbyPipes = pipes
        .Where(p => {
            var pb = p.get_BoundingBox(null);
            return pb != null
                && pb.Max.X >= beamBB.Min.X && pb.Min.X <= beamBB.Max.X
                && pb.Max.Y >= beamBB.Min.Y && pb.Min.Y <= beamBB.Max.Y
                && pb.Max.Z >= beamBB.Min.Z && pb.Min.Z <= beamBB.Max.Z;
        }).ToList();

    foreach (var pipe in nearbyPipes)
    {
        Solid pipeSolid = GetSolid(pipe);
        if (pipeSolid == null) continue;

        try
        {
            Solid intersection = BooleanOperationsUtils
                .ExecuteBooleanOperation(beamSolid, pipeSolid,
                    BooleanOperationsType.Intersect);

            if (intersection?.Volume > 1e-9)
            {
                double vol = intersection.Volume * 0.0283168;  // ft³→m³
                clashes.Add((beam.Id, pipe.Id, vol));
            }
        }
        catch { /* incompatible geometry — skip */ }
    }
}

// Report results
TaskDialog.Show("Clash Report",
    $"Found {clashes.Count} clashes\n" +
    string.Join("\n",
        clashes.Take(10).Select(c =>
            $"Beam {c.beamId} ↔ Pipe {c.pipeId}: {c.volumeM3 * 1e6:F1}cm³")));

sections.title: Complete BIM Data Extraction to JSON
sections.content:
Pattern 3Complete BIM Data Extraction to JSON
🟢 Export Room + Wall + MEP data to structured JSON
// Example — Extract all room and fixture data to JSON-ready dictionary
var output = new List<Dictionary<string, object>>();

var rooms = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
    .Cast<Room>()
    .Where(r => r.Area > 0).ToList();

foreach (var room in rooms)
{
    var roomData = new Dictionary<string, object>
    {
        ["id"]          = room.Id.IntegerValue,
        ["uniqueId"]    = room.UniqueId,
        ["name"]        = room.Name,
        ["number"]      = room.Number,
        ["level"]       = room.Level?.Name,
        ["department"]  = room.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ROOM_DEPARTMENT)
                              ?.AsString() ?? "",
        ["areaSqM"]     = Math.Round(room.Area * 0.092903, 2),
        ["perimeterMm"] = Math.Round(room.get_Parameter(
                              BuiltInParameter.ROOM_PERIMETER)
                              ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0, 0),
        ["heightMm"]    = Math.Round(room.get_Parameter(
                              BuiltInParameter.ROOM_HEIGHT)
                              ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0, 0),
    };

    // Fixtures in this room
    XYZ centre = new XYZ(
        room.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ROOM_AREA) != null
            ? room.Location is LocationPoint lp ? lp.Point.X : 0
            : 0,
        0, room.Level?.Elevation + 1.5 ?? 1.5);

    var fixtures = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_LightingFixtures)
        .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
        .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
        .Where(f => {
            var pt = (f.Location as LocationPoint)?.Point;
            return pt != null
                && room.IsPointInRoom(
                    new XYZ(pt.X, pt.Y, room.Level.Elevation + 0.5));
        }).Count();

    roomData["fixtureCount"] = fixtures;
    output.Add(roomData);
}

// Serialize to JSON using Newtonsoft or System.Text.Json
string json = Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert.SerializeObject(output, Formatting.Indented);
System.IO.File.WriteAllText(@"C:\Output\rooms.json", json);

TaskDialog.Show("Export", $"Exported {output.Count} rooms to JSON");

Final Completion

sections.title: ElementTransformUtils
sections.content:
ClassElementTransformUtils
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Static utility class for all element transform operations: move, rotate, mirror, and copy (within and across documents). All methods require an open Transaction. CopyElements across documents is one of the most powerful API features — it preserves type information and hosted elements.
// Copy vs DuplicateUse ElementTransformUtils.CopyElements() for copying with position offsets. Use doc.GetElement(id).Clone() or duplicate-specific methods for types. Cross-document copy preserves all parameter values, hosted elements, and materials.
🟢 Complete Method Reference
🟢public static void MoveElement(Document doc, ElementId elementId, XYZ translation)
Moves a single element by the given translation vector. Moves the element's location curve or point by the vector — same as dragging in the UI. Hosted elements (doors in walls) move with their host when the host moves.
ParameterTypeDescription
docDocumentThe document containing the element.
elementIdElementIdID of the element to move.
translationXYZTranslation vector in internal units (feet). E.g. new XYZ(0,0,1) moves 1 foot upward.
// Example — Move, Rotate, Mirror
Wall      wall  = ...;
Document  doc   = wall.Document;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Transform"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // ── Move element 2000mm in Y direction ──
    double moveFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(2000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    ElementTransformUtils.MoveElement(doc, wall.Id, new XYZ(0, moveFt, 0));

    // ── Move multiple elements ──
    var idsToMove = walls.Select(w => w.Id).ToList();
    ElementTransformUtils.MoveElements(doc, idsToMove, new XYZ(0, moveFt, 0));

    // ── Rotate element around Z axis at its midpoint ──
    LocationCurve lc    = wall.Location as LocationCurve;
    XYZ           mid   = lc.Curve.Evaluate(0.5, true);
    Line          axis  = Line.CreateUnbound(mid, XYZ.BasisZ);
    double        angle = Math.PI / 2;  // 90 degrees

    ElementTransformUtils.RotateElement(doc, wall.Id, axis, angle);

    // ── Mirror element across the YZ plane ──
    Plane mirrorPlane = Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(
        XYZ.BasisX, XYZ.Zero);  // mirror across X=0

    ElementTransformUtils.MirrorElement(doc, wall.Id, mirrorPlane);

    // ── Mirror with copy (original stays, mirrored copy created) ──
    ICollection<ElementId> mirrored =
        ElementTransformUtils.MirrorElements(
            doc,
            idsToMove,
            mirrorPlane,
            true);   // true = keep original, false = move original

    tx.Commit();
}
🟢public static void RotateElement(Document doc, ElementId elementId, Line axis, double angle)
Rotates a single element around the given axis line by angle (radians). The axis is a line in 3D space — both its direction and position matter. Use XYZ.BasisZ through the element's centre for standard plan-rotation.
🟢public static ICollection<ElementId> CopyElement(Document doc, ElementId elementId, XYZ translation)
Copies a single element with the given offset. Returns the ID of the new copy. The original is unchanged. All parameters, hosted elements, and associations are copied.
Returns: ICollection<ElementId> — IDs of the newly created copies (usually 1 element).
// Example — Copy elements with offsets (array pattern)
// ── Copy one wall offset by 5m ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Copy Wall"))
{
    tx.Start();
    double offsetFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(5000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    ICollection<ElementId> newIds =
        ElementTransformUtils.CopyElement(doc, wall.Id, new XYZ(0, offsetFt, 0));
    Wall newWall = doc.GetElement(newIds.First()) as Wall;
    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Array: copy a column 5 times at equal spacing ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Column Array"))
{
    tx.Start();
    double spacingFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(6000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++)
    {
        ElementTransformUtils.CopyElement(
            doc, column.Id, new XYZ(i * spacingFt, 0, 0));
    }
    tx.Commit();
}
🟢public static ICollection<ElementId> CopyElements(Document document, ICollection<ElementId> elementIds, XYZ translation)
Copies multiple elements with a translation offset. Returns IDs of all newly created copies in the same order as input. The most common copy overload for batch operations.
🟢public static ICollection<ElementId> CopyElements(Document sourceDocument, ICollection<ElementId> elementIds, Document destinationDocument, Transform transform, CopyPasteOptions options)
Copies elements from one document to another. This is the most powerful overload — it transfers element types, materials, nested families, and all associated data. The transform positions the copied elements in the destination document's coordinate space. Pass Transform.Identity to preserve original positions.
ParameterTypeDescription
sourceDocumentDocumentThe document to copy from.
elementIdsICollection<ElementId>IDs of elements to copy from the source.
destinationDocumentDocumentThe document to paste into (must be open).
transformTransformTransform applied to copied elements. Use Transform.Identity to keep original positions.
optionsCopyPasteOptionsControls duplicate type handling. Can be null.
Returns: ICollection<ElementId> — IDs of newly created elements in the destination document.
// Example — Cross-document copy: transfer walls + rooms from template
Application app = commandData.Application.Application;

// Open source (template) document
Document sourceDoc = app.OpenDocumentFile(@"C:\Templates\CoreLayout.rvt");
Document destDoc   = commandData.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Document;

// Get elements to copy from template
var toCopy = new FilteredElementCollector(sourceDoc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .ToElementIds()
    .ToList();

// CopyPasteOptions — control how duplicate types are handled
var cpOpts = new CopyPasteOptions();
cpOpts.SetDuplicateTypeNamesHandler(new DuplicateTypeNamesHandler());

using (var tx = new Transaction(destDoc, "Import Template Walls"))
{
    tx.Start();

    ICollection<ElementId> newIds =
        ElementTransformUtils.CopyElements(
            sourceDoc,
            toCopy,
            destDoc,
            Transform.Identity,   // preserve original positions
            cpOpts);

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Copied {newIds.Count} walls into destination");

    tx.Commit();
}

// Close the source template
sourceDoc.Close(false);

// ── DuplicateTypeNamesHandler for cross-doc copy ──
public class DuplicateTypeNamesHandler : IDuplicateTypeNamesHandler
{
    public DuplicateTypeAction OnDuplicateTypeNamesFound(
        DuplicateTypeNamesHandlerArgs args)
    {
        // Options:
        // UseDestinationTypes — keep existing types in dest (ignore source)
        // UseSourceTypes      — overwrite dest types with source types
        
        return DuplicateTypeAction.UseDestinationTypes;
    }
}

sections.title: GeometryCreationUtilities
sections.content:
ClassGeometryCreationUtilities
Inherits: Static utility
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Static factory class for creating Solid geometry objects programmatically without a family document. The resulting Solids can be used with DirectShape.SetShape(), BooleanOperationsUtils, or for geometric analysis. All methods take profile CurveLoops and return a Solid.
🟢 All Factory Methods
🟢public static Solid CreateExtrusionGeometry(IList<CurveLoop> profileLoops, XYZ extrusionDir, double extrusionDist)
Creates a solid extrusion from a list of CurveLoops. profileLoops[0] = outer boundary, subsequent loops = holes. extrusionDir is the direction of extrusion (must be perpendicular to profile plane). extrusionDist is the length in feet.
ParameterTypeDescription
profileLoopsIList<CurveLoop>Profile boundary loops. [0]=outer, [1..n]=holes.
extrusionDirXYZDirection to extrude (unit vector, perpendicular to profile).
extrusionDistdoubleExtrusion depth in feet. Must be positive.
Returns: Solid — The extruded solid geometry object.
// Example — Create L-shaped solid + use with DirectShape
// ── Create an L-shaped column cross-section ──
double w1 = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(400, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double w2 = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double h  = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

// L-shape profile (6 points)
XYZ[] pts = {
    new XYZ(0,  0,  0),
    new XYZ(w1, 0,  0),
    new XYZ(w1, w2, 0),
    new XYZ(w2, w2, 0),
    new XYZ(w2, w1, 0),
    new XYZ(0,  w1, 0)
};
CurveLoop profile = new CurveLoop();
for (int i = 0; i < pts.Length; i++)
    profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(pts[i], pts[(i + 1) % pts.Length]));

// Create the solid (extrude upward)
Solid lColumnSolid = GeometryCreationUtilities.CreateExtrusionGeometry(
    new List<CurveLoop> { profile },
    XYZ.BasisZ,    // extrude upward
    h);

// Use it as a DirectShape
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "L-Column"))
{
    tx.Start();
    DirectShape ds = DirectShape.CreateElement(doc,
        new ElementId(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralColumns));
    ds.SetShape(new GeometryObject[] { lColumnSolid });
    ds.Name = "L-Column 400×400";
    tx.Commit();
}
🟢public static Solid CreateRevolvedGeometry(Frame coordinateFrame, IList<CurveLoop> profileLoops, double startAngle, double endAngle)
Creates a solid by revolving a profile around the Z-axis of the given coordinate frame. The profile must be on one side of the revolution axis. startAngle=0, endAngle=2π creates a full revolution (cylinder, cone, torus etc.).
// Example — Create a cylinder and a pipe-shaped solid
// ── Cylinder: revolve a rectangle ──
double radius = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(300, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double cylH   = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(1000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

// Profile: rectangle from (radius,0,0) to (radius,0,cylH)
// on the XZ plane — revolution axis is Z
CurveLoop cylProfile = new CurveLoop();
cylProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(radius, 0, 0),   new XYZ(radius, 0, cylH)));
cylProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(radius, 0, cylH), new XYZ(0, 0, cylH)));
cylProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0, 0, cylH),      new XYZ(0, 0, 0)));
cylProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0, 0, 0),        new XYZ(radius, 0, 0)));

// Frame: origin at base, Z = revolution axis
var frame = new Frame(XYZ.Zero, XYZ.BasisX, XYZ.BasisY, XYZ.BasisZ);

Solid cylinder = GeometryCreationUtilities.CreateRevolvedGeometry(
    frame,
    new List<CurveLoop> { cylProfile },
    0,
    2 * Math.PI);   // full revolution

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Cylinder volume: {cylinder.Volume * 0.0283168:F4}m³");
🟢public static Solid CreateSweptGeometry(Curve sweepPath, int pathAttachmentCurveIndex, double pathAttachmentParameter, IList<CurveLoop> profileLoops)
Creates a solid by sweeping a profile along a path curve. The profile is placed perpendicular to the path at the attachment point. Used for pipes, beams, rails, and any linear solid following a curve.
// Example — Swept rectangular section along an arc path
// Path: semicircle arc
Arc sweepPath = Arc.Create(XYZ.Zero, 5.0, 0, Math.PI,
    XYZ.BasisX, XYZ.BasisZ);

// Profile: 200×400mm rectangle centred at origin
double pw = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters) / 2;
double ph = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(400, UnitTypeId.Millimeters) / 2;
CurveLoop profile = new CurveLoop();
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(-pw,-ph,0),new XYZ( pw,-ph,0)));
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ( pw,-ph,0),new XYZ( pw, ph,0)));
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ( pw, ph,0),new XYZ(-pw, ph,0)));
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(-pw, ph,0),new XYZ(-pw,-ph,0)));

Solid sweptBeam = GeometryCreationUtilities.CreateSweptGeometry(
    sweepPath,
    0,      // path attachment curve index (0 = the path itself)
    0.0,    // attachment parameter (0.0 = start of path)
    new List<CurveLoop> { profile });

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Swept beam volume: {sweptBeam.Volume * 0.0283168:F4}m³");
🟢public static Solid CreateBlendGeometry(CurveLoop topProfile, CurveLoop bottomProfile, IList<VertexPair> vertexPairing)
Creates a solid by blending (lofting) between two CurveLoops. The vertexPairing specifies which vertex in the top profile corresponds to which vertex in the bottom profile — controls the twist of the blend.
// Example — Blend from square base to circular top (tapered column)
double baseSize = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(500, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double topRad   = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double colH     = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

// Bottom: square
double h = baseSize / 2;
CurveLoop bottom = new CurveLoop();
bottom.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(-h,-h,0),new XYZ( h,-h,0)));
bottom.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ( h,-h,0),new XYZ( h, h,0)));
bottom.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ( h, h,0),new XYZ(-h, h,0)));
bottom.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(-h, h,0),new XYZ(-h,-h,0)));

// Top: circle (approximate with arc)
Plane     topPlane  = Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisZ, new XYZ(0,0,colH));
CurveLoop top       = new CurveLoop();
top.Append(Arc.Create(topPlane, topRad, 0, Math.PI));
top.Append(Arc.Create(topPlane, topRad, Math.PI, 2 * Math.PI));

Solid taperedCol = GeometryCreationUtilities.CreateBlendGeometry(
    top, bottom, null);  // null = auto vertex pairing

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
    $"Tapered column volume: {taperedCol.Volume * 0.0283168:F4}m³");

sections.title: DefinitionFile (Shared Parameter File)
sections.content:
ClassDefinitionFile (Shared Parameter File)
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Accessed via app.OpenSharedParameterFile()
Represents the shared parameter .txt file. The file is plain text — Revit creates and manages it. Each file contains groups of parameter definitions, each with a unique GUID. You must set app.SharedParametersFilename before calling OpenSharedParameterFile().
🟢 Complete Shared Parameter Workflow
// Example — Complete shared parameter creation + binding workflow
Application app    = commandData.Application.Application;
Document    doc    = commandData.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Document;
string      spPath = @"C:\BIM\StudioSharedParams.txt";

// ── STEP 1: Create or open the shared parameter file ──
if (!System.IO.File.Exists(spPath))
    System.IO.File.Create(spPath).Close();  // create empty file

app.SharedParametersFilename = spPath;
DefinitionFile defFile = app.OpenSharedParameterFile();

// ── STEP 2: Get or create a definition group ──
const string GROUP_NAME = "Studio BIM Data";
DefinitionGroup group = defFile.Groups.get_Item(GROUP_NAME)
    ?? defFile.Groups.Create(GROUP_NAME);

// ── STEP 3: Create parameter definitions ──
var paramDefs = new[]
{
    // (name, spec type, is visible)
    ("FireRating",     SpecTypeId.String.Text,             true),
    ("UValue",         SpecTypeId.ThermalTransmittance,    true),
    ("CarbonKgM2",     SpecTypeId.MassPerUnitArea,         true),
    ("ReviewStatus",   SpecTypeId.String.Text,             true),
    ("IsExternal",     SpecTypeId.Boolean.YesNo,           true),
};

var createdDefs = new Dictionary<string, ExternalDefinition>();
foreach (var (name, spec, visible) in paramDefs)
{
    Definition existing = group.Definitions.get_Item(name);
    if (existing is ExternalDefinition ext)
    {
        createdDefs[name] = ext;  // already exists
    }
    else
    {
        var opts = new ExternalDefinitionCreationOptions(name, spec)
        {
            Visible = visible,
            UserModifiable = true
        };
        createdDefs[name] = group.Definitions.Create(opts) as ExternalDefinition;
    }
}

// ── STEP 4: Bind parameters to categories ──
CategorySet wallCats = app.Create.NewCategorySet();
wallCats.Insert(doc.Settings.Categories.get_Item(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls));
wallCats.Insert(doc.Settings.Categories.get_Item(BuiltInCategory.OST_Floors));
wallCats.Insert(doc.Settings.Categories.get_Item(BuiltInCategory.OST_Roofs));

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Bind Shared Params"))
{
    tx.Start();

    foreach (var kvp in createdDefs)
    {
        ExternalDefinition def = kvp.Value;
        if (def == null) continue;

        // Check if already bound
        BindingMap bm = doc.ParameterBindings;
        if (bm.Contains(def)) continue;  // skip if already bound

        // Create instance binding (appears on instances, not types)
        InstanceBinding binding = app.Create.NewInstanceBinding(wallCats);

        // OR: TypeBinding (appears on types/families)
        // TypeBinding typeBinding = app.Create.NewTypeBinding(wallCats);

        bool ok = doc.ParameterBindings.Insert(def, binding,
            GroupTypeId.Data);  // which group in Properties panel

        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Bound '{kvp.Key}': {(ok ? "OK" : "FAILED")}");
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── STEP 5: Write values to elements ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Set Shared Params"))
{
    tx.Start();
    foreach (var wall in walls)
    {
        // Access by GUID (most reliable)
        ExternalDefinition frDef = createdDefs["FireRating"];
        Parameter frParam = wall.get_Parameter(frDef.GUID);
        frParam?.Set("2HR");

        Parameter uvParam = wall.get_Parameter(createdDefs["UValue"].GUID);
        uvParam?.Set(0.35);  // W/m²K — Revit stores as internal units

        Parameter extParam = wall.get_Parameter(createdDefs["IsExternal"].GUID);
        extParam?.Set(1);  // 1 = Yes, 0 = No for YesNo params
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

// ── STEP 6: Read all shared parameters on an element ──
foreach (Parameter p in wall.Parameters)
{
    if (!p.IsShared) continue;
    string val = p.StorageType switch
    {
        StorageType.String    => p.AsString(),
        StorageType.Double   => (p.AsDouble() * 304.8).ToString("F2"),
        StorageType.Integer  => p.AsInteger().ToString(),
        StorageType.ElementId => p.AsElementId()?.IntegerValue.ToString(),
        _                    => "—"
    };
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"  [{p.Definition.Name}] = {val}");
}

sections.title: GlobalParameter
sections.content:
ClassGlobalParameter
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
A project-level parameter that can control dimensions and constrain element parameters. Global parameters appear in the Manage → Global Parameters dialog. They can drive wall heights, grid spacing, facade module widths, and any dimension or constrained parameter — making the model fully parametric.
🟢 Complete GlobalParameter Workflow
// Example — Create, read, write, and link global parameters
// ── Create global parameters ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Global Params"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create a length global parameter
    GlobalParameter floorHeight = GlobalParameter.Create(
        doc,
        "FloorToCeilingHeight",
        SpecTypeId.Length);   // or SpecTypeId.String, SpecTypeId.Boolean.YesNo etc.

    // Set the value (in internal units)
    floorHeight.SetValue(
        new DoubleParameterValue(
            UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters)));

    // Create a text global parameter
    GlobalParameter projectCode = GlobalParameter.Create(
        doc, "ProjectCode", SpecTypeId.String.Text);
    projectCode.SetValue(new StringParameterValue("2024-BIM-001"));

    // Create a boolean
    GlobalParameter isMetric = GlobalParameter.Create(
        doc, "UseMetric", SpecTypeId.Boolean.YesNo);
    isMetric.SetValue(new IntegerParameterValue(1));  // 1=Yes

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Read all global parameters ──
var globalParamIds = GlobalParametersManager.GetAllGlobalParameters(doc);
foreach (var gpId in globalParamIds)
{
    GlobalParameter gp = doc.GetElement(gpId) as GlobalParameter;
    if (gp == null) continue;

    ParameterValue val    = gp.GetValue();
    string         valStr = "?";

    if (val is DoubleParameterValue dpv)
        valStr = $"{dpv.Value * 304.8:F0}mm";
    else if (val is StringParameterValue spv)
        valStr = spv.Value;
    else if (val is IntegerParameterValue ipv)
        valStr = ipv.Value == 1 ? "Yes" : "No";

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"GlobalParam: {gp.Name} = {valStr} | Formula: {gp.GetFormula() ?? "(none)"}");
}

// ── Update a global parameter value ──
GlobalParameter fh = GlobalParametersManager
    .FindByName(doc, "FloorToCeilingHeight");

if (fh != null)
{
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Update GlobalParam"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        fh.SetValue(new DoubleParameterValue(
            UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(3200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters)));
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── Set formula on a global parameter ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "GP Formula"))
{
    tx.Start();
    // Formula uses other global parameter names
    fh?.SetFormula("FloorHeight * 0.85");
    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Check what dimensions/params are driven by a global param ──
if (fh != null)
{
    var users = GlobalParametersManager.GetUsedGlobalParameters(doc, fh.Id);
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"GlobalParam '{fh.Name}' drives {users.Count} elements/dimensions");
}

sections.title: Phase
sections.content:
ClassPhase
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a construction phase in the Revit project. All model elements have a Phase Created and optionally a Phase Demolished parameter. Phases are stored in doc.Phases (a PhaseArray) and are always ordered chronologically.
🟢 Complete Phasing Workflow
// Example — Create phases, assign elements, create phase filter, phase-aware query
// ── Read existing phases ──
PhaseArray phases = doc.Phases;
System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Project has {phases.Size} phases:");
foreach (Phase ph in phases)
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"  {ph.Id}: {ph.Name}");

Phase existingPhase      = phases.get_Item(0);   // first phase
Phase newConstructPhase  = phases.get_Item(phases.Size - 1); // last phase

// ── Create a new phase ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Phase"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Insert after a specific phase
    Phase phase2 = doc.Phases.get_Item(0);  // insert after phase index 0
    Phase newPhase = doc.Phases.Insert(phase2.Id, "Phase 2 - Extension");

    // Rename existing phase
    existingPhase.Name = "Phase 1 - Existing";

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Assign phase to elements ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Assign Phases"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Phase extension = doc.Phases.get_Item(doc.Phases.Size - 1);

    foreach (var wall in newWalls)
    {
        // Set phase created
        wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PHASE_CREATED)
            .Set(extension.Id);

        // Demolish existing walls in the extension zone
        // wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PHASE_DEMOLISHED).Set(extension.Id);
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Phase-aware collector: get elements existing in Phase 2 ──
Phase queryPhase = doc.Phases.get_Item(1);
var phaseFilter  = new ElementPhaseStatusFilter(queryPhase.Id,
    ElementPhaseStatus.Existing);

var existingWalls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WherePasses(phaseFilter)
    .ToList();

// ElementPhaseStatus values:
// New          — created in this phase
// Existing     — existed before this phase and not demolished
// Demolished   — demolished in this phase
// Temporary    — created and demolished in same phase
// None         — not part of this phase

// ── Set PhaseFilter on a view ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Set View Phase"))
{
    tx.Start();
    ViewPlan vp = ...;

    // Set view's active phase
    vp.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.VIEW_PHASE)
        .Set(queryPhase.Id);

    // Set phase filter (how demolished/new elements appear)
    PhaseFilter pf = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(PhaseFilter)).Cast<PhaseFilter>()
        .FirstOrDefault(f => f.Name.Contains("Show All"));

    if (pf != null)
        vp.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.VIEW_PHASE_FILTER)
            .Set(pf.Id);

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: ElevationMarker
sections.content:
ClassElevationMarker
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents an elevation marker — the circular annotation symbol that creates elevation views in a floor plan. An ElevationMarker can hold up to 4 elevation views (N/S/E/W). You place the marker, then call CreateElevation() to generate each directed elevation view.
🟢 Creation + All View Types
// Example — Create all 4 room elevations + drafting view + callout
// Get elevation view family type
ViewFamilyType elevType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType)).Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.Elevation);

ViewPlan floorPlan = doc.ActiveView as ViewPlan;
XYZ      markerPos = new XYZ(5, 5, 0);  // position in floor plan

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Elevations"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // ── Create elevation marker ──
    ElevationMarker marker = ElevationMarker.CreateElevationMarker(
        doc, elevType.Id, markerPos, 50);  // scale 1:50

    // ── Create elevation views from the marker (indices 0-3 = N/E/S/W) ──
    ViewSection northElev = marker.CreateElevation(doc, floorPlan.Id, 0);
    northElev.Name = "Elevation North";

    ViewSection eastElev  = marker.CreateElevation(doc, floorPlan.Id, 1);
    eastElev.Name = "Elevation East";

    ViewSection southElev = marker.CreateElevation(doc, floorPlan.Id, 2);
    southElev.Name = "Elevation South";

    ViewSection westElev  = marker.CreateElevation(doc, floorPlan.Id, 3);
    westElev.Name = "Elevation West";

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Create Drafting View ──
ViewFamilyType draftType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType)).Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.DraftingView);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Drafting View"))
{
    tx.Start();

    ViewDrafting draftView = ViewDrafting.Create(doc, draftType.Id);
    draftView.Name  = "Detail - Door Frame";
    draftView.Scale = 5;   // 1:5

    // Add detail lines to the drafting view
    SketchPlane sp = SketchPlane.Create(doc,
        Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisZ, XYZ.Zero));

    DetailLine dl = doc.Create.NewDetailCurve(draftView,
        Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(1,0,0))) as DetailLine;

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Create Callout View from an existing section ──
ViewFamilyType sectionType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType)).Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .First(v => v.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.Section);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Callout"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create callout in the active view (zooms into a specific area)
    BoundingBoxXYZ calloutBox = new BoundingBoxXYZ();
    calloutBox.Min = new XYZ(2,  2, 0);
    calloutBox.Max = new XYZ(8,  8, 0);

    ViewSection callout = ViewSection.CreateCallout(
        doc, floorPlan.Id, sectionType.Id, calloutBox);
    callout.Name = "Callout - Stair Detail";

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: StairsEditScope
sections.content:
ClassStairsEditScope
Inherits: IDisposable
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Architecture
A special scope required for all stair creation and modification. Works alongside a Transaction — the Transaction records the operation in the undo stack, while StairsEditScope manages the stair-specific editing state. Both must be started before making stair changes.
🟢 Complete Stair Creation + Modification
// Example — Create straight stair + add landing + modify
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Architecture;

Level baseLvl = ...;   // ground floor
Level topLvl  = ...;   // first floor

Stairs stair;
ElementId stairId;

// ── STEP 1: Create stair using StairsEditScope + Transaction ──
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Stairs"))
{
    tx.Start();

    using (var scope = new StairsEditScope(doc, "Create Stairs"))
    {
        stairId = scope.Start(baseLvl.Id, topLvl.Id);
        // Start() creates an empty stair assembly and returns its ID

        // Create a straight run
        StairsRun run = StairsRun.CreateStraightRun(
            doc,
            stairId,
            Line.CreateBound(
                new XYZ(0, 0, baseLvl.Elevation),
                new XYZ(0, 12, baseLvl.Elevation)),  // path of run
            StairsRunJustification.Center);

        // Configure run properties
        run.ActualRunWidth = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(
            1200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

        // Commit the scope (validates stair geometry)
        scope.Commit(new StairsEditScopeFailuresPreprocessor());
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── STEP 2: Modify existing stair ──
stair = doc.GetElement(stairId) as Stairs;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Modify Stair"))
{
    tx.Start();
    using (var scope = new StairsEditScope(doc, "Modify"))
    {
        scope.Start(stairId);  // pass existing stair ID to edit it

        // Read stair properties
        int    risers   = stair.DesiredRisersNumber;
        double riserH   = stair.ActualRiserHeight;

        // Modify desired riser count
        stair.DesiredRisersNumber = 18;

        // Access runs and landings
        foreach (ElementId rid in stair.GetStairsRuns())
        {
            StairsRun r = doc.GetElement(rid) as StairsRun;
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"  Run: risers={r.ActualRisersNumber} width={r.ActualRunWidth * 304.8:F0}mm");
        }

        foreach (ElementId lid in stair.GetStairsLandings())
        {
            StairsLanding land = doc.GetElement(lid) as StairsLanding;
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"  Landing area: {land.get_BoundingBox(null)?.Max.X:F2}");
        }

        scope.Commit(new StairsEditScopeFailuresPreprocessor());
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

// ── StairsEditScopeFailuresPreprocessor (suppress common warnings) ──
public class StairsEditScopeFailuresPreprocessor : IFailuresPreprocessor
{
    public FailureProcessingResult PreprocessFailures(FailuresAccessor acc)
    {
        foreach (var f in acc.GetFailureMessages())
            if (f.GetSeverity() == FailureSeverity.Warning)
                acc.DeleteWarning(f);
        return FailureProcessingResult.Continue;
    }
}

sections.title: ElectricalSetting
sections.content:
ClassElectricalSetting
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Electrical · Accessed via ElectricalSetting.GetElectricalSettings(doc)
Contains all project-level electrical engineering settings: voltage definitions, distribution systems (single-phase, three-phase), wire sizes, and conduit/cable tray default settings. Equivalent to the Manage → MEP Settings → Electrical Settings dialog.
🟢 Read + Modify MEP Settings
// Example — ElectricalSetting + MechanicalSettings audit
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Electrical;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Mechanical;

// ── Electrical Settings ──
ElectricalSetting elecSettings =
    ElectricalSetting.GetElectricalSettings(doc);

if (elecSettings != null)
{
    // Read voltage definitions
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine("Voltage Definitions:");
    foreach (VoltageType vt in elecSettings.GetVoltageTypes())
    {
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"  {vt.Name}: {vt.Voltage:F0}V min={vt.MinVoltage:F0} max={vt.MaxVoltage:F0}");
    }

    // Read distribution systems
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine("Distribution Systems:");
    foreach (DistributionSysType dst in elecSettings.GetDistributionSysTypes())
    {
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"  {dst.Name}: Phase={dst.ElectricalPhase} Wires={dst.NumWires}");
    }

    // Get/set hidden line settings
    bool useSinglePhase = elecSettings.GroundConductorSizing != null;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Wire size method: {elecSettings.WireSizeCalculationMethod}");

    // Modify settings in transaction
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Elec Settings"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        // Add a new voltage definition
        VoltageType newVt = elecSettings.AddVoltageType();
        newVt.Name       = "230V";
        newVt.Voltage    = 230;
        newVt.MinVoltage = 216;
        newVt.MaxVoltage = 253;
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── Mechanical Settings ──
MechanicalSettings mechSettings =
    MechanicalSettings.GetMechanicalSettings(doc);

if (mechSettings != null)
{
    // Read duct sizing method
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Duct friction: {mechSettings.GetDuctFriction()} Pa/m");

    // Read pipe settings
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Pipe friction: {mechSettings.GetPipeFriction()} Pa/m");

    // Read air density and viscosity
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Air density: {mechSettings.AirDensity}");
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Air viscosity: {mechSettings.AirViscosity}");

    // Hidden line settings
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Rectangular duct tee sizing: {mechSettings.RectangularMajorSizes}");
}

sections.title: PathReinforcement
sections.content:
ClassPathReinforcement
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure
Represents a linear reinforcement (rebar distributed along a path/curve) within a structural host element (floor, wall, or foundation). Unlike individual Rebar elements, PathReinforcement creates evenly-spaced bars along a curve with configurable spacing and cover.
🟢 Creation + AreaReinforcement + BeamSystem
// Example — PathReinforcement, AreaReinforcement, BeamSystem
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure;

// ═══════════════════════════════════
// PATH REINFORCEMENT
// ═══════════════════════════════════
Floor        slab    = ...;
RebarBarType barType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(RebarBarType)).Cast<RebarBarType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "PathReinforcement"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Define the path curve (along the slab centreline)
    var pathCurves = new CurveArray();
    pathCurves.Append(Line.CreateBound(
        new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(10,0,0)));

    PathReinforcement pathReinf =
        PathReinforcement.Create(
            doc,
            slab,           // host
            pathCurves,     // path
            true,           // flipYDir
            barType.Id,     // primary bar type
            ElementId.InvalidElementId,  // no hook type start
            ElementId.InvalidElementId); // no hook type end

    // Set spacing
    pathReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_SPACING_VALUE)
        ?.Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    tx.Commit();
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════
// AREA REINFORCEMENT
// ═══════════════════════════════════
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "AreaReinforcement"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Define boundary of reinforcement area
    var boundary = new CurveArray();
    boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(10,0,0)));
    boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(10,0,0), new XYZ(10,8,0)));
    boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(10,8,0), new XYZ(0,8,0)));
    boundary.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,8,0), new XYZ(0,0,0)));

    AreaReinforcement areaReinf =
        AreaReinforcement.Create(
            doc,
            slab,
            boundary,
            XYZ.BasisX,        // major bar direction
            barType.Id,        // major bar type
            ElementId.InvalidElementId,
            barType.Id,        // minor bar type
            ElementId.InvalidElementId);

    tx.Commit();
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════
// BEAM SYSTEM
// ═══════════════════════════════════
FamilySymbol beamSym = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralFraming)
    .Cast<FamilySymbol>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Beam System"))
{
    tx.Start();
    if (!beamSym.IsActive) beamSym.Activate();

    // Create beam system from boundary curves
    var bsProfile = new CurveArray();
    bsProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,3),  new XYZ(15,0,3)));
    bsProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(15,0,3), new XYZ(15,10,3)));
    bsProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(15,10,3),new XYZ(0,10,3)));
    bsProfile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,10,3), new XYZ(0,0,3)));

    BeamSystem bs = BeamSystem.Create(doc, bsProfile, level, XYZ.BasisX);
    bs.BeamSystemType = beamSym.Id;

    // Set layout rule
    bs.ChangeTypeId(bs.GetTypeId());

    // Read beam count
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Beam system created with {bs.GetBeamIds().Count} beams");

    tx.Commit();
}

hero.title:
Structural
Reinforcement
hero.description: Complete real-code reference for placing reinforcement in beams, columns, slabs, and foundations — bottom bars, top bars, stirrups, ties, area reinforcement, punching zones — plus steel connections (base plates, end plates, cleats, gussets) using the Advance Steel API.
