sections.title: Rebar
sections.content:
ClassRebar — Core placement class
Inherits: Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure
The primary class for placing individual reinforcement bars. Every reinforcement bar — whether bottom, top, stirrup, or tie — is a Rebar element. Bars are created from curves, a bar type, optional hook types, a host structural element, and a normal vector defining the bar's plane.
🟢 Essential Helper — Get Bar Type by Diameter
// Helpers used throughout ALL examples in this book
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure;

// ── Get RebarBarType by nominal diameter (mm) ──
RebarBarType GetBarType(Document doc, double diamMm)
{
    return new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(RebarBarType))
        .Cast<RebarBarType>()
        .OrderBy(b => Math.Abs(b.BarNominalDiameter * 304.8 - diamMm))
        .FirstOrDefault();
}

// ── Get hook type (90°, 135°, 180°) ──
RebarHookType GetHookType(Document doc, double angleDeg)
{
    return new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(RebarHookType))
        .Cast<RebarHookType>()
        .OrderBy(h => Math.Abs(h.HookAngle * (180 / Math.PI) - angleDeg))
        .FirstOrDefault();
}

// ── Get RebarShape by name (straight, stirrup, U-bar etc.) ──
RebarShape GetShape(Document doc, string nameContains = "Straight")
{
    return new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(RebarShape))
        .Cast<RebarShape>()
        .FirstOrDefault(s => s.Name.Contains(nameContains));
}

// ── Convert mm to feet ──
double mmToFt(double mm) =>
    UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(mm, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

// ── Single bar placement wrapper ──
Rebar PlaceBar(Document doc, Element host,
    RebarBarType barType, Curve curve,
    XYZ normal, RebarStyle style = RebarStyle.Standard,
    RebarHookType startHook = null, RebarHookType endHook = null)
{
    return Rebar.CreateFromCurves(
        doc, style, barType,
        startHook, endHook,
        host, normal,
        new List<Curve> { curve },
        RebarHookOrientation.Left,
        RebarHookOrientation.Left);
}

// ── RebarStyle values ──
// RebarStyle.Standard       — normal straight or shaped bar
// RebarStyle.StirrupTie     — closed stirrup/tie (links)
// RebarStyle.Unknown        — not used in new code

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Bottom Longitudinal Bars
🟢 Place bottom tension bars in a concrete beam
// Bottom bars — 3T20 at beam soffit with 35mm cover
/// <summary>
/// Place bottom longitudinal reinforcement bars in a concrete beam.
/// 3 x Ø20mm bottom bars at 35mm cover from soffit and sides.
/// </summary>
public void ReinforcBeamBottom(Document doc, FamilyInstance beam)
{
    // ── Get beam geometry ──
    LocationCurve lc       = beam.Location as LocationCurve;
    Line          beamLine = lc?.Curve as Line;
    if (beamLine == null) return;

    XYZ start = beamLine.GetEndPoint(0);
    XYZ end   = beamLine.GetEndPoint(1);
    XYZ dir   = (end - start).Normalize();

    // ── Read beam cross-section dimensions ──
    double beamWidthFt  = beam.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_BF)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(300);
    double beamDepthFt  = beam.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_HW)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(600);

    // ── Bar configuration ──
    double coverFt   = mmToFt(35);  // Eurocode nominal cover
    double barDiamFt = mmToFt(20);  // Ø20 bars
    int    numBars   = 3;             // 3T20 bottom

    RebarBarType bt20 = GetBarType(doc, 20);
    RebarHookType hook90 = GetHookType(doc, 90);

    // ── Calculate bar positions across bottom of beam ──
    // centreZ = beam bottom + cover + half bar diameter
    double barZOffset = -beamDepthFt / 2 + coverFt + barDiamFt / 2;

    // Distribute bars across width
    double availWidth = beamWidthFt - 2 * (coverFt + barDiamFt / 2);
    double spacing    = numBars > 1 ? availWidth / (numBars - 1) : 0;

    // Normal vector for longitudinal bar plane = vertical (Z)
    // — perpendicular to beam axis, in vertical plane
    XYZ normal = XYZ.BasisZ;

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Beam Bottom Bars"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        for (int i = 0; i < numBars; i++)
        {
            double xOffset = -availWidth / 2 + i * spacing;

            // Build offset transform: shift bar to correct position
            XYZ perp   = dir.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ).Normalize();
            XYZ barOff = perp * xOffset + XYZ.BasisZ * barZOffset;

            XYZ barStart = start + barOff;
            XYZ barEnd   = end   + barOff;

            // Shorten bar by cover at each end (bar stops before the face)
            XYZ s = barStart + dir * coverFt;
            XYZ e = barEnd   - dir * coverFt;

            Line barCurve = Line.CreateBound(s, e);

            Rebar bar = Rebar.CreateFromCurves(
                doc,
                RebarStyle.Standard,
                bt20,
                hook90,   // start hook 90°
                hook90,   // end hook 90°
                beam,
                normal,
                new List<Curve> { barCurve },
                RebarHookOrientation.Left,
                RebarHookOrientation.Left);

            // Set bar mark
            bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
               ?.Set($"B{i+1}-BOT-Ø20");
        }

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Top Longitudinal Bars
🟢 Place top compression/hogging bars
// Top bars — 2T16 at top of beam with 35mm cover
public void ReinforcBeamTop(Document doc, FamilyInstance beam)
{
    LocationCurve lc    = beam.Location as LocationCurve;
    Line          line  = lc?.Curve as Line;
    if (line == null) return;

    XYZ start = line.GetEndPoint(0);
    XYZ end   = line.GetEndPoint(1);
    XYZ dir   = (end - start).Normalize();

    double beamWidthFt = beam.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_BF)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(300);
    double beamDepthFt = beam.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_HW)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(600);

    double coverFt   = mmToFt(35);
    double barDiamFt = mmToFt(16);
    int    numBars   = 2;   // 2T16 top bars

    RebarBarType bt16 = GetBarType(doc, 16);

    // ++ for top: offset UPWARD from beam centre
    double barZOffset = +beamDepthFt / 2 - coverFt - barDiamFt / 2;
    double availWidth = beamWidthFt - 2 * (coverFt + barDiamFt / 2);
    double spacing    = numBars > 1 ? availWidth / (numBars - 1) : 0;
    XYZ    perp      = dir.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ).Normalize();

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Beam Top Bars"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        for (int i = 0; i < numBars; i++)
        {
            double xOff  = -availWidth / 2 + i * spacing;
            XYZ    off   = perp * xOff + XYZ.BasisZ * barZOffset;
            XYZ    s     = start + off + dir * coverFt;
            XYZ    e     = end   + off - dir * coverFt;
            Rebar  bar   = PlaceBar(doc, beam, bt16,
                Line.CreateBound(s, e), XYZ.BasisZ);
            bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
               ?.Set($"B{i+1}-TOP-Ø16");
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Stirrups / Shear Links
🟢 Place vertical closed stirrups along beam
// Stirrups — Ø10 links at 150mm centres along full beam length
public void ReinforcBeamStirrups(Document doc, FamilyInstance beam,
    double spacingMm = 150, double linkDiamMm = 10)
{
    LocationCurve lc   = beam.Location as LocationCurve;
    Line          line = lc?.Curve as Line;
    if (line == null) return;

    XYZ start     = line.GetEndPoint(0);
    XYZ end       = line.GetEndPoint(1);
    XYZ dir       = (end - start).Normalize();
    double lenFt  = line.Length;

    double coverFt    = mmToFt(35);
    double spacingFt  = mmToFt(spacingMm);
    double dFt        = mmToFt(linkDiamMm);

    double beamW = beam.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_BF)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(300);
    double beamH = beam.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_HW)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(600);

    RebarBarType  bt10 = GetBarType(doc, linkDiamMm);
    RebarHookType h135 = GetHookType(doc, 135);  // 135° hook for seismic

    // Inner dimensions of stirrup
    double sw = beamW - 2 * coverFt;  // stirrup inner width
    double sh = beamH - 2 * coverFt;  // stirrup inner height

    // Perpendicular to beam axis (horizontal, for stirrup plane)
    XYZ perp = dir.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ).Normalize();

    int numLinks = (int)Math.Floor((lenFt - 2 * coverFt) / spacingFt) + 1;

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Beam Stirrups"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        for (int i = 0; i < numLinks; i++)
        {
            double dist = coverFt + i * spacingFt;
            if (dist > lenFt - coverFt) break;

            // Centre of stirrup along beam axis
            XYZ ctr = start + dir * dist;

            // 4 corners of the closed stirrup rectangle
            // p0=bottom-left, p1=bottom-right, p2=top-right, p3=top-left
            XYZ p0 = ctr - perp * sw / 2 - XYZ.BasisZ * sh / 2;
            XYZ p1 = ctr + perp * sw / 2 - XYZ.BasisZ * sh / 2;
            XYZ p2 = ctr + perp * sw / 2 + XYZ.BasisZ * sh / 2;
            XYZ p3 = ctr - perp * sw / 2 + XYZ.BasisZ * sh / 2;

            // Stirrup = 4 lines forming closed rectangle
            var curves = new List<Curve>
            {
                Line.CreateBound(p0, p1),  // bottom
                Line.CreateBound(p1, p2),  // right
                Line.CreateBound(p2, p3),  // top
                Line.CreateBound(p3, p0)   // left
            };

            // Normal = beam direction (stirrup plane perpendicular to beam)
            Rebar stirrup = Rebar.CreateFromCurves(
                doc,
                RebarStyle.StirrupTie,   // ← MUST be StirrupTie for closed links
                bt10,
                h135, h135,              // 135° hooks for seismic
                beam,
                dir,                     // ← normal = beam direction
                curves,
                RebarHookOrientation.Left,
                RebarHookOrientation.Right);

            stirrup.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
                   ?.Set($"L{i+1}-Ø{linkDiamMm}@{spacingMm}");
        }

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Complete Beam Command
🟢 One command — full beam cage: bottom + top + stirrups
// IExternalCommand — reinforce all selected beams automatically
[Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
public class ReinforcBeamsCommand : IExternalCommand
{
    public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd, ref string msg, ElementSet els)
    {
        Document doc   = cd.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Document;
        UIDocument uid  = cd.Application.ActiveUIDocument;

        // Let user pick beams
        IList<Reference> refs;
        try
        {
            refs = uid.Selection.PickObjects(
                Autodesk.Revit.UI.Selection.ObjectType.Element,
                new BeamSelectionFilter(),
                "Select concrete beams to reinforce");
        }
        catch (Autodesk.Revit.Exceptions.OperationCanceledException)
        {
            return Result.Cancelled;
        }

        int count = 0;
        foreach (var r in refs)
        {
            FamilyInstance beam = doc.GetElement(r) as FamilyInstance;
            if (beam == null) continue;

            ReinforcBeamBottom(doc, beam);       // 3T20 bottom
            ReinforcBeamTop(doc, beam);           // 2T16 top
            ReinforcBeamStirrups(doc, beam,
                spacingMm: 150, linkDiamMm: 10); // Ø10@150
            count++;
        }

        TaskDialog.Show("Done",
            $"Reinforced {count} beam(s) with:\n" +
            "  3T20 bottom bars\n  2T16 top bars\n  Ø10 stirrups @150mm");

        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
}

public class BeamSelectionFilter : ISelectionFilter
{
    public bool AllowElement(Element e)
        => e is FamilyInstance fi
           && fi.StructuralType == StructuralType.Beam;
    public bool AllowReference(Reference r, XYZ p) => true;
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Longitudinal Column Bars
🟢 8T20 longitudinal bars around column perimeter
// Column longitudinal — 8×Ø20 perimeter bars at 40mm cover
public void ReinforcColumnLongitudinal(Document doc,
    FamilyInstance column, int numBars = 8, double barDiamMm = 20)
{
    // ── Get column geometry ──
    LocationPoint lp  = column.Location as LocationPoint;
    if (lp == null) return;
    XYZ colBase = lp.Point;

    // Get column height from base to top level
    double colHeightFt = column.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.INSTANCE_LENGTH_PARAM)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(3000);

    // Get cross-section dimensions
    double colBFt = column.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_BF)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(400);
    double colHFt = column.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_HW)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(400);

    double coverFt   = mmToFt(40);
    double dFt        = mmToFt(barDiamMm);
    double innerW     = colBFt - 2 * (coverFt + dFt / 2);
    double innerH     = colHFt - 2 * (coverFt + dFt / 2);

    RebarBarType bt = GetBarType(doc, barDiamMm);

    // ── Calculate bar positions around perimeter ──
    // For 8 bars: 4 corners + 1 intermediate per long side
    // Layout: 
    //   (0)-----(1)-----(2)   ← top
    //    |               |
    //   (7)             (3)   ← sides
    //    |               |
    //   (6)-----(5)-----(4)   ← bottom
    double x0 = colBase.X - innerW / 2;
    double x1 = colBase.X + innerW / 2;
    double y0 = colBase.Y - innerH / 2;
    double y1 = colBase.Y + innerH / 2;

    var positions = new List<XYZ>
    {
        new XYZ(x0,               y1, colBase.Z),  // TL corner
        new XYZ((x0+x1)/2,       y1, colBase.Z),  // T mid
        new XYZ(x1,               y1, colBase.Z),  // TR corner
        new XYZ(x1, (y0+y1)/2,       colBase.Z),  // R mid
        new XYZ(x1,               y0, colBase.Z),  // BR corner
        new XYZ((x0+x1)/2,       y0, colBase.Z),  // B mid
        new XYZ(x0,               y0, colBase.Z),  // BL corner
        new XYZ(x0, (y0+y1)/2,       colBase.Z),  // L mid
    };

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Column Longitudinal"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        for (int i = 0; i < Math.Min(numBars, positions.Count); i++)
        {
            XYZ  pos  = positions[i];
            XYZ  ptS  = new XYZ(pos.X, pos.Y, colBase.Z + coverFt);
            XYZ  ptE  = new XYZ(pos.X, pos.Y, colBase.Z + colHeightFt - coverFt);
            Line barL = Line.CreateBound(ptS, ptE);

            // Normal for vertical bar = horizontal (perpendicular to bar)
            // Use XYZ.BasisX for bars in YZ plane, BasisY for bars in XZ plane
            XYZ normal = Math.Abs(pos.X - colBase.X) >
                         Math.Abs(pos.Y - colBase.Y)
                ? XYZ.BasisY   // bar on left/right face: normal = Y
                : XYZ.BasisX;  // bar on front/back face: normal = X

            Rebar bar = Rebar.CreateFromCurves(
                doc,
                RebarStyle.Standard,
                bt,
                null, null,   // no hooks on vertical column bars
                column,
                normal,
                new List<Curve> { barL },
                RebarHookOrientation.Left,
                RebarHookOrientation.Left);

            bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
               ?.Set($"VB{i+1}-Ø{barDiamMm}");
        }

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Column Ties / Rectangular Links
🟢 Rectangular ties at specified vertical spacing
// Column ties — Ø10 rectangular links at 200mm vertical centres
public void ReinforcColumnTies(Document doc, FamilyInstance column,
    double spacingMm = 200, double tieDiamMm = 10)
{
    LocationPoint lp = column.Location as LocationPoint;
    if (lp == null) return;
    XYZ base  = lp.Point;

    double colH = column.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.INSTANCE_LENGTH_PARAM)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(3000);
    double colB = column.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_BF)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(400);
    double colD = column.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_HW)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(400);

    double coverFt  = mmToFt(40);
    double dFt      = mmToFt(tieDiamMm);
    double spFt     = mmToFt(spacingMm);

    // Inner rectangle of tie
    double tw = colB - 2 * coverFt;
    double td = colD - 2 * coverFt;

    RebarBarType  bt  = GetBarType(doc, tieDiamMm);
    RebarHookType h90 = GetHookType(doc, 90);

    int numTies = (int)Math.Floor((colH - 2 * coverFt) / spFt) + 1;

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Column Ties"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        for (int i = 0; i < numTies; i++)
        {
            double z = base.Z + coverFt + i * spFt;

            // 4 corners of horizontal tie at elevation z
            XYZ p0 = new XYZ(base.X - tw/2, base.Y - td/2, z);
            XYZ p1 = new XYZ(base.X + tw/2, base.Y - td/2, z);
            XYZ p2 = new XYZ(base.X + tw/2, base.Y + td/2, z);
            XYZ p3 = new XYZ(base.X - tw/2, base.Y + td/2, z);

            var curves = new List<Curve>
            {
                Line.CreateBound(p0, p1),
                Line.CreateBound(p1, p2),
                Line.CreateBound(p2, p3),
                Line.CreateBound(p3, p0)
            };

            // Normal = Z (tie lies in horizontal plane)
            Rebar tie = Rebar.CreateFromCurves(
                doc, RebarStyle.StirrupTie,
                bt, h90, h90,
                column, XYZ.BasisZ,
                curves,
                RebarHookOrientation.Left,
                RebarHookOrientation.Right);

            tie.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
               ?.Set($"T{i+1}-Ø{tieDiamMm}@{spacingMm}");
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── Full column command ──
public void ReinforcColumn(Document doc, FamilyInstance col)
{
    ReinforcColumnLongitudinal(doc, col, numBars: 8, barDiamMm: 20);
    ReinforcColumnTies(doc, col, spacingMm: 200, tieDiamMm: 10);
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Slab AreaReinforcement — Two-Way Mesh
🟢 Bottom and top mesh for a two-way concrete slab
// Two-way slab mesh — T12@150 bottom + T12@150 top
public void ReinforcSlabMesh(Document doc, Floor slab,
    double barDiamMm = 12, double spacingMm = 150)
{
    RebarBarType bt = GetBarType(doc, barDiamMm);
    if (bt == null) return;

    // Get slab boundary as CurveArray
    Options opts = new Options { ComputeReferences = false };
    BoundingBoxXYZ bb = slab.get_BoundingBox(null);
    if (bb == null) return;

    // Extract slab boundary curves from geometry
    CurveArray boundary = new CurveArray();
    foreach (GeometryObject obj in slab.get_Geometry(opts))
    {
        if (obj is Solid solid)
        {
            foreach (Face face in solid.Faces)
            {
                XYZ n = face.ComputeNormal(new UV(.5,.5));
                if (Math.Abs(n.Z) > 0.9 && n.Z > 0)   // top face
                {
                    foreach (var loop in face.GetEdgesAsCurveLoops())
                        foreach (var c in loop)
                            boundary.Append(c);
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    if (boundary.Size == 0) return;

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Slab Mesh Reinf"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // ── BOTTOM MESH ──
        AreaReinforcement botReinf = AreaReinforcement.Create(
            doc,
            slab,
            boundary,
            XYZ.BasisX,      // major direction = X
            bt.Id,            // major bar type
            ElementId.InvalidElementId,
            bt.Id,            // minor bar type (Y direction)
            ElementId.InvalidElementId);

        // Set spacing for both directions
        botReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_AREA_REINF_SPACING_BOTTOM_DIR_1)
            ?.Set(mmToFt(spacingMm));
        botReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_AREA_REINF_SPACING_BOTTOM_DIR_2)
            ?.Set(mmToFt(spacingMm));

        // Bottom cover
        botReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_AREA_REINF_BOTTOM_COVER_1)
            ?.Set(mmToFt(25));
        botReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_AREA_REINF_BOTTOM_COVER_2)
            ?.Set(mmToFt(25 + barDiamMm));  // 2nd layer sits above 1st

        botReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
            ?.Set($"BOT-T{barDiamMm}@{spacingMm}");

        // ── TOP MESH ── (same boundary, different cover)
        AreaReinforcement topReinf = AreaReinforcement.Create(
            doc, slab, boundary,
            XYZ.BasisX, bt.Id,
            ElementId.InvalidElementId,
            bt.Id,
            ElementId.InvalidElementId);

        topReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_AREA_REINF_SPACING_TOP_DIR_1)
            ?.Set(mmToFt(spacingMm));
        topReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_AREA_REINF_SPACING_TOP_DIR_2)
            ?.Set(mmToFt(spacingMm));
        topReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_AREA_REINF_TOP_COVER_1)
            ?.Set(mmToFt(25));
        topReinf.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
            ?.Set($"TOP-T{barDiamMm}@{spacingMm}");

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Punching Shear Zone
🟢 Radial punching shear bars around a column head
// Punching zone — radial T12 bars on 1.5d perimeter around column
public void ReinforcPunchingZone(Document doc,
    Floor slab, FamilyInstance column,
    double slabDepthMm = 250, double barDiamMm = 12)
{
    // Column centre
    XYZ colCtr = ((column.Location as LocationPoint)?.Point)
        ?? XYZ.Zero;

    // Effective depth d = slab - cover - bar diameter
    double d       = slabDepthMm - 25 - barDiamMm;
    double perimFt = mmToFt(1.5 * d);  // critical perimeter at 1.5d

    // Get column half-dimensions
    double cHalf = (double)(column.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_SECTION_ISHAPE_BF)?.AsDouble()
        ?? mmToFt(400)) / 2;

    double slabElev = (slab.get_BoundingBox(null)?.Max.Z ?? colCtr.Z) - mmToFt(50);

    RebarBarType bt = GetBarType(doc, barDiamMm);

    // Place 8 radial bars at 45° intervals around column
    int numRadial = 8;
    double innerR = cHalf + mmToFt(50);   // start 50mm from column face
    double outerR = perimFt + cHalf;       // out to 1.5d perimeter

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Punching Reinf"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        for (int i = 0; i < numRadial; i++)
        {
            double angle = i * (2 * Math.PI / numRadial);
            double cosA  = Math.Cos(angle);
            double sinA  = Math.Sin(angle);

            // Bar runs radially outward from column face to 1.5d perimeter
            XYZ s = new XYZ(
                colCtr.X + cosA * innerR,
                colCtr.Y + sinA * innerR,
                slabElev);
            XYZ e = new XYZ(
                colCtr.X + cosA * outerR,
                colCtr.Y + sinA * outerR,
                slabElev);

            // Normal = perpendicular to bar in horizontal plane
            XYZ radialDir = new XYZ(cosA, sinA, 0);
            XYZ normal    = radialDir.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ).Normalize();

            Rebar bar = PlaceBar(doc, slab, bt,
                Line.CreateBound(s, e), normal);

            bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
               ?.Set($"PZ{i+1}-Ø{barDiamMm}");
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Pad Footing Reinforcement
🟢 Two-way bottom mesh + starter bars for isolated pad footing
// Pad footing — T16@150 two-way mesh + 4 T20 starter bars at 75mm cover
public void ReinforcPadFooting(Document doc,
    FamilyInstance footing,  // structural foundation family instance
    double meshBarDiamMm = 16,
    double meshSpacingMm = 150,
    double starterBarDiamMm = 20,
    double starterLapMm = 600)
{
    LocationPoint lp = footing.Location as LocationPoint;
    if (lp == null) return;
    XYZ ctr = lp.Point;

    // Read footing dimensions
    double fW = footing.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_FOUNDATION_WIDTH)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(1500);
    double fD = footing.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_FOUNDATION_LENGTH)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(1500);
    double fH = footing.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.STRUCTURAL_FOUNDATION_THICKNESS)?.AsDouble() ?? mmToFt(500);

    double coverFt  = mmToFt(75);  // 75mm cover to earth
    double mDFt     = mmToFt(meshBarDiamMm);
    double spFt     = mmToFt(meshSpacingMm);

    double btmZ     = ctr.Z + coverFt + mDFt / 2;  // first layer Z
    double btmZ2    = btmZ + mDFt + mmToFt(5);      // second layer Z

    RebarBarType btMesh    = GetBarType(doc, meshBarDiamMm);
    RebarBarType btStarter = GetBarType(doc, starterBarDiamMm);

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Pad Footing Reinf"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // ── Bottom mesh — X direction (first layer) ──
        int numX = (int)Math.Floor((fD - 2 * coverFt) / spFt) + 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < numX; i++)
        {
            double y = ctr.Y - fD/2 + coverFt + i * spFt;
            XYZ s = new XYZ(ctr.X - fW/2 + coverFt, y, btmZ);
            XYZ e = new XYZ(ctr.X + fW/2 - coverFt, y, btmZ);
            var bar = PlaceBar(doc, footing, btMesh,
                Line.CreateBound(s, e), XYZ.BasisZ);
            bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
               ?.Set($"FBX{i+1}-Ø{meshBarDiamMm}");
        }

        // ── Bottom mesh — Y direction (second layer) ──
        int numY = (int)Math.Floor((fW - 2 * coverFt) / spFt) + 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < numY; i++)
        {
            double x = ctr.X - fW/2 + coverFt + i * spFt;
            XYZ s = new XYZ(x, ctr.Y - fD/2 + coverFt, btmZ2);
            XYZ e = new XYZ(x, ctr.Y + fD/2 - coverFt, btmZ2);
            PlaceBar(doc, footing, btMesh,
                Line.CreateBound(s, e), XYZ.BasisY)
              .get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
              ?.Set($"FBY{i+1}-Ø{meshBarDiamMm}");
        }

        // ── Starter bars — 4 corner starters projecting above footing ──
        double colBFt  = mmToFt(400) / 2 - mmToFt(50);  // approx column face
        double startLp = mmToFt(starterLapMm);
        double topZ    = ctr.Z + fH + startLp;         // project above footing
        double sBaseZ  = ctr.Z + coverFt;              // starts at footing bottom

        double[] xs = { ctr.X - colBFt, ctr.X + colBFt,
                         ctr.X + colBFt, ctr.X - colBFt };
        double[] ys = { ctr.Y - colBFt, ctr.Y - colBFt,
                         ctr.Y + colBFt, ctr.Y + colBFt };

        for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
        {
            XYZ s = new XYZ(xs[i], ys[i], sBaseZ);
            XYZ e = new XYZ(xs[i], ys[i], topZ);
            PlaceBar(doc, footing, btStarter,
                Line.CreateBound(s, e),
                (i == 0 || i == 2) ? XYZ.BasisX : XYZ.BasisY)
              .get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
              ?.Set($"ST{i+1}-Ø{starterBarDiamMm}");
        }

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Strip Footing Reinforcement
🟢 Longitudinal + transverse bars for strip/wall footing
// Strip footing — 3T16 bottom longitudinal + T10 transverse @300mm
public void ReinforcStripFooting(Document doc, Wall footingWall)
{
    LocationCurve lc   = footingWall.Location as LocationCurve;
    Line          line = lc?.Curve as Line;
    if (line == null) return;

    XYZ    s     = line.GetEndPoint(0);
    XYZ    e     = line.GetEndPoint(1);
    XYZ    dir   = (e - s).Normalize();
    double lenFt  = line.Length;

    double fWidthFt = footingWall.Width;
    double fDepthFt = (mmToFt(450)); // typical strip depth
    double cvFt     = mmToFt(75);

    RebarBarType bt16 = GetBarType(doc, 16);
    RebarBarType bt10 = GetBarType(doc, 10);
    XYZ          perp = dir.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ).Normalize();

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Strip Footing Reinf"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // ── 3 bottom longitudinal bars ──
        double[] xOffsets = {
            -fWidthFt/2 + cvFt + mmToFt(8),
            0,
            +fWidthFt/2 - cvFt - mmToFt(8)
        };
        double botZ = s.Z + cvFt + mmToFt(8);  // bottom of footing + cover + r

        foreach (var xOff in xOffsets)
        {
            XYZ off   = perp * xOff + XYZ.BasisZ * (botZ - s.Z);
            XYZ barS  = s + off + dir * cvFt;
            XYZ barE  = e + off - dir * cvFt;
            PlaceBar(doc, footingWall, bt16,
                Line.CreateBound(barS, barE), XYZ.BasisZ)
              .get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
              ?.Set("SF-LONG-Ø16");
        }

        // ── Transverse distribution bars @300mm ──
        double spT = mmToFt(300);
        int    nT  = (int)((lenFt - 2 * cvFt) / spT) + 1;

        for (int i = 0; i < nT; i++)
        {
            double dist  = cvFt + i * spT;
            if (dist > lenFt - cvFt) break;

            XYZ ctrPt = s + dir * dist + XYZ.BasisZ * (botZ - s.Z + mmToFt(16));
            XYZ tS    = ctrPt - perp * (fWidthFt/2 - cvFt);
            XYZ tE    = ctrPt + perp * (fWidthFt/2 - cvFt);
            PlaceBar(doc, footingWall, bt10,
                Line.CreateBound(tS, tE), dir)
              .get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
              ?.Set($"SF-T{i+1}-Ø10");
        }

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Base Plate Connection
🟢 Column base plate — read and create via FamilyInstance
// Base plate — place structural family + read/write parameters
/// Base plate connection for a steel column.
/// In Revit API, steel connections are FamilyInstance elements
/// in the Structural Connections category.

public void PlaceBasePlate(Document doc, FamilyInstance column)
{
    // ── Find base plate family symbol ──
    FamilySymbol bpSym = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructConnections)
        .Cast<FamilySymbol>()
        .FirstOrDefault(s => s.Name.Contains("Base Plate")
                          || s.Family.Name.Contains("Base"));

    if (bpSym == null)
    {
        TaskDialog.Show("Error",
            "No 'Base Plate' structural connection family found.\n" +
            "Load it from the Structural Connections library first.");
        return;
    }

    // ── Column base point ──
    XYZ basePoint = (column.Location as LocationPoint)?.Point;
    if (basePoint == null) return;

    Level level = doc.GetElement(column.LevelId) as Level
        ?? new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Base Plate"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        if (!bpSym.IsActive) bpSym.Activate();

        FamilyInstance bp = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
            basePoint, bpSym, level, StructuralType.NonStructural);

        // ── Set plate dimensions ──
        void SetP(string name, double mm)
        {
            bp.LookupParameter(name)?.Set(mmToFt(mm));
        }
        SetP("Plate Width",     450);
        SetP("Plate Length",    450);
        SetP("Plate Thickness", 25);
        SetP("Anchor Bolt Dia", 24);
        SetP("Bolt Gauge X",    250);
        SetP("Bolt Gauge Y",    250);

        // Steel grade
        bp.LookupParameter("Material Grade")?.Set("S275");

        // Mark
        bp.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
          ?.Set($"BP-{column.Id.IntegerValue}");

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── Read existing base plate data ──
public void AuditBasePlates(Document doc)
{
    var plates = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructConnections)
        .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
        .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
        .Where(fi => fi.Symbol.Family.Name.Contains("Base"))
        .ToList();

    var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
    sb.AppendLine("BASE PLATE AUDIT");
    sb.AppendLine($"{"Mark",-12} {"W(mm)",-8} {"L(mm)",-8} {"T(mm)",-8} {"BoltDia",-10} {"Grade",-8}");
    sb.AppendLine(new string('-', 60));

    foreach (var bp in plates)
    {
        string mark = bp.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
                        ?.AsString() ?? "—";
        double w    = bp.LookupParameter("Plate Width")    ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;
        double l    = bp.LookupParameter("Plate Length")   ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;
        double t    = bp.LookupParameter("Plate Thickness")?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;
        double bolt = bp.LookupParameter("Anchor Bolt Dia")?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;
        string grd  = bp.LookupParameter("Material Grade")  ?.AsString() ?? "—";

        sb.AppendLine($"{mark,-12} {w,-8:F0} {l,-8:F0} {t,-8:F0} {bolt,-10:F0} {grd,-8}");
    }

    TaskDialog.Show("Base Plates", sb.ToString());
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
End Plate Connection
🟢 Beam-to-column end plate — parameters + bolt pattern
// End plate beam-to-column — M20 bolts at 4-bolt pattern
public void PlaceEndPlateConnection(Document doc,
    FamilyInstance beam, FamilyInstance column)
{
    // Find end plate connection symbol
    FamilySymbol epSym = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructConnections)
        .Cast<FamilySymbol>()
        .FirstOrDefault(s => s.Name.Contains("End Plate")
                          || s.Family.Name.Contains("EndPlate"));

    if (epSym == null) return;

    // Position: end of beam
    LocationCurve lc      = beam.Location as LocationCurve;
    XYZ           beamEnd = lc?.Curve.GetEndPoint(1) ?? XYZ.Zero;

    Level level = doc.GetElement(beam.LevelId) as Level
        ?? new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "End Plate"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        if (!epSym.IsActive) epSym.Activate();

        FamilyInstance ep = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
            beamEnd, epSym, level, StructuralType.NonStructural);

        // ── Configure plate ──
        ep.LookupParameter("Plate Width")     ?.Set(mmToFt(180));
        ep.LookupParameter("Plate Depth")     ?.Set(mmToFt(300));
        ep.LookupParameter("Plate Thickness") ?.Set(mmToFt(15));

        // ── Configure bolts (4-bolt pattern) ──
        ep.LookupParameter("Bolt Diameter")  ?.Set(mmToFt(20));  // M20
        ep.LookupParameter("Bolt Grade")     ?.Set("8.8");
        ep.LookupParameter("No. of Bolt Rows")?.Set(2);
        ep.LookupParameter("Bolt Gauge")     ?.Set(mmToFt(90));
        ep.LookupParameter("Bolt Pitch")     ?.Set(mmToFt(100));
        ep.LookupParameter("Top Edge Dist")  ?.Set(mmToFt(50));

        ep.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
          ?.Set($"EP-{beam.Id.IntegerValue}-{column.Id.IntegerValue}");

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Class
sections.content:
Complete Steel Connection Audit
🟢 Full steel model audit — all connections, types, and bolt grades
// Audit all structural connections + generate CSV report
public void AuditAllSteelConnections(Document doc)
{
    // ── 1. Get all structural connections ──
    var connections = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructConnections)
        .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
        .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
        .ToList();

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Total structural connections: {connections.Count}");

    // ── 2. Group by connection type ──
    var byType = connections
        .GroupBy(c => c.Symbol?.Family?.Name ?? "Unknown")
        .OrderByDescending(g => g.Count());

    foreach (var grp in byType)
    {
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"  {grp.Key}: {grp.Count()} instances");
    }

    // ── 3. Full data extraction ──
    var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
    sb.AppendLine("Mark,Family,Type,BoltDia,BoltGrade,PlateThk,SteelGrade,X,Y,Z");

    foreach (var conn in connections)
    {
        string mark      = conn.get_Parameter(
            BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)?.AsString() ?? "";
        string famName   = conn.Symbol?.Family?.Name ?? "";
        string typeName  = conn.Symbol?.Name ?? "";

        // Try common parameter names across different families
        double boltDia   = (conn.LookupParameter("Bolt Diameter")
                         ?? conn.LookupParameter("Anchor Bolt Dia")
                         ?? conn.LookupParameter("Bolt"))
                         ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;

        string boltGrade = conn.LookupParameter("Bolt Grade")
                            ?.AsString() ?? "";

        double plateThk  = (conn.LookupParameter("Plate Thickness")
                         ?? conn.LookupParameter("Thickness"))
                         ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;

        string steelGrd  = conn.LookupParameter("Material Grade")
                            ?.AsString() ?? "S275";

        // Position
        XYZ pos = (conn.Location as LocationPoint)?.Point ?? XYZ.Zero;

        sb.AppendLine($"{mark},{famName},{typeName}," +
                       $"{boltDia:F0},{boltGrade},{plateThk:F0}," +
                       $"{steelGrd}," +
                       $"{pos.X * 304.8:F0},{pos.Y * 304.8:F0},{pos.Z * 304.8:F0}");
    }

    // Write CSV
    string csvPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(
        System.Environment.GetFolderPath(
            System.Environment.SpecialFolder.Desktop),
        "SteelConnections.csv");
    System.IO.File.WriteAllText(csvPath, sb.ToString());

    TaskDialog.Show("Audit Complete",
        $"Found {connections.Count} connections\n" +
        $"CSV saved to: {csvPath}");
}

sections.title: Rebar Schedule + Auto Mark + CSV Export
sections.content:
ScheduleRebar Schedule + Auto Mark + CSV Export
🟢 Complete reinforcement reporting workflow
// Auto-mark all bars + generate rebar schedule view + export CSV
// ═══════════════════════════════════
// STEP 1: Auto-number all rebar bars
// ═══════════════════════════════════
public void AutoMarkAllRebar(Document doc)
{
    var bars = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar)
        .Cast<Rebar>()
        .OrderBy(b => b.Id.IntegerValue)
        .ToList();

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Auto Mark Rebar"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // Group by diameter and style, then assign sequential marks
        var groups = bars
            .GroupBy(b => (GetDiamMm(doc, b), b.RebarStyle))
            .OrderBy(g => g.Key.Item1);

        int barMark = 1;
        foreach (var grp in groups)
        {
            (double diam, RebarStyle style) = grp.Key;
            string prefix = style == RebarStyle.StirrupTie ? "L" : "B";
            int    idx    = 1;

            foreach (var bar in grp)
            {
                string mark = $"{prefix}{barMark:D2}-Ø{diam:F0}";
                bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
                   ?.Set(mark);
                idx++;
            }
            barMark++;
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

double GetDiamMm(Document doc, Rebar bar)
{
    var bt = doc.GetElement(bar.GetTypeId()) as RebarBarType;
    return bt?.BarNominalDiameter * 304.8 ?? 0;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════
// STEP 2: Create rebar schedule view
// ═══════════════════════════════════
public ViewSchedule CreateRebarSchedule(Document doc)
{
    ViewSchedule sched;

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Rebar Schedule"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        sched = ViewSchedule.CreateSchedule(doc,
            new ElementId(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar));
        sched.Name = "Bar Bending Schedule";

        ScheduleDefinition def    = sched.Definition;
        IList<SchedulableField> sf = def.GetSchedulableFields();

        // Add key fields for a bar bending schedule
        var wantedBIPs = new[]
        {
            BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK,              // Bar Mark
            BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_BAR_DIAMETER,     // Diameter
            BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_LENGTH,            // Total Length
            BuiltInParameter.REBAR_NUMBER_OF_BARSINSET,    // No. of Bars
            BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_BAR_STYLE,         // Style
            BuiltInParameter.HOST_ID_PARAM,               // Host Element
            BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_VOLUME,            // Volume (for weight)
        };

        foreach (var bip in wantedBIPs)
        {
            var field = sf.FirstOrDefault(
                f => f.ParameterId == new ElementId(bip));
            if (field != null) def.AddField(field);
        }

        // Sort by mark
        var markField = def.GetField(0);
        var sortGrp   = new ScheduleSortGroupField(markField.FieldId)
        {
            SortOrder = ScheduleSortOrder.Ascending
        };
        def.SetSortGroupFields(
            new List<ScheduleSortGroupField> { sortGrp });

        tx.Commit();
    }
    return sched;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════
// STEP 3: Export rebar data to CSV
// ═══════════════════════════════════
public void ExportRebarToCSV(Document doc, string csvPath)
{
    var bars = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar)
        .Cast<Rebar>()
        .ToList();

    var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
    sb.AppendLine("Mark,Diameter(mm),Length(mm),Count,Style,Volume(m³),Weight(kg),Host");

    // Steel density: 7850 kg/m³
    const double STEEL_DENSITY = 7850;

    foreach (var bar in bars.OrderBy(b =>
        b.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)?.AsString()))
    {
        string mark   = bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
                            ?.AsString() ?? "";
        double diam   = GetDiamMm(doc, bar);
        double lenMm  = bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_LENGTH)
                            ?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;
        int    count  = bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_NUMBER_OF_BARSINSET)
                            ?.AsInteger() ?? 1;
        string style  = bar.RebarStyle.ToString();
        double volM3  = bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_VOLUME)
                            ?.AsDouble() * 0.0283168 ?? 0;
        double weightKg = volM3 * STEEL_DENSITY;

        // Get host name
        var    hostId   = bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.HOST_ID_PARAM)
                             ?.AsElementId();
        string hostName = hostId != null
            ? doc.GetElement(hostId)?.Name ?? ""
            : "";

        sb.AppendLine($"{mark},{diam:F0},{lenMm:F0},{count}," +
                       $"{style},{volM3:F6},{weightKg:F2},{hostName}");
    }

    System.IO.File.WriteAllText(csvPath, sb.ToString());

    // Summary
    double totalWeight = bars
        .Sum(b => (b.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_VOLUME)
                    ?.AsDouble() * 0.0283168 ?? 0) * STEEL_DENSITY);

    TaskDialog.Show("Rebar Export",
        $"Exported {bars.Count} bar records\n" +
        $"Total steel weight: {totalWeight:F0} kg\n" +
        $"CSV: {csvPath}");
}

sections.title: Rebar — Layout Rules
sections.content:
ClassRebar — Layout Rules
Inherits: DB.Structure.Rebar
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure
The professional way to place a full set of bars — create ONE Rebar element then apply a layout rule. Revit distributes the bars automatically. This is how Revit's own reinforcement tool works internally and produces proper bar sets that schedule correctly.
// Book 17 of 19 vs Book 18Book 17 loops to place individual bars (great for learning). Production add-ins use Layout Rules: place one Rebar, set the rule, Revit creates the full set with correct scheduling.
🟢 All 4 Layout Modes
// All 4 Rebar Layout Rules with real examples
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure;

double mmToFt(double mm) =>
    UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(mm, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

RebarBarType bt = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(RebarBarType)).Cast<RebarBarType>()
    .OrderBy(b => Math.Abs(b.BarNominalDiameter * 304.8 - 20)).First();

// ── Place ONE bar (position 0) ──
LocationCurve lc     = beam.Location as LocationCurve;
XYZ           bStart = lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(0);
XYZ           bEnd   = lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(1);
XYZ           dir    = (bEnd - bStart).Normalize();
XYZ           perp   = dir.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ).Normalize();

double beamW  = mmToFt(300);
double beamH  = mmToFt(600);
double cvFt   = mmToFt(35);

XYZ off   = perp * (-beamW/2 + cvFt + mmToFt(10))
           + XYZ.BasisZ * (-beamH/2 + cvFt + mmToFt(10));
XYZ barS  = bStart + off + dir * cvFt;
XYZ barE  = bEnd   + off - dir * cvFt;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Rebar Layout"))
{
    tx.Start();

    Rebar rebar = Rebar.CreateFromCurves(
        doc, RebarStyle.Standard, bt,
        null, null, beam, XYZ.BasisZ,
        new List<Curve> { Line.CreateBound(barS, barE) },
        RebarHookOrientation.Left,
        RebarHookOrientation.Left);

    // ════ LAYOUT 1: Fixed Number ════
    // Place exactly 5 bars evenly distributed across beam width
    rebar.SetLayoutAsFixedNumber(
        5,                         // number of bars
        beamW - 2 * cvFt,          // array length (total span)
        true,                      // include first bar
        true,                      // include last bar
        true);                     // on normal side

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"Fixed: {rebar.NumberOfBarPositions} bars at {rebar.MaxSpacing*304.8:F0}mm");

    // ════ LAYOUT 2: Maximum Spacing ════
    // Fill the zone with bars — Revit calculates how many fit at max 150mm
    rebar.SetLayoutAsMaximumSpacing(
        mmToFt(150),               // maximum spacing
        beamW - 2 * cvFt,          // array length
        true, true, true);

    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"MaxSpacing: {rebar.NumberOfBarPositions} bars");

    // ════ LAYOUT 3: Minimum Clear Spacing ════
    // Bars with 25mm minimum clear gap between them
    rebar.SetLayoutAsMinimumClearSpacing(
        mmToFt(25),                // minimum clear gap
        beamW - 2 * cvFt,
        true, true, true);

    // ════ LAYOUT 4: Single Bar (reset to just 1) ════
    rebar.SetLayoutAsSingleBar();

    tx.Commit();
}

// ── Read layout properties of any rebar ──
var bars = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar)
    .Cast<Rebar>().ToList();

foreach (var bar in bars)
{
    RebarLayoutRule rule  = bar.LayoutRule;
    int             count = bar.NumberOfBarPositions;
    double          sp    = bar.MaxSpacing * 304.8;

    // Get transform for each bar position in the set
    for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
    {
        Transform t = bar.GetShapeDrivenAccessor()
                          .GetBarPositionTransform(i);
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Bar {i}: Rule={rule} Count={count} Sp={sp:F0}mm Pos={t.Origin}");
    }
}

// RebarLayoutRule enum:
// Single | FixedNumber | MaximumSpacing | MinimumClearSpacing | NumberWithSpacing

sections.title: GetBendData · RebarShapeDrivenAccessor · ComputeDrivingCurves
sections.content:
MethodsGetBendData · RebarShapeDrivenAccessor · ComputeDrivingCurves
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure
Access bend leg lengths, bend angles, and mandrel sizes for bar bending schedule generation. GetShapeDrivenAccessor() returns a RebarShapeDrivenAccessor that exposes ComputeDrivingCurves() for individual leg lengths.
🟢 Bend data extraction for bar bending schedules
// Extract bend data → CSV for bar bending schedule
public void ExtractBendData(Document doc)
{
    var bars = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar)
        .Cast<Rebar>().ToList();

    var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
    sb.AppendLine("Mark,Shape,Dia(mm),TotalLen(mm),Legs,A(mm),B(mm),C(mm),BendDia(mm)");

    foreach (var bar in bars)
    {
        string mark  = bar.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)
                           ?.AsString() ?? "";
        RebarShape shape = doc.GetElement(bar.RebarShapeId) as RebarShape;
        string shpName   = shape?.Name ?? "Straight";
        double totalMm   = bar.get_Parameter(
            BuiltInParameter.REBAR_ELEM_LENGTH)?.AsDouble() * 304.8 ?? 0;

        RebarBarType bt  = doc.GetElement(bar.GetTypeId()) as RebarBarType;
        double dMm       = bt?.BarNominalDiameter * 304.8 ?? 0;
        double bendDiaMm = bt?.StandardBendDiameter * 304.8 ?? dMm * 4;

        // Get individual leg lengths from shape accessor
        RebarShapeDrivenAccessor acc = bar.GetShapeDrivenAccessor();
        IList<Curve> legs = acc?.ComputeDrivingCurves();
        var legsMm = legs?.Select(c => c.Length * 304.8).ToList()
                    ?? new List<double>();

        double A = legsMm.Count > 0 ? legsMm[0] : 0;
        double B = legsMm.Count > 1 ? legsMm[1] : 0;
        double C = legsMm.Count > 2 ? legsMm[2] : 0;

        sb.AppendLine($"{mark},{shpName},{dMm:F0},{totalMm:F0},"
                    + $"{legsMm.Count},{A:F0},{B:F0},{C:F0},{bendDiaMm:F0}");
    }

    string path = System.IO.Path.Combine(
        System.Environment.GetFolderPath(
            System.Environment.SpecialFolder.Desktop), "BendData.csv");
    System.IO.File.WriteAllText(path, sb.ToString());
    TaskDialog.Show("Bend Data", $"Exported {bars.Count} bars → {path}");
}

sections.title: RebarCoverType · RebarHostData
sections.content:
ClassRebarCoverType · RebarHostData
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure
Project-level cover type definitions (nominal cover by exposure class) assigned to faces of structural hosts. Define a RebarCoverType once, then assign it to any host face via RebarHostData.
🟢 Create cover types + assign to host faces
// Create cover types + assign to beam/column faces
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure;

public void SetupCoverTypes(Document doc, FamilyInstance beam)
{
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Cover Types"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // ── Create named cover type definitions ──
        RebarCoverType cv35 = RebarCoverType.Create(
            doc, "Internal 35mm",
            UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(35, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

        RebarCoverType cv40 = RebarCoverType.Create(
            doc, "External 40mm",
            UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(40, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

        RebarCoverType cv75 = RebarCoverType.Create(
            doc, "Foundation 75mm",
            UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(75, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

        // ── Assign to host faces ──
        RebarHostData hd = RebarHostData.GetRebarHostData(beam);
        if (hd != null)
        {
            hd.SetFaceCoverTypeId(RebarHostFace.Bottom, cv35.Id);
            hd.SetFaceCoverTypeId(RebarHostFace.Top,    cv35.Id);
            hd.SetFaceCoverTypeId(RebarHostFace.Left,   cv35.Id);
            hd.SetFaceCoverTypeId(RebarHostFace.Right,  cv35.Id);
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }

    // ── Read all cover types in project ──
    var covers = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(RebarCoverType)).Cast<RebarCoverType>();
    foreach (var c in covers)
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Cover: {c.Name} = {c.CoverDistance * 304.8:F0}mm");
}
// RebarHostFace: Bottom Top Left Right Front Back

sections.title: FabricSheet · FabricSheetType
sections.content:
ClassFabricSheet · FabricSheetType
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Structure
Represents a welded wire mesh / BRC fabric sheet placed in a slab or wall. FabricSheetType defines wire diameters, wire spacings, and sheet dimensions. FabricSheet is the placed instance.
🟢 Place fabric sheet + read type properties
// Place A393 BRC mesh in a concrete slab
public void PlaceFabricSheet(Document doc, Floor slab)
{
    FabricSheetType fst = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(FabricSheetType)).Cast<FabricSheetType>()
        .FirstOrDefault(t => t.Name.Contains("A393")
                          || t.Name.Contains("A252"));

    if (fst == null) {
        TaskDialog.Show("Info",
            "Load a Fabric Reinforcement type from the Structural library first.");
        return;
    }

    BoundingBoxXYZ bb = slab.get_BoundingBox(null);
    if (bb == null) return;

    double cvFt    = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(25, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
    XYZ    origin  = new XYZ(bb.Min.X + cvFt, bb.Min.Y + cvFt, bb.Min.Z + cvFt);

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Fabric Sheet"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        FabricSheet sheet = FabricSheet.Create(
            doc, fst.Id, slab, origin, XYZ.BasisX);
        sheet.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK)?.Set("FS-A393-BOT");
        tx.Commit();
    }

    // Read FabricSheetType properties
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Sheet: {fst.Name}");
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"  Major: Ø{fst.MajorWireDiameter*304.8:F1}mm @{fst.MajorWireSpacing*304.8:F0}mm");
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"  Minor: Ø{fst.MinorWireDiameter*304.8:F1}mm @{fst.MinorWireSpacing*304.8:F0}mm");
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
        $"  Size: {fst.SheetWidth*304.8:F0}mm × {fst.SheetLength*304.8:F0}mm");
}

sections.title: Ceiling.Create
sections.content:
ClassCeiling.Create
Inherits: HostObject → Element
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Creates a ceiling from CurveLoops — identical API pattern to Floor.Create(). Use room boundaries as the profile. The Height Offset from Level parameter controls how far below the level the ceiling sits.
🟢 Create ceiling + batch create all room ceilings
// Ceiling.Create from room boundary + batch all rooms
public void CreateCeilingFromRoom(Document doc, Room room)
{
    CeilingType ct = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(CeilingType)).Cast<CeilingType>().First();

    var bOpts = new SpatialElementBoundaryOptions();
    var segs  = room.GetBoundarySegments(bOpts);
    if (segs.Count == 0) return;

    var loop = new CurveLoop();
    foreach (var seg in segs[0]) loop.Append(seg.GetCurve());

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Ceiling"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // Identical to Floor.Create — just different element type
        Ceiling ceiling = Ceiling.Create(
            doc,
            new List<CurveLoop> { loop },
            ct.Id,
            room.LevelId);

        // Height offset below the level (negative = below)
        double off = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
        ceiling.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.CEILING_HEIGHTABOVELEVEL_PARAM)
               ?.Set(-off);

        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── Batch: create ceilings for ALL rooms on a level ──
public void BatchCeilings(Document doc, Level level)
{
    var rooms = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
        .Cast<Room>()
        .Where(r => r.Area > 0 && r.LevelId == level.Id).ToList();

    CeilingType ct = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(CeilingType)).Cast<CeilingType>().First();
    int n = 0;

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Batch Ceilings"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        foreach (var room in rooms)
        {
            try {
                var bOpts = new SpatialElementBoundaryOptions();
                var segs  = room.GetBoundarySegments(bOpts);
                if (segs.Count == 0) continue;
                var loop = new CurveLoop();
                foreach (var seg in segs[0]) loop.Append(seg.GetCurve());
                Ceiling.Create(doc,
                    new List<CurveLoop>{ loop }, ct.Id, room.LevelId);
                n++;
            } catch { }
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
    TaskDialog.Show("Done", $"Created {n} ceilings on {level.Name}");
}

sections.title: Group · GroupType
sections.content:
ClassGroup · GroupType
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
A named, reusable collection of elements. Groups have types (GroupType) and instances (Group). All instances update when the type is edited. Used for repeating room layouts, structural bays, and unit plans.
🟢 Create, place, audit, and ungroup
// Complete ModelGroup workflow
// ── CREATE group from element IDs ──
public Group CreateGroup(Document doc,
    ICollection<ElementId> ids, string name)
{
    Group grp;
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Group"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        grp = doc.Create.NewGroup(ids);
        grp.GroupType.Name = name;
        tx.Commit();
    }
    return grp;
}

// ── PLACE group type at multiple positions ──
public void PlaceGroups(Document doc,
    GroupType gt, IList<XYZ> positions)
{
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Place Groups"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        foreach (var pos in positions)
            doc.Create.PlaceGroup(pos, gt);
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── AUDIT all groups ──
public void AuditGroups(Document doc)
{
    var gts = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(GroupType)).Cast<GroupType>();
    foreach (var gt in gts)
    {
        int count = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfClass(typeof(Group)).Cast<Group>()
            .Count(g => g.GroupType.Id == gt.Id);
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Group '{gt.Name}': {count} instances");
    }
}

// ── UNGROUP (explode) all instances ──
public void UngroupAll(Document doc, GroupType gt)
{
    var instances = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Group)).Cast<Group>()
        .Where(g => g.GroupType.Id == gt.Id).ToList();
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Ungroup"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        foreach (var g in instances)
        {
            ICollection<ElementId> members = g.UngroupMembers();
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"Ungrouped {members.Count} elements");
        }
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: Revision · RevisionCloud
sections.content:
ClassRevision · RevisionCloud
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents a project revision. Revisions appear in the revision schedule on title blocks. RevisionClouds mark areas of change in views. Full workflow: Create Revision → add to Sheet → place RevisionCloud → Issue.
🟢 Complete revision workflow — create, cloud, issue, read
// Revision workflow: create → add to sheet → cloud → issue
public void FullRevisionWorkflow(Document doc,
    ViewSheet sheet, View view)
{
    Revision rev;

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Revision"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // ── STEP 1: Create Revision ──
        rev              = Revision.Create(doc);
        rev.Description  = "Structural amendments per engineer review";
        rev.IssuedBy     = "Studio Architecture Ltd";
        rev.IssuedTo     = "Client: Acme Corp";
        rev.RevisionDate = "2024-06-01";
        rev.NumberType   = RevisionNumberType.Numeric;  // 1,2,3 or Alphanumeric A,B,C

        // ── STEP 2: Add to sheet (appears in title block table) ──
        var ids = sheet.GetAdditionalRevisionIds().ToList();
        ids.Add(rev.Id);
        sheet.SetAdditionalRevisionIds(ids);

        tx.Commit();
    }

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Revision Cloud"))
    {
        tx.Start();

        // ── STEP 3: Place RevisionCloud in a view ──
        var loop = new CurveLoop();
        loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0),new XYZ(5,0,0)));
        loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(5,0,0),new XYZ(5,3,0)));
        loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(5,3,0),new XYZ(0,3,0)));
        loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,3,0),new XYZ(0,0,0)));

        RevisionCloud cloud = RevisionCloud.Create(
            doc, view, rev.Id,
            new List<CurveLoop> { loop });
        cloud.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_COMMENTS)
             ?.Set("Wall moved 300mm N per RFI-042");

        tx.Commit();
    }

    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Issue Revision"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        rev.Issued = true;   // locks it — no more editing
        tx.Commit();
    }

    // ── Read all revisions on a sheet ──
    foreach (var rid in sheet.GetAllRevisionIds())
    {
        Revision r = doc.GetElement(rid) as Revision;
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"{r?.RevisionDate} | {r?.Description} | Issued={r?.Issued}");
    }
}

sections.title: SpatialFieldManager · AnalysisResultSchema
sections.content:
ClassSpatialFieldManager · AnalysisResultSchema
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB.Analysis
Creates custom analysis display overlays on element faces in Revit 3D views — colour-mapped results like heat flux, stress contours, or daylighting. Used when a plugin needs to display numerical analysis results visually on the model geometry.
🟢 Display custom analysis results on wall faces
// Colour-map custom heat flux values on exterior wall faces
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Analysis;

public void ShowAnalysisOverlay(Document doc, View3D view3D)
{
    // ── STEP 1: Get or create SpatialFieldManager for the view ──
    SpatialFieldManager sfm =
        SpatialFieldManager.GetSpatialFieldManager(view3D)
        ?? SpatialFieldManager.CreateSpatialFieldManager(view3D, 1);

    // ── STEP 2: Register result schema ──
    var schema = new AnalysisResultSchema("HeatFlux", "W/m²");
    schema.SetUnits(new List<string> { "W/m²" },
                    new List<double> { 1.0 });
    int schIdx = sfm.RegisterResult(schema);

    // ── STEP 3: For each exterior wall face — upload values ──
    var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>()
        .Where(w => w.WallType.Function == WallFunction.Exterior).ToList();

    var opts = new Options { ComputeReferences = true };
    foreach (var wall in walls)
    {
        foreach (GeometryObject obj in wall.get_Geometry(opts))
        {
            if (!(obj is Solid solid)) continue;
            foreach (Face face in solid.Faces)
            {
                XYZ n = face.ComputeNormal(new UV(.5,.5));
                if (Math.Abs(n.Z) > 0.3 || face.Reference == null) continue;

                int primIdx = sfm.AddSpatialFieldPrimitive(face.Reference);
                var uvPts = new FieldDomainPointsByUV();
                var vals  = new FieldValues();
                BoundingBoxUV bb = face.GetBoundingBox();

                // 5×5 grid sample
                for (int iu = 0; iu < 5; iu++)
                for (int iv = 0; iv < 5; iv++)
                {
                    double u = bb.Min.U + (bb.Max.U-bb.Min.U)*iu/4;
                    double v = bb.Min.V + (bb.Max.V-bb.Min.V)*iv/4;
                    uvPts.Add(new UV(u,v));
                    vals.Add(new ValueAtPoint(
                        new List<double> { 5.0 + v * 15.0 })); // fake heat flux
                }
                sfm.UpdateSpatialFieldPrimitive(primIdx, uvPts, vals, schIdx);
            }
        }
    }

    // ── STEP 4: Create + apply AnalysisDisplayStyle ──
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Analysis Style"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        AnalysisDisplayStyle style =
            AnalysisDisplayStyle.CreateAnalysisDisplayStyle(
                doc, "Heat Flux Style",
                new AnalysisDisplayColoredSurfaceSettings(),
                new AnalysisDisplayColorSettings(),
                new AnalysisDisplayLegendSettings());
        view3D.AnalysisDisplayStyleId = style.Id;
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: ImportInstance · CAD Linking
sections.content:
ClassImportInstance · CAD Linking
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB
Represents an imported or linked CAD file (DWG/DXF/DGN). Use Document.Import() for embedded imports and Document.Link() for live-linked files. ImportInstance gives access to transform, file path, and layer visibility.
🟢 Import, Link, Audit, Explode CAD files
// Import DWG + Link DWG + Audit all CAD + Explode
// ── IMPORT DWG (embedded) ──
public void ImportDWG(Document doc, string dwgPath, View view)
{
    var opts = new DWGImportOptions
    {
        ColorMode    = ImportColorMode.BlackAndWhite,
        Unit         = ImportUnit.Millimeter,
        Placement    = ImportPlacement.Origin,
        ThisViewOnly = true
    };
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Import DWG"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        ElementId id;
        doc.Import(dwgPath, opts, view, out id);
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── LINK DWG (live link) ──
public void LinkDWG(Document doc, string dwgPath, View view)
{
    var opts = new DWGImportOptions
    {
        Unit      = ImportUnit.Millimeter,
        Placement = ImportPlacement.Shared
    };
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Link DWG"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        ElementId id;
        doc.Link(dwgPath, opts, view, out id);
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

// ── AUDIT all CAD imports/links ──
public void AuditCAD(Document doc)
{
    var cads = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(ImportInstance))
        .Cast<ImportInstance>().ToList();

    foreach (var cad in cads)
    {
        bool   isLink = cad.IsLinked;
        string name   = cad.get_Parameter(
            BuiltInParameter.IMPORT_SYMBOL_SYMBOL_NAME)?.AsString() ?? "";
        CADLinkType lt = doc.GetElement(cad.GetTypeId()) as CADLinkType;
        string status = lt?.GetLinkedFileStatus().ToString() ?? "—";
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"CAD: {name} | Linked={isLink} | Status={status}");
    }
}

// ── EXPLODE import into Revit lines ──
public void ExplodeCAD(Document doc, ImportInstance cad)
{
    using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Explode CAD"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        ICollection<ElementId> ids =
            ImportInstance.ExplodeAndDelete(doc, cad.Id);
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Exploded → {ids.Count} elements");
        tx.Commit();
    }
}

Production-Grade

sections.title: INotifyPropertyChanged — ViewModelBase
sections.content:
InterfaceINotifyPropertyChanged — ViewModelBase
The foundation of WPF data binding. Any property change on the ViewModel automatically updates the bound WPF control. Implement once in a base class, inherit everywhere.
🟢 ViewModelBase + CallerMemberName pattern
// ViewModelBase.cs — implement once, inherit everywhere
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;

namespace StudioTools.ViewModels
{
    /// Base class for all ViewModels.
    /// Inherit this to get automatic WPF data binding.
    public abstract class ViewModelBase : INotifyPropertyChanged
    {
        public event PropertyChangedEventHandler PropertyChanged;

        // Call this inside any property setter to notify WPF
        protected void OnPropertyChanged(
            [CallerMemberName] string name = null)
            => PropertyChanged?.Invoke(this,
                new PropertyChangedEventArgs(name));

        // Set backing field and raise event in one call
        protected bool SetField<T>(ref T field, T value,
            [CallerMemberName] string name = null)
        {
            if (EqualityComparer<T>.Default.Equals(field, value)) return false;
            field = value;
            OnPropertyChanged(name);
            return true;
        }
    }
}

// ── Usage in a ViewModel ──
public class RoomInspectorViewModel : ViewModelBase
{
    private string _roomName;
    private double _roomArea;
    private bool   _isLoading;

    // WPF binds to these properties
    public string RoomName
    {
        get => _roomName;
        set => SetField(ref _roomName, value);  // auto raises PropertyChanged
    }

    public double RoomArea
    {
        get => _roomArea;
        set => SetField(ref _roomArea, value);
    }

    public bool IsLoading
    {
        get => _isLoading;
        set
        {
            SetField(ref _isLoading, value);
            OnPropertyChanged(nameof(LoadingVisibility));  // update derived
        }
    }

    // Computed property — no backing field, notified manually
    public System.Windows.Visibility LoadingVisibility
        => IsLoading ? System.Windows.Visibility.Visible
                     : System.Windows.Visibility.Collapsed;

    // Formatted display — recomputed when RoomArea changes
    public string RoomAreaDisplay => $"{RoomArea:F1} m²";
}

sections.title: RelayCommand — ICommand Implementation
sections.content:
ClassRelayCommand — ICommand Implementation
ICommand implementation that binds WPF buttons to ViewModel methods. RelayCommand wraps an Action (execute) and optional Func<bool> (canExecute). When canExecute returns false, the button is automatically disabled.
🟢 RelayCommand + ViewModel button binding
// RelayCommand.cs + ViewModel binding + XAML
// ── RelayCommand.cs ──
using System.Windows.Input;

public class RelayCommand : ICommand
{
    private readonly Action<object>       _execute;
    private readonly Func<object, bool> _canExecute;

    public RelayCommand(Action<object> execute,
        Func<object, bool> canExecute = null)
    {
        _execute    = execute    ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(execute));
        _canExecute = canExecute;
    }

    public event EventHandler CanExecuteChanged
    {
        add    => System.Windows.Input.CommandManager.RequerySuggested += value;
        remove => System.Windows.Input.CommandManager.RequerySuggested -= value;
    }

    public bool   CanExecute(object p) => _canExecute?.Invoke(p) ?? true;
    public void   Execute(object p)    => _execute(p);

    // Force WPF to recheck CanExecute on all commands
    public static void RaiseCanExecuteChanged()
        => System.Windows.Input.CommandManager.InvalidateRequerySuggested();
}

// Parameterless shortcut
public class RelayCommand<T> : ICommand
{
    private readonly Action<T>       _execute;
    private readonly Func<T, bool> _canExecute;
    public event EventHandler CanExecuteChanged
    {
        add    => CommandManager.RequerySuggested += value;
        remove => CommandManager.RequerySuggested -= value;
    }
    public RelayCommand(Action<T> exec, Func<T,bool> canExec = null)
    { _execute = exec; _canExecute = canExec; }
    public bool CanExecute(object p)
        => _canExecute?.Invoke((T)p) ?? true;
    public void Execute(object p) => _execute((T)p);
}

// ── ViewModel with commands ──
public class RoomInspectorViewModel : ViewModelBase
{
    private ObservableCollection<RoomData> _rooms
        = new ObservableCollection<RoomData>();
    private RoomData _selectedRoom;
    private bool _hasRooms;

    public ObservableCollection<RoomData> Rooms
    {
        get => _rooms;
        set => SetField(ref _rooms, value);
    }

    public RoomData SelectedRoom
    {
        get => _selectedRoom;
        set
        {
            SetField(ref _selectedRoom, value);
            // Notify that ExportCommand.CanExecute may have changed
            RelayCommand.RaiseCanExecuteChanged();
        }
    }

    // Commands bound to buttons in XAML
    public ICommand RefreshCommand { get; }
    public ICommand ExportCommand  { get; }
    public ICommand SelectInRevit  { get; }

    public RoomInspectorViewModel()
    {
        RefreshCommand = new RelayCommand(_ => OnRefresh());
        ExportCommand  = new RelayCommand(
            _ => OnExport(),
            _ => SelectedRoom != null);  // disabled if nothing selected
        SelectInRevit  = new RelayCommand(
            _ => OnSelectInRevit(),
            _ => SelectedRoom != null);
    }

    private void OnRefresh()
    {
        // Raise external event to load rooms from Revit on the main thread
        StudioApplication.LoadRoomsEvent.Raise();
    }

    private void OnExport()
    {
        // Export selected room data — no Revit API needed
        System.Windows.MessageBox.Show(
            $"Exporting {SelectedRoom?.Name}");
    }

    private void OnSelectInRevit()
    {
        StudioApplication.SelectElementHandler.ElementId =
            SelectedRoom?.RevitId;
        StudioApplication.SelectElementEvent.Raise();
    }

    // Called by ExternalEventHandler after Revit loads data
    public void UpdateRooms(IEnumerable<RoomData> rooms)
    {
        Rooms.Clear();
        foreach (var r in rooms) Rooms.Add(r);
    }
}

/* ── XAML (RoomInspectorPane.xaml) ──
<UserControl x:Class="StudioTools.Views.RoomInspectorPane"
             DataContext="{Binding Source={x:Static vm:ViewModelLocator.RoomInspector}}">
    <Grid>
        <Button Content="Refresh"
                Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"/>
        <ListView ItemsSource="{Binding Rooms}"
                  SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedRoom, Mode=TwoWay}">
            <ListView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <StackPanel Orientation="Horizontal">
                        <TextBlock Text="{Binding Number}" Width="50"/>
                        <TextBlock Text="{Binding Name}"   Width="150"/>
                        <TextBlock Text="{Binding AreaDisplay}"/>
                    </StackPanel>
                </DataTemplate>
            </ListView.ItemTemplate>
        </ListView>
        <Button Content="Export"
                Command="{Binding ExportCommand}"
                IsEnabled="{Binding ExportCommand, Converter={...CanExecuteConverter}}"/>
    </Grid>
</UserControl>
*/

sections.title: MVVM + Revit ExternalEvent Bridge
sections.content:
PatternMVVM + Revit ExternalEvent Bridge
The complete pattern connecting a WPF ViewModel to Revit's API via ExternalEvents. The ViewModel never touches the Revit API directly — it only calls ExternalEvent.Raise(). The handler reads/writes Revit, then updates the ViewModel on the main WPF thread.
🟢 Full MVVM + Revit Bridge
// Complete MVVM bridge — ViewModel → ExternalEvent → Revit → ViewModel
// ── RoomData.cs (Model — pure C#, no Revit refs) ──
public class RoomData
{
    public ElementId  RevitId    { get; set; }
    public string     Name       { get; set; }
    public string     Number     { get; set; }
    public string     Department { get; set; }
    public double     AreaSqM    { get; set; }
    public string     Level      { get; set; }
    public string     AreaDisplay => $"{AreaSqM:F1} m²";
}

// ── LoadRoomsHandler.cs (IExternalEventHandler) ──
public class LoadRoomsHandler : IExternalEventHandler
{
    private readonly RoomInspectorViewModel _vm;

    public LoadRoomsHandler(RoomInspectorViewModel vm) => _vm = vm;
    public string GetName() => "Load Rooms";

    public void Execute(UIApplication uiapp)
    {
        Document doc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
        if (doc == null) return;

        // Query Revit on the main thread
        var rooms = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
            .Cast<Room>()
            .Where(r => r.Area > 0)
            .Select(r => new RoomData
            {
                RevitId    = r.Id,
                Name       = r.Name,
                Number     = r.Number,
                Department = r.get_Parameter(
                    BuiltInParameter.ROOM_DEPARTMENT)?.AsString() ?? "",
                AreaSqM    = r.Area * 0.092903,
                Level      = r.Level?.Name ?? ""
            })
            .OrderBy(r => r.Number)
            .ToList();

        // Update ViewModel on the UI dispatcher thread
        System.Windows.Application.Current?.Dispatcher.Invoke(() =>
            _vm.UpdateRooms(rooms));
    }
}

// ── SelectElementHandler.cs ──
public class SelectElementHandler : IExternalEventHandler
{
    public ElementId ElementId { get; set; }
    public string GetName() => "Select Element";
    public void Execute(UIApplication uiapp)
    {
        if (ElementId == null) return;
        var uidoc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument;
        uidoc?.Selection.SetElementIds(
            new List<ElementId> { ElementId });
        uidoc?.ShowElements(ElementId);
    }
}

// ── Application.cs — wire everything up ──
public class StudioApplication : IExternalApplication
{
    public static ExternalEvent         LoadRoomsEvent;
    public static ExternalEvent         SelectElementEvent;
    public static SelectElementHandler  SelectElementHandler;
    public static RoomInspectorViewModel ViewModel;

    public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        ViewModel = new RoomInspectorViewModel();

        // Create events BEFORE registering pane
        LoadRoomsEvent     = ExternalEvent.Create(new LoadRoomsHandler(ViewModel));
        SelectElementHandler = new SelectElementHandler();
        SelectElementEvent   = ExternalEvent.Create(SelectElementHandler);

        // Register pane (WPF UserControl uses ViewModel)
        var paneId = new DockablePaneId(new Guid("AABB1122-3344-5566-ROOM-INSP00000001"));
        var pane   = new RoomInspectorPane(ViewModel);
        app.RegisterDockablePane(paneId, "Room Inspector", pane);

        return Result.Succeeded;
    }

    public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        LoadRoomsEvent?.Dispose();
        SelectElementEvent?.Dispose();
        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
}

sections.title: RevitTestFramework Project Setup
sections.content:
SetupRevitTestFramework Project Setup
RevitTestFramework (RTF) runs NUnit tests inside Revit process. Your test DLL is loaded into Revit, and each test method gets a real Document context. Tests can create elements, read parameters, and validate API behaviour against real Revit geometry.
🟢 Project setup + writing tests + running
// StudioTools.Tests.csproj + test fixtures
<!-- StudioTools.Tests.csproj -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net48</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="NUnit" Version="3.13.3"/>
    <PackageReference Include="RevitTestFramework" Version="3.0.0"/>
    <Reference Include="RevitAPI">
      <HintPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2024\RevitAPI.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
    <!-- Reference your main plugin DLL -->
    <ProjectReference Include="..\StudioTools\StudioTools.csproj"/>
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

// ── WallCountTests.cs ──
using NUnit.Framework;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using RevitTestFramework;

[TestFixture]
public class WallCountTests
{
    // TestModel attribute loads a specific .rvt file for this test
    [Test]
    [TestModel(@"TestModels\SampleBuilding.rvt")]
    public void WallCount_SampleBuilding_Returns47Walls()
    {
        // RTF injects the Document via RevitTestFramework.RevitTestBase
        Document doc = RevitTestBase.CurrentDocument;
        Assert.IsNotNull(doc);

        int wallCount = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).GetElementCount();

        Assert.AreEqual(47, wallCount,
            "Expected 47 walls in sample building");
    }

    [Test]
    [TestModel(@"TestModels\SampleBuilding.rvt")]
    public void ReinforcBeam_CreatesCorrectBarCount()
    {
        Document doc = RevitTestBase.CurrentDocument;

        // Get the first beam in the test model
        FamilyInstance beam = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_StructuralFraming)
            .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
            .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
            .FirstOrDefault(fi => fi.StructuralType == StructuralType.Beam);

        Assert.IsNotNull(beam, "No beam found in test model");

        int rebarBefore = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
