            .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar)
            .GetElementCount();

        // Run the reinforcement command
        var cmd = new StudioTools.Commands.ReinforcBeamCommand();
        cmd.ReinforceSingleBeam(doc, beam);

        int rebarAfter = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rebar)
            .GetElementCount();

        // Expect: 3 bottom + 2 top + ~10 stirrups = 15 bars minimum
        Assert.Greater(rebarAfter - rebarBefore, 14,
            "Expected at least 15 rebar elements after reinforcing beam");
    }

    [Test]
    [TestModel(@"TestModels\Empty.rvt")]
    public void CreateWall_ValidLine_ReturnsWallElement()
    {
        Document doc = RevitTestBase.CurrentDocument;

        Level level = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

        Wall wall = null;
        using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Test Wall"))
        {
            tx.Start();
            wall = Wall.Create(doc,
                Line.CreateBound(XYZ.Zero, new XYZ(10,0,0)),
                level.Id, false);
            tx.Commit();
        }

        Assert.IsNotNull(wall);
        Assert.AreEqual(ElementId.InvalidElementId, wall.LevelId == null
            ? ElementId.InvalidElementId : level.Id);
    }
}

// ── Unit tests for pure C# logic (no Revit needed) ──
[TestFixture]
public class ViewModelTests
{
    [Test]
    public void ViewModel_UpdateRooms_RaisesPropertyChanged()
    {
        var vm = new RoomInspectorViewModel();
        bool raised = false;
        vm.PropertyChanged += (s, e) =>
        {
            if (e.PropertyName == nameof(vm.Rooms)) raised = true;
        };

        vm.UpdateRooms(new[] {
            new RoomData { Name = "Test Room", AreaSqM = 25 }
        });

        Assert.IsTrue(raised);
        Assert.AreEqual(1, vm.Rooms.Count);
    }
}

sections.title: Multi-Version .csproj — One Codebase, Many Revit Versions
sections.content:
PatternMulti-Version .csproj — One Codebase, Many Revit Versions
🟢 Multi-target .csproj + conditional compilation
// StudioTools.csproj — build for Revit 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025
<!-- Multi-target: builds 4 DLLs, one per Revit version -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <!-- Semicolon-separated list of target frameworks -->
    <TargetFrameworks>net48-Revit2022;net48-Revit2023;net48-Revit2024;net48-Revit2025</TargetFrameworks>
    <AssemblyName>StudioTools</AssemblyName>
    <RootNamespace>StudioTools</RootNamespace>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Per-version RevitAPI reference + defines -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(TargetFramework.Contains('Revit2022'))">
    <DefineConstants>REVIT2022</DefineConstants>
    <RevitVersion>2022</RevitVersion>
    <RevitApiPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2022</RevitApiPath>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(TargetFramework.Contains('Revit2023'))">
    <DefineConstants>REVIT2023</DefineConstants>
    <RevitVersion>2023</RevitVersion>
    <RevitApiPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2023</RevitApiPath>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(TargetFramework.Contains('Revit2024'))">
    <DefineConstants>REVIT2024</DefineConstants>
    <RevitVersion>2024</RevitVersion>
    <RevitApiPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2024</RevitApiPath>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(TargetFramework.Contains('Revit2025'))">
    <DefineConstants>REVIT2025</DefineConstants>
    <RevitVersion>2025</RevitVersion>
    <RevitApiPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2025</RevitApiPath>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="RevitAPI">
      <HintPath>$(RevitApiPath)\RevitAPI.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
    <Reference Include="RevitAPIUI">
      <HintPath>$(RevitApiPath)\RevitAPIUI.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Output each version to its own folder -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputPath>bin\$(RevitVersion)\</OutputPath>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

// ── Use #if in code for version-specific API differences ──
public void DoVersionSpecificWork(Document doc)
{
#if REVIT2025
    // Revit 2025 has new generic GetElement<T>
    var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>();
#elif REVIT2024
    // Revit 2024 specific pattern
    var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>();
#else
    // Revit 2022/2023 fallback
    var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>();
#endif
}

sections.title: Per-Version .addin Manifest Files
sections.content:
PatternPer-Version .addin Manifest Files
🟢 Deploy .addin for each Revit version
// Folder structure for multi-version deployment
// ── Installation folder structure ──
// C:\Program Files\StudioTools\
//   ├── 2022\
//   │   └── StudioTools.dll        ← built for Revit 2022
//   ├── 2023\
//   │   └── StudioTools.dll        ← built for Revit 2023
//   ├── 2024\
//   │   └── StudioTools.dll        ← built for Revit 2024
//   └── 2025\
//       └── StudioTools.dll        ← built for Revit 2025
//
// %AppData%\Autodesk\Revit\Addins\2022\StudioTools.addin
// %AppData%\Autodesk\Revit\Addins\2023\StudioTools.addin
// %AppData%\Autodesk\Revit\Addins\2024\StudioTools.addin
// %AppData%\Autodesk\Revit\Addins\2025\StudioTools.addin

// ── StudioTools_2024.addin ──
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RevitAddIns>
  <AddIn Type="Application">
    <AddInId>AABB1122-3344-5566-7788-CCDD9900AABB</AddInId>
    <Name>Studio Tools</Name>
    <FullClassName>StudioTools.StudioApplication</FullClassName>
    <!-- Version-specific DLL path -->
    <Assembly>C:\Program Files\StudioTools\2024\StudioTools.dll</Assembly>
    <VendorId>STUDIO</VendorId>
    <VendorDescription>Studio Architecture, www.studio.com</VendorDescription>
  </AddIn>
</RevitAddIns>

// ── PowerShell script: generate .addin files for all versions ──
$versions = @("2022", "2023", "2024", "2025")
$installRoot = "C:\Program Files\StudioTools"
$addinRoot = "$env:APPDATA\Autodesk\Revit\Addins"
$guid = "AABB1122-3344-5566-7788-CCDD9900AABB"

foreach ($ver in $versions) {
    $dllPath = "$installRoot\$ver\StudioTools.dll"
    $addinDir = "$addinRoot\$ver"
    $addinFile = "$addinDir\StudioTools.addin"

    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $addinDir | Out-Null

    $content = @"
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RevitAddIns>
  <AddIn Type="Application">
    <AddInId>$guid</AddInId>
    <Name>Studio Tools $ver</Name>
    <FullClassName>StudioTools.StudioApplication</FullClassName>
    <Assembly>$dllPath</Assembly>
    <VendorId>STUDIO</VendorId>
  </AddIn>
</RevitAddIns>
"@
    Set-Content -Path $addinFile -Value $content
    Write-Host "Created: $addinFile"
}

sections.title: Auto-Update Check on Startup
sections.content:
PatternAuto-Update Check on Startup
🟢 Check GitHub releases for new version on startup
// Check latest version from GitHub API + prompt user to update
public static class UpdateChecker
{
    private const string CURRENT_VERSION    = "2.1.0";
    private const string GITHUB_RELEASES_URL =
        "https://api.github.com/repos/yourstudio/studiotools/releases/latest";

    /// Check for updates asynchronously — called from ApplicationInitialized event
    public static async void CheckAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            using var client = new System.Net.Http.HttpClient();
            client.DefaultRequestHeaders.UserAgent.ParseAdd("StudioTools/2.1.0");

            string json = await client.GetStringAsync(GITHUB_RELEASES_URL);
            dynamic release = Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert
                .DeserializeObject(json);

            string latestTag = (string)release.tag_name;  // e.g. "v2.2.0"
            string latestVer = latestTag.TrimStart('v');

            if (new Version(latestVer) > new Version(CURRENT_VERSION))
            {
                string notes = (string)release.body ?? "";
                string url   = (string)release.html_url;

                // Show on UI thread
                System.Windows.Application.Current?.Dispatcher.Invoke(() =>
                {
                    var result = System.Windows.MessageBox.Show(
                        $"Studio Tools v{latestVer} is available!\n\n" +
                        $"You have v{CURRENT_VERSION}.\n\n" +
                        $"Changes:\n{notes}\n\n" +
                        "Download now?",
                        "Update Available",
                        System.Windows.MessageBoxButton.YesNo,
                        System.Windows.MessageBoxImage.Information);

                    if (result == System.Windows.MessageBoxResult.Yes)
                        System.Diagnostics.Process.Start(url);
                });
            }
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            // Silently ignore — don't crash plugin if network unavailable
            System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
                $"Update check failed: {ex.Message}");
        }
    }
}

// ── Call from OnStartup ──
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    // Subscribe to ApplicationInitialized (fires after all addins load)
    app.ControlledApplication.ApplicationInitialized +=
        (s, e) => UpdateChecker.CheckAsync();
    return Result.Succeeded;
}

sections.title: Serilog — Structured Logging Setup
sections.content:
LibrarySerilog — Structured Logging Setup
🟢 Complete Serilog setup + log patterns + crash handler
// Logger.cs — global logger + crash handler
using Serilog;
using Serilog.Events;

public static class PluginLogger
{
    public static ILogger Log { get; private set; }

    public static void Initialise(string revitVersion)
    {
        string logDir = System.IO.Path.Combine(
            System.Environment.GetFolderPath(
                System.Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData),
            "StudioTools", "Logs");

        System.IO.Directory.CreateDirectory(logDir);

        Log = new LoggerConfiguration()
            // Minimum level
            .MinimumLevel.Information()
            .MinimumLevel.Override("Microsoft", LogEventLevel.Warning)

            // Add context to every message
            .Enrich.WithProperty("RevitVersion", revitVersion)
            .Enrich.WithProperty("PluginVersion", "2.1.0")
            .Enrich.WithProperty("MachineName", System.Environment.MachineName)

            // Rolling file: new file per day, keep 30 days
            .WriteTo.File(
                path: System.IO.Path.Combine(logDir, "StudioTools_.log"),
                rollingInterval: RollingInterval.Day,
                retainedFileCountLimit: 30,
                outputTemplate:
                    "{Timestamp:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff} [{Level:u3}] {Message:lj} {Properties}{NewLine}{Exception}")

            // Also write to Debug output in VS
            .WriteTo.Debug()
            .CreateLogger();

        Log.Information("=== Studio Tools started. Revit {RevitVersion} ===", revitVersion);
    }

    public static void Shutdown()
    {
        Log.Information("=== Studio Tools shutting down ===");
        Serilog.Log.CloseAndFlush();
    }
}

// ── Usage in commands ──
public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd, ref string msg, ElementSet els)
{
    PluginLogger.Log.Information("ReinforcBeam command started");
    try
    {
        // ... work ...
        PluginLogger.Log.Information(
            "ReinforcBeam completed. Beams reinforced: {Count}", 3);
        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        PluginLogger.Log.Error(ex,
            "ReinforcBeam failed. Document: {DocTitle}",
            cd.Application.ActiveUIDocument?.Document?.Title);
        msg = $"Error: {ex.Message}\nCheck log: %AppData%\\StudioTools\\Logs\\";
        return Result.Failed;
    }
}

// ── Global crash handler in Application.OnStartup ──
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    PluginLogger.Initialise(app.ControlledApplication.VersionName);

    // Catch ALL unhandled exceptions — last resort
    AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += (s, e) =>
    {
        var ex = e.ExceptionObject as Exception;
        PluginLogger.Log.Fatal(ex,
            "UNHANDLED EXCEPTION. IsTerminating={IsTerminating}",
            e.IsTerminating);
        Serilog.Log.CloseAndFlush();
    };

    // Catch unhandled exceptions on background threads
    System.Threading.Tasks.TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += (s, e) =>
    {
        PluginLogger.Log.Error(e.Exception,
            "Unobserved Task exception");
        e.SetObserved();  // prevent crash
    };

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

// Log levels:
// Log.Verbose("Very detailed debug info")      — development only
// Log.Debug("Debug info")                      — development
// Log.Information("Normal operation event")    — production events
// Log.Warning("Unexpected but recoverable")    — warnings
// Log.Error(ex, "Operation failed")            — recoverable errors
// Log.Fatal(ex, "Crash")                       — unrecoverable

sections.title: HttpClient — REST API Integration
sections.content:
PatternHttpClient — REST API Integration
Call external REST APIs from a Revit plugin. Always use async/await with Task.Run to avoid blocking the Revit UI thread. Bridge back to Revit using ExternalEvent.Raise() after the async operation completes.
🟢 POST room data + GET configuration from REST API
// ApiService.cs — thread-safe HTTP client singleton
using System.Net.Http;
using System.Net.Http.Headers;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

// ── Singleton HttpClient (reuse — never new up per request) ──
public static class ApiService
{
    private static readonly HttpClient _client;
    private const string BASE_URL = "https://api.yoursystem.com/v1";

    static ApiService()
    {
        _client = new HttpClient();
        _client.BaseAddress = new Uri(BASE_URL);
        _client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Accept.Add(
            new MediaTypeWithQualityHeaderValue("application/json"));
        _client.Timeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30);
    }

    // ── POST room data to external facilities management system ──
    public static async Task<bool> PostRoomsAsync(
        IList<RoomData> rooms, string apiKey)
    {
        try
        {
            _client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Authorization =
                new AuthenticationHeaderValue("Bearer", apiKey);

            var payload = new
            {
                project  = "Building A",
                exported = DateTime.UtcNow,
                rooms    = rooms.Select(r => new
                {
                    id         = r.RevitId?.IntegerValue,
                    name       = r.Name,
                    number     = r.Number,
                    area_sqm   = r.AreaSqM,
                    level      = r.Level,
                    department = r.Department
                })
            };

            string json    = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(payload);
            var    content  = new StringContent(json, Encoding.UTF8, "application/json");
            HttpResponseMessage resp = await _client.PostAsync("/rooms", content);

            resp.EnsureSuccessStatusCode();
            PluginLogger.Log.Information(
                "Posted {Count} rooms to API. Status: {Status}",
                rooms.Count, resp.StatusCode);
            return true;
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            PluginLogger.Log.Error(ex, "Failed to POST rooms to API");
            return false;
        }
    }

    // ── GET config from API ──
    public static async Task<PluginConfig> GetConfigAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            string json = await _client.GetStringAsync("/config/studiotools");
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<PluginConfig>(json);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            PluginLogger.Log.Warning(ex, "Could not fetch config from API, using defaults");
            return new PluginConfig();  // return defaults
        }
    }
}

// ── Call from a command using ExternalEvent bridge ──
public class ExportToApiHandler : IExternalEventHandler
{
    public string GetName() => "Export to API";

    public void Execute(UIApplication uiapp)
    {
        Document doc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
        if (doc == null) return;

        // 1. Read room data from Revit (on main thread)
        var rooms = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
            .Cast<Room>()
            .Where(r => r.Area > 0)
            .Select(r => new RoomData
            {
                RevitId = r.Id, Name = r.Name,
                Number = r.Number, AreaSqM = r.Area * 0.092903
            }).ToList();

        // 2. POST async on background thread (don't block Revit)
        Task.Run(async () =>
        {
            bool ok = await ApiService.PostRoomsAsync(
                rooms, PluginSettings.Current.ApiKey);

            System.Windows.Application.Current?.Dispatcher.Invoke(() =>
                System.Windows.MessageBox.Show(
                    ok ? $"Exported {rooms.Count} rooms successfully."
                       : "Export failed. Check log for details."));
        });
    }
}

sections.title: SQLite — Local Data Cache
sections.content:
LibrarySQLite — Local Data Cache
Embed a SQLite database in your plugin for local caching, audit logs, user history, or offline data. Microsoft.Data.Sqlite is lightweight and requires no installation. The .db file lives in %AppData%\YourPlugin\.
🟢 SQLite setup + CRUD operations
// LocalDatabase.cs — SQLite local cache
using Microsoft.Data.Sqlite;

public class LocalDatabase
{
    private readonly string _connectionString;

    public LocalDatabase()
    {
        string dbDir = System.IO.Path.Combine(
            System.Environment.GetFolderPath(
                System.Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData),
            "StudioTools");
        System.IO.Directory.CreateDirectory(dbDir);

        string dbPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(dbDir, "studiotools.db");
        _connectionString = $"Data Source={dbPath}";
        InitialiseSchema();
    }

    private void InitialiseSchema()
    {
        using var conn = new SqliteConnection(_connectionString);
        conn.Open();

        // Create tables if they don't exist
        using var cmd = conn.CreateCommand();
        cmd.CommandText = @"
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ExportHistory (
                Id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                ProjectName TEXT    NOT NULL,
                ExportedAt  TEXT    NOT NULL,
                ItemCount   INTEGER NOT NULL,
                ExportType  TEXT    NOT NULL
            );

            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS RoomCache (
                RevitId     INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                ProjectPath TEXT    NOT NULL,
                Name        TEXT,
                Number      TEXT,
                AreaSqM     REAL,
                Department  TEXT,
                CachedAt    TEXT    NOT NULL
            );";
        cmd.ExecuteNonQuery();
    }

    // ── INSERT export history record ──
    public void LogExport(string project, int count, string type)
    {
        using var conn = new SqliteConnection(_connectionString);
        conn.Open();
        using var cmd = conn.CreateCommand();
        cmd.CommandText = 
            "INSERT INTO ExportHistory (ProjectName, ExportedAt, ItemCount, ExportType)
             VALUES (@p, @d, @c, @t)";
        cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@p", project);
        cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@d", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o"));
        cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@c", count);
        cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@t", type);
        cmd.ExecuteNonQuery();
    }

    // ── SELECT recent export history ──
    public List<(string Project, DateTime When, int Count, string Type)>
        GetRecentExports(int limit = 20)
    {
        var result = new List<(string, DateTime, int, string)>();
        using var conn = new SqliteConnection(_connectionString);
        conn.Open();
        using var cmd = conn.CreateCommand();
        cmd.CommandText = 
            "SELECT ProjectName, ExportedAt, ItemCount, ExportType
             FROM ExportHistory ORDER BY Id DESC LIMIT @limit";
        cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@limit", limit);

        using var reader = cmd.ExecuteReader();
        while (reader.Read())
            result.Add((
                reader.GetString(0),
                DateTime.Parse(reader.GetString(1)),
                reader.GetInt32(2),
                reader.GetString(3)));

        return result;
    }

    // ── Cache rooms from a project ──
    public void CacheRooms(string projectPath,
        IEnumerable<RoomData> rooms)
    {
        using var conn = new SqliteConnection(_connectionString);
        conn.Open();
        using var txn = conn.BeginTransaction();

        foreach (var r in rooms)
        {
            using var cmd = conn.CreateCommand();
            cmd.Transaction = txn;
            cmd.CommandText = @"
                INSERT OR REPLACE INTO RoomCache
                    (RevitId,ProjectPath,Name,Number,AreaSqM,Department,CachedAt)
                VALUES (@id,@pp,@n,@nu,@a,@d,@ca)";
            cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@id", r.RevitId?.IntegerValue ?? 0);
            cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@pp", projectPath);
            cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@n",  r.Name ?? "");
            cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@nu", r.Number ?? "");
            cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@a",  r.AreaSqM);
            cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@d",  r.Department ?? "");
            cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@ca", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o"));
            cmd.ExecuteNonQuery();
        }
        txn.Commit();
    }
}

sections.title: ClosedXML — Excel Read/Write
sections.content:
LibraryClosedXML — Excel Read/Write
Read Excel templates (room schedules, specification sheets) and write Revit data to Excel. ClosedXML works with .xlsx files without needing Excel installed. Requires ClosedXML NuGet package.
🟢 Read Excel data → write to Revit + Export Revit data → Excel
// ExcelService.cs — read spec sheet + export room schedule
using ClosedXML.Excel;

public static class ExcelService
{
    // ── READ: Import room data from Excel template ──
    public static List<RoomData> ReadRoomSchedule(string xlsxPath)
    {
        var result = new List<RoomData>();

        using var wb = new XLWorkbook(xlsxPath);
        var ws = wb.Worksheet(1);  // first sheet

        // Find header row — look for "Room Number" header
        int startRow = 2;  // row 1 = headers
        int lastRow  = ws.LastRowUsed()?.RowNumber() ?? 1;

        for (int row = startRow; row <= lastRow; row++)
        {
            string number = ws.Cell(row, 1).GetString();
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(number)) continue;

            result.Add(new RoomData
            {
                Number     = number,
                Name       = ws.Cell(row, 2).GetString(),
                Department = ws.Cell(row, 3).GetString(),
                AreaSqM    = ws.Cell(row, 4).GetDouble()
            });
        }

        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Read {result.Count} rooms from {xlsxPath}");
        return result;
    }

    // ── WRITE: Export Revit rooms to formatted Excel ──
    public static void ExportRoomSchedule(
        IEnumerable<RoomData> rooms, string outputPath)
    {
        using var wb = new XLWorkbook();
        var ws = wb.Worksheets.Add("Room Schedule");

        // ── Header row ──
        string[] headers = { "Room No.", "Room Name", "Department",
                              "Level", "Area (m²)", "Revit ID" };
        for (int c = 0; c < headers.Length; c++)
        {
            var cell = ws.Cell(1, c + 1);
            cell.Value = headers[c];
            cell.Style.Font.Bold = true;
            cell.Style.Fill.SetBackgroundColor(XLColor.FromArgb(68, 114, 196));
            cell.Style.Font.SetFontColor(XLColor.White);
        }

        // ── Data rows ──
        int row = 2;
        foreach (var r in rooms)
        {
            ws.Cell(row, 1).Value = r.Number;
            ws.Cell(row, 2).Value = r.Name;
            ws.Cell(row, 3).Value = r.Department;
            ws.Cell(row, 4).Value = r.Level;
            ws.Cell(row, 5).Value = r.AreaSqM;
            ws.Cell(row, 5).Style.NumberFormat.Format = "0.00";
            ws.Cell(row, 6).Value = r.RevitId?.IntegerValue ?? 0;

            // Alternate row colour
            if (row % 2 == 0)
                ws.Row(row).Style.Fill.SetBackgroundColor(
                    XLColor.FromArgb(242, 242, 242));
            row++;
        }

        // ── Auto-fit columns + freeze header row ──
        ws.Columns().AdjustToContents();
        ws.SheetView.FreezeRows(1);

        // ── Add total row ──
        ws.Cell(row, 4).Value = "TOTAL";
        ws.Cell(row, 4).Style.Font.Bold = true;
        ws.Cell(row, 5).FormulaA1 = $"=SUM(E2:E{row-1})";
        ws.Cell(row, 5).Style.Font.Bold = true;

        wb.SaveAs(outputPath);
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(
            $"Exported {row-2} rooms to {outputPath}");
    }
}

sections.title: Progress Reporting + Cancellation for Long Operations
sections.content:
PatternProgress Reporting + Cancellation for Long Operations
Show a WPF progress bar during batch operations (e.g. reinforcing 200 beams, exporting 100 sheets) with a Cancel button. Uses IProgress<T> for thread-safe progress updates and CancellationToken for clean cancellation.
🟢 Progress bar + cancellation + WPF dialog
// ProgressViewModel.cs + batch operation with cancellation
// ── ProgressViewModel.cs ──
public class ProgressViewModel : ViewModelBase
{
    private int    _value;
    private int    _max = 100;
    private string _message;
    private bool   _isCancelled;

    public int    Value     { get => _value;   set => SetField(ref _value,   value); }
    public int    Maximum   { get => _max;     set => SetField(ref _max,     value); }
    public string Message   { get => _message; set => SetField(ref _message, value); }
    public bool   IsCancelled { get => _isCancelled; set => SetField(ref _isCancelled, value); }

    public string PercentText => $"{(Maximum > 0 ? Value * 100 / Maximum : 0)}%";

    public CancellationTokenSource CancelSource { get; } = new();
    public ICommand CancelCommand { get; }

    public ProgressViewModel()
    {
        CancelCommand = new RelayCommand(_ =>
        {
            CancelSource.Cancel();
            IsCancelled = true;
            Message = "Cancelling...";
        });
    }
}

// ── Batch operation handler with progress ──
public class BatchReinforcementHandler : IExternalEventHandler
{
    public IList<ElementId>   BeamIds      { get; set; }
    public ProgressViewModel ProgressVm   { get; set; }

    public string GetName() => "Batch Reinforce";

    public void Execute(UIApplication uiapp)
    {
        Document doc     = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument?.Document;
        var      ct      = ProgressVm.CancelSource.Token;
        int      total   = BeamIds.Count;
        int      done    = 0;

        // Update progress on UI thread
        void Report(string msg)
        {
            System.Windows.Application.Current?.Dispatcher.Invoke(() =>
            {
                ProgressVm.Value   = done;
                ProgressVm.Maximum = total;
                ProgressVm.Message = msg;
                OnPropertyChanged(nameof(ProgressVm.PercentText));
            });
        }

        Report("Starting reinforcement...");

        foreach (var id in BeamIds)
        {
            // Check cancellation before each beam
            if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) break;

            FamilyInstance beam = doc?.GetElement(id) as FamilyInstance;
            if (beam == null) continue;

            try
            {
                Report($"Reinforcing beam {done+1} of {total}: {beam.Name}");
                // Do the reinforcement (Book 17 methods)
                ReinforceSingleBeam(doc, beam);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                PluginLogger.Log.Warning(ex,
                    "Failed to reinforce beam {Id}", id);
            }
            finally { done++; }
        }

        System.Windows.Application.Current?.Dispatcher.Invoke(() =>
        {
            ProgressVm.Value   = done;
            ProgressVm.Message = ProgressVm.IsCancelled
                ? $"Cancelled after {done} beams"
                : $"Complete — {done} beams reinforced";
        });
    }
}

/* ── ProgressWindow.xaml ──
<Window Title="Processing..." Height="160" Width="400">
    <StackPanel Margin="20">
        <TextBlock Text="{Binding Message}" Margin="0,0,0,8"/>
        <ProgressBar Value="{Binding Value}" Maximum="{Binding Maximum}" Height="20"/>
        <TextBlock Text="{Binding PercentText}" HorizontalAlignment="Right" FontSize="11"/>
        <Button Content="Cancel" Command="{Binding CancelCommand}"
                HorizontalAlignment="Center" Margin="0,12,0,0" Width="80"
                IsEnabled="{Binding IsCancelled, Converter={...InverseBool}}"/>
    </StackPanel>
</Window>
*/

sections.title: Settings Persistence — JSON to %AppData%
sections.content:
PatternSettings Persistence — JSON to %AppData%
🟢 User settings persisted to JSON file
// PluginSettings.cs — load/save user preferences
public class PluginSettings
{
    // Settings properties with defaults
    public string   ApiKey          { get; set; } = "";
    public string   ApiBaseUrl      { get; set; } = "https://api.example.com";
    public bool     CheckUpdates    { get; set; } = true;
    public bool     UseMetric       { get; set; } = true;
    public double   DefaultCoverMm  { get; set; } = 35.0;
    public string   LastProjectPath { get; set; } = "";
    public DateTime LastExportDate  { get; set; } = DateTime.MinValue;

    // Singleton current settings
    public static PluginSettings Current { get; private set; } = new();

    private static string GetSettingsPath() =>
        System.IO.Path.Combine(
            System.Environment.GetFolderPath(
                System.Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData),
            "StudioTools", "settings.json");

    // Load from disk (call in OnStartup)
    public static void Load()
    {
        try
        {
            string path = GetSettingsPath();
            if (!System.IO.File.Exists(path))
            {
                Current = new PluginSettings();
                Save();  // create default settings file
                return;
            }
            string json = System.IO.File.ReadAllText(path);
            Current = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<PluginSettings>(json)
                   ?? new PluginSettings();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            PluginLogger.Log.Warning(ex, "Could not load settings, using defaults");
            Current = new PluginSettings();
        }
    }

    // Save to disk (call after any change)
    public static void Save()
    {
        try
        {
            string path = GetSettingsPath();
            System.IO.Directory.CreateDirectory(
                System.IO.Path.GetDirectoryName(path));
            string json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(Current, Formatting.Indented);
            System.IO.File.WriteAllText(path, json);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            PluginLogger.Log.Warning(ex, "Could not save settings");
        }
    }
}

// ── OnStartup ──
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    PluginLogger.Initialise(app.ControlledApplication.VersionName);
    PluginSettings.Load();
    return Result.Succeeded;
}

// ── OnShutdown ──
public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    PluginSettings.Save();
    PluginLogger.Shutdown();
    return Result.Succeeded;
}

sections.title: License Key Validation
sections.content:
PatternLicense Key Validation
Simple HMAC-based license key validation — no external server needed for offline license checks. For commercial plugins: generate keys tied to machine ID + expiry date, validate on startup.
🟢 Machine-locked license validation
// LicenseManager.cs — offline HMAC license validation
public static class LicenseManager
{
    private const string SECRET = "your-secret-key-change-this-in-production";

    // Get unique machine fingerprint
    public static string GetMachineId()
    {
        string cpu = System.Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("PROCESSOR_IDENTIFIER")
                  ?? System.Environment.MachineName;
        string user = System.Environment.UserName;
        string raw  = $"{cpu}|{user}";
        using var sha = System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256.Create();
        var hash = sha.ComputeHash(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(raw));
        return Convert.ToBase64String(hash)[..16];
    }

    // Generate a license key (run server-side when customer buys)
    public static string GenerateKey(string machineId, DateTime expiry)
    {
        string data = $"{machineId}|{expiry:yyyyMMdd}";
        using var hmac = new System.Security.Cryptography.HMACSHA256(
            Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(SECRET));
        var hash = hmac.ComputeHash(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(data));
        string sig = Convert.ToBase64String(hash)[..16];
        return $"{machineId}|{expiry:yyyyMMdd}|{sig}";
    }

    // Validate a license key (run on every startup)
    public static (bool IsValid, string Reason, DateTime Expiry) Validate(string licenseKey)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(licenseKey))
            return (false, "No license key provided", DateTime.MinValue);

        try
        {
            string[] parts = licenseKey.Split('|');
            if (parts.Length != 3)
                return (false, "Invalid key format", DateTime.MinValue);

            string keyMachineId = parts[0];
            string expiryStr    = parts[1];
            string signature    = parts[2];

            // Check machine lock
            string myMachineId = GetMachineId();
            if (keyMachineId != myMachineId)
                return (false, "License is not valid for this machine", DateTime.MinValue);

            // Verify HMAC signature
            string data = $"{keyMachineId}|{expiryStr}";
            using var hmac = new System.Security.Cryptography.HMACSHA256(
                Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(SECRET));
            var    hash       = hmac.ComputeHash(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(data));
            string expectedSig = Convert.ToBase64String(hash)[..16];
            if (signature != expectedSig)
                return (false, "Invalid license signature", DateTime.MinValue);

            // Check expiry
            DateTime expiry = DateTime.ParseExact(
                expiryStr, "yyyyMMdd", null);
            if (DateTime.Today > expiry)
                return (false,
                    $"License expired on {expiry:dd MMM yyyy}", expiry);

            return (true,
                $"Valid until {expiry:dd MMM yyyy}", expiry);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            return (false, $"License error: {ex.Message}", DateTime.MinValue);
        }
    }
}

// ── Check on startup ──
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    var (isValid, reason, expiry) = LicenseManager.Validate(
        PluginSettings.Current.LicenseKey);

    if (!isValid)
    {
        TaskDialog.Show("Studio Tools — License",
            $"License validation failed:\n{reason}\n\n" +
            "Please contact support@yourstudio.com");
        // Return Succeeded but disable commands via IExternalCommandAvailability
        MyApp.IsLicensed = false;
    }
    else
    {
        MyApp.IsLicensed = true;
        // Warn if expiring within 30 days
        if ((expiry - DateTime.Today).TotalDays < 30)
            TaskDialog.Show("License Expiry",
                $"Your license expires in {(expiry-DateTime.Today).Days} days.\n" +
                "Please renew at https://yourstudio.com/renew");
    }
    return Result.Succeeded;
}

desc: Building shippable plugins: WPF/MVVM, Unit Testing, Deployment, Logging, REST/SQLite, and professional UX patterns.

title: Geometry Elements

hero.label: // Book 02 of 19 — Geometry Elements
hero.title:
Walls, Floors, Roofs,
Levels & Instances
hero.description: Create, modify and read every major geometric element in the Revit model — from walls and floors to family instances and openings.

sections.title: XYZ, Vectors & Transform
sections.content:
// 01

XYZ, Vectors & Transform

XYZ is used for both points and vectors. Transform handles rotation, translation and scaling.

C#
// Creating points and vectors
XYZ origin  = XYZ.Zero;                         // (0,0,0)
XYZ pt      = new XYZ(3.0, 0.0, 0.0);          // in feet!
XYZ up      = XYZ.BasisZ;                        // (0,0,1)

// Vector operations
XYZ    a      = new XYZ(1,0,0);
XYZ    b      = new XYZ(0,1,0);
double dot    = a.DotProduct(b);                // scalar product
XYZ    cross  = a.CrossProduct(b);              // perpendicular
XYZ    norm   = a.Normalize();                   // unit vector
double len    = a.GetLength();
double dist   = a.DistanceTo(b);

// Transform (position + orientation)
Transform t = Transform.Identity;
t.Origin     = new XYZ(5, 0, 0);
t.BasisX     = XYZ.BasisX;
t.BasisY     = XYZ.BasisY;
t.BasisZ     = XYZ.BasisZ;

// Create rotation transform
Transform rot = Transform.CreateRotation(
    XYZ.BasisZ, Math.PI / 2);          // 90° around Z

// Line creation
Line line  = Line.CreateBound(pt, pt + new XYZ(10,0,0));

sections.title: Walls
sections.content:
// 02

Walls

There are four wall kinds: Basic, Stacked, Curtain, Unknown. The API works best with Basic walls.

WallKindDescriptionAPI Support
BasicStandard single-layer or compound wallFull — create, modify, read
StackedMultiple walls stacked verticallyLimited — cannot get sub-walls via API
CurtainGlass curtain wall systemLimited — panels/mullions separate
UnknownUnresolved typeRead only
C#
// Create a wall
Line     line     = Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(10,0,0));
Level    level    = GetLevel(doc, "Level 1");
WallType wallType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(WallType)).Cast<WallType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Wall"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Wall wall = Wall.Create(doc, line, wallType.Id, level.Id,
        3.0,    // height in feet
        0.0,    // offset from level
        false, // flip
        false); // structural
    tx.Commit();
}

// Read wall properties
Wall w = ...;
WallKind     kind     = w.WallType.Kind;
bool         flipped  = w.Flipped;
double       width    = w.Width;                // in feet
LocationCurve loc     = w.Location as LocationCurve;
Curve        curve    = loc.Curve;
XYZ          start    = curve.GetEndPoint(0);
XYZ          end      = curve.GetEndPoint(1);
double       length   = curve.Length;           // in feet

// Flip wall orientation
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Flip"))
{
    tx.Start();
    w.flip();   // flips exterior/interior face
    tx.Commit();
}

// Wall Location Line values (WALL_KEY_REF_PARAM)
// 0=Centerline, 1=Core Centerline
// 2=Finish Face:Exterior, 3=Finish Face:Interior
// 4=Core Face:Exterior, 5=Core Face:Interior
Parameter locLine = w.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.WALL_KEY_REF_PARAM);
locLine.Set(2); // Finish Face Exterior
// Wall.Flipped The Wall.Flipped property and Wall.flip() method control which face is the exterior. Orientation is (0,1,0) before flip and (0,-1,0) after. Location line value 3 = Finish Face Interior means the wall moved but location not changed.

Compound Structure

C#
WallType wt = wall.WallType;
CompoundStructure cs = wt.GetCompoundStructure();

int layerCount = cs.LayerCount;
for (int i = 0; i < layerCount; i++)
{
    double   thickness = cs.GetLayerWidth(i);           // feet
    ElementId matId    = cs.GetMaterialId(i);
    MaterialFunctionAssignment fn = cs.GetLayerFunction(i);
    // fn: Structure, Finish1, Finish2, Membrane, etc.
}

sections.title: Floors & Ceilings
sections.content:
// 03

Floors & Ceilings

C#
// Create a floor from a profile
FloorType floorType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FloorType)).Cast<FloorType>().First();

// Build a closed loop (CurveLoop)
var profile = new CurveLoop();
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(10,0,0)));
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(10,0,0), new XYZ(10,10,0)));
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(10,10,0), new XYZ(0,10,0)));
profile.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,10,0), new XYZ(0,0,0)));

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Floor"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Floor floor = Floor.Create(doc,
        new List<CurveLoop> { profile },
        floorType.Id,
        level.Id);
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Roofs
sections.content:
// 04

Roofs

Roofs are created using FootPrintRoof (most common), ExtrusionRoof, or via RoofBase.

C#
RoofType roofType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(RoofType)).Cast<RoofType>().First();

ModelCurveArray footprint = new ModelCurveArray();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Roof"))
{
    tx.Start();
    FootPrintRoof roof = doc.Create.NewFootPrintRoof(
        footprint, level, roofType, out ModelCurveArray curveMapping);

    // Set slope for each edge
    foreach (ModelCurve mc in curveMapping)
    {
        roof.set_DefinesSlope(mc, true);
        roof.set_SlopeAngle(mc, 0.5236); // 30 degrees in radians
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Levels & Grids
sections.content:
// 05

Levels & Grids

C#
// Get all levels sorted by elevation
var levels = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Level))
    .Cast<Level>()
    .OrderBy(l => l.Elevation)
    .ToList();

// Level properties
Level lvl = levels[0];
string name      = lvl.Name;
double elevation = lvl.Elevation;   // feet
double elev_mm   = elevation * 304.8;

// Create a new level
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "New Level"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Level newLevel = Level.Create(doc, 10.0);   // 10 feet elevation
    newLevel.Name = "Level 4";
    tx.Commit();
}

// Grids
var grids = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Grid)).Cast<Grid>().ToList();

// Create a grid
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "New Grid"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Line gridLine = Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(0,20,0));
    Grid grid = Grid.Create(doc, gridLine);
    grid.Name = "A";
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Openings & Rooms
sections.content:
// 06

Openings & Rooms

C#
// Create a rectangular opening in a wall
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Opening"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Opening op = doc.Create.NewOpening(
        wall,
        new XYZ(2, 0, 0.5),    // lower-left corner
        new XYZ(4, 0, 2.5));   // upper-right corner
    tx.Commit();
}

// Rooms — get all rooms
var rooms = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Rooms)
    .Cast<Room>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var room in rooms)
{
    string name   = room.Name;
    string number = room.Number;
    double area   = room.Area;           // sq ft
    double areaSM = area * 0.0929;      // to sq metres

    // Room boundaries
    SpatialElementBoundaryOptions opts =
        new SpatialElementBoundaryOptions();
    IList<IList<BoundarySegment>> boundaries =
        room.GetBoundarySegments(opts);
}

sections.title: Family Instances
sections.content:
// 07

Family Instances

C#
// Get a FamilySymbol (door type)
FamilySymbol sym = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Doors)
    .Cast<FamilySymbol>()
    .First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Place Instance"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Activate symbol if not already active
    if (!sym.IsActive) sym.Activate();

    // Place on a host (wall)
    FamilyInstance fi = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
        new XYZ(5,0,0),      // location
        sym,               // symbol
        wall,              // host
        StructuralType.NonStructural);

    tx.Commit();
}

// Read family instance properties
FamilyInstance door = ...;
Family       family = door.Symbol.Family;
string         fname  = family.Name;
FamilySymbol   sym2   = door.Symbol;
string         sname  = sym2.Name;
Element        host   = door.Host;          // the wall
LocationPoint  lp     = door.Location as LocationPoint;
XYZ            pos    = lp.Point;
double         rot    = lp.Rotation;        // radians

sections.title: Geometry Extraction
sections.content:
// 08

Geometry Extraction

Access solids, faces and edges from any element using get_Geometry(). Always set ComputeReferences = true when dimensioning.

C#
var opts = new Options()
{
    View                    = doc.ActiveView,
    ComputeReferences       = true,    // REQUIRED for dimensioning!
    IncludeNonVisibleObjects = false
};

GeometryElement geomElem = element.get_Geometry(opts);

foreach (GeometryObject obj in geomElem)
{
    if (obj is Solid solid && solid.Volume > 0)
    {
        foreach (Face face in solid.Faces)
        {
            Reference faceRef  = face.Reference;     // for dimensions
            XYZ       normal   = face.ComputeNormal(new UV(.5,.5));
            double    area     = face.Area;           // sq ft
        }
        foreach (Edge edge in solid.Edges)
        {
            Curve edgeCurve = edge.AsCurve();
            double length   = edgeCurve.Length;
        }
    }
    else if (obj is GeometryInstance gi)
    {
        // FamilyInstance contains a GeometryInstance
        GeometryElement instGeom = gi.GetInstanceGeometry();
    }
}

desc: XYZ and Transforms, Walls, Floors, Roofs, Levels, Grids, Rooms, Family Instances and geometry extraction.

title: Views & Annotations

hero.label: // Book 03 of 19 — Views & Annotations
hero.title:
Views, Sheets,
Dimensions & Tags
hero.description: Create and manage all view types, place them on sheets, add dimensions, text notes, tags and detail lines programmatically.

sections.title: View Types Overview
sections.content:
// 01

View Types Overview

View ClassViewTypeNotes
ViewPlanFloorPlan / CeilingPlan / AreaPlan / EngineeringPlanPlan views — require a Level
ViewSectionSection / Detail / ElevationRequires BoundingBoxXYZ + Transform
View3DThreeD3D views — perspective or orthographic
ViewDraftingDraftingView2D only, no model geometry
ViewSheetDrawingSheetA sheet/print layout
ViewScheduleSchedule / PanelScheduleData tables
C# — Collect all views
// All views (excluding templates)
var allViews = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(View))
    .Cast<View>()
    .Where(v => !v.IsTemplate)
    .ToList();

foreach (var view in allViews)
{
    string   name     = view.Name;
    ViewType type     = view.ViewType;
    int      scale    = view.Scale;       // e.g. 100 for 1:100
    bool     isTmpl  = view.IsTemplate;
    ElementId tmplId = view.ViewTemplateId;
}

sections.title: Create Floor Plan Views
sections.content:
// 02

Create Floor Plan Views

// Critical Use ViewFamilyType ID (not a ViewPlan ID) in ViewPlan.Create(). These are completely different types.
C#
// Get the ViewFamilyType for FloorPlan
ElementId floorPlanTypeId = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType))
    .Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(x => x.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.FloorPlan)?.Id;

var levels = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().ToList();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Floor Plans"))
{
    tx.Start();
    foreach (var lvl in levels)
    {
        var name = $"Auto_FloorPlan_{lvl.Name}";

        bool exists = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfClass(typeof(ViewPlan)).Any(v => v.Name == name);
        if (exists) continue;

        ViewPlan vp = ViewPlan.Create(doc, floorPlanTypeId, lvl.Id);
        vp.Name = name;
        vp.Scale = 100;
    }
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Create Section Views
sections.content:
// 03

Create Section Views

Sections require a BoundingBoxXYZ with a Transform that defines position and orientation.

C#
ElementId sectionTypeId = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType))
    .Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .FirstOrDefault(x => x.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.Section)?.Id;

// For a wall cross-section:
LocationCurve lc       = wall.Location as LocationCurve;
XYZ           midPt    = lc.Curve.Evaluate(0.5, true);
XYZ           wallDir  = (lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(1) - lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(0)).Normalize();
XYZ           upDir    = XYZ.BasisZ;
XYZ           viewDir  = wallDir.CrossProduct(upDir).Normalize();

Transform t = Transform.Identity;
t.Origin  = midPt;
t.BasisX  = wallDir;   // horizontal in view
t.BasisY  = upDir;     // vertical in view
t.BasisZ  = viewDir;   // camera direction (into wall)

BoundingBoxXYZ bb = new BoundingBoxXYZ
{
    Transform = t,
    Min = new XYZ(-10, 0, -5),
    Max = new XYZ( 10, 10,  5)
};

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Section"))
{
    tx.Start();
    ViewSection section = ViewSection.CreateSection(doc, sectionTypeId, bb);
    section.Name = $"Section_{wall.Id}";
    tx.Commit();
}

Rotate a Section View 90°

C#
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Rotate Section"))
{
    tx.Start();
    BoundingBoxXYZ bb  = section.GetCropBox();
    XYZ            oldX = bb.Transform.BasisX;
    XYZ            oldY = bb.Transform.BasisY;
    bb.Transform.BasisX = -oldY;    // 90° rotation
    bb.Transform.BasisY =  oldX;
    section.SetCropBox(bb);
    section.CropBoxActive = true;
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Sheets
sections.content:
// 04

Sheets

C#
// Get a title block family symbol
FamilySymbol titleBlock = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_TitleBlocks)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilySymbol))
    .Cast<FamilySymbol>()
    .First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Sheet"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create a new sheet
    ViewSheet sheet = ViewSheet.Create(doc, titleBlock.Id);
    sheet.Name          = "Floor Plans";
    sheet.SheetNumber   = "A-101";

    // Place a view on the sheet
    XYZ viewportCenter = new XYZ(0.5, 0.35, 0); // in sheet coordinates
    Viewport vp = Viewport.Create(doc, sheet.Id, floorPlanView.Id, viewportCenter);

    tx.Commit();
}

// Get all viewports on a sheet
ViewSheet existingSheet = ...;
foreach (ElementId vpId in existingSheet.GetAllViewports())
{
    Viewport vp   = doc.GetElement(vpId) as Viewport;
    View     view = doc.GetElement(vp.ViewId) as View;
}

sections.title: View Filters & Graphic Overrides
sections.content:
// 05

View Filters & Graphic Overrides

C#
// 1. Build the parameter filter rule (exterior walls = function 0)
var categories = new List<ElementId>
    { new ElementId(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls) };

var provider  = new ParameterValueProvider(
    new ElementId(BuiltInParameter.FUNCTION_PARAM));
var rule      = new FilterDoubleRule(
    provider, new FilterNumericEquals(), 0, 1e-6);
var elemFilter = new ElementParameterFilter(rule);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Add View Filter"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // 2. Create the ParameterFilterElement (check for duplicates first)
    bool exists = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(ParameterFilterElement))
        .Any(f => f.Name == "Exterior Walls");

    ParameterFilterElement pfe = exists
        ? new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
            .OfClass(typeof(ParameterFilterElement))
            .Cast<ParameterFilterElement>()
            .First(f => f.Name == "Exterior Walls")
        : ParameterFilterElement.Create(
            doc, "Exterior Walls", categories, elemFilter);

    // 3. Build color override
    var overrides = new OverrideGraphicSettings();
    overrides.SetCutFillColor(new Color(255, 0, 0));
    overrides.SetProjectionLineColor(new Color(255, 0, 0));
    overrides.SetSurfaceTransparency(50);

    // 4. Apply to all floor plan views
    var plans = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(ViewPlan)).Cast<ViewPlan>()
        .Where(v => !v.IsTemplate).ToList();

    foreach (var view in plans)
    {
        view.AddFilter(pfe.Id);
        view.SetFilterOverrides(pfe.Id, overrides);
        view.SetFilterVisibility(pfe.Id, true);
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Dimensions
sections.content:
// 06

Dimensions

See Book 3 Supplement for the complete dimensions handbook. Here is the core pattern.

C#
// Always: View + ReferenceArray + Line → NewDimension()
ReferenceArray refs = new ReferenceArray();
refs.Append(curve.GetEndPointReference(0));   // start
refs.Append(curve.GetEndPointReference(1));   // end

Line dimLine = Line.CreateBound(
    curve.GetEndPoint(0) + new XYZ(0,3,0),
    curve.GetEndPoint(1) + new XYZ(0,3,0));

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Dimension"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Dimension dim = doc.Create.NewDimension(doc.ActiveView, dimLine, refs);
    tx.Commit();

    // Read value (internal feet → mm)
    if (dim.Value.HasValue)
    {
        double mm = dim.Value.Value * 304.8;
    }
}

// Reference types summary:
// Grids:        new Reference(grid)                ← CORRECT
// Wall faces:   face.Reference (ComputeReferences=true)
// Endpoints:    curve.GetEndPointReference(0 or 1)
// Families:     fi.GetReferences(FamilyInstanceReferenceType.xxx)

sections.title: Text Notes & Tags
sections.content:
// 07

Text Notes & Tags

C#
// Get a TextNoteType
TextNoteType tnt = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(TextNoteType))
    .Cast<TextNoteType>()
    .First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Add Text"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create text note
    TextNote note = TextNote.Create(doc, doc.ActiveView.Id,
        new XYZ(5, 5, 0),    // position
        "Hello Revit API",  // text
        tnt.Id);            // type

    // Tag an element (IndependentTag)
    IndependentTag tag = IndependentTag.Create(
        doc,
        doc.ActiveView.Id,
        new Reference(wall),
        false,               // hasLeader
        TagMode.TM_ADDBY_CATEGORY,
        TagOrientation.Horizontal,
        new XYZ(5, 5, 0));  // tag head position

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Detail Lines & Filled Regions
sections.content:
// 08

Detail Lines & Filled Regions

C#
// Get a line style
Category   lineCat    = doc.Settings.Categories.get_Item(BuiltInCategory.OST_Lines);
GraphicsStyle lineStyle = lineCat.SubCategories.get_Item("Wide Lines")
    .GetGraphicsStyle(GraphicsStyleType.Projection);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Detail Line"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Detail line in active view
    DetailLine dl = doc.Create.NewDetailCurve(
        doc.ActiveView,
        Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(5,0,0)))
        as DetailLine;
    dl.LineStyle = lineStyle;

    // Filled region
    FilledRegionType frt = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(FilledRegionType)).Cast<FilledRegionType>().First();

    var loop = new CurveLoop();
    // ... add curves to loop ...

    FilledRegion fr = FilledRegion.Create(
        doc, frt.Id, doc.ActiveView.Id,
        new List<CurveLoop> { loop });

    tx.Commit();
}

desc: Floor plans, sections, sheets, viewports, view filters, dimensions, text notes, tags and detail lines.

title: Events & Automation

hero.label: // Book 04 of 19 — Events & Automation
hero.title:
Events, External Handlers,
DMU & Failures
hero.description: React to Revit actions, drive the model from modeless dialogs, implement dynamic model updates, and handle failures gracefully.

sections.title: Events Overview
sections.content:
// 01

Events Overview

Revit exposes events at three levels. Subscribe in OnStartup(), unsubscribe in OnShutdown().

LevelObjectExamples
Applicationapp.ControlledApplicationDocumentOpened, DocumentSaving, DocumentCreated, DocumentClosed
UIuiapp (UIControlledApplication)ViewActivated, SelectionChanged, Idling, ApplicationInitialized
Elementapp.ControlledApplicationDocumentChanged (read-only), use DMU for write reactions
// Golden Rule You CANNOT call Revit API from outside threads (background tasks, modeless dialogs, timers). The only safe bridges are: Idling event and ExternalEvent + IExternalEventHandler. Use ExternalEvent — it's more efficient.

sections.title: Application Events
sections.content:
// 02

Application Events

C# — IExternalApplication
public class MyApp : IExternalApplication
{
    public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        var cApp = app.ControlledApplication;

        // Document lifecycle events
        cApp.DocumentOpened   += OnDocumentOpened;
        cApp.DocumentCreated  += OnDocumentCreated;
        cApp.DocumentClosing  += OnDocumentClosing;
        cApp.DocumentSaving   += OnDocumentSaving;
        cApp.DocumentSaved    += OnDocumentSaved;

        // Changed event (read-only, fires after every tx commit/undo/redo)
        cApp.DocumentChanged  += OnDocumentChanged;

        // UI events
        app.ViewActivated     += OnViewActivated;
        app.Idling            += OnIdling;
        app.ApplicationInitialized += OnAppInit;

        return Result.Succeeded;
    }

    public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        // Always unsubscribe to prevent memory leaks!
        app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened  -= OnDocumentOpened;
        app.ControlledApplication.DocumentSaving  -= OnDocumentSaving;
        app.ControlledApplication.DocumentChanged -= OnDocumentChanged;
        app.ViewActivated                         -= OnViewActivated;
        app.Idling                                -= OnIdling;
        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
}

sections.title: Document Events — Implementations
sections.content:
// 03

Document Events — Implementations

C#
private void OnDocumentOpened(object sender, DocumentOpenedEventArgs e)
{
    Document doc = e.Document;
    TaskDialog.Show("Opened", $"Document opened: {doc.Title}");
    // e.IsCancelled — check if open was cancelled
    // e.Status — TransactionStatus
}

private void OnDocumentSaving(object sender, DocumentSavingEventArgs e)
{
    // Fires BEFORE saving — can cancel!
    Document doc = e.Document;

    // Optional: cancel the save
    // e.Cancel(); // uncomment to prevent saving
}

private void OnDocumentChanged(object sender, DocumentChangedEventArgs e)
{
    // READ-ONLY context — cannot modify the document here!
    // Use DMU (IUpdater) for write reactions instead.

    ICollection<ElementId> added    = e.GetAddedElementIds();
    ICollection<ElementId> deleted  = e.GetDeletedElementIds();
    ICollection<ElementId> modified = e.GetModifiedElementIds();

    foreach (var id in added)
    {
        Element elem = e.GetDocument().GetElement(id);
        // sync to external DB, log changes etc.
    }
}

private void OnViewActivated(object sender, ViewActivatedEventArgs e)
{
    View current  = e.CurrentActiveView;
    View previous = e.PreviousActiveView;
    // Update UI based on active view type
}

All Document Event Args

EventArgs ClassKey Properties
DocumentOpenedDocumentOpenedEventArgs.Document, .IsCancelled
DocumentCreatedDocumentCreatedEventArgs.Document
DocumentSavingDocumentSavingEventArgs.Document, .Cancel()
DocumentSavedDocumentSavedEventArgs.Document, .Status
DocumentClosingDocumentClosingEventArgs.DocumentId, .Cancel()
DocumentChangedDocumentChangedEventArgsGetAddedElementIds(), GetDeletedElementIds(), GetModifiedElementIds()
ViewActivatedViewActivatedEventArgs.CurrentActiveView, .PreviousActiveView

sections.title: UI Events
sections.content:
// 04

UI Events

C#
private void OnAppInit(object sender, ApplicationInitializedEventArgs e)
{
    // Safe place to do one-time startup work
    // Revit is fully loaded at this point
    UIApplication uiapp = new UIApplication(
        sender as Autodesk.Revit.ApplicationServices.Application);
}

private void OnIdling(object sender, IdlingEventArgs e)
{
    // Fires during idle time — valid API context
    // WARNING: will hog CPU if you don't manage frequency!

    // Set frequency to reduce CPU load
    e.SetRaiseWithoutDelay(false);  // slower, kinder to CPU

    UIApplication uiapp = sender as UIApplication;
    if (uiapp?.ActiveUIDocument == null) return;
    // ... do periodic work ...
}

sections.title: External Events — The Bridge Pattern
sections.content:
// 05

External Events — The Bridge Pattern

External Events are the correct way to call Revit API from a modeless window, background task, or any non-API thread. Idling works but consumes CPU — use ExternalEvent instead.

ApproachWhen to UseCPU Impact
ExternalEvent + IExternalEventHandlerModeless dialogs, async workflows, WPF formsLow — fires only when Raise() called
Idling EventPeriodic polling, simple animationsHigh — fires constantly
Direct callFrom IExternalCommand.Execute() onlyN/A — synchronous
// How it works 1. Create a class implementing IExternalEventHandler. 2. Register it with ExternalEvent.Create(handler) in OnStartup or Execute. 3. From your WPF button: call externalEvent.Raise(). 4. Revit calls handler.Execute(uiapp) at the next idle cycle in a valid API context.

sections.title: IExternalEventHandler — Full Implementation
sections.content:
// 06

IExternalEventHandler — Full Implementation

C# — Step 1: The Handler
// Step 1: Implement the handler
public class MyEventHandler : IExternalEventHandler
{
    // Thread-safe data passing via public properties
    public string ParameterName  { get; set; }
    public string ParameterValue { get; set; }
    public bool   Success        { get; private set; }

    // Execute is called by Revit in a valid API context
    public void Execute(UIApplication uiapp)
    {
        try
        {
            UIDocument uidoc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument;
            if (uidoc == null) { Success = false; return; }

            Document doc = uidoc.Document;

            using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "External Event Action"))
            {
                tx.Start();

                // Use the data passed via properties
                var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
                    .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>().ToList();

                foreach (var wall in walls)
                {
                    var p = wall.LookupParameter(ParameterName);
                    if (p != null && !p.IsReadOnly)
                        p.Set(ParameterValue);
                }

                tx.Commit();
                Success = true;
            }
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Success = false;
            TaskDialog.Show("Error", ex.Message);
        }
    }

    public string GetName() => "My External Event Handler";
}
C# — Step 2: Register in OnStartup
public class MyApplication : IExternalApplication
{
    // Static so WPF window can access it
    public static ExternalEvent   MyExternalEvent  { get; private set; }
    public static MyEventHandler  MyHandler        { get; private set; }

    public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        // Create handler and register it
        MyHandler       = new MyEventHandler();
        MyExternalEvent = ExternalEvent.Create(MyHandler);

        return Result.Succeeded;
    }

    public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
    {
        MyExternalEvent.Dispose();
        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
}
C# — Step 3: Raise from WPF button
// Inside your WPF window button click handler:
private void BtnApply_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
{
    // Pass data to the handler via properties
    MyApplication.MyHandler.ParameterName  = txtParamName.Text;
    MyApplication.MyHandler.ParameterValue = txtValue.Text;

    // Raise the event — Revit will call Execute() at next idle cycle
    ExternalEventRequest req = MyApplication.MyExternalEvent.Raise();

    // Check that the request was accepted
    if (req == ExternalEventRequest.Accepted)
        lblStatus.Content = "Request queued...";
    else
        lblStatus.Content = $"Request failed: {req}";
}

ExternalEventRequest Status Values

StatusMeaning
AcceptedRevit accepted and queued the request ✅
PendingA previous raise is still pending — wait
DeniedRevit is busy (modal dialog open, not in idle state)
TimedOutRevit did not process the event in time

sections.title: Modeless Dialog — Complete Pattern
sections.content:
// 07

Modeless Dialog — Complete Pattern

The full pattern for a WPF modeless window that interacts with Revit.

C# — Full modeless pattern
// The modeless WPF window
public partial class MyModelessWindow : Window
{
    private readonly ExternalEvent  _event;
    private readonly MyEventHandler _handler;

    public MyModelessWindow(
        ExternalEvent ev, MyEventHandler handler)
    {
        InitializeComponent();
        _event   = ev;
        _handler = handler;
    }

    private void BtnRename_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
    {
        _handler.ParameterName  = "Mark";
        _handler.ParameterValue = txtNewValue.Text;

        var result = _event.Raise();

        lblStatus.Content = result == ExternalEventRequest.Accepted
            ? "Queued ✔"
            : $"Failed: {result}";
    }

    // Important: don't close, just hide when user clicks X
    protected override void OnClosing(CancelEventArgs e)
    {
        e.Cancel = true;
        Hide();
    }
}

// Launch from an IExternalCommand
[Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
public class ShowWindowCommand : IExternalCommand
{
    private static MyModelessWindow _window;

    public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData cd,
        ref string msg, ElementSet els)
    {
        if (_window == null || !_window.IsLoaded)
        {
            _window = new MyModelessWindow(
                MyApplication.MyExternalEvent,
                MyApplication.MyHandler);
        }
        _window.Show();
        return Result.Succeeded;
    }
}

sections.title: Dynamic Model Update (DMU)
sections.content:
// 08

Dynamic Model Update (DMU)

DMU lets you modify the model in response to changes — unlike DocumentChanged which is read-only. Implement IUpdater and register triggers.

C# — IUpdater Implementation
public class WallTagUpdater : IUpdater
{
    private static AddInId   _appId;
    private static UpdaterId _updaterId;

    public WallTagUpdater(AddInId appId)
    {
        _appId     = appId;
        _updaterId = new UpdaterId(appId,
            new Guid("12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc"));
    }

    // Called when a watched element changes
    public void Execute(UpdaterData data)
    {
        Document doc = data.GetDocument();

        foreach (ElementId id in data.GetModifiedElementIds())
        {
            Wall wall = doc.GetElement(id) as Wall;
            if (wall == null) continue;

            // Example: auto-update wall mark when wall moves
            Parameter mark = wall.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);
            if (mark != null && !mark.IsReadOnly)
                mark.Set($"W-{wall.Id.IntegerValue}");
        }
    }

    public string    GetUpdaterName()    => "Wall Tag Updater";
    public string    GetAdditionalInformation() => "Auto-tags walls on change";
    public ChangePriority GetChangePriority() => ChangePriority.MEPFixtures;
    public UpdaterId GetUpdaterId()      => _updaterId;
}

// Register the updater in OnStartup:
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    var updater = new WallTagUpdater(app.ActiveAddInId);
    UpdaterRegistry.RegisterUpdater(updater);

    // Add trigger: fire on geometry change of any wall
    ElementClassFilter wallFilter = new ElementClassFilter(typeof(Wall));
    UpdaterRegistry.AddTrigger(
        updater.GetUpdaterId(),
        wallFilter,
        Element.GetChangeTypeGeometry());

    return Result.Succeeded;
}

// Unregister in OnShutdown:
public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    UpdaterRegistry.UnregisterUpdater(_updater.GetUpdaterId());
    return Result.Succeeded;
}

Change Types for DMU Triggers

Change TypeFires When
Element.GetChangeTypeGeometry()Element geometry changes (move, resize)
Element.GetChangeTypeParameter(param)A specific parameter value changes
Element.GetChangeTypeAny()Any change to the element
Element.GetChangeTypeElementAddition()Element is added to the document
Element.GetChangeTypeElementDeletion()Element is deleted

sections.title: Failure Handling
sections.content:
// 09

Failure Handling

Implement IFailuresPreprocessor to suppress or handle Revit warnings and errors that occur during a transaction.

C#
// Suppress all warnings silently
public class SuppressWarnings : IFailuresPreprocessor
{
    public FailureProcessingResult PreprocessFailures(
        FailuresAccessor accessor)
    {
        var failures = accessor.GetFailureMessages();

        foreach (var failure in failures)
        {
            if (failure.GetSeverity() == FailureSeverity.Warning)
                accessor.DeleteWarning(failure);   // suppress warnings
            else
                return FailureProcessingResult.ProceedWithRollBack; // errors → rollback
        }

        return FailureProcessingResult.Continue;
    }
}

// Use it in a transaction:
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Safe Create"))
{
    FailureHandlingOptions opts = tx.GetFailureHandlingOptions();
    opts.SetFailuresPreprocessor(new SuppressWarnings());
    tx.SetFailureHandlingOptions(opts);

    tx.Start();
    // ... work that might cause warnings ...
    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Idling Event
sections.content:
// 10

Idling Event

The Idling event fires between user interactions. Use it for periodic tasks but prefer ExternalEvent for modeless dialogs.

C#
private int _idleCount = 0;

private void OnIdling(object sender, IdlingEventArgs e)
{
    UIApplication uiapp = sender as UIApplication;
    if (uiapp?.ActiveUIDocument == null) return;

    _idleCount++;

    // Only run every 100 idle cycles to save performance
    if (_idleCount % 100 != 0) return;

    // Valid API context — can make changes
    Document doc = uiapp.ActiveUIDocument.Document;

    // Tell Revit not to call this as fast as possible
    e.SetRaiseWithoutDelay(false);

    // Use ExternalEvent instead of Idling when possible!
}
// Recommendation Prefer ExternalEvent over Idling for modeless dialog interactions. Idling fires constantly and degrades performance. ExternalEvent only fires when you explicitly call Raise(), giving Revit full control.

desc: Application and document events, IExternalEventHandler, modeless dialogs, DMU (IUpdater), failure handling and the Idling event.

title: Families & Conceptual Design

hero.label: // Book 05 of 19 — Families & Conceptual Design
hero.title:
Family Creation,
Parameters & Materials
hero.description: Load and place families, create family documents programmatically, manage parameters, build geometry and work with materials.

sections.title: Family Concepts
sections.content:
// 01

Family Concepts

ClassDescriptionAnalogy
FamilyThe family container (.rfa)A class definition
FamilySymbolA specific type/size within the familyA class with specific values
FamilyInstanceA placed instance in the modelAn object/instance of the class
C#
// Navigate the Family → Symbol → Instance hierarchy
FamilyInstance fi = ...;
FamilySymbol   sym    = fi.Symbol;
Family         family = sym.Family;

string familyName = family.Name;
string typeName   = sym.Name;

// Get all types in a family
foreach (ElementId symId in family.GetFamilySymbolIds())
{
    FamilySymbol s = doc.GetElement(symId) as FamilySymbol;
}

// Get all family instances of a category
var doors = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Doors)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilyInstance))
    .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
    .ToList();

sections.title: Load & Place Families
sections.content:
// 02

Load & Place Families

C#
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Load Family"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Load a .rfa family into the project
    Family family;
    bool loaded = doc.LoadFamily(
        @"C:\Families\MyDoor.rfa", out family);

    if (loaded)
    {
        // Get the first symbol from the loaded family
        FamilySymbol sym = doc.GetElement(
            family.GetFamilySymbolIds().First()) as FamilySymbol;

        if (!sym.IsActive) sym.Activate();   // REQUIRED before placing!

        // Place on a wall host
        FamilyInstance fi = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
            new XYZ(5, 0, 0),
            sym,
            wall,
            StructuralType.NonStructural);

        // Place free-standing (no host)
        FamilyInstance chair = doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(
            new XYZ(3, 3, 0),
            chairSym,
            level,
            StructuralType.NonStructural);
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

// Load with duplication handling option
doc.LoadFamily(path, new FamilyLoadOptions(), out var f2);

// Implement IFamilyLoadOptions to control overwrite behavior
public class FamilyLoadOptions : IFamilyLoadOptions
{
    public bool OnFamilyFound(bool familyInUse, out bool overwriteParamValues)
    { overwriteParamValues = true; return true; }
    public bool OnSharedFamilyFound(Family sf, bool familyInUse,
        out FamilySource src, out bool overwriteParams)
    { src = FamilySource.Family; overwriteParams = true; return true; }
}

sections.title: Family Documents
sections.content:
// 03

Family Documents

Open a family document programmatically to modify or create types.

C#
// Edit an existing family
Document famDoc = doc.EditFamily(family);

// Or open a .rfa directly
Document famDoc2 = app.Application.OpenDocumentFile(@"C:\Fam.rfa");

if (famDoc.IsFamilyDocument)
{
    using (var tx = new Transaction(famDoc, "Edit Family"))
    {
        tx.Start();
        // ... edit family content ...
        tx.Commit();
    }

    // Load back into the host project
    famDoc.LoadFamily(doc, new FamilyLoadOptions());
    famDoc.Close(false);    // false = don't save separately
}

sections.title: Family Parameters
sections.content:
// 04

Family Parameters

C#
// FamilyManager manages types and parameters
FamilyManager mgr = famDoc.FamilyManager;

using (var tx = new Transaction(famDoc, "Family Params"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Add a new parameter
    FamilyParameter param = mgr.AddParameter(
        "MyWidth",                          // name
        GroupTypeId.Dimensions,             // group
        SpecTypeId.Length,                  // type
        true);                               // isInstance

    // Set value for current type
    mgr.Set(param, UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(900, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    // Add a new family type
    mgr.NewType("1200 Wide");
    mgr.Set(param, UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(1200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    // Iterate all types
    foreach (FamilyType ft in mgr.Types)
    {
        string typeName = ft.Name;
        mgr.CurrentType = ft;
        double val = mgr.GetValue(param).AsDouble();
    }

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Family Geometry — Extrusions & Blends
sections.content:
// 05

Family Geometry — Extrusions & Blends

C#
// Create an extrusion in a family document
SketchPlane sketchPlane = SketchPlane.Create(famDoc,
    Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(XYZ.BasisZ, XYZ.Zero));

// Build a closed CurveArrArray profile
var loop = new CurveArray();
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(1,0,0)));
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(1,0,0), new XYZ(1,1,0)));
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(1,1,0), new XYZ(0,1,0)));
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,1,0), new XYZ(0,0,0)));

var profile = new CurveArrArray();
profile.Append(loop);

using (var tx = new Transaction(famDoc, "Create Extrusion"))
{
    tx.Start();

    Extrusion extrusion = famDoc.FamilyCreate.NewExtrusion(
        true,         // isSolid
        profile,
        sketchPlane,
        2.0);         // height in feet

    // Assign a material
    ElementId matId = new FilteredElementCollector(famDoc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Material)).Cast<Material>()
        .First(m => m.Name.Contains("Concrete")).Id;

    extrusion.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.MATERIAL_ID_PARAM)
        .Set(matId);

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Conceptual Design & Adaptive Components
sections.content:
// 06

Conceptual Design & Adaptive Components

C#
// Work with adaptive components (mass families)
FamilyInstance adaptive = ...;

// Check if it's adaptive
bool isAdaptive = AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils
    .IsAdaptiveComponentInstance(adaptive);

if (isAdaptive)
{
    // Get adaptive points
    IList<ElementId> ptIds = AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtils
        .GetInstanceAdaptivePointElementIds(adaptive);

    foreach (var ptId in ptIds)
    {
        ReferencePoint rp  = doc.GetElement(ptId) as ReferencePoint;
        XYZ            pos = rp.Position;
    }
}

// Get mass geometry
var masses = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Mass)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .ToList();

sections.title: Materials
sections.content:
// 07

Materials

C#
// Get all materials
var materials = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Material))
    .Cast<Material>()
    .ToList();

// Read material properties
Material mat = materials.First();
string  name       = mat.Name;
Color   surfaceColor = mat.Color;
Color   cutColor     = mat.CutForegroundPatternColor;
int     transparency = mat.Transparency;

// Create a new material
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Material"))
{
    tx.Start();

    ElementId newMatId = Material.Create(doc, "My Red Material");
    Material  newMat   = doc.GetElement(newMatId) as Material;

    newMat.Color        = new Color(200, 50, 50);
    newMat.Transparency = 20;

    tx.Commit();
}

// Apply material to a wall
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Apply Material"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Parameter matParam = wall.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.MATERIAL_ID_PARAM);
    if (matParam != null) matParam.Set(newMatId);
    tx.Commit();
}

desc: Load and place families, edit family documents, manage types and parameters, create geometry and work with materials.

title: MEP Systems

hero.label: // Book 06 of 19 — MEP Systems
hero.title:
Pipes, Ducts, Electrical
& MEP Connectors
hero.description: Create and connect MEP elements — pipes, ducts, conduits, cable trays — and work with system connectors, MEP systems and spaces.

sections.title: MEP Overview
sections.content:
// 01

MEP Overview

ClassCategorySystem
PipeOST_PipeCurvesPlumbing — domestic water, fire protection
PipeFittingOST_PipeFittingElbows, tees, reducers for pipes
DuctOST_DuctCurvesHVAC — supply, return, exhaust air
DuctFittingOST_DuctFittingElbows, tees, transitions for ducts
ConduitOST_ConduitElectrical — wire protection
CableTrayOST_CableTrayElectrical — cable support trays
WireConduitOST_WireElectrical wiring
MechanicalEquipmentOST_MechanicalEquipmentChillers, AHUs, fans
// MEP Key Concept All MEP elements connect through Connectors. A Pipe end has a Connector; a fitting has Connectors. Use ConnectorManager to navigate and connect MEP elements together.

sections.title: Pipes & Plumbing
sections.content:
// 02

Pipes & Plumbing

C#
// Get pipe type and system type
PipeType pipeType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PipeType)).Cast<PipeType>().First();

PipingSystemType sysType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PipingSystemType))
    .Cast<PipingSystemType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Pipe"))
{
    tx.Start();

    // Create a straight pipe segment
    Pipe pipe = Pipe.Create(
        doc,
        sysType.Id,          // system type
        pipeType.Id,         // pipe type
        level.Id,            // reference level
