        new XYZ(0,0,3),     // start point
        new XYZ(5,0,3));    // end point

    // Set diameter
    pipe.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_PIPE_DIAMETER_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(100, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    tx.Commit();
}

// Read pipe properties
Pipe p = ...;
double diameter  = p.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_PIPE_DIAMETER_PARAM).AsDouble();
double length    = p.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.CURVE_ELEM_LENGTH).AsDouble();
XYZ    direction = (p.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.GetEndPoint(1)
                 - (p.Location as LocationCurve).Curve.GetEndPoint(0);

sections.title: Ducts & Mechanical
sections.content:
// 03

Ducts & Mechanical

C#
DuctType ductType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(DuctType)).Cast<DuctType>().First();

MechanicalSystemType mSysType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(MechanicalSystemType))
    .Cast<MechanicalSystemType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Duct"))
{
    tx.Start();

    Duct duct = Duct.Create(
        doc,
        mSysType.Id,
        ductType.Id,
        level.Id,
        new XYZ(0,0,3),
        new XYZ(5,0,3));

    // Set width and height (rectangular duct)
    duct.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CURVE_WIDTH_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(400, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));
    duct.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.RBS_CURVE_HEIGHT_PARAM)
        .Set(UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(200, UnitTypeId.Millimeters));

    tx.Commit();
}

sections.title: Electrical & Cable Trays
sections.content:
// 04

Electrical & Cable Trays

C#
// Cable Tray
CableTrayType ctType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(CableTrayType)).Cast<CableTrayType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create CableTray"))
{
    tx.Start();
    CableTray ct = CableTray.Create(
        doc, ctType.Id, level.Id,
        new XYZ(0,0,3), new XYZ(5,0,3));
    tx.Commit();
}

// Conduit
ConduitType conduitType = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ConduitType)).Cast<ConduitType>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Conduit"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Conduit conduit = Conduit.Create(
        doc, conduitType.Id, level.Id,
        new XYZ(0,0,2.5), new XYZ(3,0,2.5));
    tx.Commit();
}

// Get all electrical equipment
var panels = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_ElectricalEquipment)
    .OfClass(typeof(FamilyInstance))
    .Cast<FamilyInstance>()
    .ToList();

sections.title: Connectors
sections.content:
// 05

Connectors

Connectors are the endpoints of MEP elements. Connect two elements by linking their connectors.

C#
// Get connectors from a pipe
ConnectorManager cm   = pipe.ConnectorManager;
ConnectorSet     cons = cm.Connectors;

Connector start = null, end = null;
foreach (Connector c in cons)
{
    if (c.ConnectorType == ConnectorType.End)
    {
        if (start == null) start = c;
        else               end   = c;
    }
}

// Check connector properties
XYZ    origin   = start.Origin;
double diameter = start.Radius * 2;
bool   isConnected = start.IsConnected;

// Connect two pipes end-to-end
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Connect Pipes"))
{
    tx.Start();

    Connector c1 = GetConnectorAt(pipe1, pipe2.GetEndPoint(0));
    Connector c2 = GetConnectorAt(pipe2, pipe1.GetEndPoint(1));

    if (c1 != null && c2 != null)
        c1.ConnectTo(c2);

    tx.Commit();
}

// Helper: find closest connector to a point
Connector GetConnectorAt(Element elem, XYZ pt, double tol = 0.001)
{
    foreach (Connector c in elem.GetMEPModel().ConnectorManager.Connectors)
        if (c.Origin.DistanceTo(pt) < tol) return c;
    return null;
}

sections.title: MEP Systems
sections.content:
// 06

MEP Systems

C#
// Get all piping systems
var pipingSystems = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(PipingSystem))
    .Cast<PipingSystem>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var sys in pipingSystems)
{
    string name     = sys.Name;
    double flow     = sys.GetFlow();

    // Get all elements in the system
    ConnectorSet sysElems = sys.ConnectorManager.Connectors;
}

// Mechanical systems
var mechSystems = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(MechanicalSystem))
    .Cast<MechanicalSystem>()
    .ToList();

// Electrical systems
var elecSystems = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ElectricalSystem))
    .Cast<ElectricalSystem>()
    .ToList();

sections.title: Rooms & Spaces
sections.content:
// 07

Rooms & Spaces

Spaces are MEP-specific versions of Rooms — they carry load calculations and HVAC data.

C#
// Get MEP Spaces (different from architectural Rooms!)
var spaces = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_MEPSpaces)
    .Cast<Space>()
    .ToList();

foreach (var space in spaces)
{
    string name   = space.Name;
    double area   = space.Area;          // sq ft
    double volume = space.Volume;        // cubic ft

    // HVAC loads
    double coolingLoad = space.get_Parameter(
        BuiltInParameter.ROOM_ACTUAL_COOLING_LOAD_PARAM)
        ?.AsDouble() ?? 0;

    // Check space/room relationship
    Room linkedRoom = space.Room;
    if (linkedRoom != null)
    {
        string roomName = linkedRoom.Name;
    }
}

// Create a Space
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Space"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Space newSpace = doc.Create.NewSpace(
        level,
        new UV(5, 5));    // location as UV on the level
    newSpace.Name = "Office 101";
    tx.Commit();
}

desc: Pipes, ducts, conduits, cable trays, connectors, MEP system traversal and MEP spaces with HVAC loads.

sections.title: Elements & Parameters
sections.content:
🧱
// CHAPTER 01

Elements & Parameters

FilteredElementCollector, BuiltInParameter, StorageType, reading and writing values

26 challenges
#001BEGINNER

Element Counter

Write a command that counts all walls, floors, ceilings and doors in the active document and displays the totals.
Expected Result
A TaskDialog showing "Walls: 42 | Floors: 8 | Ceilings: 8 | Doors: 24" with accurate counts from the model.
FilteredElementCollectorOfClassOfCategory
#002BEGINNER

Wall Mark Reporter

List all walls that have an empty or null "Mark" parameter and display their Element IDs in a dialog.
Expected Result
Dialog listing all wall IDs where the Mark parameter is empty or has never been set.
BuiltInParameterAsStringALL_MODEL_MARK
#003BEGINNER

Auto-Number Walls

Assign sequential Mark values (W-001, W-002, …) to all walls in the project sorted by their X-coordinate position.
Expected Result
All walls have unique Mark values starting from W-001, ordered left-to-right. Transaction committed successfully.
TransactionParameter.SetLocationCurve
#004BEGINNER

Level Element Lister

Collect all elements at a specific level (prompt the user to pick a level from a list) and display the category name and count for each category found.
Expected Result
A report showing category-by-category breakdown of all elements on the chosen level, e.g. "Walls: 12, Doors: 6, Windows: 8".
ElementLevelFilterCategoryLINQ GroupBy
#005BEGINNER

Type vs Instance Parameter Detector

For a selected element, list all its parameters indicating whether each one is an Instance or Type parameter, its StorageType, and whether it is ReadOnly.
Expected Result
A full parameter report: Name | Instance/Type | StorageType | ReadOnly | Current Value for every parameter on the element.
ParametersIsSharedStorageType
#006INTERMEDIATE

Parameter Filter — Fire Rating

Use ParameterFilterElement (not LINQ) to find all walls where the Fire Rating parameter equals "2HR". Use FilterStringRule and native parameter filter.
Expected Result
List of walls collected using only native Revit filter (no LINQ), confirmed with count and element IDs.
ParameterValueProviderFilterStringRuleElementParameterFilter
#007INTERMEDIATE

Bulk Parameter Copy

Copy the value of a user-specified parameter from one selected element to all elements of the same category. Handle all StorageTypes gracefully.
Expected Result
All same-category elements now share the same parameter value as the source. Read-only parameters are skipped with a count reported.
StorageType switchCategory matchIsReadOnly
#008INTERMEDIATE

Shared Parameter Reader

Access a shared parameter on walls using its GUID (hardcode a test GUID). If not present, report it's missing. If present, display its value.
Expected Result
Dialog indicating either "Shared param not found" or displaying the value found via get_Parameter(Guid).
get_Parameter(Guid)SharedParameters
#009INTERMEDIATE

Multi-Category Collector

Collect elements from at least 5 different categories simultaneously using ElementMulticategoryFilter (not separate queries). Measure and display the time taken vs separate queries.
Expected Result
Total count and per-category breakdown. Timing comparison showing multi-category filter is faster.
ElementMulticategoryFilterStopwatch
#010INTERMEDIATE

Bounding Box Inspector

For a user-selected element, display its bounding box Min, Max and computed center point in both internal units and millimetres.
Expected Result
Dialog or output with Min/Max/Center in feet and mm, correct for both instance and type elements.
get_BoundingBoxBoundingBoxXYZUnitUtils
#011ADVANCED

Universal Parameter Exporter

Export ALL parameters of every wall in the project to a CSV-formatted string. Handle all StorageTypes, show values in user-facing units (mm for lengths), and include both instance and type parameters.
Expected Result
A multi-column CSV with columns: ElementId | ParameterName | InstanceOrType | Value (converted) | Unit — ready to paste into Excel.
CSV exportUnitUtilsGetTypeId
#012ADVANCED

Exclusion Filter Chain

Collect all model elements but exclude: elements on hidden categories, elements with no geometry, elements that are ElementType, and elements belonging to linked files. Chain all exclusions natively without LINQ Where().
Expected Result
A count of "real" model elements using only native Revit filter chain. Verify result matches LINQ-filtered count.
ExclusionFilterWhereElementIsNotElementTypeLogicalAndFilter
#013INTERMEDIATE

Selection Parameter Editor

Prompt the user to select multiple elements, then display a text input dialog to set a common parameter value on all selected elements at once.
Expected Result
Selected elements all have the new parameter value. Report how many were updated vs skipped (read-only).
Selection.GetElementIdsPickObjectsParameter.Set
#014BEGINNER

Room Area Calculator

List all rooms in the project with their name, number, level and area in square metres. Show total gross floor area at the bottom.
Expected Result
Sorted room list with area in m², total GFA at bottom. Unplaced rooms (area = 0) flagged separately.
OST_RoomsRoom.AreaRoom.Number
#015INTERMEDIATE

Global Parameter Reporter

List all Global Parameters in the document with their name, type, current value and a count of how many elements are driven by each one.
Expected Result
Report showing each Global Parameter name, value, and driven element count. Highlight any global params with no driven elements.
GlobalParametersManagerGetDrivenElements
#016ADVANCED

Duplicate Mark Detector & Fixer

Find all elements across all categories where the Mark parameter is duplicated within the same category. List the duplicates, then offer a "Fix" button that appends a suffix to make them unique.
Expected Result
Report of duplicated marks grouped by category. After fix, zero duplicates remain. Undo works correctly as a single transaction.
LINQ GroupByALL_MODEL_MARKTransactionGroup
#017BEGINNER

Unused Views Cleanup

Find all views that are not placed on any sheet and are not the active view. List them and offer to delete them. Show count before and after.
Expected Result
List of sheet-less, inactive views. After confirmation, all are deleted. Document element count reduced accordingly.
ViewSheet.GetAllPlacedViewsdoc.DeleteActiveView
#018INTERMEDIATE

Custom Selection Filter — Structural Only

Implement ISelectionFilter that allows picking only structural elements (structural walls, columns, beams, foundations). Prompt user to select, then report the structural type of each picked element.
Expected Result
Non-structural elements are not selectable. Picked elements all confirmed structural. StructuralType displayed for each.
ISelectionFilterAllowElementStructuralType
#019ADVANCED

Computed Parameter Writer

For each room, compute: area × ceiling height = volume. Write this computed value into a project parameter called "Computed Volume" on each room element. Create the parameter if it doesn't exist.
Expected Result
Every placed room has "Computed Volume" populated with correct value in m³. Parameter visible in properties panel.
Room.VolumeLookupParameterParameter.Set(double)
#020BEGINNER

Workset Element Counter

List all worksets in the model and count how many elements belong to each workset. Only run if the model is workshared.
Expected Result
Dialog listing every workset name and its element count. "Not a workshared model" message if run on a non-workshared file.
WorksetTableELEM_PARTITION_PARAMIsWorkshared
#021INTERMEDIATE

Phase-Aware Element Filter

Filter walls by phase created and phase demolished. List walls that were created in Phase 1 but demolished in Phase 2 — i.e. walls that no longer exist in the current phase.
Expected Result
List of demolished walls with phase created and phase demolished values. Correct for multi-phase models.
PHASE_CREATEDPHASE_DEMOLISHEDPhaseFilter
#022ADVANCED

Parameter Rename Tool

Find all project parameters with a given name pattern (e.g. starts with "OLD_") across all categories and rename them to replace "OLD_" with "NEW_". Handle parameters used in formulas gracefully.
Expected Result
All matching parameters renamed. Formula-dependent parameters flagged and skipped with a report. Undo works correctly.
ParameterElementInternalDefinitionName setter
#023EXPERT

Linked File Element Inspector

Iterate all Revit link instances in the host model. For each link, open its document and count elements by category. Report totals from linked files separately from host elements.
Expected Result
Full element count report: "Host: Walls 42 | Link A: Walls 18 | Link B: Walls 31" — accurate per-file breakdown.
RevitLinkInstanceGetLinkDocumentRevitLinkType
#024INTERMEDIATE

Hidden Category Toggler

List all model categories and their visibility state in the active view. Provide a way to toggle visibility for a chosen category without opening the Visibility/Graphics dialog.
Expected Result
Category visibility flipped in active view. Elements in that category appear/disappear immediately.
View.SetCategoryHiddenGetCategoryHiddenCategories
#025BEGINNER

Design Option Reporter

List all Design Options in the project with their name, group, and whether they are the primary option. Count elements in each design option.
Expected Result
List of every design option, its group, primary status, and element count.
DesignOptionDESIGN_OPTION_IDDesignOptionSet
#026ADVANCED

Assembly Creator

Let the user select a group of structural elements. Create an AssemblyInstance from the selection, name it automatically based on a naming convention, and generate an assembly view.
Expected Result
An Assembly created, named, and visible in the Project Browser. Assembly view generated with the correct elements highlighted.
AssemblyInstance.CreateAssemblyViewUtilsNamingCategory

sections.title: Geometry & Spatial Analysis
sections.content:
📐
// CHAPTER 02

Geometry & Spatial Analysis

XYZ, Transform, Solid, Face, BoundingBox, Intersection, Proximity

22 challenges
#027BEGINNER

Element Distance Calculator

Let the user pick two elements. Calculate and display the 3D distance between their centroid points in millimetres.
Expected Result
Dialog: "Distance between elements: 4523mm". Correct for any two elements with bounding boxes.
XYZ.DistanceToBoundingBoxXYZUnitUtils
#028BEGINNER

Solid Volume Reporter

For a user-selected element, extract all Solid objects from its geometry, sum their volumes, and display the total in cubic metres. Also report surface area.
Expected Result
Total solid volume in m³ and total surface area in m² displayed. Handles GeometryInstance for family instances.
get_Geometry(Options)Solid.VolumeSolid.SurfaceArea
#029INTERMEDIATE

Clash Detector (BBox)

Find all pairs of walls whose bounding boxes overlap (intersect) in 3D space. Report how many clashes exist and list the element ID pairs.
Expected Result
List of (WallId A, WallId B) pairs where bounding boxes overlap. Zero false negatives for clearly intersecting walls.
BoundingBoxIntersectsFilterget_BoundingBoxOutline
#030INTERMEDIATE

Face Normal Analyser

For each face of a selected element's solid, compute the face normal direction and classify it as: Top, Bottom, North, South, East, West or Diagonal. Report counts per direction.
Expected Result
Face count per orientation direction for the selected element. Useful for verifying wall/floor face directions.
Face.ComputeNormalXYZ.DotProductSolid.Faces
#031INTERMEDIATE

Ray Cast Hit Test

Let the user click a point in the model. Cast a vertical ray (down) from that point and report all elements the ray intersects, ordered by distance from origin.
Expected Result
Ordered list of elements hit by the ray with intersection distance. Floors, walls and structure correctly detected.
ReferenceIntersectorFindNearestFindAll
#032ADVANCED

Element Rotator

Let the user select a family instance and input a rotation angle. Rotate the element around its own Z-axis (using its LocationPoint) by the specified angle in degrees.
Expected Result
Element visually rotated by correct angle. LocationPoint.Rotation updated. Works for any FamilyInstance with a point location.
LocationPoint.RotateTransform.CreateRotationLine.CreateBound
#033ADVANCED

Point-on-Curve Projection

Given a picked point in 3D space, find the nearest wall in the model and project the point onto the wall's centerline curve. Display the projected point coordinate and distance.
Expected Result
Projected XYZ point, parameter along the wall curve (0–1), and distance from original point to projected point in mm.
Curve.ProjectIntersectionResultLocationCurve
#034INTERMEDIATE

Floor Area by Material

For all floors, extract compound structure layers, identify the structural layer, and group total floor area by material name. Output m² per material.
Expected Result
Dictionary of material name → total floor area in m². Correct for multi-layer floors.
CompoundStructureGetMaterialIdMaterialFunctionAssignment
#035BEGINNER

Wall Midpoint Marker

For every wall in the active view, place a model text label showing the wall's Mark at the midpoint of the wall's location curve. Label must lie on the wall.
Expected Result
Every wall has a text label at its visual centre showing the Mark value. Labels remain attached to walls after model changes.
Curve.Evaluate(0.5, true)TextNote.CreateLocationCurve
#036ADVANCED

Solid Boolean Intersection

Select two elements. Extract their solids and perform a Boolean intersection using BooleanOperationsUtils. Report the volume of the intersection solid in m³.
Expected Result
Intersection volume in m³. If solids don't intersect, report 0. Handles null/empty solid results gracefully.
BooleanOperationsUtilsExecuteBooleanOperationBooleanOperationsType.Intersect
#037INTERMEDIATE

Total Wall Run Length

Calculate the total run length of all walls in the project, grouped by wall type name. Include only straight walls (Line curves). Report in metres.
Expected Result
Dictionary of WallType → total length in metres. Curved walls excluded with a count of how many were skipped.
LocationCurve.CurveCurve.Lengthis Line check
#038EXPERT

Building Footprint Outline

Compute the overall building footprint by projecting all wall centerlines to Z=0 and computing a convex hull of the resulting 2D points. Draw the result as detail lines in a floor plan view.
Expected Result
A closed convex polygon of detail lines in the active floor plan representing the overall building extent. Corners are correct within 10mm.
Convex hull algorithmDetailCurveUV projection
#039INTERMEDIATE

Point-in-Room Finder

Let the user click a point in a floor plan view. Determine which room (if any) the clicked point lies inside and display the room name, number, and area.
Expected Result
"Point is inside Room: Office 101 (R-01), 24.5m²" or "Point is not inside any room" — accurate for all room shapes.
Room.IsPointInRoomPickPointXYZ
#040ADVANCED

Wall Offset Creator

For a selected wall, create a parallel wall at a user-specified offset distance. Preserve the original wall height and type. Handle walls at any orientation.
Expected Result
New wall created parallel to original at the correct offset. Same height and type. Works for any wall direction.
Curve.CreateOffsetXYZ.CrossProductWall.Create
#041INTERMEDIATE

Arc Wall Radius Reporter

Find all curved (arc) walls in the model. For each, report the radius, arc length, and whether it is convex or concave relative to the building interior.
Expected Result
Report: WallId | Radius (mm) | Arc Length (mm) | Convex/Concave. Radius correct within 1mm.
ArcArc.RadiusWall.Flipped
#042ADVANCED

Adjacent Room Finder

For a selected room, find all rooms that share a wall boundary with it (adjacent rooms). List the shared wall element and the adjacent room name.
Expected Result
List of (Adjacent Room Name, Shared Wall Id) pairs. All shared walls found including very short walls ≥ 100mm.
GetBoundarySegmentsBoundarySegment.ElementIdRoom lookup
#043INTERMEDIATE

Reference Plane Grid Creator

Create a 5×5 grid of reference planes at a user-specified spacing, starting from origin. Name them systematically (RP_X_1 through RP_X_5, RP_Y_1 through RP_Y_5).
Expected Result
10 named reference planes visible in floor plan, correctly spaced, all in a single transaction.
ReferencePlane.CreatePlaneLoop
#043bEXPERT

Geometry Mesh Extractor

For a selected element, extract all triangulated mesh data (Mesh objects) from its geometry. Count total triangles, vertices, and compute total mesh area. Export vertex list to a CSV-formatted string.
Expected Result
Triangle count, vertex count, mesh area, and CSV of vertex XYZ coordinates. Correct for both native and family geometry.
Face.TriangulateMeshMesh.Vertices
#044INTERMEDIATE

Project Base Point Inspector

Read the Project Base Point and Survey Point coordinates (E/N/Elev and Angle to True North). Display both in the model's project units and in decimal degrees for the angle.
Expected Result
E, N, Elevation, and True North angle for both Project Base Point and Survey Point. Correct shared coordinates shown.
BasePointOST_ProjectBasePointBASEPOINT_EASTWEST_PARAM
#045ADVANCED

Opening Geometry Validator

For each door and window, verify that the opening cuts completely through the host wall. Check if the opening solid fully penetrates the wall solid using boolean subtraction. Flag any openings where the cut is incomplete.
Expected Result
List of problematic openings (partial cuts, gaps). Healthy openings confirmed with "Full penetration: Yes".
BooleanOperationsUtilsGetDependentElementsOpening geometry
#046BEGINNER

Top-of-Wall Elevation Checker

For all walls, compare their top-of-wall elevation to the floor above. Flag any wall whose top doesn't reach the underside of the floor above within 50mm tolerance.
Expected Result
Report of walls that don't properly connect to the floor above. Gap amount in mm per wall shown.
WALL_TOP_OFFSETLevel.ElevationTolerance check
#047INTERMEDIATE

Slab Edge Profile Extractor

For a selected floor, extract the top face boundary curves and report the perimeter length and the total number of edges in metres. Handle floors with holes (inner boundaries).
Expected Result
Outer perimeter, inner perimeter(s) if holes exist, and total edge count. All in metres.
Face.GetEdgesAsCurveLoopsCurveLoop.GetExactLengthFloor geometry
#048EXPERT

Element Geometry to JSON

For a selected element, extract all solid geometry (faces and edges) and serialise it as a JSON structure with arrays of vertices and triangle indices — suitable for rendering in a web viewer.
Expected Result
Valid JSON with "vertices": [[x,y,z],...], "triangles": [[i,j,k],...]. Parseable and renderable in a Three.js or Babylon.js scene.
Face.TriangulateJSON serialisationMesh.Vertices

sections.title: Walls, Floors, Roofs & Structure
sections.content:
🏗️
// CHAPTER 03

Walls, Floors, Roofs & Structure

Creating, modifying and analysing host elements and structural components

24 challenges
#049BEGINNER

Rectangular Room of Walls

Create four walls forming a 6000mm × 4000mm rectangular room at Level 1, using the first available wall type. All corners must join correctly.
Expected Result
A closed rectangular room visible in floor plan. Wall corners auto-joined. All four walls same height and type.
Wall.CreateLine.CreateBoundLevel
#050INTERMEDIATE

Wall Type Swapper

Let the user select multiple walls. Present a list of available wall types and replace all selected walls with the chosen type while preserving location, height and flipping.
Expected Result
All selected walls changed to new type. Location curve unchanged. Height unchanged. Wall.WallType correct after swap.
wall.WallType setterChangeTypeIdWallType collector
#051INTERMEDIATE

Wall Layer Thickness Exporter

For every unique wall type in the project, export a report listing each compound layer's function, material name, and thickness in mm.
Expected Result
CSV or formatted report: WallType | LayerIndex | Function | Material | Thickness(mm) for every layer of every type.
CompoundStructureGetLayerFunctionGetLayerWidth
#052ADVANCED

Wall Splitter at Point

Let the user pick a point on an existing wall. Split the wall at that point into two walls, each preserving the original wall's type, height, and parameters. Handle the join/unjoin at the split point.
Expected Result
Two walls where one existed. No geometry gap at split. Both walls same type, height, and Mark suffix (e.g. W-01A, W-01B).
LocationCurve splitWall.Create x2unjoinAtEnd
#053INTERMEDIATE

Room-to-Floor Generator

For every room on a selected level, automatically create a floor element whose boundary matches the room boundary. Use the default floor type.
Expected Result
One floor per room, matching room boundary exactly. No floors for unplaced rooms. Floors at the level elevation.
Room.GetBoundarySegmentsCurveLoopFloor.Create
#054ADVANCED

Floor Opening Auto-Creator

For every vertical pipe that passes through a floor slab, create a rectangular shaft opening in the floor sized 50mm larger than the pipe diameter on all sides.
Expected Result
One shaft opening per pipe-floor intersection. Opening correctly sized and positioned. No openings where pipes don't intersect floors.
ReferenceIntersectordoc.Create.NewOpeningPipe diameter
#055BEGINNER

Simple Hip Roof Generator

Create a footprint (hip) roof over a user-selected floor element. Use a 30° slope on all edges. The roof should match the floor boundary exactly.
Expected Result
A hip roof visible in 3D view. All edges sloped at 30°. Footprint matches floor boundary. Correct roof type used.
NewFootPrintRoofset_DefinesSlopeset_SlopeAngle
#056INTERMEDIATE

Wall Join Checker

Find all wall endpoints that are within 50mm of another wall endpoint but are not joined. Report them and offer a "Fix All" to join them with JoinGeometryUtils.
Expected Result
List of unjoined near-endpoint pairs. After fix, WallUtils.IsWallJoinAllowedAtEnd confirmed true for all fixed pairs.
JoinGeometryUtils.JoinGeometryWallUtilsXYZ.DistanceTo
#057ADVANCED

Beam Grid Creator

Create a regular grid of structural beams spanning between the vertical grid lines at the first level. Beam spacing and family type are user-specified. Auto-connect to columns at intersections.
Expected Result
Grid of beams visible in structural plan. Beams start/end at grid intersections. StructuralType.Beam confirmed. Beam joins at columns.
NewFamilyInstance StructuralType.BeamGridLevel
#058INTERMEDIATE

Curtain Wall Panel Inspector

For all curtain walls in the project, count and report: total panel count, number of empty panels, number of filled panels, and the area of each panel type.
Expected Result
Per curtain wall: panel count, empty/filled split, area of each filled panel type in m².
CurtainGridGetPanelIdsPanel.Area
#059ADVANCED

Embedded Curtain Wall Replacer

Find all basic wall types that contain an embedded curtain system. For each, replace the embedded curtain system with a new one loaded from a specified family path.
Expected Result
Embedded curtain systems replaced. New system visible and attached to host walls. No host wall geometry lost.
EmbeddedWallGetEmbeddedWallTypeIdCurtain system replacement
#060INTERMEDIATE

Multi-Level Generator

Create multiple levels from a user-specified list of floor-to-floor heights. Auto-name them (Ground Floor, Level 1, Level 2…) and create a floor plan view for each.
Expected Result
Correct number of levels with correct elevations. One floor plan view per level in Project Browser. All in a single TransactionGroup.
Level.CreateViewPlan.CreateTransactionGroup
#061BEGINNER

Wall Area Take-Off

Sum the gross wall area and net wall area (minus openings) for all exterior walls in the project. Group by wall type. Report in m².
Expected Result
Per wall type: Gross Area (m²), Opening Area (m²), Net Area (m²). Consistent with Revit's own wall schedule.
HOST_AREA_COMPUTEDWALL_ATTR_AREA_PARAMFunction filter
#062ADVANCED

Stacked Wall Analyser

For all Stacked Walls, identify and report the constituent basic wall segments (sub-walls), their individual heights and types. Stacked walls have WallKind.Stacked.
Expected Result
For each stacked wall: list of sub-wall ids, their WallType, and height range (from/to elevation).
WallKind.StackedGetStackedWallMemberIdsSub-wall properties
#063EXPERT

Toposurface Grader

Read the topography surface, find all points within a user-drawn boundary, raise or lower them by a user-specified offset. Preview the change before applying.
Expected Result
Selected topo points moved by offset. Toposurface visually updated. Points outside boundary unchanged. Undo works.
TopographySurfaceGetPointsChangeTopographyPointsAndCurves
#064INTERMEDIATE

Stair Riser Counter

For all stairs in the model, report the actual number of risers, the calculated riser height, and whether the stair meets the building code (e.g. max riser 180mm, min going 250mm).
Expected Result
Per stair: riser count, actual riser height mm, tread depth mm, PASS/FAIL against code. Non-compliant stairs highlighted.
StairsSTAIRS_ACTUAL_RISER_HEIGHTSTAIRS_RISER_NUMBER
#065ADVANCED

Columns-Not-On-Grid Detector

Find all structural columns whose base point is not within 25mm of any grid intersection. List and highlight them. Consider all active grid combinations.
Expected Result
List of off-grid columns with their offset distance from the nearest grid intersection. Selection set updated to show them in model.
Grid.CurveIntersection logicStructuralType.Column
#066INTERMEDIATE

Ceiling Height Checker

For each room, find the ceiling directly above it and compute the net ceiling height (ceiling elevation minus floor level elevation). Flag rooms below minimum height (2400mm).
Expected Result
Room list with actual clear height in mm and PASS/FAIL. Rooms without ceilings flagged as "No Ceiling".
ReferenceIntersectorOST_CeilingsRoom.UpperLimit
#067BEGINNER

Building Grid System Creator

Create a complete grid system: 6 vertical grids at 6000mm spacing (A–F) and 4 horizontal grids at 8000mm spacing (1–4). All grids extend 2000mm beyond the outermost elements.
Expected Result
10 named grids visible in plan. Correct spacing, correct naming convention A–F and 1–4. All in one transaction.
Grid.CreateGrid.NameLine.CreateBound
#068EXPERT

Parametric Facade Panel System

Divide a selected curtain wall surface into a parametric grid of panels. For each panel, place a loaded facade panel family instance, scaled to fit the panel opening exactly.
Expected Result
Each curtain wall panel filled with correctly-scaled family instance. Panel family parameters width/height match opening dimensions.
CurtainGrid.GetPanelIdsPanel.GetTransformFamilyInstance parameters
#069INTERMEDIATE

Sloped Slab Creator

Create a floor with a slope arrow. The floor should slope from 0mm offset at one edge to 150mm offset at the opposite edge using a slope arrow (not manual point editing).
Expected Result
Floor visible in 3D with visible slope. Slope arrow displayed in plan. Elevation check at both edges confirms 150mm differential.
SlabShapeEditorAddSlopeArrowFloor slope
#070ADVANCED

Profiled Wall Creator

Create a wall with a custom non-rectangular profile (e.g. arch top). Use the Wall.Create overload that accepts a profile CurveLoop instead of a straight line, creating an architectural feature wall.
Expected Result
Wall with arched top profile visible in elevation. Profile curve matches the intended arch geometry. Wall type applied correctly.
Wall.Create(doc, loops, typeId, levelId, false)CurveLoop with Arc
#071INTERMEDIATE

Foundation Wall Under Column

For every structural column, auto-create a matching foundation wall beneath it extending 1000mm below the base level. Match the column's footprint width for the wall thickness.
Expected Result
One foundation wall per column, correctly positioned, 1000mm deep, wall width = column depth. Structural type = StructuralWall.
StructuralType.Column locationWall.Create structuralLevel offset
#072BEGINNER

Exterior Wall Highlighter

Find all walls with Function = Exterior (WallFunction.Exterior). Apply a solid red fill override to them in the active view using OverrideGraphicSettings.
Expected Result
All exterior walls visually red in the active view. Interior/other walls unchanged. Override survives view switch and returns after.
WallFunction.ExteriorOverrideGraphicSettingsSetCutFillColor

sections.title: Views & Sheets
sections.content:
🗺️
// CHAPTER 04

Views & Sheets

Creating views, sheets, viewports, view templates, crop regions and view properties

22 challenges
#073BEGINNER

Batch Floor Plan Generator

Create one floor plan view for every level in the project. Name each view "FP - [LevelName]". Skip levels that already have a floor plan. Set scale to 1:100.
Expected Result
One floor plan per level, correctly named, 1:100 scale. No duplicates created. Existing views preserved.
ViewPlan.CreateViewFamilyType.FloorPlanDuplicate check
#074INTERMEDIATE

Grid Section Creator

For every vertical grid line, create a section view that cuts perpendicular to the grid. Section depth = 1000mm, height spans full building height.
Expected Result
One section per vertical grid. Each section perpendicular to its grid, correct depth and height. Named "Section - Grid [name]".
ViewSection.CreateSectionBoundingBoxXYZ + TransformGrid curve
#075INTERMEDIATE

Sheet Set Generator

Create a standard sheet set (A0 to A5) from a CSV-like list of sheet numbers and names provided as a hardcoded string in the command. Place all floor plan views on relevant sheets.
Expected Result
Full sheet set created with correct numbering and naming. Floor plan views placed at sheet centre. Title block applied to all.
ViewSheet.CreateViewport.CreateSheetNumber / Name
#076ADVANCED

Room-Cropped View Creator

For each room, create a dedicated floor plan view cropped exactly to the room boundary (with 300mm offset). Name the view "Room - [RoomNumber] [RoomName]".
Expected Result
One cropped floor plan per room. Crop region is room boundary + 300mm offset. Correct name in Project Browser.
View.SetCropRegionShapeActiveCropRegionShapeManagerRoom boundary to CurveLoop
#077INTERMEDIATE

View Template Applier

Apply a specified view template to all floor plan views in the project that currently have no template assigned. Report how many views were updated.
Expected Result
All untemplate'd floor plans now have the template applied. Count of updated views reported. Already-templated views unchanged.
View.ViewTemplateIdView.IsTemplateApplyViewTemplateParameters
#078BEGINNER

Isometric 3D View Creator

Create a named 3D orthographic view with an isometric camera orientation. Ensure the view shows all elements (no section box). Name it "ISO - Full Building".
Expected Result
A new 3D view in Project Browser showing the full model in classic isometric orientation. IsSectionBoxActive = false.
View3D.CreateIsometricViewOrientation3DIsSectionBoxActive
#079ADVANCED

Room Section Box

For a selected room, create a 3D view with the section box exactly fitting the room's bounding box (+ 500mm offset all around). Isolate only elements inside the section box.
Expected Result
New 3D view with active section box fitted to the room. Only elements within the room visible. Section box correct dimensions.
View3D.SetSectionBoxIsSectionBoxActiveRoom bounding box
#080INTERMEDIATE

Viewport Scale Standardiser

Find all viewports on sheets where the view scale does not match the sheet's intended scale (e.g. not 1:100 for floor plans). List mismatches and offer a batch fix.
Expected Result
Report of viewports with non-standard scale. After fix, all floor plan viewports set to 1:100. Other view types unchanged.
Viewport.ViewIdView.ScaleViewSheet.GetAllViewports
#079bADVANCED

Auto Callout Generator

For every room flagged with a parameter "Needs Enlarged Plan = Yes", automatically create a callout view in the parent floor plan, cropped to the room, at 1:50 scale.
Expected Result
One callout view per flagged room, correctly cropped and scaled. Callout bubble visible in parent floor plan.
ViewPlan.CreateCalloutRoom boundaryScale = 50
#080bBEGINNER

Four Elevation Views Creator

Create North, South, East and West elevation views of the whole building. Set each view's far clip plane to the building depth + 5000mm. Name them "Elevation - N/S/E/W".
Expected Result
Four correctly-oriented elevation views in Project Browser. Building fully visible in each. Far clip correct.
ViewSection.CreateElevationElevationMarkerViewFamilyType.Elevation
#081INTERMEDIATE

Category Visibility Snapshot

Capture the category visibility settings of the active view (all categories, on/off/halftone). Export as JSON. Restore the snapshot on a different view of the same type.
Expected Result
JSON snapshot captured and applied correctly to target view. All category visibilities match source exactly.
View.GetCategoryHiddenSetCategoryHiddenHalftone
#082EXPERT

View Naming Convention Enforcer

Enforce a naming convention across all views: floor plans must be "FP-[Level]-[Scale]", sections "SEC-[Number]", elevations "EL-[Direction]". Detect and report violations. Offer a bulk rename that fixes each pattern.
Expected Result
All views renamed to match convention. Report of original → new name for every changed view. No name collisions after renaming.
View.NameViewTypeRegex pattern match
#083INTERMEDIATE

Legend Creator from Categories

Create a drafting view and populate it with one filled region per wall type, each labelled with the wall type name and total usage count from the model.
Expected Result
A drafting view containing coloured filled regions representing each wall type, with text labels showing name and count.
ViewDrafting.CreateFilledRegion.CreateTextNote
#084ADVANCED

Title Block Parameter Auto-Filler

Read project information parameters (project name, number, address, architect) and write them to all title block instances on all sheets. Handle missing parameters gracefully.
Expected Result
All title blocks across all sheets show correct project data. Missing parameters reported with a fallback default value used.
ProjectInfoFamilyInstance (title block)Parameter.Set
#085INTERMEDIATE

Element Override by Level

Apply a different line weight and colour override to elements on each level in the active 3D view. Level 1 = blue, Level 2 = green, Level 3 = yellow, etc.
Expected Result
3D view shows elements colour-coded by level. Each level's colour is distinct. Override applied per-element, not per-category.
View.SetElementOverridesOverrideGraphicSettingsper-element override
#086BEGINNER

Scope Box Creator

Create a scope box that fits the extents of all elements on Level 1. Assign this scope box to all Level 1 floor plan views.
Expected Result
A scope box named "Level 1 Extents" fitted to model extents. All Level 1 plan views use this scope box.
ScopeBoxView.get_Parameter(VIEW_SCOPE_BOX_PARAM)BoundingBoxXYZ
#087EXPERT

Room Interior Perspective Generator

For each room, create a perspective 3D camera view positioned at standing eye level (1700mm) at the room centre, looking toward the main entrance door. Name each view "INT - [RoomName]".
Expected Result
One interior perspective per room. Camera at correct position and height. View direction toward the door. Perspective camera (not orthographic).
View3D.CreatePerspectiveViewOrientation3DRoom centroid + door lookup
#088INTERMEDIATE

Phase View Set Creator

Duplicate each floor plan view once per construction phase defined in the project. Assign the corresponding phase to each duplicate. Name: "[OriginalName] - Phase [N]".
Expected Result
One copy of each floor plan per phase. Each copy's view phase set correctly. All in Project Browser with correct names.
View.DuplicateVIEW_PHASEPhaseArray
#089ADVANCED

View Filter Manager

Build a tool that lists all ParameterFilterElements in the project. For each filter, show: name, categories it applies to, rules defined, and which views use it. Allow deleting unused filters.
Expected Result
Complete audit of all view filters. Unused filters (applied to zero views) clearly flagged. Deletion removes filter from all references.
ParameterFilterElementView.GetFiltersView.RemoveFilter
#090INTERMEDIATE

Revision Cloud Auto-Tagger

Find all revision clouds in the project. For each cloud, ensure it has a revision tag placed at the cloud centroid. Create missing tags for untagged clouds.
Expected Result
Every revision cloud has a revision tag at its centre. Count of newly-added tags reported. No duplicate tags created.
RevisionCloudIndependentTagOST_RevisionClouds
#091BEGINNER

Dependent View Creator

For the active view, create 4 dependent views representing North/South/East/West quadrants of the building. Set each dependent view's crop region to its quadrant.
Expected Result
4 dependent views created from active parent view. Each cropped to one quadrant. All 4 together = full building without overlap.
View.Duplicate(WithDetailing)CropRegionDependentView
#092ADVANCED

Smart Sheet Layout Engine

Given a list of views and a sheet size, automatically calculate optimal viewport positions to fill the sheet with minimal wasted space. Place all viewports at calculated positions.
Expected Result
All views placed on sheet with no overlapping viewports. Sheet space utilisation > 80%. Viewport positions respect title block margins.
Viewport.CreateViewport.SetBoxCenterbin-packing algorithm
#093INTERMEDIATE

Room Color Scheme Creator

Create a color fill scheme for rooms that assigns a unique colour to each department (grouped by a "Department" parameter). Apply the scheme to the active floor plan.
Expected Result
Rooms colour-coded by department in active plan view. Legend visible. Each department gets a distinct, readable colour.
ColorFillSchemeSpatialFieldViewColorSchemeEntry
#094EXPERT

View to Image Exporter

Export the active view as a PNG image at 300 DPI to a user-specified file path. Handle views of any type. Set export resolution, image size, and pixel rounding.
Expected Result
PNG file created at specified path. Image correctly shows the view content at 300 DPI. File size proportional to view size.
ImageExportOptionsdoc.ExportImageExportRange.CurrentView

sections.title: Dimensions, Tags & Annotations
sections.content:
📏
// CHAPTER 05

Dimensions, Tags & Annotations

Dimensions, TextNotes, Tags, Keynotes, Symbols and Detail components

22 challenges
#095BEGINNER

Auto-Dimension All Walls

Automatically place dimensions on all walls in the active floor plan view. Dimension from face-to-face using wall face references. Group dimensions by direction (horizontal/vertical).
Expected Result
All walls dimensioned in active floor plan. Horizontal walls have horizontal dimension strings, vertical walls have vertical. No dimension line overlaps.
doc.Create.NewDimensionReferenceArrayWall face references
#096INTERMEDIATE

Overall Building Dimension

Place a single overall dimension string showing the total building width and total building height in both X and Y directions in the active floor plan.
Expected Result
Two dimension strings (X and Y) showing overall building extents. Dimensions reference the outermost wall faces. Correct total value in mm.
Outermost face referencesNewDimensionLine placement offset
#097INTERMEDIATE

Grid Dimension String

Create a dimension string that runs through all vertical grid lines in order, showing the spacing between them. Place it 3000mm above the top wall.
Expected Result
One dimension string with a segment per grid bay. All grid spacings shown. Dimension references the actual grid line references (not endpoints).
new Reference(grid)ReferenceArraySorted grids
#098ADVANCED

Spot Elevation on Slabs

Place spot elevation annotations at the four corners and centre of every floor in the active floor plan view. Format: "FL +3.200".
Expected Result
5 spot elevations per floor. Correct elevation value formatted to 3 decimal places in metres. No missing corners.
SpotDimensionNewSpotElevationFloor corner points
#099INTERMEDIATE

Door Tag Auto-Placer

Place a door tag on every untagged door in the active view. Position the tag 300mm above the door centre. Ensure no duplicate tags are created.
Expected Result
All doors have exactly one tag. Tags correctly positioned above each door. Already-tagged doors skipped.
IndependentTag.CreateTagMode.TM_ADDBY_CATEGORYGetTaggedLocalElements
#100BEGINNER

Room Tag Placer

Place a room tag at the centroid of every untagged room in the active view. Ensure tags are inside the room boundary and not overlapping.
Expected Result
All rooms have a room tag at their centre point. No tags outside room boundary. No duplicate tags.
SpatialElementTagOST_RoomTagsRoom.IsPointInRoom
#101ADVANCED

Dimension Value Override Tool

Find all dimensions whose actual value has been manually overridden (dimension value text ≠ actual measurement). List them with original vs override text and offer to clear all overrides.
Expected Result
List of overridden dimensions with actual measurement and override text. After fix, DimensionSegment.ValueOverride = null for all.
DimensionSegment.ValueOverrideDimension.SegmentsValueString
#102INTERMEDIATE

Keynote Audit Tool

List all keynotes in the project. Group by keynote key number. Find any keynotes whose key doesn't exist in the keynote table and flag them as broken.
Expected Result
Complete keynote audit: valid keynotes grouped by key, plus a list of broken keynotes that reference non-existent keys.
KeynoteParametersTEXT_KEYNOTE_NUMBERKeynoteTable
#103INTERMEDIATE

Detail Component Placer

Load a detail component family (insulation symbol) and place it along a selected wall's face in the active detail view at 1000mm intervals.
Expected Result
Insulation symbols spaced 1000mm apart along the selected wall face. All in the active view's plane. Correct family loaded and used.
NewDetailComponentDetailFamilyCurve.Evaluate intervals
#104BEGINNER

North Arrow Placer

Load a North Arrow family and place it in the bottom-right corner of the active view's crop region. Scale to match the view scale.
Expected Result
North arrow visible in active view's bottom-right area. Arrow correctly oriented. Scale proportional to view scale.
ViewCropRegionShapeManagerNewFamilyInstance annotationCrop box corners
#105ADVANCED

Multi-Segment Dimension Editor

Find all multi-segment dimension strings with more than 5 segments. For each, add a text note showing the total cumulative length above the dimension. Update the total when individual segments change.
Expected Result
Multi-segment dims have a text note with correct total. Total = sum of all segments. Text positioned above centre of dimension line.
Dimension.SegmentsDimensionSegment.ValueTextNote.Create
#106EXPERT

Automatic Section Callout Dimensioner

For every section view, automatically place a full set of dimensions: floor-to-floor heights, slab thickness, door/window rough openings, and overall building height. All in a single run.
Expected Result
Each section view fully dimensioned with floor heights, slab thicknesses, and all openings. All references valid and dimension lines non-overlapping.
ViewSection elementsMultipleReference dimsLevel references
#107INTERMEDIATE

Text Style Standardiser

Find all TextNotes in the project that use a non-standard text type. Replace them with the approved text type while preserving position and content.
Expected Result
All text notes using approved type. Content and position unchanged. Count of replaced notes reported.
TextNote.TextNoteTypeChangeTypeIdTextNoteType collector
#108BEGINNER

Level Elevation Label Creator

Place a spot elevation annotation on every level in the active section or elevation view, labeled with the level name and its elevation value in metres (e.g. "L2 +3.600").
Expected Result
Every level line in the section/elevation view has a text annotation. Correct elevation in metres. Annotations don't overlap.
Level.ElevationTextNote.CreateSection view elements
#109ADVANCED

Structural Beam Annotation

In a structural framing plan view, place a tag on every beam showing its span length and profile size (from type parameters). Position tags at beam midpoints along the beam direction.
Expected Result
Every beam tagged at midpoint with "[ProfileSize] L=[SpanMM]mm". Tags aligned with beam direction. No overlapping tags for parallel beams.
STRUCTURAL_SECTION_SHAPECurve.LengthTag orientation
#110INTERMEDIATE

Filled Region Hatch Style Changer

Find all filled regions in the project using a specific fill pattern. Replace that pattern with a different fill pattern across all views. Report how many regions were updated.
Expected Result
All filled regions using old pattern now show new pattern. No orphaned pattern references. Count of updated regions per view.
FilledRegion.GetFillPatternIdFillPatternElementOverrideGraphicSettings
#111BEGINNER

Area Tag Auto-Placer

Place area tags on all area elements in the active area plan view. Position tags at each area's centroid point.
Expected Result
Every area element has one tag at its centre. Areas not in the active view are skipped. No duplicate tags.
AreaOST_AreasSpatialElementTag
#112ADVANCED

Window Opening Dimensioner

In the active elevation view, automatically dimension all window openings: width and height of each rough opening. Place width dims below window, height dims to the right.
Expected Result
Every window in the elevation view has width and height dimensions. References are to the rough opening faces. Non-overlapping layout.
FamilyInstance.GetReferencesFamilyInstanceReferenceTypeNewDimension
#113INTERMEDIATE

Detail Line to Model Line Converter

Find all detail lines in the active view. For each, create an equivalent model line at the same position and with the same line style. Report the count converted.
Expected Result
Model lines created matching every detail line. Same start/end points and line style. Original detail lines optionally deleted (offer choice).
DetailLine → ModelCurvedoc.Create.NewModelCurveGraphicsStyle
#114EXPERT

Full Drawing Set Annotator

Process every sheet in the project. For each floor plan viewport, place: north arrow, scale bar, key plan, and zone labels. For each section viewport, place level elevation markers. All annotations parametrically positioned relative to viewport boundaries.
Expected Result
Every sheet has complete annotation set. All elements correctly positioned relative to their viewport. No annotations outside sheet boundary.
Viewport.GetBoxOutlineMulti-annotation placementSheet iteration
#115INTERMEDIATE

Orphaned Tag Cleaner

Find all tags whose host element has been deleted (orphaned tags). List them with their view and position. Offer to delete all orphaned tags in one operation.
Expected Result
All orphaned tags identified. After cleanup, zero orphaned tags remain. Count of deleted tags reported per view.
IndependentTag.GetTaggedLocalElementNull checkdoc.Delete
#116BEGINNER

Symbol Placer — North Arrow Grid

Place a generic annotation symbol (e.g. cloud bubble) at every grid intersection in the active view to visually mark intersection points for review.
Expected Result
One annotation symbol at every grid intersection. Correct count = (X-grids) × (Y-grids). All visible in the active plan view.
Grid intersection calcNewFamilyInstance annotationXYZ from grid intersect

sections.title: Families & Types
sections.content:
🧩
// CHAPTER 06

Families & Types

Loading, placing, editing, creating family types and geometry programmatically

20 challenges
#117BEGINNER

Family Audit Report

List all families loaded in the project: name, category, number of types, whether it's an in-place family, and how many instances are placed in the model.
Expected Result
Complete family inventory: Family Name | Category | Type Count | In-Place | Instance Count. Sorted by category then name.
FamilyGetFamilySymbolIdsFamilyInstance count
#118INTERMEDIATE

Unused Type Purger

Find all FamilySymbols with zero placed instances. List them grouped by family. Offer to delete all unused types (keeping at least one type per family).
Expected Result
Unused type list. After purge, zero unused types remain. Each family retains ≥1 type. Total memory reduction reported.
FamilySymbol instance countdoc.DeleteKeep-one logic
#119INTERMEDIATE

Type Catalog Viewer

For a user-selected family, display all its types with all their type parameters and values in a formatted report. Highlight differences between types.
Expected Result
Table: TypeName | Param1 | Param2 | … for all types. Cells that differ from the first type are highlighted in the output.
FamilySymbol.ParametersFamilyManager.TypesType comparison
#120ADVANCED

Family Type Creator from CSV

Read a CSV string of type names and parameter values. Open the family document of a selected family. Create one new type per CSV row with the specified parameter values. Save and reload.
Expected Result
New family types visible in project browser matching CSV data. Parameter values correct. Family reloaded with new types applied to existing instances.
FamilyManager.NewTypeFamilyManager.Setdoc.LoadFamily back
#121ADVANCED

Adaptive Component Array

Place an adaptive component family along a user-selected spline curve at equal intervals. Each instance's adaptive points snap to the curve at the correct parameter value.
Expected Result
N evenly-spaced adaptive component instances along the spline. Adaptive points on the curve. Instances correctly oriented to the curve tangent.
AdaptiveComponentInstanceUtilsSetInstanceAdaptivePointElementIdsCurve.Evaluate
#122EXPERT

Programmatic Family Builder

Create a new family document from scratch (Generic Model template), add two instance parameters (Width, Height), create a rectangular extrusion driven by these parameters, add reference planes and dimensions, save and load into the project.
Expected Result
New parametric family loaded into project. Changing Width/Height parameters updates geometry. Family has correct reference planes and dimensions.
Application.NewFamilyDocumentFamilyManager.AddParameterFamilyCreate.NewExtrusion
#123INTERMEDIATE

In-Place Family Converter

Find all in-place families in the project. For each, extract its geometry and recreate it as a loadable generic model family. Report which in-place families were successfully converted.
Expected Result
Equivalent loadable families created. Geometry matches original visually. In-place families can be deleted after verification.
Family.IsInPlaceGeometry extractionFamilyCreate.NewExtrusion
#124BEGINNER

Missing Family Loader

Scan the document for any unresolved family references (families that fail to load). For each, prompt the user to locate the .rfa file and reload it. Report success/failure per family.
Expected Result
All previously unresolved families reloaded. Element display restored where family was missing. Failed reloads listed with file path issues.
Family.IsEditabledoc.LoadFamilyUnresolvedElements
#125INTERMEDIATE

Shared Parameter Adder to Families

Add a shared parameter from a shared parameter file to all door and window families in the project. The parameter should be an instance text parameter visible in schedules.
Expected Result
New shared parameter visible on all door and window instances in properties panel and in schedules. Correct group assignment.
SharedParameterFileDefinitionGroupFamilyManager.AddParameter(ExternalDefinition)
#126ADVANCED

Family Parameter Formula Setter

Open a family document. Set a formula on a type parameter so that it equals 2× another parameter. Verify the formula evaluates correctly for all existing types.
Expected Result
Formula set and evaluated. All types show double the source parameter value. Formula visible in Family Types dialog.
FamilyManager.SetFormulaFamilyParameterFormula string
#127INTERMEDIATE

Nested Family Analyser

For a selected family, identify all nested families inside it. For each nested family, report name, category, and how many times it appears inside the parent.
Expected Result
Complete nesting tree for the selected family. Each nested family name, category, and instance count within the parent. Works recursively 2 levels deep.
FamilyDocument nested familiesFamilyInstance within familyFamily.IsEditable
#128BEGINNER

Family Instance Substitutor

Let the user select multiple family instances of one type. Replace all of them with a different type from the same family (user chooses target type from a list).
Expected Result
All selected instances changed to new type. Location and host preserved. Host constraints re-applied correctly.
fi.Symbol setter or ChangeTypeIdFamilyInstance type change
#129ADVANCED

Family Geometry Validator

Open each family in the project. Validate: all reference planes named, no empty solids, extrusions have material assigned, subcategory set correctly. Report violations per family.
Expected Result
Validation report per family: PASS/FAIL per check with details. Works across all families without user intervention.
ReferencePlane.NameExtrusion materialFamily.GetFamilySymbolIds
#130EXPERT

Curtain Wall Panel Family Generator

Programmatically create a curtain panel family with a frame extrusion and a glazing panel. The frame width should be a type parameter. Load into project and apply to all curtain walls.
Expected Result
Custom curtain panel family loaded. Applied to all curtain walls. Frame width parameter drives frame geometry correctly when changed.
Curtain panel family templateFamilyCreate.NewExtrusionCurtainGrid.SetPanelType
#131INTERMEDIATE

Family Reload Batch Tool

Given a folder path, reload all families found in that folder into the current project, overwriting existing families with matching names. Log which families were reloaded vs skipped (not found in project).
Expected Result
All matching families reloaded from folder. Existing instances updated. Log: "Reloaded: 12, Not found in project: 3, Errors: 0".
Directory.GetFilesdoc.LoadFamily with IFamilyLoadOptionsBatch loop
#132BEGINNER

Furniture Layout Assistant

Place a desk family at the centre of every room tagged as "Office" at 1700mm from the main wall, oriented to face the entrance door.
Expected Result
One desk per Office room, 1700mm from wall, facing door. All desks placed at floor level. Correct family type used.
Room.Name filterNewFamilyInstance level-basedLocation offset from wall
#133ADVANCED

Column Cap Generator

For every structural column, create an in-place solid extrusion at the top of the column representing a column cap (wider than column by 100mm, 200mm tall). Each extrusion aligned to column top.
Expected Result
One solid cap extrusion per column, positioned correctly at column top. Cap wider than column by 100mm. All visible in 3D.
In-place family or DirectShapeColumn top elevationDirectShape.CreateElement
#134INTERMEDIATE

Symbol Scale Adjuster

Find all annotation symbols placed in views with a scale different from the intended symbol scale. Adjust each symbol's scale to match the view scale so symbols appear consistently sized on paper.
Expected Result
All annotation symbols scaled correctly relative to their view. Paper size of symbol uniform across different-scale views.
AnnotationSymbolFamilyInstance scaleView.Scale
#135EXPERT

Direct Shape IFC Importer

Read a simplified IFC-like JSON structure describing a set of box-shaped spaces. Create a DirectShape for each space using a solid box geometry, assigning the correct IFC category. Show all shapes in a 3D view.
Expected Result
One DirectShape per JSON entry, correct size and position, correct BuiltInCategory. All visible and selectable in 3D. Category matches IFC type.
DirectShape.CreateElementTessellatedShapeBuilderBuiltInCategory mapping
#136INTERMEDIATE

Type Mark Auto-Generator

For every family type in the project, generate a unique Type Mark following the convention "[CategoryCode]-[FamilyInitials]-[SequenceNumber]". Write it to the SYMBOL_ID_PARAM parameter.
Expected Result
Every family type has a unique Type Mark. Format matches convention. No two types share the same mark. Visible in type properties.
FamilySymbolSYMBOL_ID_PARAMNaming convention logic

sections.title: Transactions & Error Handling
sections.content:
⚙️
// CHAPTER 07

Transactions & Error Handling

Transaction patterns, rollback strategies, failure preprocessors, and model integrity

14 challenges
#137BEGINNER

Safe Batch Deleter

Delete a list of element IDs provided as input. Wrap each deletion in a SubTransaction so failures on individual elements don't abort the whole operation. Report per-element success/failure.
Expected Result
Successfully deleted elements removed. Failed deletions rolled back individually. Main transaction still commits. Full per-element log provided.
SubTransactiondoc.DeletePer-item try/catch
#138INTERMEDIATE

Undo-Friendly Multi-Step Operation

Perform 5 model changes (create 5 walls) using a TransactionGroup so the entire operation is a single Undo step for the user. Verify with a single Ctrl+Z undoes all 5 walls.
Expected Result
5 walls created. One Ctrl+Z removes all 5. TransactionGroup.Assimilate() used correctly.
TransactionGroup.AssimilateNested Transactions
#139INTERMEDIATE

Warning Suppressor

Build a command that creates 10 walls deliberately causing "Highlighted walls overlap" warnings. Implement IFailuresPreprocessor to silently dismiss all warnings of this type only.
Expected Result
Walls created with no warning dialogs appearing. Warnings of the specified FailureDefinitionId suppressed. Other warnings still shown.
IFailuresPreprocessorFailureHandlingOptionsDeleteWarning
#140ADVANCED

Transaction Status Monitor

Build a wrapper class for Transaction that logs start time, end time, status, and any exception to a List<string>. Use it for all operations in a complex workflow and display the log after.
Expected Result
Full transaction log: "TX 'Create Walls' Started 14:02:01 | Committed 14:02:02 | Duration 834ms". All transactions tracked automatically.
Transaction wrapper patternTransactionStatusStopwatch
#141ADVANCED

Failure Resolution Handler

Implement IFailuresProcessor (not just preprocessor) to intercept errors during transaction. For "Wall is slightly off axis" errors, offer two resolution choices: Fix automatically or keep as-is.
Expected Result
Custom failure dialog shown instead of Revit's default. User choice applied correctly. Off-axis walls either straightened or kept per choice.
IFailuresProcessorProcessFailuresFailureResolution
#142INTERMEDIATE

Locked Element Handler

Attempt to modify an element that may be locked/pinned. Detect if the element is pinned, unpin it, modify it, then re-pin it. Handle the case where unpinning is not allowed.
Expected Result
Pinned element modified successfully after temporary unpin. Element re-pinned after change. If element can't be unpinned, graceful error message.
Element.PinnedElement.Pinned setterException handling
#143BEGINNER

Document Modified Checker

Before running any operation, check if the document has unsaved changes. If it does, prompt the user to save first. Only proceed with the operation after a clean save or user override.
Expected Result
Modified document triggers save prompt. Operation proceeds only after save or explicit override. Unsaved document abort is graceful.
doc.IsModifieddoc.Save()TaskDialog with options
#144EXPERT

Model Health Check Suite

Run a suite of 10 model health checks: duplicate marks, orphaned rooms, off-axis walls, elements outside project extents, locked elements not on design options, etc. Generate a full HTML health report.
Expected Result
HTML report with each check: PASS/FAIL, count of issues, list of affected element IDs. Severity rating for each check category.
Multiple collectorsValidation logicHTML string builder

sections.title: Events & Automation
sections.content:
📡
// CHAPTER 08

Events & Automation

Application and document events, automatic reactions and monitoring

16 challenges
#145BEGINNER

Document Open Logger

Subscribe to DocumentOpened event. Every time a document opens, append a log entry: timestamp, document title, and file path to a text file on the C: drive.
Expected Result
Log file grows with one entry per opened document. Entries have correct timestamp, title and path. Works for all document types.
DocumentOpenedDocumentOpenedEventArgsFile.AppendAllText
#146INTERMEDIATE

Auto-Save on Document Change

Subscribe to DocumentChanged. After every 10 change events on the same document, automatically save the document. Use a counter per document to track changes.
Expected Result
Document auto-saves every 10 change events. Counter resets after save. Works independently for multiple open documents.
DocumentChangedDictionary<Document, int> counterdoc.Save()
#147INTERMEDIATE

New Element Alert

Subscribe to DocumentChanged. When a new wall is added, immediately show a non-blocking status bar message: "Wall added: ID [x] of type [walltype]". Do not block the user workflow.
Expected Result
Status bar updates within 1 second of wall creation. Message shows correct ID and type. No dialogs interrupt workflow.
GetAddedElementIdsuiapp.SetStatusbarTextDocumentChanged
#148ADVANCED

View Activation Logger

Subscribe to ViewActivated event. Log every view switch: timestamp, previous view name/type, new view name/type. Store in memory and provide a command to display the view history timeline.
Expected Result
Chronological view history available on demand. Entry per view switch with correct names, types and timestamps. Works across multiple documents.
ViewActivatedViewActivatedEventArgsIn-memory log
#149ADVANCED

Pre-Save Validator

Subscribe to DocumentSaving event. Before every save, run a quick validation: check for duplicate marks, untagged rooms, and missing sheet numbers. If violations found, cancel the save and show a report.
Expected Result
Save blocked when violations exist. Report shows what's wrong. Clean model saves normally. Cancel works without data loss.
DocumentSavinge.Cancel()Validation logic
#150INTERMEDIATE

Document Close Backup

Subscribe to DocumentClosing. Before a document closes, copy it to a backup folder with a timestamp in the filename. Skip if document has no changes since last backup.
Expected Result
Backup file created in designated folder with timestamped name. Unchanged documents skipped (no redundant backups). Backup path configurable.
DocumentClosingdoc.PathNameFile.Copy
#151EXPERT

Element Genealogy Tracker

Track the "lifecycle" of every wall: when it was created (added event), when it was modified (changed event with what changed), and when it was deleted. Store in a JSON log per session and display on demand.
Expected Result
Full wall lifecycle log: created timestamp, list of change events with parameter names, deleted timestamp. JSON output well-formed.
DocumentChanged GetAdded/Modified/DeletedGetModifiedElementIdsJSON serialisation
#152BEGINNER

Application Startup Greeter

Subscribe to ApplicationInitialized event. When Revit fully loads, show a status bar message: "Studio Plugin v2.0 Ready — [n] commands loaded". Load a configuration file at startup.
Expected Result
Status message appears once on Revit startup. Config file loaded. No blocking dialogs. Message disappears after 3 seconds.
ApplicationInitializedUIApplicationSetStatusbarText
#153ADVANCED

Undo/Redo Monitor

Subscribe to DocumentChanged and detect when an undo or redo operation occurs (TransactionNames will be from previous transactions). Log each undo/redo with the name of the operation that was undone/redone.
Expected Result
Undo/redo events correctly identified and logged. Transaction name restored correctly. Distinguishes undo from redo events.
DocumentChangedEventArgs.GetTransactionNamesUndo detection

sections.title: External Events & Modeless UI
sections.content:
🪟
// CHAPTER 09

External Events & Modeless UI

IExternalEventHandler, ExternalEvent, WPF modeless dialogs and thread-safe Revit calls

14 challenges
#154INTERMEDIATE

Modeless Parameter Editor

Build a modeless WPF window that lists all walls and lets the user edit the Mark parameter of any wall in a text box. Clicking Apply uses ExternalEvent to write the value to Revit.
Expected Result
WPF window stays open while working in Revit. Edited mark written correctly via ExternalEvent. Window can be re-opened without duplicate events.
IExternalEventHandlerExternalEvent.CreateWPF Window
#155INTERMEDIATE

Real-Time Element Picker

Modeless window with a "Pick Element" button. Clicking starts a PickObject session. Selected element ID and category appear in the window immediately after picking.
Expected Result
Window remains open through pick session. Selected element details update after pick. Multiple picks work without reopening window.
ExternalEvent + IExternalEventHandleruidoc.Selection.PickObjectResult displayed in WPF
#156ADVANCED

Live Wall Counter Dock

Create a docked panel (IDockablePaneProvider) that shows a live count of all walls, floors, and doors in the current document. Count updates whenever the document changes (using Idling or DocumentChanged).
Expected Result
Dockable panel visible in Revit UI. Counts accurate and update within 2 seconds of model changes. Shows "No document open" when no doc loaded.
IDockablePaneProviderDockablePaneIdRegisterDockablePane
#157ADVANCED

Background Sync Handler

Simulate syncing selected element data to an external API. Use a background Task to simulate the HTTP call (Thread.Sleep), then use ExternalEvent to write the API response back as an element parameter.
Expected Result
Revit UI stays responsive during "API call". After sync, element parameter updated with API response. No cross-thread exceptions.
Task.RunExternalEvent.Raise on completionThread safety
#158EXPERT

BIM Dashboard Panel

Create a full dockable WPF dashboard showing: element counts by category (bar chart), recent transaction history, active view name, and a "Quick Actions" toolbar. All data live-updating via ExternalEvent.
Expected Result
Rich dockable panel with bar chart and live data. All counts update when model changes. Quick action buttons trigger ExternalEvent operations correctly.
IDockablePaneProviderExternalEventWPF DataBinding + LiveCharts or custom canvas
#159INTERMEDIATE

Async Room Tagger

Build a command that tags all rooms using a background thread for preparation (collecting room data) and then uses ExternalEvent to place the tags in the Revit document. Show a progress dialog during processing.
Expected Result
Progress dialog updates during collection phase. Tags placed after ExternalEvent fires. Revit remains responsive throughout. Tags correctly placed.
Task + ExternalEvent patternIProgress<T>SpatialElementTag
#160ADVANCED

Multi-Command Queue

Implement a command queue system: multiple IExternalEventHandler operations queued from a WPF window. A single ExternalEvent processes one command per Raise(). Show queue status in the window.
Expected Result
Commands processed in FIFO order. WPF queue display updates after each command. Failed commands report error and continue. Queue can be paused.
Queue<IOperation>Single ExternalEventDequeue in Execute()

sections.title: Dynamic Model Update (DMU)
sections.content:
🔄
// CHAPTER 10

Dynamic Model Update (DMU)

IUpdater, change triggers, automatic parameter propagation and constraint maintenance

12 challenges
#161INTERMEDIATE

Auto Wall Mark Updater

Register an IUpdater that fires when a wall's geometry changes. When triggered, update the wall's Mark to reflect its current length (e.g. "W-4500mm" for a 4500mm wall).
Expected Result
Wall Mark auto-updates when wall length changes. No manual intervention needed. Correct length in mm reflected in Mark immediately after change.
IUpdater.ExecuteElement.GetChangeTypeGeometryRegisterUpdater + AddTrigger
#162ADVANCED

Room Area to Parameter Propagator

Register an IUpdater on Room elements. When room boundaries change (geometry change), recompute the area and write it in m² to a custom "Computed Area m2" project parameter on the room.
Expected Result
Computed Area m2 parameter auto-updates whenever room boundaries move. Value accurate to 3 decimal places. Works for complex room shapes.
IUpdater on OST_RoomsRoom.Area × 0.0929GetChangeTypeGeometry
#163ADVANCED

Door Host Wall Validator

Register an IUpdater on doors. When a door is added or its host wall changes, verify the host wall is of type "Exterior" or "Interior". If not, add a warning text note next to the door.
Expected Result
Warning text note placed near any door on a non-standard wall type. Note removed automatically when door moved to a standard wall. No DMU infinite loops.
IUpdater GetAddedElementIds + GetModifiedElementIdsDoor.HostTextNote.Create in sub-tx
#164INTERMEDIATE

Level Elevation Sync

Register an IUpdater on Level elements. When any level's elevation changes, automatically update all elements attached to that level (walls, floors) to maintain their offset from the level.
Expected Result
Moving Level 1 up by 300mm keeps all walls/floors at the same relative position. No elements fall through or float in air.
IUpdater on LevelGetChangeTypeParameter(LEVEL_ELEV)Wall offset update
#165EXPERT

Parametric Railing Auto-Updater

Register an IUpdater on stair elements. When stair run height changes, automatically update the attached railing top rail height to maintain code-compliant height (1100mm above each nosing).
Expected Result
Railing height adjusts automatically when stair riser height changes. Maintains 1100mm from nosing. Works for all stair types.
Stairs → Railing relationshipIUpdater on StairsRailing parameter update
#166ADVANCED

Cross-Document DMU Mirror

Register an IUpdater in Document A. When a wall moves in Doc A, mirror that change to an equivalent wall in Document B (both documents open). Use the wall's Mark to identify the corresponding wall.
Expected Result
Moving Wall "W-001" in Doc A moves the wall with Mark "W-001" in Doc B by the same amount. Works when both documents are open simultaneously.
IUpdater with multi-documentapp.DocumentsTransaction on Doc B from DMU

sections.title: MEP Systems
sections.content:
🔧
// CHAPTER 11

MEP Systems

Pipes, ducts, conduits, connectors, MEP systems, spaces and HVAC

20 challenges
#167BEGINNER

Pipe Network Reporter

List all pipes in the project with diameter, length, system type, and material. Group by system type. Calculate total pipe run length per system in metres.
Expected Result
Report grouped by piping system type. Total length per system in metres. Overall total pipe length across all systems at bottom.
OST_PipeCurvesRBS_PIPE_DIAMETER_PARAMCURVE_ELEM_LENGTH
#168INTERMEDIATE

Pipe Slope Checker

For all pipes, calculate the actual slope (elevation change / horizontal run). Flag pipes that fall below the minimum required slope (1:100 = 1%). Report pipe ID, calculated slope, and PASS/FAIL.
Expected Result
Report of all pipes with their slope percentage. Non-compliant pipes highlighted. Horizontal pipes (no slope required) excluded with a filter.
Pipe endpoint elevationsLocationCurve endpointsSlope calculation
#169INTERMEDIATE

Duct Pressure Zone Analyser

Traverse a duct network from a selected air handling unit to all terminal devices. Map the network topology (parent-child relationships via connectors) and report the total duct length per branch.
Expected Result
Network tree structure: AHU → main duct branches → terminal devices. Length per branch in mm. Total network length reported.
ConnectorManagerConnector.AllRefsRecursive traversal
#170ADVANCED

Pipe Auto-Router

Given two selected pipe fittings (start and end), automatically route a straight pipe (or L-shaped run with one elbow) between them. The pipe should avoid walls by offsetting if needed.
Expected Result
Pipe run created between start and end fittings. Connectors joined at both ends. No wall penetration. L-shape with one elbow if not in line.
Pipe.CreateConnectorManager.Connectorsc1.ConnectTo(c2)
#171INTERMEDIATE

MEP Space to Room Linker

For all MEP Spaces in the model, link each space to the corresponding architectural Room (matching by location point overlap). Write the room name and number into the space's comments parameter.
Expected Result
All MEP spaces linked to their matching room. Space.ROOM_TAG_COMMENTS shows "Office 101". Unmatched spaces flagged with "No matching room".
Space.RoomRoom.IsPointInRoomOST_MEPSpaces
#172BEGINNER

Duct Clash with Structure

Find all ducts whose bounding box intersects the bounding box of any structural beam. Report the duct-beam clash pairs with estimated conflict zone location.
Expected Result
List of (DuctId, BeamId, ClashLocation) triples. Correct for all bounding box overlaps. Zero false positives for ducts well clear of beams.
BoundingBoxIntersectsFilter per ductOST_StructuralFramingOutline
#173ADVANCED

Cable Tray Fill Calculator

For all cable trays, calculate the fill ratio: (sum of contained cable cross-sections) / (tray cross-section area). Flag trays exceeding 40% fill. Report in a schedule-format output.
Expected Result
Fill ratio per cable tray. Over-capacity trays flagged in red. Summary: total trays, compliant count, non-compliant count.
CableTray dimensionsConduit diameter aggregationFill ratio calc
#174INTERMEDIATE

Electrical Panel Load Calculator

For each electrical panel in the model, traverse its connected circuits. Sum the connected loads (in VA) and compare to panel capacity. Flag overloaded panels.
Expected Result
Report per panel: capacity VA, connected load VA, utilisation %. Panels over 80% flagged as warning, over 100% as critical.
ElectricalSystemRBS_ELEC_PANEL_TOTALLOAD_PARAMCircuit traversal
#175ADVANCED

MEP Clearance Zone Creator

For each mechanical equipment item (AHU, chiller), create a DirectShape volume representing the manufacturer's recommended maintenance clearance zone (from parameters). Show clearance in a 3D view.
Expected Result
Semi-transparent clearance volume around each piece of equipment. Volume sized from equipment parameters. Clashing structure highlighted.
DirectShape.CreateElementBRepBuilder or extrusionOST_MechanicalEquipment
#176EXPERT

HVAC Load to Space Writer
