Compute HVAC heating and cooling loads for each MEP Space using the space's HVAC load parameters. Run a simplified load calculation (area × U-value × ΔT) and write the result back to a custom Space parameter.
Expected Result
Custom HVAC load parameter populated on each space. Value in kW. Calculation based on space area, external wall U-value, and temperature differential.
Space HVAC parametersCompoundStructure U-valueLoad calculation
#177INTERMEDIATE

Pipe Insulation Checker

Find all hot water pipes. For each, check if insulation has been applied (InsulationLiningBase or pipe insulation parameter). Flag uninsulated hot water pipes and calculate missing insulation area in m².
Expected Result
Uninsulated hot water pipes listed. Total missing insulation area in m². Pipes with insulation confirmed. System type filter works correctly.
PipeInsulationOST_PipeInsulationsPipe system type
#178BEGINNER

Floor Drain Placer

Place a floor drain family at the lowest point of each sloped floor in the model. The drain should be positioned at the point of maximum slope convergence.
Expected Result
One drain per sloped floor at the lowest elevation point. Drain hosted on the floor. All drains placed in a single transaction.
SlabShapeEditor.GetPointsMinimum elevation pointNewFamilyInstance on floor

sections.title: Schedules & Data Export
sections.content:
📊
// CHAPTER 12

Schedules & Data Export

ViewSchedule, schedule fields, filters, formatting, and data export

14 challenges
#179BEGINNER

Door Schedule Creator

Programmatically create a door schedule with columns: Mark, Width, Height, Type, Level, Fire Rating. Sort by Level then Mark. Apply a filter to show only ground floor doors.
Expected Result
Door schedule visible in Project Browser with correct columns, sorted and filtered correctly. Matches manual schedule creation.
ViewSchedule.CreateScheduleScheduleFieldScheduleFilter
#180INTERMEDIATE

Multi-Category Quantity Schedule

Create a multi-category schedule listing walls, floors, and roofs together. Include: category, type, area, volume, and level. Group by category and sort by level within each group.
Expected Result
Multi-category schedule showing all three element types. Grouped by category. Area and volume in correct project units.
ViewSchedule.CreateSchedule for multi-catScheduleDefinitionScheduleGrouping
#181ADVANCED

Schedule Data Exporter

Read an existing schedule's data row by row using TableData API. Export every row and column to a CSV file on disk, including the header row with column names.
Expected Result
Valid CSV file with header and all data rows. Encoding correct (UTF-8). Special characters escaped. Large schedules (1000+ rows) exported without timeout.
TableDataGetCellSectionData
#182INTERMEDIATE

Schedule Totals Row

For all area or quantity schedules in the project, ensure a "Totals" row is configured at the bottom of each schedule. Set Grand Total row to visible with custom label "PROJECT TOTAL".
Expected Result
All quantity schedules show grand total row at bottom. Label "PROJECT TOTAL". Numeric columns correctly summed.
ScheduleDefinition.GrandTotalTitleShowGrandTotalScheduleFieldTotals
#183ADVANCED

BOQ Generator

Generate a complete Bill of Quantities for all MEP elements. Group by system type and element category. For each group: element type, count, unit, total quantity, and unit cost (from a parameter). Output as formatted HTML table.
Expected Result
Complete BOQ HTML file. Groups correct. Quantities in correct units. Total cost row per group and grand total. Suitable for printing.
Multiple MEP collectorsLINQ groupingHTML builder
#184EXPERT

Schedule Comparison Tool

Compare two versions of the same schedule (from two documents open simultaneously). Highlight rows where values differ, added rows in green, deleted rows in red. Export as a colour-coded HTML diff report.
Expected Result
HTML diff report showing additions, deletions and changes. Row-level comparison correct. Works for schedules with 200+ rows.
TableData from two docsDiff algorithmHTML colour coding
#185INTERMEDIATE

Calculated Parameter in Schedule

Add a calculated parameter to a wall schedule that computes "Cost per m²" = (wall area × cost rate from type parameter). Configure it as a currency-formatted calculated field.
Expected Result
Calculated field visible in schedule showing cost per m² for each wall. Value correct for all walls. Currency format applied.
ScheduleField.SetFormulaTextCalculatedFieldTypeNumberFormatOptions

sections.title: Structural API
sections.content:
🏛️
// CHAPTER 13

Structural API

Analytical model, load cases, structural connections, reinforcement

14 challenges
#186INTERMEDIATE

Analytical Model Validator

Check all structural elements for a valid analytical model. Flag elements with "Analytical Model Inactive" or mismatched analytical/physical geometry. Report element ID and issue type.
Expected Result
List of elements with analytical model issues. Categories: inactive, geometry mismatch, disconnected. Used in structural QA workflow.
AnalyticalModelGetAnalyticalModelAnalyticalModelStatus
#187ADVANCED

Rebar Cover Checker

For all reinforcement bars in concrete elements, read the cover distance parameter. Flag any rebar where the cover is below the minimum (25mm for internal, 40mm for external elements). Report with element ID and current cover value.
Expected Result
Non-compliant rebar list with element ID, cover distance, and environment type. Compliant rebar count also shown.
RebarOST_RebarREBAR_COVER_TYPE
#188ADVANCED

Structural Load Assigner

Create point loads at all column base locations. Load value = column self-weight (volume × density = 7850 kg/m³ for steel). Assign to "Dead" load case. Show loads in structural plan.
Expected Result
One point load per column base. Load magnitude = self-weight in kN. Assigned to Dead load case. Visible in structural analysis view.
PointLoad.CreateLoadCaseColumn Solid.Volume
#189EXPERT

Structural Connection Auto-Creator

Detect all beam-to-column connections in the structural model. For each connection point, create a structural connection family instance from a loaded connection family. Set connection type based on beam size.
Expected Result
Connection family placed at every beam-column node. Correct connection type assigned based on beam cross-section. All connections visible in structural views.
StructuralConnectionHandlerStructuralFraming intersectionConnection family placement
#190INTERMEDIATE

Footing Size from Column Load

For each isolated foundation, read the column above it, compute the required footing size based on a simplified bearing pressure formula (Load / Bearing Capacity = Area), and write the required size to a parameter.
Expected Result
Required footing size in mm² written to each foundation element. Values based on column self-weight and soil bearing capacity (hardcoded).
StructuralFoundationColumn volume × densityFooting parameters

sections.title: Materials & Rendering
sections.content:
🎨
// CHAPTER 14

Materials & Rendering

Creating and editing materials, render appearances, fill patterns and line styles

12 challenges
#191BEGINNER

Material Usage Report

List all materials in the project with: name, class, colour, transparency, how many element types reference it, and total surface area that material covers in m².
Expected Result
Complete material inventory. Usage count per material. Total surface area in m². Materials with zero usage flagged.
MaterialCompoundStructure.GetMaterialIdFace.Area
#192INTERMEDIATE

Material Colour Standardiser

Find all materials whose name matches a known material (Concrete, Brick, Glass, Steel) but whose surface colour doesn't match the company standard colours. Update non-standard colours to the standard.
Expected Result
All concrete materials now show correct grey, brick materials correct red, etc. Report of how many materials were corrected.
Material.ColorColor(R,G,B)Material.Name.Contains
#193ADVANCED

Fill Pattern Creator

Programmatically create a custom diagonal hatch fill pattern (45° lines at 3mm spacing). Assign it as the cut fill pattern for a new concrete material.
Expected Result
New fill pattern visible in Manage → Fill Patterns. Applied to concrete material's cut representation. Visible in cut views at correct spacing.
FillPatternElement.CreateFillPatternFillGrid
#194INTERMEDIATE

Render Appearance Batch Setter

For all materials in the project, if the render appearance (Asset) is missing, assign a default render appearance from the material library matching the material's class.
Expected Result
All materials have a render appearance asset assigned. Materials with existing appearances untouched. Correct asset type matched per material class.
Material.RenderAppearanceAssetIdAppearanceAssetElementAsset assignment
#195EXPERT

Photorealistic Render Queue

For every 3D perspective view in the project, configure render settings (resolution, quality, lighting scheme) and queue them for batch rendering. Export rendered images to a folder with the view name as filename.
Expected Result
Each perspective view rendered and saved as PNG. Resolution 1920×1080. Correct lighting scheme per view. Batch completes without user interaction.
RenderingSettingsRenderCommandView3D.IsPerspective
#196INTERMEDIATE

Line Style Creator and Applier

Create three new line styles with different weights and colours (thick red, dashed blue, dotted green). Apply each to a set of existing detail lines matching a naming pattern.
Expected Result
Three new line styles in Manage → Line Styles. Target detail lines updated with matching line style. Visual difference visible in the view.
Category.SubCategoriesGraphicsStyleLineStyle creator

sections.title: Advanced Real-World Plugins
sections.content:
🚀
// CHAPTER 15

Advanced Real-World Plugins

End-to-end plugins that combine multiple API areas into complete professional tools

18 challenges
#197ADVANCED

IFC Property Set Writer

Add IFC-compliant Pset_WallCommon properties to all walls: IsExternal, ThermalTransmittance, FireRating. Read values from existing Revit parameters and map to IFC property set convention.
Expected Result
All walls have IFC shared parameters set. Property values match source Revit parameters. Correct IFC export shows Pset_WallCommon populated.
IFC shared parametersExternalDefinitionPset mapping
#198EXPERT

Clash Detection Engine

Build a full clash detection tool: collect all MEP elements and structural elements. Find solid-level geometry intersections (not just bounding box). For each clash, report element IDs, clash location, and severity. Create detail annotations at clash points in affected views.
Expected Result
Clash report with exact intersection volumes, not false positives. Annotation placed at each clash. Export as CSV. Zero missed hard clashes.
BooleanOperationsUtils.ExecuteBooleanOperationBoundingBoxIntersectsFilter pre-filterAnnotation at clash
#199EXPERT

BIM Coordination Report Generator

Generate a full coordination report comparing two model versions (current document vs. a saved copy). Report: new elements, deleted elements, moved elements (location change), and parameter changes with before/after values.
Expected Result
HTML report with clearly sections for additions, deletions, moves, and changes. Element IDs, types, and parameter change details. Exportable for coordination meetings.
Multi-document comparisonLINQ diffsHTML report builder
#200EXPERT

Automatic Drawing Package Builder

One-click drawing issue tool: create all missing floor plan/section/elevation views, place them on correctly-numbered sheets, apply view templates, fill title block parameters, add north arrows and scale bars. Export all sheets as PDFs.
Expected Result
Complete drawing package created from an empty sheet set. All views on sheets. All title blocks filled. PDFs exported named by sheet number.
ViewPlan/Section/Sheet creationViewport placementdoc.Print or PDF export
#201ADVANCED

Specification Writer

Extract all element types from the project and generate a formatted NBS-style specification document. For each type: description, material, finish, dimensions, and applicable standard. Export as a rich text document.
Expected Result
Formatted specification with one entry per element type. NBS-style numbering. Material and parameter data extracted correctly. RTF or HTML output.
ElementType parametersCompoundStructure materialsRich text export
#202EXPERT

Digital Twin Data Connector

Build a plugin that maps Revit element GUIDs to an external digital twin database (simulated with a local JSON file). On model open, sync element data from the JSON. On model change, push updates back. Flag new elements not yet in the database.
Expected Result
Bi-directional sync between Revit model and JSON database. New/changed elements correctly identified. Sync completes within 5 seconds for 1000-element models.
Element.UniqueIdJSON read/writeDocumentChanged + ExternalEvent
#203ADVANCED

Accessibility Compliance Checker

Check all doors for accessibility compliance: min clear width ≥ 850mm, max threshold ≤ 20mm, door hardware type = "lever", no steps within 1500mm of door. Generate compliance certificate per door.
Expected Result
Per-door compliance report with pass/fail per check. Summary: compliant %, non-compliant list. Certificate exportable as PDF per building.
Door parametersRBS_DOOR_WIDTHProximity to stairs
#204EXPERT

Parametric Cost Estimator

Build a complete cost estimating tool. For every element type, read a "Unit Cost" parameter. Multiply by quantity (area, length, volume, count). Aggregate into a Bill of Quantities with labour/material split. Update live as model changes via DMU.
Expected Result
Live cost estimate updating with model changes. BOQ with material/labour split. Total project cost. Export as formatted Excel-compatible CSV.
IUpdater for live updateQuantity extractionCost parameter × quantity
#205EXPERT

Construction Sequence Simulator

Using Revit phases, animate a construction sequence. For each phase, temporarily override element visibility to show: existing (grey), new work (coloured), demolished (red hatched). Capture a PNG per phase. Create an animated GIF from the PNG sequence.
Expected Result
One PNG per construction phase. Each PNG shows correct phase visibility. GIF animates through phases at 1 second per frame. Viewable in browser.
Phase overridesImageExportOptions per phaseGIF from PNG sequence
#206ADVANCED

Carbon Calculator

For each structural and envelope element, read the material and calculate embodied carbon using hardcoded ICE Database values (concrete: 0.159 kgCO₂e/kg, steel: 2.0, timber: 0.46). Report total project embodied carbon in tCO₂e.
Expected Result
Total embodied carbon per element and per material category. Summary: top 5 elements by carbon. Project total in tCO₂e. Exportable report.
Material density + ICE factorsSolid.Volume × densityCarbon factor mapping
#207EXPERT

NLP-Powered Command Interface

Build a text command interface (modeless WPF window). User types natural language commands like "select all walls on Level 2", "create section at grid A", "count doors of type X". Parse commands and execute corresponding API operations.
Expected Result
10+ command types parsed and executed correctly. Unknown commands return helpful error. All valid commands produce correct Revit operations.
Command parserExternalEvent routingCommand pattern
#208ADVANCED

Wayfinding Network Analyser

Build a room-to-room wayfinding graph. Use doors as edges between rooms. Find the shortest path (fewest doors) between any two rooms using Dijkstra's algorithm. Display path in model by highlighting rooms and doors.
Expected Result
Shortest room path correctly identified. Intermediate rooms and doors highlighted in the active view. Works for any two rooms in the model.
Room adjacency graphDoor GetBoundarySegmentsDijkstra algorithm
#209ADVANCED

Space Efficiency Analyser

Calculate the net usable floor area / gross floor area ratio per level. Identify the top 3 "space wasters" (circulation areas with low efficiency). Colour-code rooms in a floor plan view by efficiency band.
Expected Result
NUA/GFA ratio per level. Top 3 inefficient zones highlighted. Colour override applied per room based on efficiency band (green/amber/red).
Room.AreaLevel GFAView element override by room
#210EXPERT

Full Add-In Framework

Build a complete add-in framework: ribbon panel with icons, multiple commands, dockable settings panel, user preferences stored in XML, localised strings (English + one other language), keyboard shortcuts, and a "What's New" splash screen on first run per version.
Expected Result
Professional-grade add-in. Ribbon with grouped commands. Dockable panel. Preferences persist between sessions. Localisation switches language correctly. Splash shows on first run only.
RibbonPanelIDockablePaneProviderXML configResource strings
#211ADVANCED

As-Built Documentation Generator

On project completion, auto-generate a full as-built documentation package: updated floor plans with all room areas labeled, system schematics for MEP, wall sections with layer thicknesses, and a material finishes schedule. All on pre-formatted sheets.
Expected Result
Complete documentation set generated from current model state. All sheets correctly populated. Room areas, MEP systems, and materials accurate to current model.
Multi-view creationSheet automationSchedule creation
#212EXPERT

Real-Time Collaboration Bridge

Using ExternalEvent and a local WebSocket server (or file-based polling), build a simple real-time collaboration tool: two users on different Revit sessions can see which elements the other has selected, highlighted in blue. Changes by User A broadcast to User B's view.
Expected Result
Selection from User A session highlights in User B's view within 2 seconds. Works via file polling. No Revit crashes. ExternalEvent used for all API calls.
ExternalEvent + file pollingSelection overrideThread-safe file I/O
