sections.title: Ribbon & UI Framework
sections.content:
🎛️
// CHAPTER 01

Ribbon & UI Framework

RibbonPanel, PushButton, SplitButton, ComboBox, TextBox, keyboard shortcuts, tooltips, icons

22 challenges
#001BEGINNER

Hello Ribbon Button

Create a ribbon tab called "Studio Tools" with one panel "Utilities" containing a single PushButton that shows a TaskDialog "Hello Revit API". Include a 32×32 icon loaded from an embedded resource.
Expected Result
Custom tab and panel visible in Revit ribbon. Button click shows dialog. Icon renders at correct size without blurriness.
RibbonPanelPushButtonAddPushButtonBitmapSource
#002BEGINNER

SplitButton Family

Create a SplitButton with three sub-commands: "Create Wall", "Create Floor", "Create Roof". Each sub-command opens a simple TaskDialog confirming the action. The last-clicked command becomes the default button face.
Expected Result
SplitButton visible on ribbon. Dropdown shows three items. Last clicked becomes default. Each fires correct sub-command.
SplitButtonSplitButtonDataPulldownButton
#003BEGINNER

Ribbon ComboBox Filter

Add a ComboBox to a ribbon panel populated with all level names from the active document when the document opens. Selecting a level from the ComboBox highlights all elements on that level in yellow in the active view.
Expected Result
ComboBox shows correct level list after document loads. Selecting a level applies yellow graphic override to all elements on that level. Changing selection updates overrides.
ComboBoxComboBoxDataComboBoxMemberViewActivated to refresh
#004INTERMEDIATE

Context-Sensitive Button Availability

Implement IExternalCommandAvailability for three buttons. Button A is only available when a Wall is selected. Button B only when a Floor plan view is active. Button C only when the document has unsaved changes. Buttons grey out when conditions not met.
Expected Result
Buttons correctly enable/disable based on context. No button available in wrong context. State updates immediately on selection/view change.
IExternalCommandAvailabilityIsCommandAvailableSelection check
#005INTERMEDIATE

Ribbon TextBox Live Search

Add a TextBox to the ribbon. As the user types, filter the active view's elements by name/mark matching the input and apply a colour override to matching elements. Clear overrides when TextBox is empty.
Expected Result
Typing in TextBox instantly highlights matching elements. Partial match works. Clearing text removes all overrides. Handles special characters gracefully.
TextBoxTextBoxDataEnterPressed event
#006INTERMEDIATE

Stacked Items Panel

Create a ribbon panel with three StackedItem rows, each containing two small buttons. Row 1: Element tools. Row 2: View tools. Row 3: Export tools. Each button has a 16×16 icon and tooltip with extended description.
Expected Result
Panel shows 3 rows of stacked pairs — compact layout. Icons at 16px. Hovering shows tooltip with title + extended description. All 6 buttons functional.
StackedItemsAddStackedItemsSetToolTip
#007INTERMEDIATE

Ribbon Tab Show/Hide Toggle

Add a ToggleButton to the Add-ins tab. When ON: show a custom contextual ribbon tab with advanced tools. When OFF: hide the contextual tab. Contextual tab only appears for relevant element types.
Expected Result
Toggle shows/hides contextual tab instantly. Contextual tab contains at least 3 buttons. State persists within the session.
ToggleButtonContextualTabTabActivated
#008INTERMEDIATE

Keyboard Shortcut Registrar

Register a custom keyboard shortcut (e.g. Ctrl+Shift+W) for a ribbon command programmatically. Read the current KeyboardShortcuts.xml, add the new shortcut if not present, and save the file. Verify the shortcut works after Revit restart.
Expected Result
Shortcut visible in Keyboard Shortcuts dialog. Pressing shortcut fires the correct command. File modification persists after Revit restart.
KeyboardShortcuts.xmlXML manipulationShortcutKey
#009ADVANCED

Dynamic Ribbon Button Generator

Read a JSON configuration file listing commands (name, icon path, assembly path, class name). Dynamically create PushButtons for each entry at startup. Support adding/removing buttons by editing the JSON without recompiling.
Expected Result
Buttons created from JSON config at startup. Adding a new JSON entry and restarting Revit creates the new button automatically. Invalid entries skip gracefully.
PushButtonDataJSON configDynamic assembly loading
#010INTERMEDIATE

Ribbon Button State Indicator

Create a button whose icon changes based on model health state: green checkmark (no issues), yellow warning (minor issues), red cross (critical issues). Recheck and update icon every time the document changes.
Expected Result
Button icon updates within 1 second of a document change. Correct colour state reflects actual model health. Does not block UI thread.
PushButton.LargeImageDocumentChangedBitmapSource swap
#011ADVANCED

About & Update Checker

Add an "About" button that shows a WPF dialog with plugin version, author, and a "Check for Updates" button. The update check reads a version number from a URL (or local file) and compares to current version. Show "Up to date" or "Update available vX.Y".
Expected Result
About dialog opens modlessly. Version comparison correct. "Update available" shown when remote version is higher. Check runs asynchronously — UI stays responsive.
WPF dialogVersion comparisonAsync HTTP or file read
#012ADVANCED

Multi-Language Ribbon

Implement full localisation for all ribbon button names, tooltips and dialog strings. Support English, Arabic and French. Read locale at startup from a resource dictionary. Switching Revit language should auto-switch plugin language.
Expected Result
All UI text in correct language per Revit locale. Arabic text renders right-to-left correctly. Fallback to English for missing translations.
ResourceDictionaryCultureInfoApplication.Language
#013BEGINNER

Quick Access Toolbar Item

Add a custom command to the Revit Quick Access Toolbar (QAT) programmatically. The command should show the active document's file path. Ensure it appears only once even if OnStartup is called multiple times.
Expected Result
Custom button visible in QAT. Clicking shows file path dialog. Duplicate prevention works — only one button added per session.
QuickAccessToolBarDuplicate checkUIApplication.CreateRibbonTab
#014INTERMEDIATE

RadioButtonGroup Panel

Create a RadioButtonGroup with three options representing display modes: "Wireframe", "Hidden Line", "Shaded". Selecting an option programmatically sets the active view's visual style to the corresponding DisplayStyle.
Expected Result
RadioButtonGroup visible. Selecting each option changes active view DisplayStyle immediately. Current style reflected in RadioButtonGroup on view activation.
RadioButtonGroupToggleButton in groupView.DisplayStyle
#015EXPERT

Plugin Settings GUI with Persistence

Build a full settings dialog (WPF) with: text fields, checkboxes, dropdowns, colour pickers, and a folder browser. Persist all settings to a JSON file in %AppData%. Load on startup. Add a "Reset to Defaults" button. Settings affect plugin behaviour immediately.
Expected Result
All settings persist between Revit sessions. Reset correctly restores defaults. Settings actively change plugin behaviour (e.g. changing default wall type affects create-wall commands).
WPF settings dialogJSON persistenceINotifyPropertyChanged
#016INTERMEDIATE

Ribbon Panel Tooltip Gallery

For every PushButton in your plugin's ribbon panel, add a contextual tooltip image (screenshot of the command result) that appears on hover. Images loaded from embedded resources.
Expected Result
Hovering each button shows a tooltip with image and description. Images sharp and correctly sized. Tooltip appears within standard Revit hover delay.
ToolTipImageSetLongDescriptionBitmapImage from resource
#017ADVANCED

Recent Commands History

Track the last 10 commands run by the user (from your plugin). Add a "Recent" PulldownButton that lists them in order. Clicking a recent item re-runs the command. History persists across sessions in a JSON file.
Expected Result
Recent commands list correct and in order. Re-running from history works identically to original run. List updates immediately. History survives Revit restart.
PulldownButtonDynamic itemsJSON history file
#018INTERMEDIATE

Ribbon Visibility by Project Type

Show different ribbon button sets depending on the open document type: full set for project files (.rvt), family-only tools for .rfa, template tools for .rte. Hide irrelevant buttons automatically on document switch.
Expected Result
Correct buttons shown/hidden for each document type. Switching documents updates ribbon within 0.5 seconds. No buttons shown for wrong file type.
doc.IsFamilyDocumentViewActivated or DocumentOpenedButton.Enabled / Visible
#019BEGINNER

Progress Bar Dialog

Build a WPF progress dialog that shows during a long batch operation (processing 500 elements). Show: progress bar, current item name, count X of Y, elapsed time, and a Cancel button that gracefully aborts the operation.
Expected Result
Progress dialog shows real-time updates. Cancel stops operation cleanly with a partial result report. Elapsed time ticks correctly. Dialog closes automatically on completion.
WPF ProgressBarIProgress<T>CancellationToken
#020ADVANCED

Drag-and-Drop Element Panel

Create a dockable panel that lists element types. User can drag a type from the panel into the Revit canvas to place an instance. Implement drag-start in WPF and intercept the drop to create the family instance at the dropped location.
Expected Result
Dragging from panel to canvas places the family instance at the cursor location. Correct family type used. Instance placed on active level. Transaction committed.
DragDropIDockablePaneProviderPickPoint equivalent via PostCommand
#021EXPERT

Plugin Telemetry System

Build an optional telemetry system that logs: command name, execution time, document title hash (anonymised), Revit version, and error type if failed. Write to a local log file. Add opt-in/opt-out in settings. Export log as CSV.
Expected Result
Telemetry logs created per command run. Opt-out stops all logging. Log CSV parseable. No PII stored — document title is MD5-hashed. Execution time accurate to ms.
StopwatchMD5 hashJSON/CSV log writer
#022INTERMEDIATE

Command Error Reporter

Wrap all IExternalCommand.Execute() calls in a global exception handler. When an unhandled exception occurs, show a friendly dialog with error summary, copy-to-clipboard stack trace, and a "Send Report" button (writes report to a file).
Expected Result
All exceptions caught — no raw Revit crash dialogs. User sees friendly message. Stack trace copyable. Report file written correctly. Result.Failed returned properly.
Global try/catchmessage parameterException.StackTrace

sections.title: Worksharing & Cloud
sections.content:
☁️
// CHAPTER 02

Worksharing & Cloud

Worksets, central files, synchronisation, element ownership and Revit Cloud

20 challenges
#023BEGINNER

Workset Audit Report

For a workshared model, list every workset with: name, ID, kind (UserCreated/FamilyWorkset/ViewWorkset/StandardWorkset), element count, open/closed state, and owner (if any). Export as formatted text.
Expected Result
Complete workset list. Element counts accurate. Kind classifications correct. Report usable for worksharing coordination. "Not workshared" message for local files.
WorksetTableGetWorksetsWorksetKind
#024INTERMEDIATE

Workset Assigner by Category

Move all elements of a specific category to a target workset. For example, move all MEP elements to the "MEP" workset. Handle elements already owned by other users gracefully — report them as skipped.
Expected Result
All accessible elements of the category moved to target workset. Owned elements reported as skipped. Workset assignment verified by re-reading ELEM_PARTITION_PARAM.
ELEM_PARTITION_PARAMWorksetIdCheckoutElement
#025INTERMEDIATE

Element Ownership Checker

List all elements currently checked out (owned) by other team members. Group by owner username. Show element ID, category, name, and owner. Highlight elements owned by the current user.
Expected Result
Complete ownership report grouped by user. Current user's elements distinguished. Count per owner. "No elements checked out" if model is clean.
WorksharingUtils.GetCheckoutStatusCheckoutStatus.OwnedByOtherUserGetWorksharingTooltipInfo
#026INTERMEDIATE

Central File Path Detector

For the active document, determine if it is a local copy, central file, or standalone. If local copy, display the path to the central file. If detached, report "Detached from central". Check if model is cloud-hosted (BIM 360).
Expected Result
Correct file type identification. Central file path shown for local copies. Cloud model correctly detected. All three cases handled.
doc.IsDetachedModelPathUtils.ConvertModelPathToUserVisiblePathdoc.GetWorksharingCentralModelPath
#027ADVANCED

Auto Sync Before Close

Subscribe to DocumentClosing event. If the document is a local workshared file with pending changes, automatically trigger a Sync to Central before closing. Show a progress message. If sync fails, cancel the close and report the error.
Expected Result
Sync triggered automatically on close. Success: document closes cleanly. Failure: close cancelled and error reported. Standalone files not affected.
DocumentClosingSynchronizeWithCentralOptionsdoc.SynchronizeWithCentral
#028ADVANCED

Workset Visibility Manager

Build a modeless WPF window showing all worksets and their visibility state per view. Allow toggling workset visibility per view from the panel without opening Visibility/Graphics dialog. Changes apply immediately.
Expected Result
Panel lists worksets and views. Toggling checkbox changes workset visibility in that view. Changes visible in Revit canvas instantly. Multiple views manageable simultaneously.
View.SetWorksetVisibilityWorksetVisibilityExternalEvent for writes
#029INTERMEDIATE

Borrow Elements Batch Tool

Given a list of element IDs, attempt to check out (borrow) all of them in one operation. Report: successfully borrowed count, already-owned-by-me count, failed (owned by others) count. Retry failed elements after a delay.
Expected Result
Batch checkout attempt completes. Report accurate. Retry logic works for elements freed between attempts. All checkout results logged.
WorksharingUtils.CheckoutElementsElementId listCheckoutStatus
#028bADVANCED

Workset Template Applier

Read a workset template from an XML file: list of workset names and which categories/families belong in each. Create missing worksets and move elements to their correct workset based on the template rules.
Expected Result
Worksets created per template. Elements moved to correct worksets. Unmapped elements remain on default workset. Template XML re-readable for verification.
Workset.CreateELEM_PARTITION_PARAMXML template reader
#030EXPERT

Model History Diff Tool

Open a local copy and its central file simultaneously. Compare element states between the two: which elements were added, deleted, or modified in the local since last sync. Generate a diff report with change timestamps from model history data.
Expected Result
Accurate diff report. Added/deleted/modified elements identified. Change timestamps from SaveToLocalOptions history. Report exportable as HTML.
RevitLinkInstance or second doc.OpenElement comparisonModelHistoryOptions
#031INTERMEDIATE

Workset Colour Coding

Apply a unique graphic override colour to all elements on each workset in the active 3D view. Generate a legend with workset name → colour mapping. Update when worksets are added/removed.
Expected Result
Each workset colour distinct. Legend text note created in view. Colours consistent per workset. Legend updates when new worksets added.
ELEM_PARTITION_PARAMView.SetElementOverridesLegend detail lines

sections.title: Import / Export
sections.content:
📤
// CHAPTER 03

Import / Export

DWG, DXF, IFC, PDF, image export, linked files and import options

22 challenges
#032BEGINNER

DWG Batch Exporter

Export every floor plan view as a separate DWG file to a user-specified folder. Name each file "[SheetNumber]-[ViewName].dwg". Use the current project's DWG export settings. Show progress and report any failures.
Expected Result
One DWG per floor plan. Files named correctly. Export settings from project applied. Failed exports listed with error reason.
doc.Export(DWGExportOptions)ViewSetDWGExportOptions
#033INTERMEDIATE

IFC Export Configurator

Set up a complete IFC export configuration: IFC2x3 Coordination View, all categories included, property sets mapped from Revit parameters, site coordinates from shared coordinates, and space boundaries level 2. Export and report file size and element count.
Expected Result
Valid IFC2x3 file exported. Property sets populated. Shared coordinates correct. Space boundaries present. File opens in IFC viewer without errors.
IFCExportOptionsdoc.Export(IFC)IFCVersion.IFC2x3CV2
#034INTERMEDIATE

Linked DWG Manager

List all linked DWG files in the project with: file name, path, import type (linked/imported), layer count, and element count. Add option to reload all linked DWGs and report which were updated.
Expected Result
Complete DWG link inventory. Reload updates DWGs that have changed on disk. Reports which were refreshed. Import vs linked correctly distinguished.
CADLinkTypeImportInstanceCADLinkType.Reload
#035ADVANCED

DWG Layer to Revit Category Mapper

Import a DWG and analyse its layers. For each DWG layer, create a mapping to a Revit subcategory. Store the mapping in a JSON file. Apply the mapping to convert linework in the DWG to native Revit detail lines on the correct subcategory.
Expected Result
DWG layers mapped to Revit subcategories. Detail lines created on correct subcategories. Mapping JSON reusable. Visual result matches original DWG layer colours.
ImportInstance layer dataGraphicsStyleCategory.SubCategories
#036BEGINNER

PDF Sheet Exporter

Export all sheets in the project to individual PDF files named "[SheetNumber] [SheetName].pdf". Organise into subfolders by discipline. Use A1 paper size at 300 DPI. Show a summary report with file sizes.
Expected Result
One PDF per sheet. Correct naming. Subfolder structure by discipline. A1 format confirmed. Files open correctly in PDF viewer.
PDFExportOptionsdoc.Export(PDF)ViewSet with sheets
#037INTERMEDIATE

Image Export – All Elevations

Export all elevation views as PNG images at 150 DPI to a specified folder. Pixel dimensions should be proportional to view crop box size. Apply a standard graphic scale to each image filename.
Expected Result
One PNG per elevation view. Correct pixel dimensions. 150 DPI. View content fills the image. Named "[ViewName]_[Scale].png".
ImageExportOptionsExportRangeHLRandWFViewsFileType.PNG
#038ADVANCED

NWC Clash Export Prep

Configure and export the model as an NWC (Navisworks Cache) file. Set up export options: export model coordinates, element properties, rooms and areas. Split the export by discipline (structural, architectural, MEP) into separate NWC files.
Expected Result
Three separate NWC files for each discipline. Correct element sets in each file. Model coordinates applied. Properties exported and readable in Navisworks.
NavisworksExportOptionsdoc.Export(NWC)Category filtering per export
#039INTERMEDIATE

GBXML Energy Model Exporter

Export the building model as a gbXML file for energy analysis. Configure: spaces from rooms, construction types from wall types, window-to-wall ratio. Report number of spaces, surfaces and windows exported.
Expected Result
Valid gbXML file. Spaces match room count. Wall surfaces and windows correctly represented. File opens in an energy analysis tool without schema errors.
GBXMLExportOptionsdoc.Export(GBXML)GBXMLSettings
#040ADVANCED

Custom OBJ Exporter

Write a custom geometry exporter that iterates all visible elements in the active 3D view, extracts their triangulated mesh, and writes a .OBJ file with material groups per Revit category. Include a .MTL file with colours from element graphic overrides.
Expected Result
Valid .OBJ file parseable in Blender/Three.js. Materials grouped by category. Correct vertex coordinates in metres. MTL file with colour per category.
Face.TriangulateMaterial.ColorOBJ/MTL file writer
#041EXPERT

Custom IFC Property Set Exporter

Build a custom IFC export that goes beyond standard Revit IFC export. Map custom project parameters to IFC property sets using the IFC export API. Support user-defined parameter-to-Pset mapping from a CSV file. Verify output with an IFC validator.
Expected Result
Custom Psets in exported IFC. Parameter values correctly written. Mapping CSV drives which parameters go where. IFC validator reports zero schema errors.
IFCExportOptions.AddLinkedFilesExportProperty set configurationIFC open-source library
#042INTERMEDIATE

SAT/STEP Geometry Exporter

For a selected element, export its solid geometry as a SAT (ACIS) file using Revit's built-in solid export capability. Also try exporting as a DirectShape-equivalent STEP for use in other CAD tools.
Expected Result
SAT file created and openable in AutoCAD or similar. Geometry matches Revit solid. File size proportional to complexity. No geometry corruption.
Solid.Export (SATExportOptions)BRepBuilderShapeExporter
#043BEGINNER

Linked Revit File Manager

List all Revit link instances in the project. For each: file name, loaded/unloaded status, link type (overlay/attachment), path type (absolute/relative). Offer bulk reload-all button.
Expected Result
Complete link inventory. Status correct. Reload all updates unloaded links. Path types correctly identified. Works for both local and cloud links.
RevitLinkInstanceRevitLinkTypeIsLoaded
#044ADVANCED

Point Cloud Import Analyser

List all point cloud links in the project. For each, report: file path, scan count, region count, estimated point count, bounding box, and visibility state in active view. Offer to toggle point cloud visibility.
Expected Result
Point cloud inventory accurate. Visibility toggle works in active view. Bounding box dimensions in metres. Works for both RCP and PCG formats.
PointCloudInstancePointCloudTypeGetScans

sections.title: Design Options & Groups
sections.content:
🔀
// CHAPTER 04

Design Options & Groups

DesignOption, DesignOptionSet, Groups, ModelGroup, DetailGroup, Group placement

18 challenges
#045BEGINNER

Design Option Inventory

List all design option sets and their options. For each option show: is primary, element count, and whether any views use this option. Display as a hierarchical tree (Set → Options).
Expected Result
Complete tree of sets and options. Element counts accurate. Primary options marked. Views using each option listed.
DesignOptionDesignOptionSetDESIGN_OPTION_ID
#046INTERMEDIATE

Design Option Comparer

For two design options in the same set, count and compare their elements by category. Highlight categories that differ between options. Output a side-by-side comparison table.
Expected Result
Side-by-side table: Category | Option A count | Option B count | Difference. Categories with zero difference shown in grey. Highlighted differences accurate.
DESIGN_OPTION_ID filterLINQ comparisonDesignOption.Id
#047INTERMEDIATE

Model Group Placer

Load a group from another Revit file. Place the group at multiple user-specified locations in the current document. Rotate each instance by a different angle. Track the number of placements and report.
Expected Result
Group placed at each specified location with correct rotation. Each instance independent. Correct element count within each group instance.
doc.PlaceGroupGroupTypeGroup.Location
#048INTERMEDIATE

Group Exploder

Find all model groups in the project. For each group, ungroup it (UngroupMembers) and report which elements were released. Re-apply original element properties (type, location, parameters) from a backup taken before ungrouping.
Expected Result
All groups exploded. Elements retain correct types and locations. Parameter values preserved. No element lost during ungrouping.
Group.UngroupMembersGetMemberIdsPre-ungroup backup
#049ADVANCED

Design Option to Model Merger

Accept a user's choice of which design option to "accept" per set. Move all elements from the accepted option into the main model. Delete rejected option elements. Remove the option sets afterward. Result: one clean merged model.
Expected Result
Accepted option elements in main model. Rejected options deleted. Option sets removed. Model consistent and no dangling option references.
DesignOption.MakePrimaryAcceptPrimary conceptDesign option deletion
#050ADVANCED

Group Version Control

For each model group type, track version via a "GroupVersion" parameter. When a group type changes (via DMU), automatically increment the version number. Show a history of changes with timestamp and description of what changed.
Expected Result
Version increments automatically on group edit. History log accurate. Timestamp correct. Works for groups with nested groups.
IUpdater on GroupGroupType parameterVersion history log
#051BEGINNER

Detail Group Stamp Tool

Create a detail group containing a standard drawing stamp (north arrow, scale bar, title box). Allow the user to place this stamp on multiple sheets at a consistent position (bottom-right corner) from a single command.
Expected Result
Stamp group placed on selected sheets. Correct position on each sheet. All instances of the group update when the source group is edited.
DetailGroupdoc.PlaceGroupSheet coordinate system

sections.title: Constraints, Joins & Locks
sections.content:
🔗
// CHAPTER 05

Constraints, Joins & Locks

ElementJoining, WallUtils, pinning, locking, alignment constraints

16 challenges
#052BEGINNER

Wall Join Order Fixer

Find all wall T-intersections where the join order is wrong (structural wall being cut by non-structural). Use JoinGeometryUtils to swap the join order so structural walls always win.
Expected Result
All T-junctions with structural/non-structural walls correctly ordered. Structural wall profile shown clean at every join. Count of fixed joins reported.
JoinGeometryUtils.SwitchJoinOrderAreElementsJoinedStructuralWall check
#053INTERMEDIATE

Element Pin/Unpin Manager

Build a tool that lists all pinned elements in the project, grouped by category. Allow selectively unpinning elements by category. Provide bulk pin/unpin operations with confirmation. Log all pin state changes.
Expected Result
Complete pinned element inventory. Selective unpin by category works. Bulk operations complete in one transaction. Log records before/after state.
Element.PinnedElement.Pinned setterCategory grouping
#054INTERMEDIATE

Join Geometry Auditor

Find all pairs of elements that are geometrically intersecting (via bounding box overlap) but NOT joined. Report them and offer to join all valid pairs. Skip pairs from different categories that cannot be joined.
Expected Result
All unjoinable intersecting pairs found. Valid pairs joined after confirmation. Invalid pairs (wrong category) reported separately. No crashes from join attempts.
JoinGeometryUtils.JoinGeometryCanJoinGeometryBBox overlap
#055ADVANCED

Alignment Constraint Creator

For selected pairs of elements, create Alignment constraints between their reference planes (e.g. align left face of Wall A to left face of Wall B). Verify the constraint is locked and restricts movement.
Expected Result
Alignment constraint created and visible in Constraints view. Moving one element propagates to the other. Constraint survives model reload.
doc.Create.NewAlignmentReference from facesAlignment constraint
#056ADVANCED

Dependency Graph Builder

Build a dependency graph showing how elements are joined/constrained to each other. Use GetDependentElements() and JoinGeometryUtils to map relationships. Output as a DOT format graph file for visualisation in Graphviz.
Expected Result
Valid DOT file. Nodes = elements, edges = joins/constraints. Clusters by category. Readable in Graphviz. Islands (no connections) identified.
GetDependentElementsAreElementsJoinedDOT format string builder
#057BEGINNER

Mirrored Element Detector

Find all family instances that have been mirrored (HandFlipped or FaceFlipped = true). List them by category. Report the percentage of total instances that are mirrored per category.
Expected Result
All mirrored instances found. HandFlipped and FaceFlipped correctly distinguished. Percentage per category accurate. Zero false positives for non-mirrored instances.
FamilyInstance.HandFlippedFamilyInstance.FaceFlippedFacingFlipped

sections.title: Rebar & Reinforcement
sections.content:
🔩
// CHAPTER 06

Rebar & Reinforcement

Rebar, RebarBarType, ReinforcementData, cover, shape, hooks and fabric

18 challenges
#058BEGINNER

Rebar Quantity Takeoff

For all rebar elements in the project, group by bar diameter and bar type. For each group calculate: element count, total bar length in metres, total weight in kg (using density 7850 kg/m³). Export as CSV.
Expected Result
CSV with: BarType | Diameter | Count | Total Length (m) | Total Weight (kg). Values match manual schedule. Grand total weight at bottom.
RebarOST_RebarREBAR_BAR_DIAMETERLength × area × density
#059INTERMEDIATE

Rebar Shape Reporter

For each rebar bar, report its shape (straight, L-shape, U-shape, spiral etc.) by reading the RebarShape parameter. Group by shape type and count. List shapes that have no matching RebarShape definition in the project.
Expected Result
Shape grouping correct. Counts per shape type accurate. Orphaned shapes (no definition) flagged. Report shows shape code and count.
Rebar.GetShapeIdRebarShapeREBAR_SHAPE
#060ADVANCED

Rebar Cover Zone Visualiser

For every rebar in the project, create a DirectShape cylinder representing the cover zone around the bar. Size the cylinder to the specified cover distance parameter. Show in a dedicated 3D view and colour by compliance (green = OK, red = insufficient cover).
Expected Result
Cylinder DirectShapes around all rebar. Correct radius = cover distance. Green/red colouring per compliance. Dedicated view only shows cover zones.
Rebar centreline curveDirectShape cylinderREBAR_COVER_TYPE
#061INTERMEDIATE

Automatic Stirrup Placer

For all structural concrete beams, automatically place rectangular stirrup rebar at a calculated spacing (beam depth / 2, max 200mm). Use the correct RebarBarType for stirrups. Orient stirrups perpendicular to beam axis.
Expected Result
Stirrups placed in all concrete beams. Spacing ≤ 200mm and ≤ beam_depth/2. Correct bar type used. All stirrups visible in section cut through beam.
Rebar.CreateRebarShape rectangleBeam cross-section geometry
#062ADVANCED

Slab Rebar Mesh Creator

For each concrete floor slab, create top and bottom rebar mats with user-specified bar diameter and spacing. Bars run in both X and Y directions. Cover distance read from slab cover parameter.
Expected Result
Two rebar mats per slab (top/bottom). Bars at correct spacing in X and Y. Cover distance correct. Total bar length matches hand-calculation within 5%.
Rebar.CreateFromCurvesCover distance offsetCurveLoop from floor boundary
#063EXPERT

Fabric Sheet Auto-Placer

For all concrete slabs, place a FabricSheet (wire mesh) instance with the correct fabric type based on slab thickness (lookup table: t≤150mm→A142, t≤250mm→A193, etc.). Set cover and overlap correctly.
Expected Result
Fabric sheets placed on all slabs. Correct type per thickness. Cover and overlap parameters set. Sheets visible in rebar plan views.
FabricSheet.CreateFabricSheetTypeCoverDistance
#064INTERMEDIATE

Rebar Hook Analyser

For all rebar with hooks, report the hook type (standard/seismic/etc.), hook angle, and hook length for each end. Flag bars where hook type doesn't match the structural specification parameter.
Expected Result
Per-bar hook report. Angles and lengths correct. Non-compliant hooks listed. Matches Revit's own rebar schedule values.
Rebar.GetHookTypeIdRebarHookTypeREBAR_HOOK_ORIENTATION

sections.title: Topography & Site
sections.content:
🌍
// CHAPTER 07

Topography & Site

TopographySurface, BuildingPad, SubRegion, site components and contour lines

16 challenges
#065BEGINNER

Topo Surface Point Inspector

Read all points on the topography surface. Report: total point count, min elevation, max elevation, average elevation, and surface area in m². Export the point cloud as an XYZ CSV file.
Expected Result
Accurate stats: count, min/max/avg elevation in metres. XYZ CSV readable in GIS tools. Surface area matches Revit schedule.
TopographySurface.GetPointsXYZ elevationSurface area parameter
#066INTERMEDIATE

Contour Line Generator

From the topography surface point cloud, compute contour lines at 0.5m intervals using bilinear interpolation. Draw each contour as a series of detail lines in a site plan view, colour-coded by elevation.
Expected Result
Contour lines at 0.5m intervals. Elevation-coded colours (blue=low, red=high). Lines are closed or reach view boundary. Accuracy within ±50mm of actual surface.
GetPointsInterpolation algorithmDetailLine by elevation colour
#067INTERMEDIATE

Building Pad Creator

For each building footprint (ground floor boundary), create a BuildingPad at the correct elevation, with thickness 300mm. Assign the correct BuildingPad type. Ensure the pad matches the floor boundary exactly.
Expected Result
One BuildingPad per building footprint. Correct elevation and thickness. Pad boundary matches floor outline. Material correctly applied.
BuildingPad.CreateCurveLoop from floorBuildingPadType
#068ADVANCED

Cut and Fill Volume Calculator

Given an existing topography and a proposed finished grade surface, calculate the cut volume and fill volume using the difference between the two surfaces. Report in m³ and estimate cost at user-specified rates.
Expected Result
Cut volume m³ and Fill volume m³ calculated. Cost estimate at £/m³ rates. Values within 5% of Revit's own earthwork schedule. Net export/import shown.
Two TopographySurfacesGrid sampling methodVolume integration
#069INTERMEDIATE

Site Component Placement Grid

Place site components (trees, bollards) in a regular grid pattern on the topography surface. Each component should sit at the correct topo elevation at its XY position. Space: 10m × 10m grid.
Expected Result
Components placed at correct XY with Z snapped to topo surface. No components floating or buried. All placed at 10m spacing.
TopographySurface.GetGeometryZ from surface at XYNewFamilyInstance on surface
#070ADVANCED

Slope Analysis Colour Map

Compute slope angle at each topo point (gradient from neighbouring points). Create DirectShape triangles over the topo surface, colour-coded by slope angle: green (0-5°), yellow (5-15°), red (>15°). Show in dedicated 3D view.
Expected Result
Colour-coded slope map overlaid on topo. Correct angle classification. Smooth transitions between colours. Performance acceptable for surfaces up to 10,000 points.
GetPoints gradient calcDirectShape trianglesOverrideGraphicSettings colour
#071BEGINNER

Topo Sub-Region Splitter

Split the main topography into sub-regions by building pads and paved areas. Each sub-region should be assigned a different material (grass, asphalt, gravel). Report the area of each material in m².
Expected Result
Sub-regions created. Correct material per region. Area calculation matches Revit schedule. Main topo boundary preserved.
TopographySurface.GetSubRegionIdsTopographySurface (sub-region)Material assignment

sections.title: Assembly & Parts
sections.content:
🧰
// CHAPTER 08

Assembly & Parts

AssemblyInstance, Part, PartUtils, assembly views and part creation

14 challenges
#072BEGINNER

Assembly Inventory

List all assemblies in the project. For each: name, naming category, member count, associated views count, and whether it has a sheet. Sort by naming category then name.
Expected Result
Complete assembly list. Member counts accurate. View and sheet associations correct. Sorted output.
AssemblyInstanceGetMemberIdsGetAssemblyViewIds
#073INTERMEDIATE

Part Creator from Walls

For all selected walls, create Parts from them using PartUtils.CreateParts. Then split each part by level using PartUtils.DivideParts with a horizontal cutting plane at each level. Report parts created per wall.
Expected Result
Parts created from walls. Divided at each level. Each part spans only one level. Part count = wall count × level count in wall's height range.
PartUtils.CreatePartsPartUtils.DividePartsAreElementsValidForCreateParts
#074INTERMEDIATE

Assembly Drawing Set

For each structural assembly, automatically create a full drawing set: plan view, 2 elevations, 3D isometric view, and a parts list schedule. Place all on a dedicated sheet with assembly name as sheet name.
Expected Result
One sheet per assembly. All 4 views placed and cropped. Parts list schedule correct. Sheet title matches assembly name. All in one transaction.
AssemblyViewUtilsCreateDetailViewSectionCreateSheet
#075ADVANCED

Precast Panel Splitter

For each floor slab, use PartUtils to split it into precast panel Parts of a standard size (e.g. 2400mm × 600mm). Orient panels along the short slab axis. Number panels sequentially. Report total panel count.
Expected Result
Floor slabs split into correctly-sized parts. Panel orientation correct. Sequential numbering applied via Mark parameter. Total count matches floor area ÷ panel area.
PartUtils.DivideParts with curvesCurveLoop cutting planesPart Mark assignment
#076ADVANCED

Assembly Clash Finder

For each assembly, check if any of its members geometrically intersect members from a different assembly. Report clash pairs (Assembly A member, Assembly B member) with intersection volume.
Expected Result
All inter-assembly clashes found. Clash pairs listed with assembly names and member IDs. Intersection volume in mm³. No intra-assembly false positives.
AssemblyInstance.GetMemberIdsBooleanOperationsUtilsBBox pre-filter

sections.title: Rendering & Walkthroughs
sections.content:
🎬
// CHAPTER 09

Rendering & Walkthroughs

Render settings, camera paths, walkthrough views, lighting and visual styles

14 challenges
#077BEGINNER

Camera Path Creator

Create a walkthrough view with a camera path that circles the exterior of the building at eye level (1700mm). Generate 36 camera frames (one every 10°). Name the walkthrough "Exterior Circuit".
Expected Result
Walkthrough view with 36 frames. Camera at correct radius and height. Path completes a full circle. Playable in Revit's walkthrough player.
View3D.CreateWalkthroughViewOrientation3D per frameWalkthroughPath
#078INTERMEDIATE

Batch Renderer Settings

For all perspective 3D views, programmatically configure render settings: resolution 1920×1080, quality "High", sun from IES file (or built-in sun), interior lighting scheme. Store settings to each view without rendering.
Expected Result
All perspective views have consistent render settings applied. Settings visible in Rendering dialog for each view. No views with missing/default settings.
RenderingSettingsSunAndShadowSettingsLightingScheme
#079INTERMEDIATE

Sun Path Analyser

For the current project location and date, compute sun position (azimuth and altitude) for every hour from sunrise to sunset. Plot shadow length for a 10m vertical pole. Export results as a CSV with time and shadow data.
Expected Result
Hourly sun position data for a full day. Shadow length correctly computed from altitude angle. CSV with: time | azimuth | altitude | shadow_length_m.
SunAndShadowSettingsSunSettings.SetFrameNumberProjectLocation
#080ADVANCED

Lighting Fixture Zone Assigner

For all lighting fixtures, assign them to a lighting circuit zone based on the room they are in. Set a "Zone" parameter on each fixture. Generate a zone plan view with fixtures colour-coded by zone.
Expected Result
Every fixture has a Zone parameter. Zone assigned by room name/number. Colour-coded plan view shows zone distribution. Fixtures in corridors assigned to "Circulation" zone.
Room.IsPointInRoomOST_LightingFixturesView element override by zone
#081ADVANCED

Exploded View Creator

For a selected assembly or group, create an exploded view by displacing each member element away from the group centroid along the vector from centroid to element centroid. Factor = 1.5×. Animate with 10 intermediate positions.
Expected Result
Exploded 3D view showing all members displaced. Each member at correct direction and distance. Assembly centroid correctly calculated. 10-frame animation sequence created.
View3D displacement pathElement centroidDisplacement Set API (if available)
#082EXPERT

Daylight Analysis Simulation

For each room, compute an approximate Daylight Factor using window area / floor area ratio (simplified DF%). Colour-code rooms: DF < 1% = red, 1-2% = amber, > 2% = green. Place a text note in each room showing the DF%.
Expected Result
DF% calculated for all rooms. Colour overrides applied. Text notes at room centroid showing DF%. Value matches simplified hand-calculation.
Window area per roomRoom.AreaTextNote + override per room

sections.title: Macros, Scripting & Dynamo Bridge
sections.content:
⚡
// CHAPTER 10

Macros, Scripting & Dynamo Bridge

RevitMacros, Python scripts via API, Dynamo graph execution from API, PostCommand

14 challenges
#083BEGINNER

PostCommand Caller

Use UIApplication.PostCommand to trigger built-in Revit commands (e.g. the "Thin Lines" toggle, "Default 3D View", "Close Hidden Windows") from your custom command. Chain three PostCommand calls in sequence.
Expected Result
All three built-in commands execute in order. Thin lines toggles. Default 3D view opens. Hidden windows close. All triggered from one button click.
uiapp.PostCommandRevitCommandId.LookupPostableCommandIdPostableCommand enum
#084INTERMEDIATE

Macro-to-Command Converter

Take an existing Revit macro (C# code in a .rvt file's macro module) and convert it into a standalone IExternalCommand in a separate .dll. The functionality should be identical. Document the differences in the API context between macros and commands.
Expected Result
Standalone command DLL works identically to macro. All macro API calls translated correctly. Document clearly noting macro vs command context differences.
Macro UIDocumentIExternalCommandContext differences
#085ADVANCED

Dynamo Player Automator

Use the Dynamo Revit API to programmatically open, run, and close a specified Dynamo graph (.dyn file) from within an IExternalCommand. Pass input values to the graph before running it. Retrieve output values after run.
Expected Result
Dynamo graph executes without user interaction. Input values correctly passed. Output values retrieved and displayed. Graph closes after completion.
DynamoRevit APIICommandExecutiveDynamoModel.Run
#086INTERMEDIATE

Command History Macro Recorder

Subscribe to ApplicationInitialized and ViewActivated events. Log every built-in command the user runs (via PostCommand detection or event monitoring). Generate a "replay script" as a list of PostCommand calls that would reproduce the session.
Expected Result
Command log captured. Replay script generated as C# code. Running the replay script reproduces the same commands in sequence. Non-recordable commands flagged.
RevitCommandIdExecutedEventCommand log → C# code generator
#087ADVANCED

Python Script Runner

Build a tool that accepts a Python script (as text input in a WPF dialog), compiles it using IronPython/CPython, and executes it with access to the Revit API (doc, uidoc passed as globals). Show output in the dialog's console area.
Expected Result
Python scripts run successfully with Revit API access. doc and uidoc available as globals. Output and errors shown in console. Simple wall-creation script works end-to-end.
IronPython or Python.NETScript globals injectionConsole output capture

sections.title: Performance & Profiling
sections.content:
⏱️
// CHAPTER 11

Performance & Profiling

Collector optimisation, timer benchmarks, memory management, lazy loading

16 challenges
#088BEGINNER

Collector Benchmark Suite

Write benchmarks comparing: OfClass vs OfCategory, ToElements vs ToElementIds, LINQ Where vs native filter, filtered vs unfiltered collector. Report execution time in ms for each approach on a 10,000-element model.
Expected Result
Timing table showing ms per approach. OfClass faster than OfCategory confirmed. Native filter faster than LINQ confirmed. Report exportable.
StopwatchFilteredElementCollector variantsBenchmark loop
#089INTERMEDIATE

Transaction Batch Optimiser

Compare three approaches to setting 1000 parameters: one transaction per element, one transaction for all elements, vs TransactionGroup. Measure and report the time for each approach. Identify and explain the fastest.
Expected Result
Timing report with ms per approach. Single transaction (all elements) fastest confirmed. Explanation of Revit's commit overhead documented. Results reproducible.
StopwatchSingle vs multiple TransactionCommit overhead analysis
#090INTERMEDIATE

Element Cache Builder

Build an in-memory cache of commonly-accessed element data (walls, levels, types) that loads on document open. Commands use the cache instead of re-querying. Invalidate cache on DocumentChanged. Measure cache hit vs miss performance.
Expected Result
Cache populated on doc open. Commands faster using cache vs direct query. Cache correctly invalidated on model change. No stale data after changes.
Dictionary cacheDocumentChanged invalidationLazy vs eager loading
#091ADVANCED

Geometry Extraction Profiler

Profile geometry extraction (get_Geometry) across 500 elements with four Options configurations: with/without ComputeReferences, with/without IncludeNonVisibleObjects, in view vs not in view. Report time and memory per configuration.
Expected Result
4×4 matrix of time+memory results. Fastest configuration identified. ComputeReferences cost quantified. In-view vs no-view impact measured.
Options variantsStopwatch + GC.GetTotalMemoryget_Geometry 500 elements
#092ADVANCED

Async Pre-loader

On DocumentOpened, use a background Task to pre-load and cache: all element categories, all level elevations, all family type names. Store in a thread-safe ConcurrentDictionary. Commands check cache before querying Revit API.
Expected Result
Pre-loading completes in background without blocking UI. Cache available within 2 seconds for a typical model. Thread-safe — no race conditions. Commands use cache correctly.
Task.RunConcurrentDictionaryvolatile / Interlocked
#093EXPERT

Model Complexity Profiler

Build a full model complexity profiler that measures: element count by category, total geometry triangle count, total parameter count, warning count, link file count, and unused family/type count. Output as an interactive HTML dashboard with charts.
Expected Result
HTML dashboard with bar charts and summary cards. All metrics accurate. Dashboard loads in browser. Charts interactive (hover shows detail). Exportable as standalone HTML.
Multiple collectorsTriangle count via TriangulateChart.js HTML output

sections.title: Geometry Algorithms
sections.content:
📊
// CHAPTER 12

Geometry Algorithms

Polygon operations, curve offsetting, surface analysis, spatial algorithms

20 challenges
#094BEGINNER

Polygon Area Calculator

For any closed CurveLoop (room boundary, floor outline), implement the shoelace formula to calculate 2D area without relying on Revit's built-in area. Compare your result to Revit's Area parameter. Report discrepancy.
Expected Result
Shoelace area in m² matches Revit area within 0.1%. Works for convex and concave polygons. Correct sign convention (counter-clockwise = positive).
CurveLoop iterationShoelace formulaUV projection
#095INTERMEDIATE

Polygon Offset (Inset/Outset)

Given a room boundary CurveLoop, compute an inward offset of 200mm (Minkowski-style). Handle convex corners (extend) and concave corners (clip). Draw result as detail lines in the active view.
Expected Result
Offset polygon drawn as detail lines. 200mm inset at all points verified. Concave corners handled without self-intersection. Convex corners correctly mitered.
CurveLoop offsetMiter corner algorithmLine-line intersection
#096INTERMEDIATE

Minimum Bounding Rectangle

For a selected room, compute the minimum bounding rectangle (smallest oriented rectangle containing the boundary). Draw it as detail lines. Report the rectangle's width, depth, and orientation angle.
Expected Result
Minimum bounding rectangle drawn. Verified smaller than axis-aligned bounding box. Orientation angle in degrees. Width and depth in mm.
Rotating calipers algorithmConvex hull firstTransform rotation
#097ADVANCED

Voronoi Room Partitioner

Given a set of seed points (one per space use type), compute a Voronoi partition of the floor plan. For each Voronoi cell, create a room in Revit with the corresponding use type parameter. Use Fortune's or incremental algorithm.
Expected Result
Voronoi cells correctly bounded by floor boundary. Each cell becomes a Revit room. Use type parameter set. Cells cover entire floor with no overlaps or gaps.
Voronoi algorithmRoom.Create from CurveLoopClip to floor boundary
#098ADVANCED

Beam Centreline Graph

Extract all structural beam centrelines. Build a connected graph where nodes are beam-to-beam or beam-to-column connections and edges are beam segments. Compute and display the longest path through the structure.
Expected Result
Graph correctly built. Longest path found via BFS/DFS. Highlighted in active view. Path length in metres reported. Works for non-linear structures.
Beam LocationCurveGraph adjacency listBFS longest path
#099INTERMEDIATE

Isoline Extractor

Given a grid of Z-values sampled from the topography surface, extract isolines (contours) at specified Z-intervals using marching squares algorithm. Draw each isoline as a series of connected detail lines.
Expected Result
Closed or boundary-clipped isolines at each interval. Marching squares produces smooth-looking contours. Each isoline colour-coded by elevation. Line endpoints connected within 1mm tolerance.
Marching squaresGrid sampling from GetPointsDetailLine segments
#100ADVANCED

Shortest Path Through Rooms

Build a room adjacency graph where edge weight = distance between room centroids through the connecting door. Implement Dijkstra's algorithm to find shortest path (by distance) between two user-selected rooms.
Expected Result
Optimal path found. Total travel distance in metres reported. Path rooms and doors highlighted. Faster than BFS solution for distance-weighted graph.
Room centroidDijkstra + door edgesView element highlight
#101EXPERT

3D Convex Hull from Element Centroids

Collect centroids of all structural elements. Compute the 3D convex hull using QuickHull algorithm. Create a DirectShape representing the convex hull solid. Volume of the convex hull = overall structural envelope volume. Report the ratio: structure volume / convex hull volume.
Expected Result
Convex hull DirectShape created and visible. Ratio ≤ 1.0. QuickHull implementation correct for 3D. Works for point sets up to 1000 centroids.
QuickHull 3DDirectShape from hull facesSolid volume comparison

sections.title: Data & External Integration
sections.content:
🔌
// CHAPTER 13

Data & External Integration

REST APIs, databases, Excel, JSON, XML, webhooks and real-time data

20 challenges
#102BEGINNER

Excel Parameter Importer

Read an Excel (.xlsx) file containing columns: ElementId | ParameterName | NewValue. For each row, find the element, locate the parameter, and set the value. Handle missing elements and read-only parameters. Report results row by row.
Expected Result
All valid rows applied. Missing elements flagged with row number. Read-only params skipped with reason. Summary: applied/skipped/failed counts.
EPPlus or OpenXmldoc.GetElement(id)Parameter.Set
#103INTERMEDIATE

REST API Element Sync

Connect to a mock REST API (use JSONPlaceholder or a local Express server) to fetch room data (name, number, area, department). Sync the data back to matching rooms in Revit by room number. Handle HTTP errors gracefully.
Expected Result
Room parameters updated from API data. Matching by room number. API errors handled — partial sync reported. Async HTTP does not block Revit UI.
HttpClient asyncExternalEvent for writesJSON deserialisation
#104INTERMEDIATE

SQLite Parameter Database

Create a local SQLite database that stores all element parameters for every wall in the project. On model save, sync changes to the database. Allow querying the database from a WPF window using SQL (e.g. "SELECT * FROM walls WHERE mark LIKE 'EXT%'").
Expected Result
SQLite database created and synced. SQL queries return correct results. Changes on model save reflected in DB within one sync cycle. WPF query UI functional.
System.Data.SQLiteDocumentSaved event syncSQL query from WPF
#105ADVANCED

Webhook Event Broadcaster

On every DocumentChanged event, serialise the list of added/modified/deleted element IDs as JSON and POST to a webhook URL (configurable in settings). Include model title, timestamp, and change counts. Handle connection failures gracefully.
Expected Result
Webhook receives POST within 500ms of model change. JSON payload correct. Connection failures don't block Revit. Retry logic for failed posts.
HttpClient.PostAsyncDocumentChangedFire-and-forget async pattern
#106ADVANCED

BIM 360 / ACC Data Bridge

Use Autodesk Forge/APS API to fetch project issues from BIM 360. Display them in a dockable panel. Allow linking each issue to a Revit element (by clicking the element). Write the element ID back to the issue's custom attribute via the API.
Expected Result
Issues loaded from BIM 360 in dockable panel. Element linking works. Element ID written to issue attribute. Panel refreshes on token expiry.
APS Issues APIOAuth2 tokenDockable panel + ExternalEvent
#107INTERMEDIATE

CSV Round-Trip Parameter Tool

Export all parameters of selected elements to CSV. Allow the user to edit the CSV externally. Re-import the CSV and apply all changed values back to elements. Show diff: which values changed, which stayed the same.
Expected Result
Export produces valid CSV. Re-import applies only changed values. Diff shows exact changes. Original values preserved for unchanged cells.
CSV export/importValue comparisonParameter.Set batch
#108EXPERT

GraphQL BIM Server

Build a lightweight local HTTP server (using HttpListener) that exposes a simple GraphQL-like query API. Support queries like: elements(category: "Walls") { id mark length } and mutations: setParameter(id: 123, name: "Mark", value: "W-001"). Test with Postman or curl.
Expected Result
Server starts on localhost:8080. Query returns correct JSON. Mutation applies parameter change via ExternalEvent. 10+ concurrent requests handled without crash.
HttpListenerJSON query parserExternalEvent for mutations
#109INTERMEDIATE

XML Project Data Importer

Parse a project data XML file containing room and space assignments. Create rooms that don't exist, update parameters on existing rooms, and delete rooms that are in Revit but not in the XML. Report all changes made.
Expected Result
Model rooms match XML after import. Created, updated, and deleted counts correct. Idempotent — running twice changes nothing on second run.
XDocument / XmlReaderRoom CRUD operationsIdempotency check

sections.title: Print & PDF Output
sections.content:
🖨️
// CHAPTER 14

Print & PDF Output

PrintManager, PrintSetup, PrintRange, virtual printer, batch printing and print queues

16 challenges
#110BEGINNER

Print Settings Reader

Read all saved print settings (PrintSetup) in the project. For each setting, display: name, paper size, orientation, colour/monochrome, print quality, and raster quality. Report how many settings exist.
Expected Result
Complete list of saved print settings. All properties read correctly. Orientation (portrait/landscape) correct. Print quality DPI value shown.
PrintManagerPrintSetupInSessionPrintSetting
#111INTERMEDIATE

Sheet Set Print Manager

Create a named ViewSet containing all sheets matching a filter (e.g. sheet number starts with "A-"). Configure print options: A1 paper, fit to page, hidden lines visible. Queue for printing to a specified printer without showing the print dialog.
Expected Result
ViewSet created with correct sheets. Print options configured. Print job sent to specified printer silently. Confirmation of pages sent shown.
ViewSetPrintRange.SelectPrintManager.SubmitPrint
#112INTERMEDIATE

PDF Bookmark Generator

Export all sheets as a single multi-page PDF. Generate a bookmark tree in the PDF with: discipline group → sheet number → sheet name hierarchy. Use PDF metadata to embed project name and author.
Expected Result
Single PDF with all sheets. Bookmark tree navigable in PDF viewer. Correct hierarchy. Project name and author in PDF metadata.
PDFExportOptionsBookmark via PDFium or iTextPDF metadata
#113ADVANCED

Print Preview Generator

For a selected sheet, generate a PNG print preview showing exactly what will be printed (paper boundary, sheet content, margins). Display in a WPF window before the user confirms printing. Respect the current print setup settings.
Expected Result
Preview PNG accurate — matches actual print output. Paper boundary shown. Margins correct. Preview generates within 3 seconds. User can confirm or cancel.
ImageExportOptions for previewPrintSetup margin dataWPF Image control
#114ADVANCED

Smart Print Scheduler

Build a print scheduler that queues jobs to print at a specified time (overnight batch). Store jobs in a JSON queue. Use Idling event to check the current time and trigger printing when scheduled. Support cancelling queued jobs.
Expected Result
Jobs queued and stored persistently. Printing triggers at scheduled time (±1 minute). Cancellation removes job from queue. Completed jobs logged with timestamp.
Idling time checkJSON print queuePrintManager.SubmitPrint
#115INTERMEDIATE

Revision Issue Printer

Find all sheets with a specific revision cloud. Export them as PDFs with a stamp "ISSUED FOR CONSTRUCTION - Rev [N] - [Date]" overlaid at the bottom right. Watermark text size proportional to paper size.
Expected Result
Only sheets with the target revision exported. Stamp visible at bottom-right. Correct revision letter and date. Text size proportional to A0/A1/A3 paper.
Revision.GetRevisionNumberingPDF overlay post-processingSheet revision check

sections.title: Revit Server & Cloud APIs
sections.content:
🌐
// CHAPTER 15

Revit Server & Cloud APIs

Revit Server REST API, BIM 360, cloud models, file management

14 challenges
#116INTERMEDIATE

Revit Server File Browser

Using the Revit Server REST API, list all projects and models available on a Revit Server instance. Display in a tree view (Server → Folder → Project → Model). Show file size and last modified date for each model.
Expected Result
Tree view populated from Revit Server API. File sizes in MB. Dates formatted correctly. Authentication handled. Error shown for unreachable server.
Revit Server REST APIHttpClientJSON deserialisation to tree
#117ADVANCED

Cloud Model Property Fetcher

Use the APS/Forge Model Derivative API to fetch element properties from a cloud-translated model without opening Revit. List all elements with their GUIDs, categories, and a selected set of parameters. Compare with the local model.
Expected Result
Element properties fetched from cloud API. GUIDs match local model elements. Parameter values comparable. Difference between cloud and local model highlighted.
Model Derivative APIObjectTree + PropertiesGUID matching
#118ADVANCED

ACC Issue Creator from Clash

For each clash found by the internal clash detection tool (Chapter 12), automatically create a BIM 360/ACC issue via the Issues API. Populate: title (element types), description (IDs + location), status "Open", discipline from element categories.
Expected Result
One ACC issue per clash. Title and description correct. Status "Open". Discipline mapped correctly. Issues visible in BIM 360 Issues page.
ACC Issues API POSTOAuth2 tokenClash data → issue payload
#119EXPERT

Live Dashboard from Cloud Model

Build a web page (simple HTML + JS) that polls the APS Model Derivative API every 60 seconds and shows: element count by category, last modified date, and a 3D thumbnail of the model. Embed the 3D Viewer (Forge Viewer) for interactive exploration.
Expected Result
Web page shows live data. 3D Viewer loads and renders the model. Element counts update on poll. Works without opening Revit locally.
APS Model DerivativeForge Viewer.jsPoll interval JS
#120INTERMEDIATE

Model Thumbnail Uploader

Export a thumbnail PNG of the default 3D view. Upload it to an ACC project folder using the Data Management API. Generate a shareable URL. Display the URL in a dialog so the user can copy it to share with stakeholders.
Expected Result
Thumbnail PNG created. Uploaded to ACC successfully. Shareable URL generated. URL opens correct image in browser. All within one command execution.
ImageExportOptionsAPS Data Management API uploadOAuth2

sections.title: Full Capstone Projects
sections.content:
🏆
// CHAPTER 16

Full Capstone Projects

End-to-end plugins combining all API areas — real production-grade tools

18 challenges
#121ADVANCED

BIM Manager Toolkit

Build a complete BIM Manager dashboard plugin with: model health score, workset audit, purge unused, sync report, family audit, warning list, sheet index, and revision tracker — all in one tabbed WPF dockable panel. All operations via ExternalEvent.
Expected Result
Full dockable BIM Manager panel. All 8 tabs functional. Model health score computed. Operations complete without blocking UI. Data refreshes on demand.
IDockablePaneProviderMultiple ExternalEventsTabbed WPF UI
#122EXPERT

Automated Project Setup Tool

One-command project setup from a JSON template: creates levels, grids, worksets, views, sheets, scope boxes, project information, shared parameters, view templates, title block parameters, and initial room layout. Template-driven and reusable across projects.
Expected Result
Fresh project fully configured from JSON in under 30 seconds. All elements created correctly. Rerunning template is idempotent. Different templates produce different project setups.
All creation APIsJSON template parserTransactionGroup for atomicity
#123EXPERT

Structural Steel Estimator

For all structural steel elements (beams, columns, braces), extract profile shape, cross-section dimensions, and length. Look up unit weight from a steel section database (CSV). Calculate total tonnage per section type and overall. Export formal steel take-off PDF.
Expected Result
Steel tonnage accurate within 2% of hand calculation. Section-by-section breakdown. PDF with formal take-off layout. Database lookup handles all standard sections (UB, UC, CHS, RHS).
StructuralFraming parametersSection database CSVPDF report generation
#124EXPERT

Parametric Tower Generator

From user inputs (floor count, floor-to-floor height, footprint shape, structural grid spacing), generate a complete tower structure: levels, grids, columns, beams, floor slabs, core walls, facade curtain walls. All parametric — changing inputs regenerates the tower.
Expected Result
Complete structural tower generated. All levels, grids and structure consistent. Regeneration (delete+recreate) works. Tower with 20 floors generates in under 60 seconds.
All structural creation APIsParametric loopTransactionGroup
#125ADVANCED

Net Zero Carbon Tracker

Build a live carbon tracker dockable panel. Tracks: embodied carbon (from materials), operational carbon estimate (from U-values + floor area + degree-days), and renewable offset (from PV panel area parameter). Show total net carbon in tCO₂e. Update on model change.
Expected Result
Panel shows embodied + operational − offset = net carbon. All three values update on model change via DMU. Values in tCO₂e with correct formula. Exportable report.
Material ICE factorsU-value from CompoundStructureDockable panel + IUpdater
#126EXPERT

Construction Safety Checker

Analyse the model for construction safety issues: no handrail on stairs, excessive ramp gradients (>1:20), doors opening into corridors narrower than 1200mm, plant rooms with insufficient maintenance access (less than 800mm clearance). Generate a formal safety report PDF.
Expected Result
All four safety checks run. Report itemises every violation with element ID and location. PASS/FAIL summary. PDF exportable and printable. Zero false positives for code-compliant models.
Stair railing checkRamp slope calcDoor swing geometryClearance ReferenceIntersector
#127ADVANCED

Drawing Issue Register

Maintain a drawing issue register: every time sheets are printed/exported, record the revision, date, recipient, and purpose. Store in a project parameter on each sheet. Display the full issue history for any sheet in a WPF dialog. Export register as Excel.
Expected Result
Issue history recorded per sheet. History survives model reload. Excel export has correct columns. History can be viewed from sheet context menu. New issues appended correctly.
Sheet parametersDocumentSaved eventExcel export
#128EXPERT

Full MEP Design Automation

Given room layouts and HVAC loads (from space parameters), automatically design: supply air grille positions per room, main duct route along corridors, branch ducts to each grille, duct sizing from air volumes, and place diffuser family instances. All in one command.
Expected Result
Complete duct network from AHU to all diffusers. Duct sizes proportional to air volumes. Grilles at correct ceiling positions. Connectors joined. System visible and traversable.
Duct.CreateRoom HVAC loadsShortest path routingConnectorManager
#129EXPERT

Code Compliance Engine

Build a comprehensive building code compliance engine with 20+ checks covering: means of egress, room areas, ceiling heights, accessibility (doors, ramps, lifts), structural element sizing, fire rating continuity, and natural light requirements. Output a formal compliance certificate PDF.
Expected Result
All 20+ checks execute. Each check: PASS/FAIL with element IDs and measured value. Formal certificate layout. Certificate number and date stamped. Rerunnable as model changes.
Multi-category checksRoom/stair/ramp geometryFormal PDF certificate
#130ADVANCED

BIM Execution Plan Auto-Generator

Read project information parameters, loaded families, view organisation, and workset structure. Automatically generate a formatted BIM Execution Plan (BEP) document as a Word-compatible HTML file. Include: project info, model structure, software versions, data exchange requirements, and LOD schedule.
Expected Result
Complete BEP document generated from model data. All sections populated correctly. Software version from Application.VersionName. LOD schedule reflects element categories. Printable A4 format.
ProjectInfo parametersApplication.VersionNameHTML document generator
#131EXPERT

AI-Assisted Room Layout Optimiser

Given a fixed floor boundary and a list of required rooms (name + min area), use a packing algorithm (simulated annealing or genetic algorithm) to generate an optimal room layout. Place rooms as rectangular bounded areas. Maximise natural light (windows on exterior walls). Output best layout as Revit rooms.
Expected Result
Valid room layout within floor boundary. All rooms meet minimum area. Exterior rooms have at least one exterior wall with windows. Layout score (light access %) shown. Revit rooms created from best solution.
Simulated annealingRoom.Create from CurveLoopExterior wall proximity
#132ADVANCED

Multi-Discipline Coordination Checker

Run a full multi-discipline coordination check across all linked models (structural, MEP, architectural). Detect: MEP-to-structure clashes, rooms missing MEP services, structural members interrupting fire escape routes, and MEP not coordinated with ceiling heights. Output one unified HTML report.
Expected Result
Cross-discipline clashes found. Missing services per room reported. Escape route blockages identified. All linked models included. Single HTML report with discipline filter buttons.
RevitLinkInstance.GetLinkDocumentCross-document geometry checkUnified clash report
#133EXPERT

Generative Design Bridge

Build a plugin that calls an external optimisation service (or local algorithm). The service returns multiple design alternatives (wall configurations, room layouts). The plugin creates each alternative as a separate Design Option in Revit. User can preview each and accept the preferred one.
Expected Result
N design alternatives created as Design Options. Each option contains correct geometry from the service response. Preview switching works. Accepting an option merges it to main model.
DesignOptionSet.CreateDesignOption.CreateExternal service call
#134ADVANCED

Project Handover Package Builder

On project completion, generate a complete handover package: O&M manual data extracted from equipment parameters, as-built drawings (all sheets as PDF), COBie spreadsheet from rooms/assets/systems, and a model health certificate. Zip all into a single deliverable archive.
Expected Result
Complete handover zip file. O&M data covers all equipment categories. COBie spreadsheet valid (COBie Lite). All sheets as PDFs. Health certificate correct. Archive under 100MB for typical project.
COBie data extractionPDF exportZip archive via System.IO.Compression
#135EXPERT

Live Sensor Data Overlay

Connect to a mock IoT sensor data feed (JSON over WebSocket or polling). Map sensor IDs to Revit room elements via a lookup table. Display live temperature, CO₂, and occupancy data as colour overlays on rooms in a floor plan view. Update every 30 seconds via ExternalEvent.
Expected Result
Rooms colour-coded by live sensor data. Colours update every 30 seconds. Sensor-to-room mapping correct. Rooms with no sensor shown in grey. Data legend displayed in view.
WebSocket or HTTP pollingExternalEvent for view overridesRoom colour by sensor value
#136ADVANCED

Specification Compliance Tagger

For each unique element type in the project, check its parameters against a specification database (CSV). Flag non-compliant types with a visual tag in all relevant views. Generate a spec compliance report sorted by specification clause number.
Expected Result
Non-compliant types visually tagged. Report sorted by clause. Compliant types shown green. Clause numbers match specification CSV. Report exportable as PDF.
Spec database CSVElementType parameter checkIndependentTag or TextNote
#137EXPERT

Full Plugin Marketplace

Build a plugin marketplace framework: a server-side JSON manifest lists available plugins with name, version, description, and download URL. Client-side WPF panel shows available plugins, allows install/uninstall/update. Installed plugins are dynamically loaded into Revit without restart using Assembly.Load.
Expected Result
Marketplace panel shows all available plugins. Install downloads and loads DLL dynamically. Uninstall removes from session. Update replaces DLL. New plugin functional immediately after install without Revit restart.
Assembly.LoadFromDynamic command registrationJSON manifestPlugin hot-loading
#138EXPERT

Digital Twin Operations Platform

Build a complete digital twin bridge: map every room and equipment item to a real-world sensor via GUID lookup. Fetch live operational data (occupancy, energy use, faults) from an API. Update Revit parameters in real time. Generate automated fault reports. Alert when sensor data exceeds thresholds.
Expected Result
Live data flowing into Revit parameters. Fault reports auto-generated on threshold breach. Parameters update every 60 seconds. Alert dialog shown for critical thresholds. Full audit trail in log file.
Element.UniqueId to sensor mappingREST API pollingExternalEvent + IUpdaterAlert threshold system

desc: Another 320+ advanced challenges focusing on Ribbon UI, Worksharing, MEP, Cloud, and full capstone projects.

Revit API
Namespace Reference
