sections.title: AddInId
sections.content:
Class AddInId
Inherits: System.Object
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents the unique identifier for a Revit add-in. Used to register updaters (DMU), identify the add-in in event handlers, and create UpdaterId instances. Every IExternalApplication and IExternalCommand has an associated AddInId provided by Revit at runtime.
🟣 Constructors
🟣 public AddInId(Guid guid)
Creates an AddInId from a GUID. This GUID must match the GUID specified in the add-in's .addin manifest file exactly. Mismatches will cause registration failures at runtime.
ParameterTypeDescription
guidGuidThe unique GUID that identifies this add-in. Must match the addin manifest GUID.
// Example — AddInId Constructor
// In IExternalApplication.OnStartup — Revit provides the AddInId
public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
{
    // Revit gives you the AddInId automatically
    AddInId addInId = app.ActiveAddInId;
    Guid guid = addInId.GetGUID();

    // Or create one manually (must match .addin file GUID)
    Guid myGuid = new Guid("12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc");
    AddInId manualId = new AddInId(myGuid);

    // Used when creating an UpdaterId for DMU
    UpdaterId updaterId = new UpdaterId(addInId,
        new Guid("aaaabbbb-cccc-dddd-eeee-ffffaaaabbbb"));

    return Result.Succeeded;
}
🟢 Methods
🟢 public Guid GetGUID()
Returns the GUID that uniquely identifies this add-in. This GUID corresponds to the <AddInId> value in the .addin manifest XML file.
Returns: Guid — The unique identifier GUID of this add-in.
// Example — GetGUID
AddInId id = app.ActiveAddInId;
Guid guid = id.GetGUID();
TaskDialog.Show("Add-In ID", $"This add-in GUID: {guid}");

sections.title: Arc
sections.content:
Class Arc
Inherits: Curve → APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a circular arc in 3D space. Arcs are defined by a center, radius, start angle and end angle. As a subclass of Curve, Arc inherits all curve operations including parameter evaluation, projection, tessellation, and endpoint access. Arcs are used for curved walls, circular dimensions, and any circular geometry in the model.
// Note Arc is immutable — all Create() methods return a new Arc instance. You cannot modify a created arc in-place. Use the appropriate Create() overload for your use case: three points, center+radius+angles, or plane+radius+angles.
🟣 Static Factory Methods (Creation)
🟣 public static Arc Create(XYZ end0, XYZ end1, XYZ pointOnCurve)
Creates a new arc passing through three points. The arc passes through end0, pointOnCurve, and end1. This is the most common overload when you know three points on the arc but not the center.
ParameterTypeDescription
end0XYZThe start point (first endpoint) of the arc.
end1XYZThe end point (second endpoint) of the arc.
pointOnCurveXYZA point that lies on the arc between the two endpoints. Cannot be collinear with end0 and end1.
Returns: Arc — A new Arc object passing through all three points.
// Example — Arc.Create(3 points)
// Create an arc from three known points
XYZ start  = new XYZ(0, 0, 0);
XYZ mid    = new XYZ(5, 5, 0);    // point on arc
XYZ end    = new XYZ(10, 0, 0);

Arc arc = Arc.Create(start, end, mid);

// Read arc properties
XYZ    center = arc.Center;                // center point
double radius = arc.Radius;               // radius in feet
double startA = arc.GetEndParameter(0);  // start angle (radians)
double endA   = arc.GetEndParameter(1);  // end angle (radians)
bool   isBound = arc.IsBound;             // true for bounded arc

TaskDialog.Show("Arc",
    $"Center: {center}\nRadius: {radius * 304.8:F1}mm");
🟣 public static Arc Create(Plane plane, double radius, double startAngle, double endAngle)
Creates a new arc on a specified plane with a given radius, start angle and end angle. The center of the arc is the origin of the plane. Angles are measured from the plane's X-axis in the plane's XY plane.
ParameterTypeDescription
planePlaneThe plane that contains the arc. The origin is the arc center.
radiusdoubleThe radius of the arc in internal units (feet).
startAngledoubleThe start angle of the arc in radians, measured from plane's X-axis.
endAngledoubleThe end angle of the arc in radians. Must be greater than startAngle.
Returns: Arc — A new bounded Arc on the specified plane.
// Example — Arc.Create(Plane, radius, angles) — Quarter circle
// Create a quarter-circle arc (0° to 90°) at radius 5000mm
Plane xyPlane = Plane.CreateByNormalAndOrigin(
    XYZ.BasisZ,
    new XYZ(0, 0, 0));

double radiusFt   = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(5000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
double startAngle = 0;                   // 0 degrees
double endAngle   = Math.PI / 2;        // 90 degrees

Arc quarterArc = Arc.Create(xyPlane, radiusFt, startAngle, endAngle);

// Use to create a curved wall
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Curved Wall"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Wall.Create(doc, quarterArc, wallType.Id, level.Id,
        3.0, 0.0, false, false);
    tx.Commit();
}
🟣 public static Arc Create(XYZ center, double radius, double startAngle, double endAngle, XYZ xAxis, XYZ yAxis)
Creates a new arc with explicit center, radius, angles, and axis vectors. This overload gives full control over the arc's orientation in 3D space without requiring a Plane object. xAxis and yAxis define the arc's reference frame.
ParameterTypeDescription
centerXYZThe center point of the arc's full circle.
radiusdoubleThe radius in internal units (feet).
startAngledoubleStart angle in radians from xAxis.
endAngledoubleEnd angle in radians from xAxis.
xAxisXYZUnit vector defining 0° direction (must be unit length).
yAxisXYZUnit vector perpendicular to xAxis in the arc plane.
Returns: Arc — A new Arc with the specified geometry.
// Example — Arc.Create(center, radius, angles, axes)
// Create a tilted arc in the XZ plane (vertical arc)
XYZ center = new XYZ(5, 0, 3);
double radius = 2.0;          // 2 feet
XYZ xAxis  = XYZ.BasisX;     // horizontal
XYZ yAxis  = XYZ.BasisZ;     // vertical — makes this a vertical arc

Arc verticalArc = Arc.Create(
    center, radius,
    0,              // start: 0°
    Math.PI,        // end: 180° (semicircle)
    xAxis, yAxis);

// Tessellate the arc to get polyline approximation
IList<XYZ> pts = verticalArc.Tessellate();
foreach (var pt in pts)
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Point: ({pt.X:F3}, {pt.Y:F3}, {pt.Z:F3})");
🟠 Properties
🟠public XYZ Center { get; }
Gets the center point of the circle of which this arc is a part. For an unbounded arc (full circle), this is the center of the circle. Available after creation.
Returns: XYZ — The center point in model coordinates (feet).
🟠public double Radius { get; }
Gets the radius of the arc in internal units (feet). Convert to mm by multiplying by 304.8, or use UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits().
Returns: double — The arc radius in feet (internal units).
🟠public XYZ XDirection { get; }
Gets the X-direction unit vector of the arc's local coordinate frame. Angles are measured from this direction. This is the direction of the arc at parameter = 0.
🟠public XYZ YDirection { get; }
Gets the Y-direction unit vector, perpendicular to XDirection in the arc's plane.
🟠public XYZ Normal { get; }
Gets the normal to the plane that contains this arc, computed as CrossProduct(XDirection, YDirection).
🟢 Methods (including Curve inherited)
🟢public XYZ Evaluate(double parameter, bool normalized)
Evaluates a point on the arc at the given parameter. If normalized = true, parameter 0.0 = start point and 1.0 = end point. If normalized = false, parameter is the raw angle in radians.
ParameterTypeDescription
parameterdouble0.0–1.0 if normalized, or raw angle if not normalized.
normalizedbooltrue = parameter is 0–1 fraction of arc length; false = raw arc parameter (angle).
Returns: XYZ — The 3D point at that parameter position.
// Example — Evaluate midpoint of arc
Arc arc = ...;

// Normalized: 0 = start, 0.5 = midpoint, 1.0 = end
XYZ midPoint = arc.Evaluate(0.5, true);
XYZ startPt  = arc.Evaluate(0.0, true);
XYZ endPt    = arc.Evaluate(1.0, true);

// Place a family instance at arc midpoint
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Place at Arc Mid"))
{
    tx.Start();
    doc.Create.NewFamilyInstance(midPoint, sym, level,
        StructuralType.NonStructural);
    tx.Commit();
}
🟢public XYZ GetEndPoint(int index)
Gets the start or end point of the bounded arc. Index 0 = start point, index 1 = end point. Throws if the arc is unbounded (full circle).
ParameterTypeDescription
indexint0 for start point, 1 for end point.
Returns: XYZ — The endpoint in model coordinates.
🟢public IntersectionResultArray Intersect(Curve curve)
Computes the intersection(s) between this arc and another curve. Returns all intersection results. Used for finding where arcs cross lines or other arcs.
ParameterTypeDescription
curveCurveThe curve to intersect with. Can be Line, Arc, Ellipse, etc.
Returns: IntersectionResultArray — Collection of intersection results, or null/empty if no intersection.
// Example — Arc.Intersect with Line
Arc  arc  = ...;
Line line = Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(-10, 0, 0), new XYZ(10, 0, 0));

IntersectionResultArray results;
SetComparisonResult res = arc.Intersect(line, out results);

if (res == SetComparisonResult.Overlap && results != null)
{
    foreach (IntersectionResult ir in results)
    {
        XYZ    pt    = ir.XYZPoint;      // 3D intersection point
        double paramA = ir.Parameter;    // param on arc
        double paramB = ir.Parameter2;   // param on line
        System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Intersection at {pt}");
    }
}
🟢public IList<XYZ> Tessellate()
Returns a polyline approximation of the arc as a list of XYZ points. The accuracy is controlled by Revit's tessellation tolerance. The first and last points match the arc endpoints exactly.
Returns: IList<XYZ> — Ordered list of points approximating the arc.
🟢public Curve CreateOffset(double offsetDist, XYZ normal)
Creates a new curve that is parallel to this arc at the specified offset distance. For arcs, the offset produces another arc with the same center but different radius. The normal parameter defines which side to offset to.
ParameterTypeDescription
offsetDistdoubleOffset distance in internal units (feet). Positive = same side as normal cross product direction.
normalXYZNormal vector of the reference plane for the offset direction.
Returns: Curve — A new Arc at the offset distance (or Line for degenerate cases).
// Example — Offset an arc outward by 500mm
Arc    arc     = ...;
double offsetMM = 500;
double offsetFt = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(offsetMM, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

// Offset outward (away from center) — normal = Z-axis for XY plane arc
Curve offsetArc = arc.CreateOffset(offsetFt, XYZ.BasisZ);
Arc   result    = offsetArc as Arc;

if (result != null)
    TaskDialog.Show("Offset", $"New radius: {result.Radius * 304.8:F1}mm");
🟢public Transform ComputeDerivatives(double parameter, bool normalized)
Returns the position and first/second derivative vectors of the arc at the given parameter. The Transform's origin is the point on the curve, BasisX is the tangent vector, BasisY is the normal (pointing toward center), and BasisZ is the binormal.
Returns: Transform — A frame where Origin=point, BasisX=tangent, BasisY=normal, BasisZ=binormal.
// Example — Tangent direction at arc midpoint
Arc       arc        = ...;
Transform derivs     = arc.ComputeDerivatives(0.5, true);

XYZ pointOnArc = derivs.Origin;   // position at parameter 0.5
XYZ tangent    = derivs.BasisX;   // tangent direction (normalized)
XYZ normal     = derivs.BasisY;   // points toward center

// Place element aligned to arc tangent at midpoint
Transform placement = Transform.Identity;
placement.Origin  = pointOnArc;
placement.BasisX  = tangent;
placement.BasisY  = XYZ.BasisZ;
placement.BasisZ  = tangent.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ);

sections.title: BoundingBoxXYZ
sections.content:
Class BoundingBoxXYZ
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents an axis-aligned bounding box defined by minimum and maximum XYZ points, plus an optional Transform. Used extensively for: getting element extents, creating section views (BoundingBoxXYZ defines the section box), defining 3D view section boxes, and spatial queries. The Transform defines a local coordinate frame — Min and Max are in the transform's local space.
// Critical — Section Views When creating section views with ViewSection.CreateSection(), the BoundingBoxXYZ MUST have its Transform set. A BoundingBoxXYZ with Transform = Identity will create a section at the world origin. Set Transform.Origin to the cut location and Transform.BasisX/Y/Z to the view orientation.
🟣 Constructors
🟣public BoundingBoxXYZ()
Creates a new empty BoundingBoxXYZ with Min and Max set to invalid values and Transform set to Identity. You must set Min, Max, and typically Transform before use.
// Example — Create BoundingBoxXYZ for a section view
// Creating a section perpendicular to a wall
Wall          wall    = ...;
LocationCurve lc      = wall.Location as LocationCurve;
XYZ           midPt   = lc.Curve.Evaluate(0.5, true);
XYZ           wallDir = (lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(1) - lc.Curve.GetEndPoint(0)).Normalize();
XYZ           viewDir = wallDir.CrossProduct(XYZ.BasisZ).Normalize();

// Build the Transform (defines section orientation)
Transform t = Transform.Identity;
t.Origin  = midPt;
t.BasisX  = wallDir;     // horizontal in view
t.BasisY  = XYZ.BasisZ; // vertical in view
t.BasisZ  = viewDir;     // camera direction

// Build the bounding box (extents in local space)
BoundingBoxXYZ bb = new BoundingBoxXYZ();
bb.Transform = t;
bb.Min = new XYZ(-5, 0, -1);    // left, bottom, near (feet)
bb.Max = new XYZ( 5, 10, 1);   // right, top, far (feet)

// Create the section view
ElementId sectionTypeId = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(ViewFamilyType))
    .Cast<ViewFamilyType>()
    .First(x => x.ViewFamily == ViewFamily.Section).Id;

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Section"))
{
    tx.Start();
    ViewSection sec = ViewSection.CreateSection(doc, sectionTypeId, bb);
    sec.Name = "Wall Section";
    tx.Commit();
}
🟠 Properties
🟠public XYZ Min { get; set; }
Gets or sets the minimum corner of the bounding box in the Transform's local coordinate space. For a standard element bounding box, this is the bottom-left-near corner.
🟠public XYZ Max { get; set; }
Gets or sets the maximum corner of the bounding box in local space. For an element bounding box this is the top-right-far corner.
🟠public Transform Transform { get; set; }
Gets or sets the transform that maps from the bounding box's local coordinate space to the world (model) coordinate space. For element bounding boxes this is often Identity. For section views this defines the cut orientation.
🟠public bool Enabled { get; set; }
Gets or sets whether the bounding box is enabled. Used primarily with View3D.SetSectionBox — setting Enabled = false on the section box disables section box clipping in the 3D view.
// Example — Element bounding box and centroid
Element elem = ...;

// Get element bounding box (pass null for model extents, view for view-clipped)
BoundingBoxXYZ bb = elem.get_BoundingBox(null);

if (bb != null)
{
    XYZ min = bb.Min;
    XYZ max = bb.Max;

    // Compute centroid
    XYZ center = new XYZ(
        (min.X + max.X) / 2,
        (min.Y + max.Y) / 2,
        (min.Z + max.Z) / 2);

    // Dimensions in mm
    double widthMM  = (max.X - min.X) * 304.8;
    double depthMM  = (max.Y - min.Y) * 304.8;
    double heightMM = (max.Z - min.Z) * 304.8;

    TaskDialog.Show("BBox",
        $"W:{widthMM:F0} D:{depthMM:F0} H:{heightMM:F0}mm");
}

sections.title: BuiltInCategory
sections.content:
Enum BuiltInCategory
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Identifies built-in Revit categories. Used with FilteredElementCollector.OfCategory() and to compare element category IDs. Contains hundreds of values — the most commonly used are listed below. The integer value of each member matches the category's internal ID in Revit.
🟡 Key Enum Values
MemberValueDescription
OST_Walls-2000011Architectural and structural walls
OST_Floors-2000032Floor slabs and floor elements
OST_Ceilings-2000038Ceiling elements
OST_Roofs-2000035Roof elements (FootPrint, Extrusion)
OST_Doors-2000023Door family instances
OST_Windows-2000014Window family instances
OST_Columns-2000168Architectural columns
OST_StructuralColumns-2001330Structural columns
OST_StructuralFraming-2001320Beams, braces and structural framing
OST_StructuralFoundation-2001300Structural foundations (isolated, wall, slab)
OST_Rooms-2000160Architectural rooms
OST_MEPSpaces-2003100MEP spaces (for HVAC calculations)
OST_PipeCurves-2008044Pipe elements in plumbing system
OST_DuctCurves-2008000Duct elements in mechanical system
OST_CableTray-2008170Cable tray elements
OST_Conduit-2008150Conduit elements
OST_LightingFixtures-2001120Lighting fixture family instances
OST_ElectricalEquipment-2001040Panels, switchboards etc.
OST_Dimensions-2000260Dimension annotations
OST_TextNotes-2000300Text note annotations
OST_Lines-2000530Model and detail lines (parent)
OST_DetailComponents-2000895Detail component 2D elements
OST_Furniture-2000080Furniture family instances
OST_Grids-2000240Grid elements
OST_Levels-2000240Level datum elements
OST_TitleBlocks-2000280Title block family instances on sheets
OST_Rebar-2009030Reinforcement bar elements
OST_Mass-2003400Conceptual mass elements
OST_Areas-2003200Area elements
OST_RevisionClouds-2000575Revision cloud annotations
OST_MechanicalEquipment-2001000Mechanical equipment (AHU, chillers etc.)
// Example — BuiltInCategory usage
// 1. Use with FilteredElementCollector
var doors = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Doors)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .ToList();

// 2. Compare an element's category
Element elem = ...;
if (elem.Category?.Id.IntegerValue == (int)BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls)
{
    Wall wall = elem as Wall;
}

// 3. Get a Category object from a BuiltInCategory
Category wallCat = doc.Settings.Categories
    .get_Item(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls);

// 4. Use in ElementMulticategoryFilter
var cats = new List<BuiltInCategory>
{
    BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls,
    BuiltInCategory.OST_Floors,
    BuiltInCategory.OST_Roofs
};
var envelopeFilter = new ElementMulticategoryFilter(cats);
var envelope = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(envelopeFilter)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .ToList();

sections.title: BuiltInParameter
sections.content:
Enum BuiltInParameter
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Identifies built-in Revit parameters that are present on elements by default (not user-created). Using BuiltInParameter is the most reliable way to access parameters — it is language-independent and faster than name-based lookup. There are thousands of values; the most important are listed below.
🟡 Key Enum Values — Common Parameters
MemberCategoryDescription · StorageType
ALL_MODEL_MARKAll elementsThe "Mark" parameter shown in properties · String
ALL_MODEL_COMMENTSAll elementsThe "Comments" parameter · String
ELEM_FAMILY_PARAMAll instancesFamily name of the element · String (read-only)
ELEM_TYPE_PARAMAll instancesType name of the element · String (read-only)
WALL_USER_HEIGHT_PARAMWallUnconnected height of wall · Double (feet)
WALL_ATTR_WIDTH_PARAMWallWall type thickness/width · Double (feet, read-only)
WALL_ATTR_AREA_PARAMWallGross wall area · Double (sq.ft)
WALL_KEY_REF_PARAMWallLocation line (0=Centerline, 2=Finish Face Ext.) · Integer
FUNCTION_PARAMWall/Floor typeFunction: 0=Interior, 1=Exterior, 2=Foundation… · Integer
FLOOR_ATTR_THICKNESS_PARAMFloor typeFloor type thickness · Double (feet)
ROOM_NAMERoomRoom name · String
ROOM_NUMBERRoomRoom number · String
ROOM_AREARoomRoom area · Double (sq.ft)
ROOM_DEPARTMENTRoomRoom department · String
LEVEL_ELEVLevelLevel elevation · Double (feet)
RBS_PIPE_DIAMETER_PARAMPipePipe diameter · Double (feet)
RBS_CURVE_WIDTH_PARAMDuctDuct width · Double (feet)
RBS_CURVE_HEIGHT_PARAMDuctDuct height · Double (feet)
CURVE_ELEM_LENGTHCurves (walls, pipes)Length of curve element · Double (feet)
MATERIAL_ID_PARAMExtrusion, geometryMaterial assignment · ElementId
SYMBOL_ID_PARAMFamilySymbolType mark of a family type · String
HOST_AREA_COMPUTEDHosted elementsGross area of the host · Double
ELEM_PARTITION_PARAMAll (workshared)Workset ID the element belongs to · Integer
PHASE_CREATEDAll elementsPhase the element was created in · ElementId
PHASE_DEMOLISHEDAll elementsPhase the element was demolished in · ElementId
STRUCTURAL_SECTION_SHAPEStructural framingProfile shape type of a structural member · Integer (enum)
STAIRS_ACTUAL_RISER_HEIGHTStairsActual riser height · Double (feet)
STAIRS_RISER_NUMBERStairsNumber of risers · Integer
VIEW_PHASEViewPhase assigned to a view · ElementId
DESIGN_OPTION_IDDesign option elementsThe design option this element belongs to · ElementId
// Example — BuiltInParameter access patterns
Element elem = ...;

// Read a string parameter
Parameter mark = elem.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);
string markVal = mark?.AsString() ?? "";

// Read a double parameter with unit conversion
Wall wall = elem as Wall;
if (wall != null)
{
    double heightFt = wall
        .get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.WALL_USER_HEIGHT_PARAM)
        .AsDouble();
    double heightMm = UnitUtils
        .ConvertFromInternalUnits(heightFt, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
}

// Set a parameter inside a transaction
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Set Mark"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Parameter p = elem.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK);
    if (p != null && !p.IsReadOnly)
        p.Set("W-001");
    tx.Commit();
}

// Read ElementId parameter (phase)
Parameter phaseParam = elem.get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter.PHASE_CREATED);
ElementId phaseId    = phaseParam?.AsElementId();
if (phaseId != ElementId.InvalidElementId)
{
    Phase phase = doc.GetElement(phaseId) as Phase;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Phase: {phase?.Name}");
}

sections.title: Color
sections.content:
Class Color
Inherits: APIObject
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents an RGB colour used throughout the Revit API for graphic overrides, material colours, line colours, and fill pattern colours. Unlike System.Drawing.Color, Revit's Color uses byte components (0–255 each) and has no alpha channel support at the API level.
🟣 Constructors
🟣public Color(byte red, byte green, byte blue)
Creates a new Color from red, green, and blue byte components (each 0–255). This is the only constructor — there are no named colour constants in the Revit Color class.
ParameterTypeDescription
redbyteRed component (0–255)
greenbyteGreen component (0–255)
bluebyteBlue component (0–255)
// Example — Color with OverrideGraphicSettings
// Define standard colours
Color red     = new Color(255, 0,   0);
Color green   = new Color(0,   200, 80);
Color blue    = new Color(30,  120, 255);
Color orange  = new Color(255, 140, 0);

// Apply to view override — highlight external walls red
var overrides = new OverrideGraphicSettings();
overrides.SetProjectionLineColor(red);
overrides.SetCutLineColor(red);
overrides.SetCutFillColor(red);
overrides.SetSurfaceForegroundPatternColor(red);

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Override"))
{
    tx.Start();
    var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
        .OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>()
        .Where(w => w.WallType.Function == WallFunction.Exterior);

    foreach (var wall in walls)
        doc.ActiveView.SetElementOverrides(wall.Id, overrides);
    tx.Commit();
}

// Apply colour to a material
using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Material Color"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Material mat = doc.GetElement(matId) as Material;
    if (mat != null)
        mat.Color = new Color(200, 180, 140); // beige
    tx.Commit();
}
🟠 Properties
🟠public byte Red { get; }
Gets the red component (0–255) of this colour.
🟠public byte Green { get; }
Gets the green component (0–255) of this colour.
🟠public byte Blue { get; }
Gets the blue component (0–255) of this colour.
🟠public bool IsValid { get; }
Returns true if this Color was created with valid RGB values. An invalid Color has undefined R,G,B values and should not be used in API calls.

sections.title: CurveLoop
sections.content:
Class CurveLoop
Inherits: APIObject · IEnumerable<Curve>
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents a closed or open sequence of connected curves. CurveLoop is the primary way to define profiles for Floor.Create(), ceiling profiles, void extrusions, filled regions, and room separators. The curves must be connected end-to-start — the endpoint of each curve must match the start of the next within tolerance.
// Warning — Curve Connectivity All curves in a CurveLoop must be end-to-end connected. If curves don't connect within Revit's tolerance, API calls using the CurveLoop will fail with an exception. Use CurveLoop.IsOpen() to verify, and ensure curves are appended in order.
🟣 Constructors
🟣public CurveLoop()
Creates an empty CurveLoop. Curves are added using Append(). The loop can also be created from an existing IEnumerable of curves using CreateViaThicken() or static factory methods.
// Example — CurveLoop for a rectangular floor
// Create a 6m × 4m rectangular room profile
XYZ p0 = new XYZ(0,  0, 0);
XYZ p1 = new XYZ(20, 0, 0);   // 6096mm ≈ 20 feet
XYZ p2 = new XYZ(20, 13, 0);  // ~4m
XYZ p3 = new XYZ(0,  13, 0);

CurveLoop loop = new CurveLoop();
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(p0, p1));
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(p1, p2));
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(p2, p3));
loop.Append(Line.CreateBound(p3, p0));

// Verify it's closed
bool isOpen = loop.IsOpen();   // should be false
double perimeter = loop.GetExactLength();

// Use to create a floor
FloorType ft = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(FloorType)).Cast<FloorType>().First();
Level lv = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Level)).Cast<Level>().First();

using (var tx = new Transaction(doc, "Create Floor"))
{
    tx.Start();
    Floor.Create(doc, new List<CurveLoop> { loop }, ft.Id, lv.Id);
    tx.Commit();
}
🟢 Methods
🟢public void Append(Curve curve)
Adds a curve to the end of this loop. The curve's start point must be within tolerance of the previous curve's end point. The order of Append() calls defines the winding direction of the loop.
🟢public bool IsOpen()
Returns true if the loop's last curve end does not connect back to the first curve's start (open chain). For use as a floor or room profile, IsOpen() must return false.
Returns: bool — false = closed loop (correct for floor profiles); true = open chain.
🟢public double GetExactLength()
Computes the exact perimeter (sum of all curve lengths) of this loop in internal units (feet). Convert to mm with ×304.8.
Returns: double — Total perimeter length in feet.
🟢public static CurveLoop CreateViaThicken(Curve curve, double thickness, XYZ normal)
Creates a closed CurveLoop by offsetting a single open curve by the specified thickness, creating a "slab" profile (two parallel curves with end caps). Very useful for creating wall-like floor profiles from a single centreline.
ParameterTypeDescription
curveCurveThe centreline curve to thicken.
thicknessdoubleThe total thickness in feet. Curve is offset thickness/2 on each side.
normalXYZNormal to the plane containing the curve (e.g. XYZ.BasisZ for horizontal curves).
Returns: CurveLoop — A closed loop representing the thickened profile.
// Example — CreateViaThicken for a strip footing
// Create a 600mm-wide strip footing profile along a wall centreline
Line   wallCentreline = Line.CreateBound(new XYZ(0,0,0), new XYZ(10,0,0));
double widthFt         = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(600, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);

CurveLoop footingProfile = CurveLoop.CreateViaThicken(
    wallCentreline,
    widthFt,
    XYZ.BasisZ);   // Z-normal = horizontal plane

TaskDialog.Show("Profile",
    $"Perimeter: {footingProfile.GetExactLength() * 304.8:F0}mm");
🟢public void Flip()
Reverses the winding direction of the loop (changes the loop from clockwise to counter-clockwise or vice versa). This also reverses the direction of each curve in the loop. Useful when Revit requires a specific winding order.
🟢public CurveLoop CreateTransformed(Transform transform)
Returns a new CurveLoop that is this loop transformed by the given Transform. The original loop is unchanged. Used to move, rotate, or scale a loop profile.
// Example — Copy loop to a different level
CurveLoop groundFloorProfile = ...;  // at Z = 0

// Move the profile up to floor 2 (elevation 4m = 13.12 ft)
double elevation = UnitUtils.ConvertToInternalUnits(4000, UnitTypeId.Millimeters);
Transform moveUp = Transform.CreateTranslation(new XYZ(0, 0, elevation));

CurveLoop upperFloorProfile = groundFloorProfile.CreateTransformed(moveUp);

sections.title: Element
sections.content:
Class Element
Inherits: APIObject · All model elements inherit from this class
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
The base class for every element in the Revit model. Both instance elements (Wall, Floor, FamilyInstance) and type elements (WallType, FamilySymbol) derive from Element. Provides access to universal properties: Id, Name, Category, Parameters, Geometry, BoundingBox, Level, and dependent elements.
🟠 Core Properties
🟠public ElementId Id { get; }
Gets the unique integer identifier of this element within the document. ElementId values are stable for the lifetime of the element in the document but can change after detach/reattach operations. Use UniqueId (GUID string) for cross-document identification.
🟠public string Name { get; set; }
Gets or sets the element's name. For instances this is typically empty; for types (WallType, FamilySymbol) this is the type name. Setting Name requires an open Transaction.
🟠public Category Category { get; }
Gets the Revit category this element belongs to. The Category.Id.IntegerValue can be cast to BuiltInCategory for comparison. May be null for some element types.
🟠public string UniqueId { get; }
Gets the globally unique identifier (GUID string) for this element. Unlike Id (integer), UniqueId is stable across documents and is used for IFC GUID mapping and cross-document element tracking.
🟠public ParameterSet Parameters { get; }
Gets the complete set of all parameters available on this element (both instance and type parameters accessible through the instance). Iterate to discover all parameters or use get_Parameter() for direct access.
🟠public ElementId LevelId { get; }
Gets the ElementId of the Level this element is associated with. Returns ElementId.InvalidElementId if the element has no level association (e.g. level elements themselves, or model-level elements).
🟠public bool Pinned { get; set; }
Gets or sets whether this element is pinned (locked from movement). Setting to true prevents the element from being moved, rotated, or deleted by the user. Setting requires an open Transaction.
🟢 Key Methods
🟢public Parameter get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter parameterId)
Retrieves a parameter by its built-in parameter identifier. Returns null if the parameter is not present on this element. This is the fastest and most reliable way to access parameters — language-independent and always consistent.
ParameterTypeDescription
parameterIdBuiltInParameterThe enum value identifying the built-in parameter to retrieve.
Returns: Parameter — The parameter object, or null if not available on this element.
🟢public Parameter get_Parameter(Guid guid)
Retrieves a shared parameter by its GUID. GUIDs are defined in the shared parameter file and are stable across documents and Revit versions — use this for shared parameters in production add-ins.
🟢public Parameter LookupParameter(string name)
Finds a parameter by name. Fragile — breaks if parameter names are translated or renamed. Use get_Parameter(BuiltInParameter) or get_Parameter(Guid) when possible. Returns null if not found.
🟢public GeometryElement get_Geometry(Options options)
Returns the geometric representation of the element. The Options object controls: which view controls visibility, whether references are computed (required for dimensioning), and whether invisible objects are included.
// Example — Extracting geometry from an element
Element elem = ...;

// Configure options
var opts = new Options()
{
    ComputeReferences       = true,    // REQUIRED for dimensioning
    IncludeNonVisibleObjects = false,
    View                    = doc.ActiveView
};

GeometryElement geomElem = elem.get_Geometry(opts);
double totalVolume = 0;

foreach (GeometryObject obj in geomElem)
{
    if (obj is Solid solid && solid.Volume > 1e-9)
    {
        totalVolume += solid.Volume;

        foreach (Face face in solid.Faces)
        {
            Reference faceRef = face.Reference;   // for dimensions
            XYZ normal = face.ComputeNormal(new UV(.5, .5));
        }
    }
    else if (obj is GeometryInstance gi)
    {
        // FamilyInstance geometry is inside GeometryInstance
        foreach (GeometryObject instObj in gi.GetInstanceGeometry())
        {
            if (instObj is Solid s && s.Volume > 1e-9)
                totalVolume += s.Volume;
        }
    }
}
TaskDialog.Show("Volume",
    $"{totalVolume * 0.0283168:F4} m³");
🟢public BoundingBoxXYZ get_BoundingBox(View view)
Gets the bounding box of this element. Pass null to get the model extents (not clipped by any view). Pass a View to get the bounding box as visible in that view. Returns null if the element has no geometry.
🟢public ElementId GetTypeId()
Returns the ElementId of the element's type (e.g. for a Wall instance, returns the WallType ID). Use doc.GetElement() on this ID and cast to the appropriate type class.
Returns: ElementId — The ID of the element's type, or InvalidElementId if it has no type.
🟢public ICollection<ElementId> GetDependentElements(ElementFilter filter)
Returns IDs of all elements that are dependent on (owned by, hosted by, or constrained to) this element. Pass null to get all dependents, or pass a filter to narrow the results. Useful for finding doors/windows in a wall, tags on an element etc.
// Example — Get all elements dependent on a wall (doors, windows, tags)
Wall wall = ...;

// Get all dependents (doors, windows, wall tags, dimension references)
ICollection<ElementId> depIds = wall.GetDependentElements(null);

// Get only doors and windows (filtered)
var openingFilter = new ElementMulticategoryFilter(
    new List<BuiltInCategory>
    {
        BuiltInCategory.OST_Doors,
        BuiltInCategory.OST_Windows
    });

ICollection<ElementId> openingIds = wall.GetDependentElements(openingFilter);

foreach (var id in openingIds)
{
    FamilyInstance fi = doc.GetElement(id) as FamilyInstance;
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"{fi?.Symbol.Family.Name}: {fi?.Symbol.Name}");
}

sections.title: ElementId
sections.content:
Class ElementId
Inherits: System.Object
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
Represents the unique integer identifier of an element within a Revit document. ElementIds are used throughout the API to reference elements, categories, parameters, and other objects. The special value ElementId.InvalidElementId (-1) indicates no element or an invalid reference.
🟣 Constructors
🟣public ElementId(int value)
Creates an ElementId from an integer value. The value should be a valid element ID from the document, or a BuiltInCategory/BuiltInParameter cast to int. Creating an ElementId with an arbitrary integer does not guarantee the element exists.
// Example — ElementId usage patterns
// 1. From a known element ID (e.g. stored externally)
ElementId id = new ElementId(123456);
Element elem = doc.GetElement(id);   // returns null if not found

// 2. From a BuiltInCategory (for category-based operations)
ElementId wallCatId = new ElementId(BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls);

// 3. Check for invalid ID
if (elem?.LevelId == ElementId.InvalidElementId)
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine("Element has no level");

// 4. Compare element IDs
ElementId id1 = elem1.Id;
ElementId id2 = elem2.Id;
bool same = id1 == id2;          // operator overload
bool same2 = id1.Equals(id2);   // or Equals()

// 5. Integer value for external storage
int intVal = elem.Id.IntegerValue;
// Store intVal in database, read back with: new ElementId(intVal)

// 6. Get all element IDs from a collector (fast — no element objects loaded)
ICollection<ElementId> allWallIds = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .ToElementIds();

System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine($"Wall count: {allWallIds.Count}");
🟠 Properties
🟠public int IntegerValue { get; }
Gets the integer value of this ElementId. Use this to store element IDs in external databases or compare with BuiltInCategory/BuiltInParameter integer values.
🟠public static ElementId InvalidElementId { get; }
Gets the singleton invalid ElementId (integer value -1). Used as a sentinel value throughout the API to indicate "no element", "not set", or "invalid reference". Always compare against this constant rather than constructing new ElementId(-1).

sections.title: FilteredElementCollector
sections.content:
Class FilteredElementCollector
Inherits: APIObject · IEnumerable<Element> · IEnumerable<ElementId>
Namespace: Autodesk.Revit.DB · Assembly: RevitAPI.dll
The primary query engine for retrieving elements from a Revit document. Supports both quick (native) filters that run in Revit's engine with near-zero overhead, and slow (LINQ) filters that iterate element by element. Always apply quick filters first, then LINQ filters. The collector is lazy — elements are retrieved when the result is enumerated.
// Performance Tip Chain quick filters in this order for maximum performance: OfClass() → OfCategory() → WhereElementIsNotElementType() → WherePasses(nativeFilter). Apply LINQ .Where() only after all native filters. Use .ToElementIds() when you only need IDs — it avoids loading full element objects.
🟣 Constructors
🟣public FilteredElementCollector(Document document)
Creates a collector that will search the entire document. This is the most common constructor — it searches all elements in all views and all levels.
🟣public FilteredElementCollector(Document document, ElementId viewId)
Creates a collector restricted to elements visible in the specified view. Only elements that are visible (not hidden, not in hidden categories) in the view will be returned. This respects crop regions, workset visibility, and view-specific overrides.
// Example — FilteredElementCollector patterns
// ── QUICK FILTERS (native, fast) ──

// By class (fastest)
var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .Cast<Wall>()
    .ToList();

// By category — instances only
var doors = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfCategory(BuiltInCategory.OST_Doors)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .ToList();

// Types only
var wallTypes = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(WallType))
    .Cast<WallType>()
    .ToList();

// In a specific view only
var visibleWalls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc, doc.ActiveView.Id)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .Cast<Wall>()
    .ToList();

// ── PARAMETER FILTER (native, slower than OfClass but still native) ──
var markProvider = new ParameterValueProvider(
    new ElementId(BuiltInParameter.ALL_MODEL_MARK));
var rule = new FilterStringRule(
    markProvider, new FilterStringContains(), "EXT");
var extWalls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .WherePasses(new ElementParameterFilter(rule))
    .Cast<Wall>()
    .ToList();

// ── MULTIPLE CATEGORIES ──
var multiFilter = new ElementMulticategoryFilter(
    new List<BuiltInCategory>
    { BuiltInCategory.OST_Walls, BuiltInCategory.OST_Floors });
var structural = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .WherePasses(multiFilter)
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()
    .ToList();

// ── ELEMENT IDs ONLY (fast, less memory) ──
ICollection<ElementId> ids = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .ToElementIds();

// ── LINQ FILTER (after native filters) ──
var longWalls = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(Wall))
    .Cast<Wall>()
    .Where(w => (w.Location as LocationCurve)?.Curve.Length > 10)
    .ToList();
🟢 Quick Filter Methods
🟢public FilteredElementCollector OfClass(Type type)
Restricts the collector to elements of the specified .NET class type. This is the fastest filter available — runs entirely in Revit's native engine. Note: OfClass(typeof(View)) includes all view subtypes; use LINQ to further refine.
🟢public FilteredElementCollector OfCategory(BuiltInCategory category)
Restricts the collector to elements belonging to the specified built-in category. By default includes both instances and types — chain with WhereElementIsNotElementType() to get instances only.
🟢public FilteredElementCollector WhereElementIsNotElementType()
Restricts results to instance elements only (excludes WallType, FamilySymbol, etc.). Pair with OfCategory() to get placed instances.
🟢public FilteredElementCollector WhereElementIsElementType()
Restricts results to type elements only (WallType, FloorType, FamilySymbol, etc.). Use this to collect all types of a category.
🟢public FilteredElementCollector WherePasses(ElementFilter filter)
Applies any ElementFilter to the collector. Use with: ElementParameterFilter, ElementLevelFilter, BoundingBoxIntersectsFilter, ElementMulticategoryFilter, LogicalAndFilter, LogicalOrFilter, and ExclusionFilter.
🟢 Retrieval Methods
🟢public IList<Element> ToElements()
Executes the query and returns all matching elements as a list. The most common retrieval method. Returns an empty list (not null) if no elements match.
🟢public ICollection<ElementId> ToElementIds()
Executes the query and returns element IDs only. Significantly faster and less memory-intensive than ToElements() when you only need IDs for deletion, selection, or external storage.
🟢public Element FirstElement()
Returns the first matching element, or null if no elements match. Use when you expect exactly one result (e.g. finding a specific level by name). More efficient than ToElements().FirstOrDefault().
